[B41] PRINTING -- B41 covers inventions concerned with printing operations, the preparation of printing forms and the transfer of images or text onto substrates, as well as the machines, devices and processes used to produce printed matter on paper, plastic, metal or other materials, encompassing both analog and digital printing methods.
[B41B] MACHINES OR ACCESSORIES FOR MAKING, SETTING, OR DISTRIBUTING TYPE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus for mechanical reproduction of printing surfaces for stereotype printing -> B41D1/00 - B41D5/00 - Shaping elastic or deformable material to form printing surfaces -> B41D7/00 - Printing plates or foils -> B41N1/00  **Glossary:** - Printing: after applying ink or colour onto the surface of said specific type faces, said inked type faces are applied by contact pressure onto the surface of a substrate, e.g. paper, to apply the inked characters or symbols or lines onto the substrate
[B41B 1/00] Elements or appliances for hand composition -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 1/02] Printing letters -- This CPC class B41B 1/02 covers elements or appliances used in hand composition for printing letters, falling under machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type within the broader printing operations.
[B41B 1/025] for languages in which the latin alphabet is not used -- This subclass concerns devices and accessories used in manual type composition for scripts that do not use the Latin alphabet, such as Cyrillic, Arabic, Greek, Chinese or other non-Latin character sets. It includes specialized type matrices, composing sticks, and related hand-operated tools designed to set and arrange characters of those languages for printing.
[B41B 1/04] Quadrats or quads: Spaces or other justifiers -- The entry defines devices such as quadrats or quads that insert uniform spaces or provide justification when arranging type manually, used in hand-composition printing presses to ensure even spacing and alignment of characters.
[B41B 1/06] for special purposes -- B41B 1/06 covers hand-operated devices and accessories used in manual type composition that are designed for special purposes, such as producing decorative characters, embossing, or other non-standard printing effects. The classification includes specialized tools and elements that modify or enhance the layout of type beyond ordinary composition tasks.
[B41B 1/08] for inserting latest news -- B41B 1/08 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to elements or appliances used in hand composition for inserting the latest news, typically in printing processes. This classification is specific to devices that allow for quick updates or additions of current information into printed materials.
[B41B 1/10] for inserting advertisements -- The CPC code B41B 1/10 covers elements or appliances for hand-composed printing that are designed to insert advertisements into printed material, typically used with machines for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 11/00] Details of, or accessories for, machines for mechanical composition using matrices for individual characters which are selected and assembled for type casting or moulding -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 11/02] Matrices -- B41B 11/02 covers the matrices themselves-individual character blocks or moulds used in mechanical type composition machines that cast or mould type by selecting and assembling matrices for each character. This includes details of, or accessories for, such matrices in printing equipment.
[B41B 11/04] Space bands -- Space bands are narrow strips inserted between individual type matrices in mechanical composition machines to provide adjustable spacing and justification, allowing precise control of word and line gaps. They are accessories for printing equipment that uses matrix-based type casting or moulding.
[B41B 11/06] Storage devices for matrices or space bands -- B41B 11/06 covers devices that store matrices or space bands used in mechanical type composition, such as trays, racks or containers that hold individual character molds for later casting or assembling.
[B41B 11/08] Magazines for matrices -- Magazines for matrices are storage or feeding devices that hold the individual character matrices used in mechanical composition machines, allowing the selected matrices to be positioned for type casting or moulding. The magazines keep the matrices organized and enable rapid selection and placement during printing operations. This sub-class falls under machines and accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 11/10] for composing by hand -- This class covers hand-operated devices and accessories used to compose type by selecting individual character matrices and arranging them for casting or moulding. It falls within printing machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 11/12] Attachment of magazines to machines -- B41B 11/12 covers the means for attaching magazines or similar loading devices to mechanical type-setting machines that use matrices for individual characters. It includes brackets, clamps, drives and other accessories that secure and supply the magazines to the machine during operation.
[B41B 11/14] with escapement devices -- The subclass B41B 11/14 covers machines or accessories for mechanical composition that employ individual character matrices for type casting or moulding and incorporate escapement devices to control the spacing or movement of the matrices during operation.
[B41B 11/16] Boxes for space bands -- Boxes for space bands are accessories for mechanical composition machines that provide storage and organization of spacing bands used to create variable gaps between characters during typesetting. They are designed to hold, protect, and dispense these flexible metal or paper strips within printing equipment.
[B41B 11/18] Devices or arrangements for assembling matrices and space bands -- B41B 11/18 covers devices or arrangements used to assemble matrices and spacing bands in machines that mechanically compose type by selecting individual character matrices for casting or moulding, facilitating the preparation of type before printing.
[B41B 1/12] for setting musical notations -- B41B 1/12 covers devices used to set musical notations in hand-composed scores, such as type-bars, punch-cards or other apparatus that place or arrange note symbols on a staff for printing or copying.
[B41B 11/20] Assembler guide channels -- B41B 11/20 covers guide channels that form part of an assembler used for arranging individual type matrices before casting or moulding. These channels direct and position the type pieces to ensure accurate mechanical composition in printing-type machines and related accessories.
[B41B 11/22] Assembler cover plates or framework -- B41B 11/22 covers protective cover plates and structural frameworks that are part of type-assembling machines used in mechanical composition with matrices. These components shield the assembler mechanism and provide rigidity, but do not perform the casting or moulding themselves. They are considered accessories for printers' type-casting equipment.
[B41B 11/24] Belt conveyors co-operating with assembler guides -- B41B 11/24 covers belt conveyors that work together with assembler guides in machines used for mechanical type composition, where individual character matrices are selected and assembled for casting or moulding. The classification applies to accessories that transport and position type elements within printing equipment. This falls under the broader groups for printing machines and type-making accessories.
[B41B 11/26] Devices for feeding or guiding space bands into association with matrices -- This subclass covers mechanisms that feed or guide spacing strips, known as space-bands, so that they are correctly positioned alongside the individual type matrices in machines used for mechanical composition, casting or moulding of characters.
[B41B 11/28] Star wheels or other stacking devices for matrices and space bands -- This subclass covers devices such as star wheels and other mechanisms that stack, hold, and transport individual type matrices together with space-band elements during the mechanical composition process for casting or molding type. The equipment aligns matrices in a predetermined sequence and may incorporate indexing features to facilitate rapid selection and placement.
[B41B 11/30] Assembler elevators or associated mechanisms, e.g. braking devices, retaining pawls, line resistants, delivery slides -- The entry covers elevator mechanisms and related components such as brakes, retaining pawls, line resistors and delivery slides used in type-casting or moulding machines that assemble individual character matrices into a composition. It applies to accessories that move or control the positioning of type elements during the assembling process.
[B41B 11/32] Transfer channels or line-delivery carriages -- B41B 11/32 relates to the transfer channels and line-delivery carriages employed in printing machines that convey type matrices or mould plates from the composing area to the casting or moulding station, facilitating the mechanical assembly of individual characters for type production.
[B41B 11/34] First elevators or associated mechanisms -- B41B 11/34 pertains to the first elevators or associated mechanisms in machines used for mechanical composition, where individual character matrices are selected and assembled for type casting or moulding. This classification is part of a detailed system covering printing operations and equipment.
[B41B 11/36] for presenting matrices or space bands directly to moulds -- This subclass covers devices that transfer type matrices or spacing bands directly into moulds for casting or molding type, enabling the mechanical composition of characters. It includes accessories that present the matrices or space bands straight to the mould without intermediate handling.
[B41B 11/38] Devices for aligning or clamping lines of matrices and space bands -- This subclass concerns devices that align or clamp rows of type matrices and the space-band strips that separate them during mechanical composition, ensuring proper positioning of characters before casting or moulding in printing machines.
[B41B 1/14] Composing rules, e.g. of brass -- B41B 1/14 covers the specifications and guidelines that define the composition of type-metal alloys, particularly brass, used in hand-composed printing. It includes formulas, proportions of copper, zinc and other alloying elements, and related instructions for achieving the desired hardness, durability and print quality of movable type. This subclass is used to classify inventions that address how brass type material is formulated and adjusted for typographic applications.
[B41B 11/40] Jaw clamps -- B41B 11/40 designates jaw clamps, which are devices that grip or secure the matrices or molds used in mechanically composed type-casting or moulding machines. These clamps are accessories for printing equipment that selects, assembles, and distributes individual characters for type production.
[B41B 11/42] for quadding and centering -- The B41B 11/42 subclass covers accessories for type-casting or moulding machines that assist in quadding and centering the matrices or characters, ensuring precise alignment before casting. These devices are used in mechanical composition systems that assemble individual type pieces for printing.
[B41B 11/44] for justifying -- B41B 11/44 relates to printing machines or their accessories that perform justification, i.e., adjusting the spacing of individual type matrices or characters during mechanical composition to produce evenly aligned text lines. This subclass covers mechanisms, controls or arrangements that automatically set or modify inter-character gaps to achieve justified text in type-casting or moulding devices.
[B41B 11/46] Aligning devices -- B41B 11/46 refers to aligning devices that are part of machines for mechanical composition using individual character matrices, where the matrices are selected, assembled and positioned for type casting or moulding; the devices ensure precise placement of the matrices before the casting process.
[B41B 11/48] Cleaning devices for aligning jaws -- B41B 11/48 covers cleaning devices designed to maintain the aligning jaws of type-casting or moulding machines that assemble individual character matrices. These accessories remove residue and ensure precise jaw positioning during mechanical composition of type.
[B41B 11/50] Vice frames -- B41B 11/50 covers vice frames, the supporting structures that hold matrices or type molds in place during mechanical composition processes such as type casting or moulding. These components are part of printing machines that assemble individual characters from selected matrices.
[B41B 11/52] Moulding or casting devices or associated mechanisms -- B41B 11/52 relates to moulding or casting devices and their associated mechanisms used in machines that mechanically compose type from individual character matrices for type-casting or moulding.
[B41B 11/54] Moulds -- B41B 11/54 covers moulds employed in machines that mechanically compose individual characters by casting or moulding type, including the moulds themselves and related accessories. It pertains to equipment used to form the matrix or punch that creates each character for printing.
[B41B 11/56] Mould wheels -- Mould wheels are rotating components that carry type matrices in mechanical composition machines, allowing individual characters to be positioned and moulded for casting or moulding. They are accessories for printing machines that produce type by assembling matrices and are classified under B41B 11/56.
[B41B 11/58] with locking wedges -- B41B 11/58 pertains to machines or accessories used in mechanical type composition that employ locking wedges to secure the matrices in place during casting or moulding of type.
[B41B 1/16] Reglets -- Reglets are small metal or plastic strips that hold individual type pieces in a line to form a hand-set composition and facilitate quick placement and removal of characters. They are used in printing machines or type casting to arrange type for printing.
[B41B 11/60] adjustable or removable -- B41B 11/60 covers adjustable or removable accessories for machines that mechanically compose type using matrices, such as printing presses that set individual characters for casting or moulding. This falls under performing operations, printing, machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 11/62] with interchangeable moulds -- The code refers to machines or accessories that form type by mechanically selecting individual character matrices and casting or moulding them, where the moulds can be exchanged for different designs.
[B41B 11/64] with a series of moulds and mechanism for selecting individual moulds -- B41B 11/64 covers machines or accessories that use a series of individual moulds and a mechanism for selecting each mould to compose type mechanically, such as for casting or moulding characters. It applies to type-composition devices that assemble characters from moulds before printing.
[B41B 11/66] mounted on reciprocable carriages -- This subclass covers type-casting or moulding devices in which the matrix or type-casting mechanism is mounted on a carriage that moves back and forth. The reciprocable carriage enables sequential positioning of characters for printing or composition and applies to machines or accessories that employ such moving mounts.
[B41B 11/68] with provision for adjusting length or width of slot -- The code B41B 11/68 covers accessories for type-casting or moulding machines that provide a means to adjust the length or width of the slot through which type matrices are fed or positioned.
[B41B 11/70] for casting type-bars of special form, e.g. with projections -- The CPC class B41B 11/70 covers devices for casting type-bars of special shapes, such as those with projections, used in mechanical composition machines that form type via matrices.
[B41B 11/72] Devices for trimming type-bars -- Devices for trimming type-bars are tools used in type-casting or moulding machines that cut excess material from assembled type bars to ensure uniform length and precise alignment before printing. These accessories aid in maintaining consistent type height and quality in mechanical composition processes.
[B41B 11/74] Devices for supplying molten metal -- This subclass defines apparatus that deliver molten metal to machines used for mechanical composition of type, such as type-casting or moulding equipment. It includes ladles, feed tubes, pumps and other mechanisms that transfer metal from a furnace to the matrix-forming station.
[B41B 11/76] Pots -- Pots in this CPC subclass refer to containers that form part of type-casting or moulding equipment used in printing, serving to hold matrices, molten metal, or other casting materials while individual characters are selected, assembled and cast into type.
[B41B 11/78] movable towards and away from mould -- A component of type-casting or moulding equipment that can be moved toward and away from the mould, enabling the positioning of matrices or type elements during the mechanical composition process for printing type.
[B41B 1/18] Chases -- Chases are components of printing machines that hold or guide type, plates or other printing elements within the press, ensuring proper alignment and registration during operation. They are typically removable frames or assemblies that secure the printing material in position for accurate impression. This element falls under the CPC class for machines or accessories used in hand composition and type setting.
[B41B 11/80] with stirrers or means for removing dross -- B41B 11/80 classifies accessories for machines that mechanically compose type by casting or molding individual character matrices, specifically those equipped with stirrers or devices for removing dross. These components are used in printing operations to improve the quality and efficiency of type production.
[B41B 11/82] associated with mechanisms for introducing metal in solid form -- B41B 11/82 pertains to mechanisms that introduce metal in solid form into machines used for mechanical composition of type, such as hoppers, feeders or delivery devices that supply solid metal to casting or moulding equipment.
[B41B 11/84] Throats or mouthpieces -- B41B 11/84 covers the throats and mouthpieces of type-casting or moulding machines that assemble individual character matrices for mechanical composition. These components guide the flow of molten metal or polymer into the matrix cavities.
[B41B 11/86] Heaters for pots -- B41B 11/86 covers heating devices for the pots of type-casting or moulding machines used in mechanical composition of type, providing temperature control for the metal or alloy during the casting of individual characters. It falls under printing machines and accessories for making, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 11/88] Pumps -- B41B 11/88 covers pumps that are part of, or are accessories for, machines used to mechanically compose type with individual character matrices, such as those employed in type casting or moulding processes.
[B41B 11/90] Arrangements or devices for distributing matrices or space bands after casting or moulding -- The subclass B41B 11/90 relates to devices that distribute or feed matrices and space-bands after they have been cast or moulded in type-casting machines. These arrangements automate the handling of individual character blocks for subsequent setting or printing operations.
[B41B 11/92] Second elevators -- B41B 11/92 designates secondary elevator mechanisms in type-casting or moulding machines that transfer matrices or individual characters between work stations. The provision covers the design, operation and accessories of a second lifting or conveying device used alongside the primary elevator to improve handling of type elements.
[B41B 11/94] Screw conveyors -- B41B 11/94 covers screw conveyors that serve as details or accessories for machines used in mechanical type composition with matrices, such as type-casting or moulding equipment. The classification includes the helical-flight mechanisms that transport material - for example molten metal, sand or other casting media - within or between stages of the type-making process.
[B41B 11/96] Devices for cooling or cleaning matrices -- This subclass covers apparatus designed to cool or clean the individual character matrices used in mechanical type composition, such as those employed in type-casting or moulding processes. The devices may be integrated with or added to printing or typesetting machines to maintain matrix quality and prevent overheating or residue buildup.
[B41B 1/20] Quoins or other binding means -- B41B 1/20 covers quoins and other binding means used in hand composition of type. It includes devices that secure, lock or hold individual type pieces together in a composing stick or case, such as adjustable wedges, clamps and similar mechanisms. These elements aid in assembling and maintaining the arrangement of type before printing.
[B41B 1/22] Composing tables -- B41B 1/22 covers composing tables, devices used in hand composition of type that support the arranging, setting and distribution of type elements for printing operations.
[B41B 1/24] Washing or cleaning devices -- B41B 1/24 pertains to washing or cleaning devices employed in hand-composition printing, covering equipment that removes ink, dust or residues from individual type pieces or type matrices. The provision includes both integral accessories on type-setting machines and separate units used before or after composition.
[B41B 1/26] Composing or setting sticks -- This CPC subclass covers devices used to compose or set individual type sticks for printing, including hand-operated mechanisms that arrange, hold, or transfer type elements during composition. It includes accessories that facilitate manual assembly of type sticks before printing.
[B41B 1/28] Galleys -- Elements or appliances used for hand-composing printed material, such as trays, frames or supports that hold individual type pieces during layout and arrangement before printing, classified under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 13/00] Photographic or photoelectronic composing devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 13/02] the characters being composed or mounted by hand, and photographed simultaneously -- The code B41B 13/02 covers photographic or photo-electronic composing devices in which individual characters are assembled by hand and captured on film or sensor in a single exposure. This method creates a printable image of the composed text directly, rather than using pre-made type plates.
[B41B 13/04] with means for justifying -- The subclass B41B 13/04 covers photographic or photo-electronic composing devices equipped with means for justifying the image, that is, aligning and correcting its position before printing. It falls under the broader group for machines or accessories used in making, setting, or distributing type in printing operations.
[B41B 13/06] by elastic or extensible type-line carriers -- The subclass B41B 13/06 covers photographic or photo-electronic composing devices that form images by using elastic or extensible type-line carriers, meaning flexible or stretchable strips that hold and position type elements for exposure or printing. This approach enables continuous or variable-length type lines to be positioned accurately during the imaging process.
[B41B 13/08] by optical line correction -- The B41B 13/08 subclass covers photographic or photo-electronic composing devices that use optical means to correct line placement during printing. It includes mechanisms that detect and adjust line position optically to improve accuracy in type-making, setting or distribution processes.
[B41B 13/10] the characters being progressively photographed one by one -- B41B 13/10 relates to photographic or photo-electronic composing devices that form text by progressively photographing each character one by one, typically used in systems that capture typed or selected letters for subsequent printing.
[B41B 15/00] Photographic composing machines with movable character carriers for composing lines of characters prior to photography -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 15/02] with means for photographing complete lines -- The classification refers to photographic composing machines that use movable character carriers to arrange a full line of type before exposure and that include a mechanism for photographing the entire line in one shot. These devices belong to the printing-related subgroup of machines for making or distributing type.
[B41B 15/04] and with carriers for individual characters -- This subclass covers photographic composing machines that employ separate, movable carriers for individual characters to assemble lines of type before exposure, allowing variable arrangement of characters in the image-forming process.
[B41B 15/06] and with carriers for complete sets of characters, e.g. slidable elongated members, rotary wheels -- Photographic type-setting machines that form lines of characters by moving entire character sets mounted on slidable elongated members or rotary wheels, allowing a complete repertoire of symbols to be positioned before the image is photographed.
[B41B 15/08] with means for photographing composed characters in succession -- Photographic composing machines that use a movable character carrier to assemble lines of type and then capture the arranged characters one after another with a camera, enabling continuous succession photography of the composed text. This technology falls under the CPC class B41B 15/08 for devices that photograph composed characters in sequence.
[B41B 15/10] Details -- B41B 15/10 pertains to the detailed aspects of photographic composing machines that feature movable character carriers, used for setting up lines of text before the photography process in printing operations. This classification focuses on the specific mechanisms and components that enable the precise arrangement of characters.
[B41B 15/12] Character carriers -- The CPC subclass B41B 15/12 covers photographic composing machines that employ movable character carriers to arrange lines of type before exposure. These devices are used in printing operations to set or distribute characters for subsequent photographic reproduction.
[B41B 15/14] with a single character, or with combinations of different styles of a single character -- The CPC subclass B41B 15/14 covers photographic composing machines that use a movable carrier holding a single character or different style variations of that character to assemble lines of type before exposure. It pertains to equipment that creates printed text by photographic means rather than mechanical type setting.
[B41B 15/16] with combinations of different characters -- Photographic composing machines that use movable carriers to arrange and combine different characters-such as letters, numbers, or symbols-into lines of text before the image is photographed. The machines allow mixed-character compositions in a single exposure, facilitating complex typographic layouts.
[B41B 15/18] with parts of characters for building up complex characters, e.g. oriental characters -- B41B 15/18 covers photographic composing machines that employ movable carriers bearing separate character elements to assemble complex glyphs, such as oriental characters, before they are photographed for printing.
[B41B 15/20] Devices for handling or storing character carriers -- Devices for handling or storing character carriers in photographic composing machines, such as trays, feeders, racks or other mechanisms that move, position, hold, or retain the strips, disks or similar carriers used to form lines of characters before they are photographed. These tools facilitate the arrangement, transport and temporary storage of type elements within the photocomposition process.
[B41B 15/22] Magazines -- The CPC entry B41B 15/22 covers photographic composing machines that use movable character carriers to arrange lines of type before they are photographed, specifically for producing magazines. It is classified under Performing Operations → Printing → Machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 15/24] for justifying purposes -- The B41B 15/24 subclass deals with photographic composing machines that employ movable character carriers to arrange lines of characters before exposure, specifically for justification of text. It includes devices that shift individual characters or groups to achieve even spacing and alignment in the final printed image.
[B41B 15/26] for repeating a character -- The subclass B41B 15/26 pertains to photographic composing machines that use movable character carriers to form lines of characters before photography, specifically addressing mechanisms that automatically repeat a chosen character within the line. It covers devices and methods for duplicating the same character multiple times in the composition.
[B41B 15/28] Photographic devices -- B41B 15/28 covers photographic devices that use movable character carriers to arrange lines of characters before they are photographed, facilitating the composition of text or symbols for printing or typesetting. The classification includes machines that position the carriers and capture the arranged characters on film or a sensor for reproduction.
[B41B 15/30] Composing cameras -- B41B 15/30 refers to composing cameras, photographic devices that use a movable character carrier to arrange lines of type or symbols before exposure. These machines are part of the printing-related subgroup of equipment for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 15/32] Film-handling mechanisms -- B41B 15/32 pertains to film-handling mechanisms used in photographic composing machines that employ movable character carriers to arrange lines of characters before exposure. It includes devices for loading, advancing, positioning, and retrieving the film within such composing apparatus.
[B41B 15/34] Adjusting enlargement or register -- The B41B 15/34 subclass covers mechanisms that adjust the enlargement or registration of a line of characters on photographic composing machines that use movable carriers, ensuring the correct size and precise positioning before the image is photographed.
[B41B 17/00] Photographic composing machines having fixed or movable character carriers and without means for composing lines prior to photography -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 17/02] with manually-adjustable character carriers to enable characters to be photographed in succession -- The subclass B41B 17/02 covers photographic composing machines that use manually-adjustable character carriers to photograph characters one after another, without any line-composing mechanism. These devices belong to printing machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 17/04] with a carrier for all characters in at least one fount -- This subclass covers photographic composition machines that employ a single carrier containing the complete character set of at least one font, allowing individual characters to be positioned without the need for pre-composed lines before exposure. It pertains to devices that set type photographically, using a full-font carrier rather than separate character sources.
[B41B 17/06] with an adjustable carrier -- B41B 17/06 classifies photographic composing machines that employ an adjustable carrier to hold characters, allowing the carrier's position to be varied during exposure. The machines produce printed images without any prior line-composing stage, fitting within the broader group of type-making and printing equipment.
[B41B 17/08] with a fixed carrier -- This subclass relates to photographic composing machines that employ a fixed character carrier, meaning the type is mounted on a stationary plate or strip and the device lacks any means for composing lines before exposure. It is part of the broader group of printing machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 17/10] with a continuously-movable carrier -- Photographic composing machines that use a continuously-movable carrier for characters, without any mechanism for composing lines before exposure, fall under this subclass. It pertains to machines or accessories that set or distribute type in a printing-type operation where the carrier advances steadily during the composing process.
[B41B 17/12] with means for moving the optical path for photographing characters, e.g. intermittently -- The CPC subclass B41B 17/12 covers photographic composing machines that include mechanisms to shift the optical path while photographing characters, such as intermittent movement systems. It applies to devices where the character carrier is fixed or movable but the line-forming optics are altered during exposure. This subclass falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 17/14] continuously -- B41B 17/14 pertains to photographic composing machines with fixed or movable character carriers that operate in a continuous manner, without pre-composing lines before the photography process. This classification is specific to the operational mode of these machines within the broader context of printing and type setting.
[B41B 17/16] with means for moving the film in its plane for photographing characters in succession -- The CPC subclass B41B 17/16 pertains to photographic composing machines that employ fixed or movable character carriers and incorporate a mechanism for moving the film within its plane so that characters can be photographed one after another; it relates to type-setting equipment that records characters directly onto film without a prior line-composing step, supporting printing operations.
[B41B 17/18] Details -- B41B 17/18 relates to the detailed features of photographic composing machines that use fixed or movable character carriers and lack any mechanism for pre-photograph line composition. The entry covers specifications, adjustments and accessories that affect the arrangement and presentation of characters on the photographic type.
[B41B 17/20] Character carriers -- A B41B 17/20 device is a photographic composing machine that uses fixed or movable character carriers to form images directly on the photosensitive medium, lacking any mechanism for pre-composing lines before exposure. It is employed in printing operations where type or graphic elements are arranged optically rather than mechanically prior to photography.
[B41B 17/22] with a single character -- Photographic composing machines that employ a single fixed or movable character carrier and provide no means for arranging lines prior to exposure are classified under B41B 17/22.
[B41B 17/24] with all characters -- Photographic composing machines that employ fixed or movable character carriers to arrange all characters on a photographic medium at once, without any prior line-forming or pre-exposure composition step. These devices create the final image directly by exposing the assembled characters in a single operation.
[B41B 17/26] on belts -- B41B 17/26 relates to photographic composing machines that transport characters on belts during exposure, without pre-composing lines. Such devices use a continuous or intermittent belt to carry fixed or movable character carriers through the imaging zone, allowing text or graphics to be formed directly on photosensitive material.
[B41B 17/28] on polygonal rods -- This subclass covers the use of polygonal rods in photographic composing machines that employ fixed or movable character carriers and lack line-compose mechanisms, specifying how such rods guide or support the carriers to form the desired image layout.
[B41B 17/30] on a sheet of square or rectangular shape -- The code B41B 17/30 covers photographic composing machines that arrange characters on a sheet that is square or rectangular in shape, using fixed or movable character carriers and lacking any means for pre-composing lines before exposure. These devices belong to the printing-related performing-operations subclass for type-making machinery.
[B41B 17/32] on a flat disc -- The subclass B41B 17/32 covers photographic composing machines that use a flat disc to hold fixed or movable character carriers, and that do not provide a separate means for composing lines before the photograph is taken. It falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 17/34] on a cylinder -- The subclass covers photographic composing machines that employ a rotating cylinder to hold fixed or movable character carriers, forming the image directly on the photographic medium without any prior line-composing step. It is situated in the printing group for machines or accessories used to make, set or distribute type.
[B41B 17/36] with selection indicators -- B41B 17/36 covers photographic composing machines that use fixed or movable character carriers to create type images directly on film, without any prior line-composing step, and that include visual selection indicators to aid the operator in choosing characters.
[B41B 17/38] with means for indicating the width -- The sub-class B41B 17/38 concerns photographic composing machines that use fixed or movable character carriers and lack any mechanism for arranging lines before exposure, but incorporate a feature that indicates the width of the image or text.
[B41B 17/40] in caliper form -- B41B 17/40 relates to photographic composing machines that employ a caliper-type character carrier, either fixed or movable, and lack any mechanism for arranging lines before exposure. These devices fall under printing operations for machines or accessories that make, set or distribute type.
[B41B 17/42] in coded form -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B41B 17/42 pertains to photographic composing machines that feature fixed or movable character carriers, specifically those lacking mechanisms for pre-photography line composition. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions related to printing technology.
[B41B 19/00] Photoelectronic composing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 19/01] having electron-beam tubes producing an image of at least one character which is photographed -- B41B 19/01 classifies photoelectronic composing machines that employ electron-beam tubes to generate an image of one or more characters, which is then recorded photographically. These devices are used for printing and include the necessary equipment for creating, setting or distributing type by means of electron-beam imaging.
[B41B 19/02] the characters appearing on the screen in succession -- The subclass deals with the way characters are shown on a screen in sequence within photo-electronic composing machines, covering scrolling, paging or otherwise successive display of text during composition. It addresses the visual presentation of characters on a display before they are transferred to a printing medium.
[B41B 19/04] at the same place -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B41B 19/04 pertains to photoelectronic composing machines where operations are performed at the same place, indicating a localized process in the setup or distribution of type for printing.
[B41B 19/06] at different predetermined places -- This subclass relates to photoelectronic composing machines that automatically place printing elements at a series of predetermined positions on a substrate, such as fixed columns, rows or other set locations, without manual adjustment. The devices use electronic control to select each location according to a programmed pattern.
[B41B 19/08] with combinations of characters appearing on the screen at the same time -- B41B 19/08 classifies photoelectronic composing machines that present multiple characters on the display simultaneously, enabling the operator to view and edit whole word or line segments at once rather than single characters. This sub-group pertains to screen-based type setting where character combinations appear together.
[B41B 19/10] in unbroken lines or columns -- The subclass covers photoelectronic composing machines that arrange their output in continuous, unbroken lines or columns rather than segmented blocks. Such devices are used in printing operations to generate uniform streams of characters or images for further processing.
[B41B 19/12] in broken lines or columns -- This subclass pertains to photoelectronic composing machines that form printed type or images in broken lines or columns, enabling segmented or raster-style layouts for printing operations.
[B41B 19/14] and presenting a text of more than one line -- B41B 19/14 covers photoelectronic composing machines that can display or present a text consisting of more than one line at a time, such as electronic typesetting or proofing devices that arrange multiple lines for viewing or output.
[B41B 19/16] Character carriers associated with electron-beam tubes -- The code covers carriers that hold characters for exposure in electron-beam typesetting devices, such as screens or drums used in photoelectronic composing machines. These carriers work with electron-beam tubes to create printable images by directly writing the character patterns onto photosensitive material.
[B41B 21/00] Common details of photographic composing machines of the kinds covered in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 21/02] Shutters in optical systems -- B41B 21/02 covers shutters used in optical systems of photographic composing machines, such as cameras and printing equipment, that control the passage of light during exposure. The classification refers to mechanisms that open and close the light path to set exposure time.
[B41B 21/04] for selecting characters -- B41B 21/04 relates to photographic composing machines that include means for selecting characters, such as type elements or symbols, for use in printing operations. It covers the mechanisms that allow an operator or a control system to choose specific characters before they are set, transferred or distributed onto a print medium.
[B41B 21/06] for adjusting exposures -- B41B 21/06 covers photographic composing machines that incorporate mechanisms for adjusting the exposure of images. It falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type, within the broader category of performing operations.
[B41B 21/08] Light sources -- B41B 21/08 covers the light sources employed in photographic composing machines, which are a subcategory of machines or accessories used for making, setting, or distributing type. It specifies the illumination devices that enable image formation and exposure control in these type-setting and printing systems.
[B41B 21/10] Single light sources -- The B41B 21/10 entry classifies printing machines or accessories that employ a single light source - such as one lamp, LED or other illumination element - to expose, view or set type in photographic composing devices. It applies to equipment where the entire image-formation or type-setting operation relies on that solitary source of illumination.
[B41B 21/12] Stroboscopic light sources -- B41B 21/12 relates to stroboscopic light sources employed in photographic composing machines that set or distribute type, covering devices that emit brief, high-intensity flashes to expose images during printing operations. This code falls under machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type within the performing-operations and printing categories.
[B41B 21/14] Combinations of light sources -- The subclass B41B 21/14 covers photographic composing or printing machines that employ two or more light sources together, such as combined lamps, LEDs or other illuminants, to achieve varied spectral characteristics, intensity levels or exposure control for image formation. It includes arrangements where the different sources are integrated in a single unit or positioned to work in concert during the printing or copying process.
[B41B 21/16] Optical systems -- B41B 21/16 covers the optical systems of photographic-composing machines used in printing, including lenses, mirrors, viewfinders, projection optics and related components that form, direct or modify the image during type setting or distribution.
[B41B 21/18] defining a single optical path -- B41B 21/18 covers photographic composing machines in which the optical system is arranged to provide a single optical path for exposing the image, such as in cameras or enlargers where light travels through one fixed route from lens to photosensitive material.
[B41B 21/20] with means for moving stepwise -- B41B 21/20 refers to photographic composing machines equipped with mechanisms that move the image carrier stepwise, such as incremental film or paper advance during exposure or printing. This subclass falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 21/22] with means for moving continuously -- B41B 21/22 classifies photographic composing or printing machines that incorporate mechanisms to move the photosensitive material continuously during operation, such as rollers or belts that advance paper, film, or plates in a steady feed. These devices are designed for uninterrupted exposure or printing processes, distinguishing them from machines that require manual or stepwise advancement.
[B41B 21/24] defining multiple optical paths -- B41B 21/24 pertains to photographic composing machines that incorporate two or more separate optical paths, such as devices with distinct viewfinder and exposure-control lenses or systems that split light for different functions. The classification is used for machines where multiple optical routes are integral to the photographing or image-forming process.
[B41B 21/26] with means for selecting individual optical paths -- This subclass covers photographic composing machines that incorporate mechanisms to select individual optical paths for image exposure, allowing different lenses or light channels to be chosen during printing. It pertains to devices that manage and switch optical routes within type-setting or distribution equipment.
[B41B 21/28] with means for fount selection -- B41B 21/28 refers to photographic composing machines that incorporate a system for selecting the type fount, enabling automatic or manual choice of type fonts during the printing process. This subclass falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type, and covers devices where fount selection is integrated into the composition mechanism.
[B41B 21/30] Electron-beam tubes or charactrons -- B41B 21/30 relates to electron-beam tubes or charactrons employed in photographic composing machines that form part of printing equipment for making, setting or distributing type. These devices generate or modulate electron beams to record or transfer images onto photosensitive media, supporting high-speed type composition in printing processes.
[B41B 21/32] Film carriers -- B41B 21/32 covers film carriers used in photographic composing machines, i.e., devices that hold, transport or guide film within cameras or printing equipment. These carriers are components of machines that assemble or process photographic images, facilitating film movement and positioning during exposure or development.
[B41B 21/34] with positioning mechanisms -- This subclass relates to photographic composing machines that incorporate mechanisms for accurately positioning the film, plate, or other imaging element during exposure. It is part of the broader group of printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type, focusing on the addition of positioning features to ensure precise image alignment.
[B41B 21/36] for feeding films stepwise in line direction -- B41B 21/36 covers stepwise, line-direction feeding mechanisms for photographic film within machines that compose, set or distribute type, detailing how films are advanced incrementally during exposure.
[B41B 21/38] for feeding films continuously in line direction -- B41B 21/38 covers devices that continuously feed photographic film in the line direction within composing or printing machines, including rollers, belts and other transport elements that move film straight through the process.
[B41B 21/40] for line spacing -- B41B 21/40 designates mechanisms that adjust the vertical distance between successive lines of type or characters in photographic composing machines, enabling controlled line spacing during printing. The classification applies to accessories or features that automatically or manually set line spacing in devices that arrange type for printing.
[B41B 21/42] for subscript or superscript notation -- B41B 21/42 covers photographic composing machines that are designed to add subscript or superscript characters to printed material. These devices belong to the class of printing equipment for making, setting, or distributing type and are used in typesetting operations.
[B41B 21/44] adjustable for enlarging purposes -- B41B 21/44 covers photographic composing machines that include adjustments for enlarging the image, allowing the operator to change the size of the picture during the printing process. These machines are part of the broader category of printing equipment used for making or distributing type, specifically designed for flexible image scaling.
[B41B 23/00] Auxiliary devices for modifying, mixing or correcting text or layout in connection with photographic or photoelectronic composing -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 23/02] for mixing by line-by-line projection of parts of texts from a number of films on to another -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary devices used in photographic or photoelectronic composing to modify, mix or correct text or layout, specifically those that mix text by projecting line-by-line portions from several film strips onto a single output film. The technology enables precise line-wise combination of characters from multiple sources for high-quality type setting.
[B41B 23/04] for facilitating hand correction of texts -- The CPC entry B41B 23/04 covers auxiliary devices used in photographic or photo-electronic composition that help modify, mix or correct text and layout, specifically tools designed to enable hand correction of texts. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 23/06] for correcting texts by removing incorrect lines from one film and splicing-in corrected lines from another film -- Auxiliary devices used in photographic or photo-electronic composition to correct printed text by cutting out erroneous lines from a film and inserting corrected lines from another film, enabling precise removal and splicing of text elements during layout preparation.
[B41B 25/00] Marking record carriers used for controlling composing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 25/10] incorporating devices for line justification, e.g. counting and indicating devices for length of line -- The subclass B41B 25/10 covers devices built into marking record carriers that aid line justification in printing or typesetting machines, such as mechanisms that count characters or spaces and display the length of a line to ensure even text alignment.
[B41B 25/12] comprising elements which circulate from and to magazines -- This class refers to marking record carriers, such as punched tapes or film strips, that are transferred between magazines of a composing machine so they can be read by the machine to control printing operations.
[B41B 25/14] operating without space bands -- B41B 25/14 concerns devices that mark record carriers such as paper or film for controlling typesetting or composing machines, wherein the marking is carried out without employing space bands to separate characters. The technology eliminates the need for physical spacing elements during operation.
[B41B 25/16] using a binary code for the width of the matrices -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B41B 25/16 pertains to marking record carriers with a binary code for the width of matrices, used in controlling composing machines. This classification is part of a detailed system for organizing patent information related to printing and typesetting machinery.
[B41B 25/18] combined with type-setting machines -- The code covers marking record carriers such as punched tape or magnetic strips that are employed to control composing or typesetting machines and are integrated with the typesetting apparatus. It includes combined systems where the carrier and the type-setting machine operate together as a single unit.
[B41B 25/20] Auxiliary devices -- The B41B 25/20 class covers auxiliary devices that mark record carriers employed to control composing machines in printing operations. These devices assist in handling or preparing the carrier elements that convey composition data to the press. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 25/22] for indicating length of line -- B41B 25/22 covers devices that mark record carriers to indicate the length of a line in typesetting or composing machines, providing precise control of line spacing and alignment during printing.
[B41B 25/24] for error correction -- The subclass B41B 25/24 covers devices that apply corrective marks to record carriers used for controlling composing machines, such as error-correction tapes, inked ribbons or other marking means placed on punched paper or similar media to indicate and correct typographic errors during printing.
[B41B 25/26] for changing the fount of type -- This CPC subclass covers mechanisms that enable the font of type to be altered in printing equipment, such as devices for inserting, removing or swapping type elements to change character styles. It applies to machines or accessories that adjust type fonts during composition.
[B41B 27/00] Control, indicating, or safety devices or systems for composing machines of various kinds or types -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 27/02] Systems for controlling all operations -- B41B 27/02 covers integrated control systems that manage every operation of printing and composition machines, including start-stop, speed regulation, positioning and safety interlocks, allowing coordinated handling of type setting, printing and distribution functions.
[B41B 27/04] Keyboards -- Keyboards are input devices used to control and operate composing or type-setting machines, allowing operators to select characters, commands, and functions during printing processes. In the CPC they fall under B41B 27/04, covering control, indicating or safety systems for machines that make, set or distribute type.
[B41B 27/06] operable by input of recorded or stored information, e.g. on punched tapes -- B41B 27/06 covers control, indicating or safety devices for type-setting or printing machines that operate from pre-recorded or stored data, such as punched-tape input, enabling automatic or semi-automatic machine functions.
[B41B 27/08] from several sources -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B41B 27/08 pertains to control, indicating, or safety devices or systems used in composing machines that integrate information or components from multiple sources. This classification is part of a detailed system for categorizing patent documents related to printing and typesetting machinery.
[B41B 27/10] with direct control of all operations by input of recorded or stored information -- B41B 27/10 covers machines for printing or setting type in which every operation is directly governed by input of recorded or stored information, such as digital or computerized control signals that dictate the entire process. This includes control, indicating, or safety systems that use pre-programmed data to manage the machine's functions.
[B41B 27/12] on tapes -- B41B 27/12 covers control, indicating or safety devices and systems that operate on tapes for composing or typesetting machines. It falls under the printing-machines group, specifically accessories that manage tape-based functions such as feed control, positioning, or error detection in type-setting equipment.
[B41B 27/14] on punched tapes -- B41B 27/14 relates to control, indicating or safety devices for type-setting or printing machines that use punched-tape mechanisms. It covers apparatus that reads, feeds or interprets punched-paper data to command printer actions, select characters or provide interlocks, functioning as accessories that manage machine operations through tape-based inputs.
[B41B 27/16] on magnetic tapes -- B41B 27/16 covers control, indication or safety systems for type-making or composing machines that operate using magnetic-tape media. It includes devices that read, write or process magnetic tape to govern functions such as character selection, line justification, feed control or other operational parameters of printing equipment.
[B41B 27/18] from memory devices -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B41B 27/18 pertains to control, indicating, or safety devices or systems for composing machines that specifically involve the use of memory devices. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European and United States patent offices to categorize inventions related to printing operations.
[B41B 27/20] from electromagnetic devices, e.g. memory matrices -- The class B41B 27/20 covers control, indicating or safety devices and systems for type-printing machines that are derived from electromagnetic components such as memory matrices, including circuits and mechanisms that regulate the operation of composing or typesetting equipment.
[B41B 27/22] from several sources -- Devices and systems that monitor, control, or provide safety for composing or printing machines, such as indicators, interlocks, and protective mechanisms, using inputs or control signals derived from several sources.
[B41B 27/24] of several associated machines -- B41B 27/24 concerns control, indication or safety devices and systems that coordinate the operation of two or more associated printing or typing machines. It covers mechanisms that synchronize different machines in a printing line, ensuring proper sequencing, speed matching and error protection across the linked equipment. This subclass is used for inventions that manage the interaction of multiple composing or printing devices as a single functional unit.
[B41B 27/26] with means for temporarily arresting input of recorded or stored information to introduce time delays -- B41B 27/26 concerns control, indicating or safety devices for printing machines that can temporarily arrest the input of recorded or stored information in order to introduce a time-delay in the printing operation.
[B41B 27/28] Control, indicating, or safety devices for individual operations or machine elements -- B41B 27/28 covers control, indicating or safety devices that act on specific operations or individual components of printing machines, such as mechanisms for regulating ink flow, paper feed, or individual printing heads, and the associated sensors, alarms, interlocks or protective systems that monitor and safeguard those elements.
[B41B 27/30] for arresting or varying speed of one operation in response to failure or variation in speed of another operation -- This CPC subgroup covers control systems or devices that automatically adjust or stop the speed of one printing operation when another operation slows down, speeds up, or fails, ensuring coordinated operation of type-making or distributing machines.
[B41B 27/32] for line-justification operations -- B41B 27/32 covers control or safety devices that perform line-justification in printing or typesetting machines, automatically adjusting the spacing of characters or words so that each line aligns with both margins. The provision applies to mechanisms that add, remove, or redistribute space during composition to produce fully justified text.
[B41B 27/34] without the use of space bands -- B41B 27/34 relates to control, indicating or safety devices for typesetting machines that operate without space-band spacers, covering mechanisms that directly position or separate type elements in the composing process rather than using elastic bands to create spacing.
[B41B 27/36] using electronic devices -- B41B 27/36 covers the use of electronic devices to control, indicate or provide safety functions in printing machines or accessories that make, set or distribute type. It includes electronic sensors, actuators, microprocessors and related circuitry that manage operation, monitor status or protect the equipment. The classification falls under performing operations, printing, machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type, and control, indicating or safety devices.
[B41B 27/38] for measuring length of composed lines -- The CPC subclass B41B 27/38 concerns devices that measure the length of composed lines in printing or typesetting machines, typically as part of control, indicating or safety systems for such equipment. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 27/40] for proof-printing operations -- The B41B 27/40 subclass covers devices and systems that control, indicate or provide safety functions specifically for proof-printing operations in composing machines, such as those used to check layout or type before final printing. It falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 27/41] Printing mechanisms coupled to typographical composing machines -- B41B 27/41 pertains to printing mechanisms that are directly linked to typographical composing machines, enabling the transfer of type to paper. It falls under the CPC group for machines or accessories for making, setting or distributing type and includes related control, indicating and safety devices.
[B41B 27/42] for controlling selection or shifting of matrix magazines -- B41B 27/42 relates to devices that control the selection or shifting of matrix magazines in printing machines. It covers mechanisms and safety systems that manage which type matrix is used during operation, ensuring correct composition of printed material.
[B41B 27/44] for controlling assembly of matrices and space bands -- B41B 27/44 covers control, indication or safety devices and systems used on composing-type printing machines that manage the assembly of matrices and space-band components, ensuring proper placement and alignment during printing operations. This subclass is part of the broader group for machines or accessories that set, make or distribute type.
[B41B 27/46] for controlling insertion of space bands -- B41B 27/46 relates to devices that control the insertion of space-bands in type-composing machines. It is classified under printing, specifically in the group for control, indicating or safety devices for machines that make, set or distribute type.
[B41B 27/48] for deleting errors or inserting corrections -- B41B 27/48 covers control or safety mechanisms for typesetting and composing equipment that enable the removal of mis-printed characters or the insertion of corrected material. The class includes devices such as correction punches, erasing units or electronic functions that automatically delete errors and add replacements during the printing process.
[B41B 27/50] operated by coupled machines -- B41B 27/50 pertains to control, indicating and safety mechanisms for type-composing machines that are driven by coupled machines. The code covers devices that coordinate or monitor the operation of such linked printing equipment, ensuring proper function and protection.
[B41B 3/00] Machines or other mechanical apparatus for composing -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 3/02] Mechanical composing machines using single types or logotypes and having slide magazines therefor, e.g. flat, cylindrical, radial, single magazines -- Mechanical composing machines that employ single metal types or logotypes and feature slide magazines-such as flat, cylindrical or radial single-magazine arrangements-to feed the characters for printing. These devices automate the selection and placement of individual type pieces during the typesetting process.
[B41B 3/04] Means for distributing type -- B41B 3/04 covers mechanisms that feed or deliver individual type elements to a composing machine, such as type carousels, magazines, shuttles or other automated distributors that move type from storage positions into the setting area of a typesetting or printing apparatus. These means ensure rapid, precise placement of type during the printing process.
[B41B 3/06] Justifying means -- B41B 3/06 relates to mechanisms that provide justification for type or printing elements in composing machines, ensuring correct alignment and uniform spacing of characters or blocks. It includes devices that adjust or set the position of type to achieve proper line justification during printing.
[B41B 5/00] Devices for making type or lines -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 5/02] Matrices -- Matrices are the plates, punches or molds that hold individual characters or lines of type for casting, embossing or otherwise reproducing printed matter, used in type-making and line-casting machines.
[B41B 5/04] Casting devices -- Devices used to form type or line matrices by pouring, injecting or otherwise introducing molten metal or alloy into a mould or cavity. These casting mechanisms are employed in printing equipment to produce the solid type pieces or line blocks required for typesetting.
[B41B 5/06] mechanically operated -- B41B 5/06 relates to devices for making type or lines that are powered by a mechanical mechanism, such as hand-cranked, gear-driven or spring-assisted type-casting machines. It falls within the printing hierarchy for machines or accessories used to produce type.
[B41B 5/08] Tools or auxiliaries -- B41B 5/08 covers any tools, fixtures or auxiliary devices that assist or supplement machines used for making type or lines, such as guides, clamps, loading aids, cleaning implements or adjustment mechanisms. These items are not the primary type-making machine itself but are employed to improve its operation, maintenance or handling of type components.
[B41B 5/10] for polishing or finishing type -- B41B 5/10 covers devices that polish or finish type, such as type-casting or line-casting elements, used in printing machines or accessories that make, set, or distribute type. It falls under the CPC hierarchy performing operations - printing - machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type.
[B41B 5/12] for making type in the form of lines, e.g. by drawing or casting -- B41B 5/12 refers to machines or accessories that produce type in the form of continuous lines for printing, using processes such as drawing or casting line type rather than individual characters.
[B41B 5/14] for cutting spaces -- Devices that cut spaces between type or line elements in printing machinery, such as spacing bars, space-cutters or mechanisms that remove material to create blanks for separating characters or lines.
[B41B 7/00] Kinds or types of apparatus for mechanical composition in which the type is cast or moulded from matrices -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 7/02] operating with fixed combinations of matrices -- B41B 7/02 covers printing equipment that forms type by casting or molding using a set of matrices that are permanently fixed together, allowing simultaneous production of multiple characters or symbols. Such devices are used for mechanical composition where the type elements are produced as a fixed array rather than individually.
[B41B 7/04] for casting individual characters or spaces, e.g. type casting machines -- B41B 7/04 relates to machines that cast individual type characters or spaces, such as type-casting equipment that forms each character from a mould or matrix. These devices are used in printing to produce metal or polymer type for setting and composition.
[B41B 7/06] for composing, justifying and casting complete lines of matrices, e.g. "monoline" machines -- The B41B 7/06 subclass covers machines that compose, justify and cast entire lines of matrices in a single operation, such as the "monoline" devices that produce continuous rows of type from moulded matrices. These apparatuses are designed for the mechanical preparation of type for printing, integrating composition and casting steps into one coordinated process.
[B41B 7/08] operating with separate matrices for individual characters or spaces which are selected and assembled -- This subclass refers to printing machines or accessories that form text by selecting individual character or space matrices and assembling them for each print run. It includes devices where separate type pieces are positioned according to the desired layout rather than using a single cast matrix. Used in typesetting and letter-press systems.
[B41B 7/10] in which a line is composed of matrices and in which single types or spaces are cast, e.g. stringer type -- B41B 7/10 relates to machines for making type in which a line is built from a series of matrices and each character or space is cast separately, such as the stringer-type process. It falls under performing operations, printing, and equipment for producing, setting or distributing type.
[B41B 7/12] in which a justified line is composed of matrices and a type line is cast in one piece -- B41B 7/12 covers printing devices that form a justified line of type by arranging individual matrices and then casting the entire line as a single piece. It relates to apparatus for mechanical composition where the type is produced in one moulded or cast unit rather than set piece by piece.
[B41B 7/14] the matrices being composed by hand -- B41B 7/14 covers printing equipment in which the type matrices are assembled manually, describing devices that allow an operator to hand-compose the matrix elements before they are cast or moulded into type. It pertains to mechanical type-making apparatus where the matrix formation is performed by hand.
[B41B 7/16] the lines of matrices being composed and justified by machine operation, e.g. line type casting machines, "intertype" machines -- B41B 7/16 covers machines that automatically compose and justify rows of type matrices, such as line-type casting or Intertype devices, arranging the matrices into lines for printing.
[B41B 7/18] the matrices being guided by wires, e.g. "typograph" machines -- B41B 7/18 covers machines in which the matrices used for forming type are guided by wires, such as typograph machines. It falls under printing machines or accessories for making, setting, or distributing type, specifically apparatus for mechanical composition where the type is cast or moulded from matrices.
[B41B 9/00] Details of, or accessories for, machines for mechanical composition using fixed combinations of matrices from which type is cast or moulded -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41B 9/02] Combinations of matrices -- B41B 9/02 covers the design and arrangement of multiple matrices that are fixed together to form a single type-casting or moulding unit. It pertains to machines or accessories that use these combined matrices for mechanically composing printed type.
[B41B 9/04] Carriers for combinations of matrices, e.g. blocks, cylinders, wheels, bars -- Carriers that hold fixed sets of matrices-such as blocks, cylinders, wheels or bars-used in machines for mechanically composing type by casting or molding. These devices are accessories for printing equipment that produce type from pre-arranged matrix combinations.
[B41B 9/06] Mechanisms for effecting relative movements of matrix carriers and moulds for character or space selection -- It covers mechanisms that move matrix carriers and moulds relative to each other to select characters or spaces in mechanical type composition. These devices are used in printing machines that cast or mould type from fixed matrix combinations.
[B41B 9/08] Moulding or casting devices -- B41B 9/08 relates to moulding or casting devices used in the mechanical production of type, where fixed combinations of matrices are employed to cast or mould the characters. It includes details of, and accessories for, machines that perform these type-casting operations.
[B41B 9/10] Moulds -- B41B 9/10 covers moulds and their accessories used in machines that form type by casting or moulding from fixed matrix combinations. It relates to the mechanical composition process for printing type, describing the design, construction, and components of the moulds that shape the type elements.
[B41B 9/12] Justifying devices -- B41B 9/12 covers justifying devices that regulate the spacing and alignment of type or matrix elements in machines that cast or mould type from fixed matrix combinations. These accessories ensure uniform line length and proper distribution of characters before printing.
[B41B 9/14] Supply devices for molten metal -- The B41B 9/14 subclass covers devices that feed or deliver molten metal to type-casting machines, such as ladle feeders, pumps and distribution tubes used in the mechanical composition of type from fixed matrix combinations.
[B41B 9/16] Devices for handling type after casting -- Devices for handling type after casting are machines or accessories that move, orient, transport, or otherwise manipulate type that has been produced by casting or moulding from fixed matrix combinations, facilitating subsequent setting, arranging or distribution in printing operations.
[B41B 9/18] Ejector mechanisms -- Ejector mechanisms are devices that remove cast or moulded type from machines used in mechanical composition with fixed matrix sets, facilitating the printing process. They form part of accessories for type-making, setting or distribution equipment under the CPC class B41B 9/18.
[B41B 9/20] Trimming devices -- B41B 9/20 pertains to trimming devices employed in type-making printing machines, specifically apparatus that cuts, trims or finishes cast or moulded type produced from fixed matrix combinations. These devices are accessories for mechanical composition equipment used to prepare type for printing.
[B41C] PROCESSES FOR THE MANUFACTURE OR REPRODUCTION OF PRINTING SURFACES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for the manufacture or reproduction of printing surfaces.  Printing forme precursors and printing formes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Photomechanical production of textured or patterned surfaces -> G03F - Electrography; electrophotography; magnetography -> G03G
[B41C 1/00] Forme preparation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing formes and methods of making the same for printing methods using said formes.  Methods for making printing plate precursors and printing plates wherein the imaging exposure is heat or infrared light.
[B41C 1/003] the relief or intaglio pattern being obtained by imagewise deposition of a liquid, e.g. by an ink jet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for preparing relief printing formes, flexographic printing formes, intaglio printing formes and.  gravure printing formes.  Printing formes obtained by said methods.
[B41C 1/006] the relief or intaglio pattern being obtained by abrasive means, e.g. by sandblasting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for preparing relief printing formes, flexographic printing formes intaglio printing formes and  gravure printing formes.  Printing formes obtained by said methods.
[B41C 1/02] Engraving -- B41C 1/02 covers engraving methods used to create the form or matrix for printing surfaces, where material is removed or incised to form a pattern that will hold ink. It includes techniques such as hand or machine engraving, etching, and similar processes that produce recessed lines or areas for intaglio and other printing types. This sub-class falls under printing processes for manufacturing or reproducing printing surfaces.
[B41C 1/025] characterised by means for the liquid etching of substrates for the manufacturing of relief or intaglio printing forms, already provided with resist pattern -- A method for producing printing plates in which a substrate that already carries a resist pattern is subjected to liquid chemical etching to create the relief or intaglio image needed for printing. This process falls under the broader categories of printing operations and the manufacture of printing surfaces.
[B41C 1/04] using heads controlled by an electric information signal -- B41C 1/04 covers methods of preparing printing formes in which the material-depositing heads are operated by electronic data signals, such as digitally controlled ink-jet, electrophotographic or similar heads that place or shape the printing surface under computer-generated control. The classification includes systems where head activation, positioning or firing is governed directly by an electric information signal.
[B41C 1/045] Mechanical engraving heads -- B41C 1/045 covers mechanical engraving heads that form the printing surface by removing material from a plate or cylinder. These heads are used in printing processes that create raised or recessed patterns for ink transfer.
[B41C 1/05] Heat-generating engraving heads, e.g. laser beam, electron beam -- A heat-generating engraving head, such as a laser or electron beam, is used to create the printable form directly on a surface by locally removing or modifying material through intense heat. This technology falls under performing operations for printing, specifically the processes for manufacturing or reproducing printing surfaces and forme preparation.
[B41C 1/055] Thermographic processes for producing printing formes -- Thermographic processes for producing printing formes involve creating a relief image on a printing plate by applying heat to a heat-sensitive coating or material. The heat causes the coating to expand, harden, or change viscosity, forming the raised areas that will receive ink. These methods are used for quick, low-cost plate making in offset or flexographic printing.
[B41C 1/06] Transferring -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for preparing printing formes by transferring the design to the printing forme, e.g. transferring by heat or by pressure.
[B41C 1/08] by embossing, e.g. with a typewriter -- B41C 1/08 covers techniques for preparing printing forms through embossing, where a pattern of raised or recessed marks is created on a surface, typically by impact devices such as typewriters. The method forms the image or text on the medium that later receives ink for printing.
[B41C 1/10] for lithographic printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The compositions of the different types of image-forming layers.
