[B41F 33/0009] Central control units -- B41F 33/0009 covers central control units that coordinate the operation of printing presses, integrating functions such as start-stop control, speed regulation, sequence management and safety monitoring. These devices serve as the main electronic or mechanical hub that synchronises printing, counting, warning and other control functions within the machine.
[B41F 33/0018] Protection means against injury to the operator -- B41F 33/0018 covers safety devices integrated into printing machines or presses that protect the operator from injury. It includes protective shields, interlocking guards, emergency-stop mechanisms, sensors and other control features that detect hazardous conditions or prevent access to moving parts during operation.
[B41F 33/0027] Devices for scanning originals, printing formes or the like for determining or presetting the ink supply -- B41F 33/0027 covers devices incorporated in printing presses that scan originals, printed forms or similar items to assess ink usage and automatically adjust or preset the ink supply. These systems belong to the safety and control sub-group of printing-machine technology, ensuring consistent print quality by monitoring and managing ink flow during operation.
[B41F 33/0036] Devices for scanning or checking the printed matter for quality control -- B41F 33/0036 covers devices that scan or otherwise examine printed output to assess its quality during or after printing. These systems detect defects, verify color consistency, registration and other parameters, enabling real-time or post-process quality control in printing machines.
[B41F 33/0045] for automatically regulating the ink supply -- The subclass B41F 33/0045 covers devices in printing machines that automatically regulate the ink supply, adjusting flow to maintain consistent print quality. It includes control and safety mechanisms that respond to demand, pressure or other print conditions without manual intervention.
[B41F 33/0054] Devices for controlling dampening -- The CPC subclass B41F 33/0054 covers devices that control the dampening system in printing machines or presses. These devices regulate ink moisture to ensure consistent print quality and prevent defects, falling under the broader group for indicating, counting, warning, control or safety devices in printing operations.
[B41F 33/0063] Devices for measuring the thickness of liquid films on rollers or cylinders -- This subclass covers instruments that determine the thickness of liquid layers applied to rotating rollers or cylinders in printing equipment, such as film gauges or sensors used to monitor coating consistency.
[B41F 33/0072] Devices for measuring the pressure between cylinders or bearer rings -- This subclass covers devices that measure the pressure between the cylinders or bearer rings of printing presses, ensuring correct contact and preventing slippage. Such instruments are used to monitor and adjust operating pressure for consistent print quality.
[B41F 33/0081] Devices for scanning register marks -- Devices for scanning register marks are sensing systems integrated into printing presses that detect the position of registration marks on printed sheets to monitor and correct alignment of multiple colours or layers. They typically use optical, infrared or laser sensors and feed data to control circuits that adjust paper feed or plate positioning, ensuring accurate print registration.
[B41F 33/009] Devices for controlling numbering -- B41F 33/009 pertains to devices that control the numbering of printed material in printing machines or presses. These devices are part of the broader group of indicating, counting, warning, control or safety equipment used during the performing operations of printing.
[B41F 33/02] Arrangements of indicating devices, e.g. counters -- B41F 33/02 covers the layout and integration of indicating devices such as counters, warning lights, or control panels within printing machines and presses, focusing on how these devices are arranged to monitor and manage the printing process. It pertains to safety, counting, and diagnostic functions built into the press.
[B41F 33/025] Counters -- The CPC class B41F 33/025 covers counters incorporated in printing machines or presses for indicating the number of printed sheets, impressions, cycles or other operational events. These devices serve as measuring or warning elements that help operators monitor production volume and control process timing.
[B41F 33/04] Tripping devices or stop-motions -- B41F 33/04 covers devices fitted to printing presses that interrupt operation by tripping or stopping the machine, including safety, warning or control mechanisms that halt motion when a limit is reached or a fault is detected.
[B41F 33/045] for starting or stopping operation of numbering devices -- B41F 33/045 covers devices that control the initiation or cessation of numbering mechanisms in printing equipment. It includes actuators, sensors or control circuits specifically designed to start or stop the operation of counters, indicia rollers or other numbering components during a printing process.
[B41F 33/06] for starting or stopping operation of sheet or web feed -- B41F 33/06 covers mechanisms that initiate or halt the feed of sheets or webs in printing presses. These devices are used to control the timing of material movement, ensuring precise start-stop operation during printing processes.
[B41F 33/08] for starting or stopping operation of cylinders -- B41F 33/08 refers to safety or control devices incorporated in printing machines or presses that are designed to start or stop the operation of cylinders. These mechanisms are used to regulate cylinder motion, ensuring proper timing, preventing accidents and facilitating precise control during printing processes. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Printing Machines or Presses.
[B41F 33/10] for starting or stopping operation of damping or inking units -- B41F 33/10 covers devices that start or stop the operation of damping or inking units in printing presses, including mechanisms that control ink flow or dampening processes for safety, precision or efficiency.
[B41F 33/12] for starting or stopping the machine as a whole -- The CPC code B41F 33/12 covers devices that start or stop a printing press or printing machine as a whole, forming part of the broader group of indicating, counting, warning, control or safety devices for printing equipment. It pertains to mechanisms that initiate or halt the operation of the entire machine rather than individual components.
[B41F 33/14] Automatic control of tripping devices by feelers, photoelectric devices, pneumatic devices, or other detectors -- B41F 33/14 pertains to printing machines or presses and covers automatic control of tripping devices using feelers, photo-electric sensors, pneumatic actuators or other detectors to activate safety, warning or control functions in the printing process.
[B41F 33/16] Programming systems for automatic control of sequence of operations -- B41F 33/16 relates to programming systems that automatically control the sequence of operations in printing machines or presses. It includes software and hardware arrangements for defining, adjusting and executing step-by-step process flows, timing and coordination of printing functions, and integration with sensors or control units to ensure correct operation.
[B41F 33/18] Web break detection -- B41F 33/18 pertains to devices that detect a break in the web or material being printed on, used in printing machines or presses. These safety or control mechanisms signal a stoppage to prevent damage or waste, and fall under the broader category of indicating, counting, warning, control or safety devices for printing operations.
[B41F 3/32] Damping devices -- B41F 3/32 covers devices that reduce vibration or oscillation in cylinder printing presses. The damping mechanisms, such as springs, pneumatic or hydraulic dampers, are installed on the cylinder or frame to stabilize the press during operation, improving print quality and reducing wear.
[B41F 3/34] for offset printing -- B41F 3/34 covers offset printing performed with cylinder presses, where at least one rotating cylinder works in conjunction with a flat type-bed to transfer an inked image onto a substrate. This subclass specifies the use of the offset process on such machines, distinguishing them from other printing methods within the cylinder-press category.
[B41F 3/36] for intaglio or heliogravure printing -- B41F 3/36 classifies cylinder presses that perform intaglio or heliogravure printing, where a rotating cylinder works with a flat type-bed to transfer ink from engraved or chemically etched plates onto a substrate. The entry covers the specific mechanisms, feeding, and control features needed for these high-detail, recessed-image printing processes.
[B41F 3/38] Wiping mechanisms -- B41F 3/38 covers wiping mechanisms used in cylinder printing presses, where a cylinder works with a flat type-bed. The mechanisms remove excess ink from the cylinder surface before or after impression, typically using blades, rollers or cloths to ensure clean transfer and prevent smearing.
[B41F 3/40] for perfecting sheets, i.e. for printing on both sides thereof -- B41F 3/40 covers cylinder press arrangements that incorporate a perfecting unit to print on both sides of a sheet in a single pass. The feature enables simultaneous front-and-back image transfer, improving speed and registration in rotary printing processes.
[B41F 3/42] for multicolour printing on sheets -- B41F 3/42 pertains to cylinder presses that are configured to print multiple colours on individual sheets, using at least one rotating cylinder in cooperation with a flat type-bed to apply different inks in a single pass.
[B41F 3/44] for monocolour or multicolour printing on one side of webs, or for perfecting webs, i.e. monocolour or multicolour printing on both sides of webs -- This subclass covers cylinder presses that apply a single colour or multiple colours to one side of a web, as well as those capable of perfecting webs by printing on both sides, using mono- or multi-colour processes.
[B41F 3/46] Details -- B41F 3/46 pertains to specific details of cylinder presses that feature at least one cylinder working with a flat type-bed, focusing on components and mechanisms unique to this press configuration.
[B41F 3/48] Press frames -- B41F 3/48 covers the structural components that support and hold the cylinder and bed in cylinder printing presses, including the framework, brackets and supporting members that provide rigidity and alignment. These frames ensure precise positioning of the printing surfaces during operation.
[B41F 35/00] Cleaning arrangements or devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for cleaning parts removed from a printing machine,  cleaning means being part of a printing press including:  means for cleaning adapted to a specific purpose, e.g. cleaning of inking rollers or offset cylinders,  or means for cleaning being characterised by the cleaning method or device, e.g. wiping devices, spraying devices, suction devices,  means for recovering used solvents or residues,  and selection of materials or products for cleaning.
[B41F 35/001] Devices for cleaning parts removed from the printing machines  B41F35/003  takes precedence -- B41F 35/001 covers devices used to clean components that have been removed from printing machines, such as rollers, plates or screens, before they are reinstalled or discarded. The more specific subgroup B41F 35/003, which deals with cleaning particular parts, takes precedence over this broader category.
[B41F 35/002] for dampening rollers -- B41F 35/02 relates to cleaning arrangements or devices for printing machines, specifically dampening rollers that apply a thin, uniform film of water or fountain solution to printing plates or blankets. These rollers are integral to offset and other water-based printing processes, helping control ink transfer and prevent unwanted ink buildup on the press.
[B41F 35/003] for screen printers or parts thereof -- B41F 35/003 covers cleaning arrangements or devices specifically designed for screen printers or their components, such as mechanisms that remove ink, debris or dried paste from the screen and related parts. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, printing machines or presses, focusing on maintenance solutions that keep screen-printing equipment functional and produce high-quality prints.
[B41F 35/004] for cylindrical screens -- The CPC subgroup B41F 35/004 covers arrangements or devices specifically designed for cleaning, treating or maintaining cylindrical screens used in printing presses, including methods such as fluid jets, brushes, ultrasonic or chemical cleaning. It addresses the upkeep of screen surfaces that transfer ink or impression material.
[B41F 35/005] for flat screens -- B41F 35/005 covers cleaning arrangements or devices that are specifically designed for flat screens used in printing machines or presses, such as the cleaning of flat-panel displays, LCD panels or similar planar imaging surfaces during or after the printing process.
[B41F 35/006] for impression cylinders -- B41F 35/006 covers cleaning arrangements or devices specifically designed for the impression cylinders of printing machines or presses, addressing the removal of ink, debris or other contaminants to maintain proper cylinder performance and print quality.
[B41F 35/007] for supports of workpieces -- B41F 35/007 relates to cleaning arrangements or devices specifically designed for the supports that hold workpieces in printing machines. These include mechanisms to remove ink, debris or residue from plates, rollers or other holding elements during or after a printing operation.
[B41F 35/008] for doctor blades -- B41F 35/008 covers cleaning arrangements or devices specifically intended for doctor blades in printing machines or presses, such as mechanisms, tools or methods that remove ink, debris or residue to maintain blade performance and consistent ink metering.
[B41F 35/02] for forme cylinders -- B41F 35/02 covers cleaning-arrangements or devices specifically designed for the forme cylinders of printing machines. It includes methods and apparatus for removing ink, paper debris or other contaminants from the cylinder surface to maintain print quality and cylinder life.
[B41F 35/04] for inking rollers -- B41F 35/04 covers devices or arrangements that provide inking rollers for printing machines or presses, supplying ink to the form cylinder or blanket. These rollers are designed to ensure uniform ink distribution and may include features for cleaning or maintenance.
[B41F 35/06] for offset cylinders -- B41F 35/06 relates to cleaning arrangements or devices specifically intended for the offset cylinders of printing machines or presses, detailing methods and apparatus that remove ink, sludge or other contaminants from cylinder surfaces to preserve print quality and equipment performance.
[B41F 3/51] Type-beds -- B41F 3/51 covers the type-bed component of cylinder printing presses, i.e. the flat work-surface that holds the printing form and cooperates with a rotating cylinder to transfer ink to the substrate. It includes designs of the bed, its mounting, adjustment and conditioning features that affect pressure distribution and image quality.
[B41F 3/52] Apparatus for cooling, heating, or drying printing surfaces -- B41F 3/52 covers devices integrated with cylinder printing presses that regulate the temperature of the printing surface, including systems that cool, heat, or dry the cylinder or type-bed during operation. Such apparatus improve ink transfer, prevent smearing, and speed up production by controlling moisture and temperature.
[B41F 3/54] Impression cylinders -- B41F 3/54 covers impression cylinders used in cylinder printing presses where at least one cylinder works with a flat type-bed. The class includes cylinders that receive ink and transfer it to the substrate and related mounting and adjustment mechanisms.
[B41F 3/56] Devices for adjusting cylinders relative to type-beds and setting in adjusted position -- B41F 3/56 relates to mechanisms that adjust the cylinder of a cylinder press relative to its flat type-bed and retain the cylinder in the set position, facilitating precise alignment and pressure control during printing.
[B41F 3/58] Driving, synchronising, or control gear -- The B41F 3/58 subclass concerns the driving, synchronising and control mechanisms used in cylinder printing presses. It includes gear trains, motor drives, clutch and brake arrangements, electronic or hydraulic control circuits and devices that coordinate the rotation of the cylinder with the flat bed.
[B41F 3/60] for type-beds -- B41F 3/60 refers to cylinder printing presses where the type-bed, the flat surface that holds the printing form, is the primary component being acted upon or moved. This subclass includes mechanisms and features specifically designed for handling, feeding, or aligning type-beds during printing operations in cylinder presses.
[B41F 3/62] Applications of linkwork or gearing for producing reciprocatory or angular motion -- B41F 3/62 pertains to cylinder printing presses that employ linkwork or gearing arrangements to convert the cylinder's rotation into reciprocating or angular movement of the type-bed or other components, enabling controlled motion for printing operations.
[B41F 3/64] of crank motions -- This CPC subclass covers cylinder printing presses in which the cylinder's rotary movement is generated by a crank mechanism. It includes designs where a crank converts reciprocating motion into the cylinder's rotation, enabling the cylinder to cooperate with a flat type-bed during the printing cycle.
[B41F 3/66] of rack-and-pinion gearing -- B41F 3/66 covers cylinder printing presses that incorporate rack-and-pinion gearing to drive or index the printing cylinder relative to the type-bed. The gearing provides precise rotational control for register and speed adjustments during printing operations.
[B41F 3/68] with pinion rotating unidirectionally -- The CPC class B41F 3/68 covers cylinder presses that employ a pinion gear which drives the cylinder in one direction only. This arrangement provides unidirectional rotation of the printing cylinder, typically used to advance the web or sheet consistently while maintaining precise timing between the cylinder and type bed.
[B41F 3/70] Applications of directly-coupled motors -- B41F 3/70 covers printing cylinder presses in which the drive motor is mounted so that its output shaft is directly linked to the cylinder, eliminating intermediate gearing or belts. The classification includes designs that integrate the motor with the press frame, methods for controlling speed and torque, and related advantages such as reduced size, noise, and maintenance.
[B41F 3/72] of fluid motors -- The subclass B41F 3/72 relates to cylinder printing presses whose driving mechanism is a fluid motor, such as hydraulic or pneumatic actuators that move the cylinder or its associated components.
[B41F 3/74] Applications of clutches -- The B41F 3/74 subclass covers the use of clutches in cylinder printing presses, where a clutch engages or disengages the drive of the rotating cylinder or controls its speed and torque. It includes mechanisms that start, stop, reverse, or vary the motion of the cylinder during printing operations. This subclass is part of the broader group dealing with cylinder presses within the printing machine category.
[B41F 3/76] Applications of braking devices or of devices for stopping type-bed in registered position -- B41F 3/76 covers inventions that use braking mechanisms or other devices to hold the type-bed of a cylinder press in a fixed, registered position during operation. The classification applies to cylinder presses that employ at least one rotating cylinder working with a flat type-bed, and relates to the control of the bed's movement for accurate printing.
[B41F 3/78] Air cushions -- B41F 3/78 relates to cylinder printing presses that incorporate air cushions as a means of supporting, separating or feeding the type-bed or printing surface. The air cushions are used to provide controlled pressure or spacing between the cylinder and the flat bed during operation, improving print quality and reducing wear. This subclass covers designs and mechanisms whereby pneumatic cushions replace or supplement mechanical supports in such presses.
[B41F 3/80] for impression cylinders -- The subclass B41F 3/80 covers features and arrangements of impression cylinders used in cylinder printing presses, which transfer ink from the type-bed to the printed sheet. It includes designs of cylinder surfaces, mounting, driving, cleaning, and temperature control elements that affect printing quality and durability.
[B41F 3/81] Inking units -- B41F 3/81 covers the inking units used in cylinder presses, which supply ink to the printing surface. The units may include ink rollers, distributing systems and related mechanisms that transfer ink from a reservoir to the cylinder.
[B41F 3/82] Auxiliary devices -- This subclass covers auxiliary devices used with cylinder printing presses, i.e. equipment that supports or enhances the main press functions. It includes mechanisms for feeding sheets, drying, cleaning, ink delivery, and other accessories that are not part of the core cylinder-bed assembly.
[B41F 3/84] Arrangements of counting devices -- B41F 3/84 concerns the layout and integration of counting devices in cylinder presses, that is, printing machines where a rotating cylinder works with a flat type-bed. It covers how mechanisms for tallying sheets, impressions or job pieces are arranged within the press structure.
[B41F 3/86] Numbering devices -- Numbering devices for cylinder printing presses are mechanisms that automatically apply sequential or coded marks-such as numbers, letters, or symbols-onto printed material during operation. They integrate with the cylinder and type-bed to imprint identification, batch numbers, dates or other data directly onto each sheet as it passes through the press. This facilitates tracking, inventory control and product authentication in high-speed printing processes.
[B41F 5/00] Rotary machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Letterpress or relief printing machines use the following printing forms or printing plates:  all parts of the surface of the printing plate accepting ink to form the printing image are raised above the level of the non-printing parts of the printing image not accepting ink.  Flexographic printing presses use a printing plate, wherein the raised parts forming the printing image are formed from flexible material, e.g. rubber.
[B41F 5/02] for printing on sheets -- B41F 5/02 covers rotary printing machines that apply ink or other marking material to individual sheets, including the mechanisms for feeding, aligning and transferring the sheet through the print zone. The subclass includes apparatus where the sheet passes past a rotary cylinder or printing plate and is printed on one or both sides.
[B41F 5/04] for printing on webs -- B41F 5/04 relates to technologies for applying images, text or other markings onto continuous web material using rotary printing machines. It covers mechanisms for feeding, registering, transferring ink or other printable media, and associated processes that enable efficient web-to-web or web-to-substrate printing.
[B41F 5/06] with several printing units in sequential arrangement -- B41F 5/06 covers rotary printing machines that incorporate several printing units placed in a sequential order along the web path, allowing successive application of inks or images as the material advances. This arrangement is typical for multicolour or multi-pass printing where each unit adds a separate layer of print.
[B41F 5/08] with several printing units in both superimposed and sequential arrangement -- This CPC subclass covers rotary printing machines that incorporate multiple printing units arranged both superimposed and sequentially. Such configurations allow successive or overlapping impression of colors or inks on a moving web in a single-production line.
[B41F 5/10] with several printing units arranged side by side -- B41F 5/10 covers rotary printing machines that incorporate multiple printing units positioned side by side, enabling simultaneous printing of different colors or formats on a single web. This arrangement allows higher productivity and more complex print jobs compared to single-unit configurations.
[B41F 5/12] for printing on one side and on the other side of webs between the same forme and impression cylinders -- B41F 5/12 covers rotary printing machines that apply ink to both sides of a continuous web while the web passes between the same form (or plate) cylinder and impression cylinder, allowing simultaneous double-sided printing in a single pass. This subclass pertains to presses where the same set of cylinders effects the impression on each side of the web.
[B41F 5/14] with several impression cylinders arranged around a forme cylinder -- B41F 5/14 covers rotary printing machines in which a single forme cylinder carrying the printing image is surrounded by several impression cylinders that press the substrate against it. This arrangement enables simultaneous handling of multiple webs or sheets, improving speed and productivity in offset, flexographic or similar printing processes.
[B41F 5/16] for multicolour printing -- B41F 5/16 covers multicolour printing processes carried out on rotary printing machines. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, printing machines or presses, and specifically rotary machines, addressing techniques for applying two or more inks in a single pass.
[B41F 5/18] using one impression cylinder co-operating with several forme cylinders -- The B41F 5/18 subclass concerns rotary printing presses in which a single impression cylinder is arranged to work together with several forme cylinders, allowing multiple printing forms or colours to be applied in one passage through the press, thereby increasing productivity and simplifying the mechanical arrangement.
[B41F 5/20] specially adapted for proof printing -- The subclass B41F 5/20 pertains to rotary printing machines that have been specially adapted for proof printing, producing preliminary copies to verify layout, colour and composition before full-scale runs. It includes modifications that enable low-volume, high-quality output with precise registration and control of ink transfer.
[B41F 5/22] for indirect printing -- B41F 5/22 pertains to rotary printing machines that employ indirect printing, where the image is transferred from a plate or similar element to an intermediate carrier such as a blanket or cylinder before being impressed onto the substrate. It includes the mechanisms and arrangements that enable this transfer in rotary presses.
[B41F 5/24] for flexographic printing -- B41F 5/24 covers rotary printing machines designed for flexographic printing, a method that uses flexible relief plates to transfer fast-drying inks onto continuous substrates such as film, paper, or foil. The process is suited for high-speed runs of packaging, labels and other thin-material products.
[B41F 7/00] Rotary lithographic machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lithographic printing machines, which use the following printing forms or printing plates:  whole surface of the printing surface or plate is essentially even,  all parts of the surface of the printing plate forming the printing image have hydrophobic characteristics accepting ink to form the printing image,  whereas all the rest parts of the surface of said printing plate forming the non-printing part of the printing image have hydrophilic characteristics for repelling ink.
[B41F 7/02] for offset printing -- B41F 7/02 refers to rotary lithographic machines that are configured for offset printing, in which the image is transferred from a plate to a rubber blanket and then onto the final substrate. The subclass includes the specific arrangements of inking, dampening and plate-handling mechanisms required for the offset process.
[B41F 7/025] Multicolour printing or perfecting on sheets or on one or more webs, in one printing unit -- B41F 7/025 covers rotary lithographic machines that perform multicolour printing or perfecting on individual sheets or multiple webs within a single printing unit. The subclass deals with simultaneous application of several inks in one pass, enabling efficient colour registration and finishing on the same machine.
[B41F 7/04] using printing units incorporating one forme cylinder, one transfer cylinder, and one impression cylinder, e.g. for printing on webs -- The B41F 7/04 subclass covers rotary lithographic machines that employ a three-cylinder printing unit composed of a forme cylinder, a transfer cylinder and an impression cylinder, chiefly for printing on continuous webs.
[B41F 7/06] for printing on sheets -- B41F 7/06 covers inventions relating to the use of rotary lithographic machines for applying ink or other imprimables onto individual sheets. The classification includes apparatus features, sheet-handling arrangements and process steps that enable sheet-by-sheet printing with rotary lithography. It is a subcategory of printing machines or presses within the performing operations field.
[B41F 7/08] using one transfer cylinder co-operating with several forme cylinders for printing on sheets or webs, e.g. sampling of colours on one transfer cylinder -- The entry covers rotary lithographic machines that use a single transfer cylinder in cooperation with several forme cylinders to print on sheets or webs, for example when sampling colours on one transfer cylinder.
[B41F 7/10] using one impression cylinder co-operating with several transfer cylinders for printing on sheets or webs, e.g. satellite-printing units -- B41F 7/10 covers printing processes in which a single impression cylinder works together with multiple transfer cylinders to apply ink to sheets or webs, as in satellite-printing arrangements. This configuration allows simultaneous printing of several zones or colors using one primary cylinder.
[B41F 7/12] using two cylinders one of which serves two functions, e.g. as a transfer and impression cylinder in perfecting machines -- This subclass covers rotary lithographic printing machines that employ two cylinders where one cylinder performs two roles, acting both as the transfer cylinder and the impression cylinder in perfecting presses.
[B41F 7/14] with two or more impression cylinders coacting with a single transfer cylinder -- B41F 7/14 pertains to rotary lithographic printing machines that employ a single transfer cylinder to supply image to two or more impression cylinders simultaneously. This arrangement is used in offset printing to increase productivity by printing on multiple sheets or webs in a single pass.
[B41F 7/15] for printing on more than one web simultaneously -- The CPC subclass B41F 7/15 covers rotary lithographic machines that are designed to print on more than one web simultaneously, allowing parallel sheet processing in offset printing.
[B41F 7/16] for printing on non-deformable material, e.g. sheet metal -- B41F 7/16 designates rotary lithographic machines adapted for printing on rigid, non-deformable substrates such as sheet metal, where the material is not fed through a deformable web. The subclass covers apparatus and methods for feeding, registering, applying ink and curing on these hard surfaces, ensuring accurate image transfer without bending or stretching the workpiece.
[B41F 7/18] specially adapted for proof printing -- Rotary lithographic machines that include specific modifications for proof printing, such as adjustable pressures, specialised ink delivery or rapid plate change mechanisms, fall under CPC B41F 7/18. These adaptations enable the production of test or sample prints to check quality before full-scale runs.
[B41F 7/20] Details -- B41F 7/20 covers the specific details of rotary lithographic printing machines, including the design and arrangement of plate cylinders, ink and water transfer systems, impression cylinders, and mechanisms for plate indexing, registration and control. It addresses particular features, adjustments and auxiliary devices that affect the operation and performance of these presses.
[B41F 7/24] Damping devices -- B41F 7/24 relates to damping devices used in rotary lithographic printing machines, a subclass within printing machines or presses that perform operations. It addresses mechanisms that reduce vibration, oscillation or noise during the lithographic printing process, improving print stability and quality.
[B41F 7/26] using transfer rollers -- The code B41F 7/26 refers to rotary lithographic printing machines that employ transfer rollers to convey the inked image from the printing plate to the substrate. It covers the design, positioning and operation of such rollers within the press, including mechanisms for ensuring uniform transfer and registration.
[B41F 7/265] for damping from the inside of the cylinders -- This subgroup covers rotary lithographic printing presses in which the damping of vibrations is achieved by mechanisms placed inside the printing cylinders. It includes designs that reduce oscillation of the cylinder surface to improve print quality.
[B41F 7/28] using brushes -- B41F 7/28 deals with the use of brushes in rotary lithographic printing machines, describing how brushes are employed to apply, remove, or otherwise treat ink or cleaning surfaces during the lithographic printing operation.
[B41F 7/30] using spraying elements -- The B41F 7/30 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers rotary lithographic printing machines that employ spraying elements to deposit ink, coating or other fluids onto the printing cylinder or web. It specifies the use of spray nozzles, atomizers or similar devices as part of the ink-application system, distinguishing these machines from those that rely solely on rollers or blankets for ink transfer.
[B41F 7/32] Ducts, containers, or like supply devices for liquids -- In rotary lithographic machines this subclass covers ducts, containers and related supply devices that convey liquids such as inks, cleaning fluids or water to the printing cylinder or other machine components.
[B41F 7/34] Endless bands -- The B41F 7/34 subclass covers endless bands used in rotary lithographic machines, describing the continuous loop media that transports paper, film or other substrates through the press. It includes the composition, dimensions, tensioning, and feeding mechanisms of these belts, as well as any special features that enable seamless operation during high-speed lithographic printing.
[B41F 7/36] Inking-rollers serving also to apply ink repellants -- In rotary lithographic presses, these are rollers that transfer ink onto the printing plate while simultaneously applying a repellant layer to prevent ink buildup in non-image areas, improving print quality and reducing waste.
[B41F 7/37] with supercooling for condensation of air moisture -- A rotary lithographic press that incorporates a supercooling system to chill the printing zone or ink film so that moisture from the surrounding air condenses and is removed, preventing water-related defects during printing. This feature refines control of humidity within the machine.
[B41F 7/38] Absorbent pads -- The subclass B41F 7/38 covers absorbent pads used in rotary lithographic printing machines, such as those that hold dampening fluid, collect excess ink, or clean printing plates during operation. It addresses the design, material composition, mounting, and service life of these pads within the printing press.
[B41F 7/40] Devices for tripping or lifting damping rollers -- These are mechanisms attached to rotary lithographic printing machines that release or raise the damping rollers, which normally press the blanket against the plate. By tripping or lifting the rollers, the device allows clean paper feeding, plate changes or start-up without unwanted ink transfer. Such equipment is essential for maintaining print quality and reducing downtime.
[B41F 9/00] Rotary intaglio printing presses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Intaglio or heliographic printing machines, which use the following printing forms or printing plates:  all parts of the surface of the printing plate accepting ink to form the printing image are below the level, e.g. engraved, of the non-printing parts of the printing surface not accepting ink.
[B41F 9/001] Heliostatic printing -- B41F 9/001 covers heliostatic printing, a method in which the printing plate or substrate remains stationary while the image-forming source moves, often employed in rotary intaglio printing presses for continuous, high-speed reproduction of cylindrical or curved items. This subclass falls under the broader category of printing machines or presses within the performing operations field.
[B41F 9/002] Sheet printing presses  B41F9/021  takes precedence -- B41F 9/002 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to sheet printing presses within the category of rotary intaglio printing presses, with a note that B41F 9/021 takes precedence when applicable. This classification is used for patents related to specific types of printing machinery.
[B41F 9/003] Web printing presses  B41F9/023  takes precedence -- B41F 9/003 covers web-printing presses, a subset of rotary intaglio printing presses that handle continuous rolls of material rather than individual sheets. These machines feed a web through the press for printing on both sides or multiple colors. The more specific subclass B41F 9/023 supersedes this class where applicable.
[B41F 9/004] Printing two webs with the same printing cylinder -- The B41F 9/004 subclass covers rotary intaglio printing presses that feed two separate webs through a single printing cylinder, enabling simultaneous impression of both webs in a single pass. This arrangement is used for high-speed, continuous-tone printing where identical or coordinated images are required on each web.
[B41F 9/005] Printing on two sides of the same web -- The subclass B41F 9/005 addresses techniques and devices for applying images or text to both faces of a single continuous web in a rotary intaglio press, including mechanisms that flip, turn, or otherwise position the web so that the opposite side receives a print during the same processing cycle.
[B41F 9/006] simultaneously -- The code B41F 9/006 covers rotary intaglio printing presses that perform two or more printing actions at the same time, such as feeding, inking, and impression occurring simultaneously. It is a sub-class of printing machines or presses within the broader category of performing operations. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO for patents involving concurrent processes in rotary intaglio equipment.
[B41F 9/007] with the same printing cylinder -- This subclass refers to rotary intaglio printing presses in which the same printing cylinder is used throughout the press, enabling multiple or repeat print operations without cylinder change. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Printing → Printing Machines or Presses → Rotary intaglio printing presses.
[B41F 9/008] Means for preventing egress of vapours -- B41F 9/008 covers devices incorporated in rotary intaglio printing presses that stop or limit the escape of vapours generated during printing. This includes seals, gaskets, enclosed chambers or vent-control systems designed to contain inks, solvents or other volatile substances, helping maintain safety, cleanliness and print quality.
[B41F 9/009] Reversible printing presses  B41F9/02  takes precedence -- The B41F 9/009 class defines reversible rotary intaglio printing presses, in which the plate or cylinder can be rotated or flipped to allow printing on opposite sides or rapid plate changes. These machines belong to the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and printing machines or presses. The narrower subclass B41F 9/02 has priority over this entry.
[B41F 9/01] for indirect printing -- B41F 9/01 pertains to rotary intaglio printing presses that employ indirect printing methods, where the image is first transferred to an intermediate surface - such as a transfer cylinder, roller or plate - before being applied to the final substrate. This subclass includes machines that use transfer inks, relief rollers or other intermediary mechanisms rather than direct contact between the engraved cylinder and the print material.
[B41F 9/02] for multicolour printing -- B41F 9/02 relates to rotary intaglio printing presses equipped for multicolour printing, featuring multiple ink stations or mechanisms that apply different inks to a rotating cylinder either simultaneously or in succession, and providing means for accurate registration of the various colours on the substrate.
[B41F 9/021] Sheet printing presses -- The CPC subclass B41F 9/021 refers to sheet printing presses, a type of rotary intaglio press designed to print on individual sheets rather than continuous webs. These machines are used in printing operations to apply high-quality images or text onto paper or similar substrates.
[B41F 9/023] Web printing presses -- B41F 9/023 covers rotary intaglio web printing presses, which feed a continuous web of substrate through gravure cylinders to transfer ink in high-speed, high-volume applications such as newspapers, magazines, and packaging. The press typically includes web unwinding, printing, drying or curing, and rewinding stations within a single rotary system.
[B41F 9/025] with horizontally arranged printing units -- Rotary intaglio printing presses that have multiple printing units placed side-by-side along a horizontal axis rather than stacked vertically. The arrangement allows simultaneous impression of several plates on a rotating cylinder, increasing speed and registration accuracy for high-security print jobs such as banknotes, passports and fine art reproductions. This configuration falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification B41F 9/025.
[B41F 9/026] with vertically arranged printing units -- Rotary intaglio printing presses that have their printing units stacked one above the other, forming a vertical column of cylinders. This arrangement enables multiple impressions to be made in a compact layout, often used for high-speed banknote or security-document production.
[B41F 9/028] of the satellite type -- This subclass pertains to rotary intaglio printing presses that employ a satellite arrangement, in which the printing unit or cylinder is mounted on a peripheral carrier that rotates around the main cylinder. The satellite design enables multiple impression stations and flexible handling of the printing web, often allowing simultaneous application of several inks or colors.
[B41F 9/04] specially adapted for proof printing -- The CPC subclass B41F 9/04 covers rotary intaglio printing presses that are specially adapted for proof printing, meaning they are configured to produce trial or test impressions of a design before full-scale production. These machines incorporate features such as adjustable pressure, limited run capability, or interchangeable plates to ensure accurate representation of the final print while using the rotary intaglio process.
[B41F 9/06] Details -- B41F 9/06 pertains to specific components or aspects of rotary intaglio printing presses, focusing on details that enhance functionality, efficiency, or precision in the printing process.
[B41F 9/061] Inking devices -- This subclass relates to rotary intaglio printing presses and covers devices that apply ink to the engraved cylinder or plate, such as ink rollers, troughs, pumps and related mechanisms that ensure uniform ink transfer, distribution, metering and cleaning during printing.
[B41F 9/063] Using inking rollers -- B41F 9/063 relates to the use of inking rollers in rotary intaglio printing presses, a subclass of printing machines or presses. It covers methods and arrangements for applying ink to the printing cylinder or plate with rollers to achieve uniform ink transfer and improve printing efficiency.
[B41F 9/065] Using inking rails -- B41F 9/065 covers rotary intaglio printing presses in which inking rails are employed to transfer ink uniformly onto the engraved printing cylinder, ensuring consistent coverage and efficient ink distribution during high-speed runs. This feature is specific to intaglio presses that use rotating cylinders rather than flat beds.
[B41F 9/066] Enclosures -- The B41F 9/066 subclass covers enclosures for rotary intaglio printing presses, including protective housings, cabinets, safety covers and other structures that surround or seal the press to contain ink, dust or debris and to provide operator safety.
[B41F 9/068] End seals between cylinder and housing -- The code B41F 9/068 covers sealing arrangements that close the gap between the rotating cylinder and its surrounding housing in rotary intaglio printing presses. These end seals prevent ink, water or air leakage while allowing the cylinder to spin at high speed, thereby maintaining print quality and protecting the press internals.
[B41F 9/08] Wiping mechanisms -- Wiping mechanisms are components in rotary intaglio printing presses that remove excess ink from the engraved cylinder after each impression, ensuring consistent ink transfer and preventing smearing. They usually consist of a blade, roller or air jet that contacts the cylinder at a controlled pressure and speed.
[B41F 9/10] Doctors, scrapers, or like devices -- B41F 9/10 covers doctors, scrapers and similar tools used in rotary intaglio printing presses to level, clean or remove excess ink from printing plates. These devices are positioned under performing operations → printing → printing machines or presses → rotary intaglio printing presses in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B41F 9/1009] with reciprocating movement -- The CPC subclass B41F 9/1009 pertains to rotary intaglio printing presses that incorporate a reciprocating movement. It covers machines where a part of the press, such as the plate cylinder or impression roller, moves back and forth relative to the rotating elements during the printing cycle.
[B41F 9/1018] using a wiping cylinder -- The classification B41F 9/1018 covers the use of a wiping cylinder in rotary intaglio printing presses, where a cylinder is employed to remove excess ink or residue from the printed surface, plate or blanket during the printing cycle, improving ink transfer and print quality.
[B41F 9/1027] Tripping devices -- Tripping devices are mechanisms that interrupt or stop the rotation of a rotary intaglio press, typically by releasing or engaging a brake, clutch or latch to control sheet feeding, printing cycles, or safety. They are used to regulate the timing of the impression and to protect the machine or operator.
[B41F 9/1036] Clamping and adjusting devices -- B41F 9/1036 covers devices that clamp or hold components of rotary intaglio printing presses and mechanisms for adjusting their position or pressure during operation. These elements ensure proper sheet registration, ink transfer and press stability.
[B41F 9/1045] using fluid pressure -- The subclass B41F 9/1045 covers rotary intaglio printing presses that employ fluid pressure, such as hydraulic or pneumatic systems, to drive the impression cylinder and transfer ink from engraved plates to a substrate. It focuses on using pressurised liquid or gas to generate the necessary printing force.
[B41F 9/1054] Sharpening, honing devices -- The B41F 9/1054 subclass covers devices specifically designed to sharpen or hone printing plates used in rotary intaglio printing presses, including mechanisms for edge refinement, surface polishing and restoring plate sharpness to maintain print quality.
[B41F 9/1063] Seals between blades and cylinder -- The code B41F 9/1063 concerns the seals positioned between the printing blades and the rotating cylinder in a rotary intaglio printing press, designed to prevent ink leakage and maintain precise pressure contact. These seals are typically made of resilient materials such as rubber or polymer and ensure consistent ink transfer during high-speed operation.
[B41F 9/1072] Blade construction -- The B41F 9/1072 subclass covers the structural design and material composition of blades used in rotary intaglio printing presses, including blade shape, mounting arrangements, tensioning mechanisms, and any features that affect ink transfer or pressure distribution during printing. It pertains specifically to the blade components of rotary intaglio machines.
[B41F 9/1081] using two or more blades -- The CPC subclass B41F 9/1081 covers rotary intaglio printing presses that employ two or more blades, typically for applying or removing ink, wiping the printing surface, or shaping the impression. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, printing machines or presses, and rotary intaglio presses.
[B41F 9/109] exchanging doctor blades -- The CPC subclass B41F 9/109 covers the procedure or mechanism for removing and installing doctor blades in rotary intaglio printing presses. Doctor blades are thin metal strips that scrape excess ink from the printing cylinder, and the subclass includes devices, tools, or methods that facilitate their exchange to maintain print quality and reduce downtime.
[B41F 9/12] Absorbent pads -- B41F 9/12 covers the use of absorbent pads in rotary intaglio printing presses, including pads that collect ink, dampen plates or clean the press surface during operation. The classification applies to any pad material or design specifically adapted for handling fluids or residues in that press type.
[B41F 9/14] Continuous flexible surfaces, e.g. endless bands -- B41F 9/14 pertains to the use of continuous flexible surfaces, such as endless bands, belts or loops, in rotary intaglio printing presses. It covers the design, material, and handling of these flexible carriers that convey the printing substrate around the press.
[B41F 9/16] Removing or recovering ink from wiping mechanisms -- B41F 9/16 pertains to rotary intaglio printing presses and covers methods or apparatus for removing ink that has adhered to wiping or cleaning mechanisms, enabling recovery and reuse of the ink. It addresses systems that extract residual ink from rollers, blades or other wiping components during the printing process.
[B41F 9/18] Auxiliary devices for exchanging forme cylinders -- Auxiliary devices for exchanging forme cylinders in rotary intaglio printing presses are mechanisms that aid the removal, insertion or swapping of the printing cylinders, improving set-up speed and reducing manual handling during press operation.
[B41G] APPARATUS FOR BRONZE PRINTING, LINE PRINTING, OR FOR BORDERING OR EDGING SHEETS OR LIKE ARTICLES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bronze printing apparatus using bronze metal material for printing types or printing plates.  Special types of printing apparatus for printing sheet like print products having a specific print image, like lines and/or prints only on sheets edges or borders,  Perforating apparatus exclusively in form of auxiliary devices being part of or in line with a printing machine or press.
[B41G 1/00] Apparatus for bronze printing or for like operations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing apparatus of flat bed-type or of rotary type having printing letters or types, or respectively a printing plate made from bronze metallic material.  Specific technology having being mostly used in previous centuries e.g. in East Asia.
[B41G 1/02] platen type -- B41G 1/02 covers platen-type devices used in bronze or line printing, where a flat plate (the platen) presses against a moving sheet to transfer ink or to apply borders or edging. The classification pertains to apparatus that perform the printing operation with a stationary or reciprocating platen rather than a rotating cylinder.
[B41G 1/04] cylinder type -- A cylinder-type device in which a rotating cylinder carries a relief matrix that transfers ink onto paper or similar sheets to produce bronze-printed, line-printed, or bordered/edged images. The cylinder rotates in synchronization with the substrate feed, allowing continuous printing of fine lines or decorative borders. This subclass falls under apparatus for bronze printing and related sheet-bordering operations.
[B41G 3/00] Apparatus for printing lines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing apparatus having printing plates specifically adapted to print specific images, essentially lines on various substrates.  Specific technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41G 5/00] Apparatus for bordering or edging sheets or like articles, e.g. for producing black rims on mourning cards -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing apparatus having printing plates specifically adapted to print specific print images essentially on borders or edges of sheet like articles.  Specific technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41G 7/00] Auxiliary perforating apparatus associated with printing devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheet or web perforating apparatus exclusively being part of or being in line with printing apparatus or presses.  Rotary and flat perforating devices.
[B41G 7/003] for platen presses -- This subclass covers auxiliary perforating devices that are used with platen printing presses to create holes or perforations in the printed sheet or its backing. The apparatus is intended to operate in conjunction with the press and may be mounted on or independent of the main printing mechanism.
[B41G 7/006] for cylinder presses -- B41G 7/006 designates auxiliary perforating devices that are integrated with or attached to cylinder-presses used in bronze, line, or edging printing. These apparatuses create perforations in sheets or similar articles during the printing process.
[B41J] TYPEWRITERS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Printing material/media: paper or temporary record carriers from which records are transferred to a paper (not: printing masters, e.g. formes or printing plates) - Paper: printing material, including similar flexible material
[B41J 1/00] Kinds of typewriters or of selective printing mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Type-writer: device with printing elements having complete characters, thus not built up from dots
[B41J 1/02] with separate or detached types or dies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In general with additional characters
[B41J 1/04] with types or dies carried upon levers or radial arms, e.g. manually operated -- This subclass covers typewriters in which the printing characters or dies are mounted on levers or on radial arms that swing to strike the paper, typically operated manually. It is part of the broader groups for printing and typewriter mechanisms.
[B41J 1/06] on power-operated levers or arms -- B41J 1/06 covers typewriter mechanisms that employ powered levers or arms to move the carriage, print head or other printing elements, rather than manual operation. It includes designs where electric, pneumatic or hydraulic actuation drives the lever's motion for selective printing.
[B41J 1/08] with types or dies carried on sliding bars or rods -- B41J 1/08 pertains to typewriters or selective printing devices that use individual types or dies mounted on sliding bars or rods, allowing each character to be positioned and struck independently during printing. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, and it specifically addresses mechanisms where the type elements move longitudinally along guides to achieve selective impression.
[B41J 1/10] on end surfaces thereof -- B41J 1/10 covers features or modifications concerning the end surfaces of typewriters or their selective printing mechanisms. It pertains to the design, treatment, or utilization of the terminal faces of parts that engage the paper or typebars.
[B41J 11/00] Devices or arrangements for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Inventions that are independent from the length of the copy material (the copy material therefore including cut sheets and continuous webs), e.g. ink-jet printers with irradiating or cutting devices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Script supports connected to the typewriter or printer -> B41J29/15
[B41J 11/0005] Curl smoothing, i.e. smoothing down corrugated printing material, e.g. by pressing means acting on wrinkled printing material -- The B41J 11/0005 classification covers devices or arrangements that smooth curls and wrinkles in printed sheets or webs by applying pressing forces. It relates to methods for flattening corrugated printing material, typically using rollers or similar mechanisms to eliminate curl before further processing.
[B41J 11/001] Handling wide copy materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Selective printing machines which are designed such that they allow to handle copy materials being wider than "usual" copy materials.
[B41J 11/0015] for treating before, during or after printing or for uniform coating or laminating the copy material before or after printing  selective coating  B41J2/2114 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Selective coating, i.e. coating dot by dot -> B41J2/2114
[B41J 11/002] Curing or drying the ink on the copy materials, e.g. by heating or irradiating -- B41J 11/002 covers methods for curing or drying ink on copy material in printing devices such as typewriters, including processes that use heat or various forms of irradiation. It falls under devices for handling sheet or web material in the broader printing and performing-operations categories.
[B41J 11/0021] using irradiation -- B41J 11/0021 refers to typewriter-style printing devices that create characters on sheet or web material by irradiating the copy, such as using laser, ultraviolet or other radiation, rather than mechanical impact. The subclass covers arrangements for supporting and handling the sheet while the image is formed through exposure.
[B41J 11/00212] Controlling the irradiation means, e.g. image-based controlling of the irradiation zone or control of the duration or intensity of the irradiation -- The B41J 11/00212 subclass covers mechanisms that regulate the light source used in printing devices, such as typewriters, by controlling where the radiation is applied, how long it is emitted, or its intensity. This includes image-based methods that adjust the irradiation zone to match the desired print pattern, ensuring precise exposure of sheet or web material during the printing process.
[B41J 11/00214] using UV radiation -- The subclass B41J 11/00214 addresses typewriter-related devices or arrangements that employ ultraviolet radiation to treat copy material in sheet or web form, such as using UV light to cure inks, harden coatings or dry printed images during or after printing operations. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, specifically focusing on UV-based processing of the media.
[B41J 11/00216] using infrared [IR] radiation or microwaves -- This subclass deals with type-writer-related printing equipment that uses infrared radiation or microwaves to support, handle, or process sheet or web copy material. It falls under performing operations, printing, typewriters, and devices or arrangements for handling copy material.
[B41J 11/00218] Constructional details of the irradiation means, e.g. radiation source attached to reciprocating print head assembly or shutter means provided on the radiation source -- B41J 11/00218 covers the structural features of irradiation devices used in printing typewriters, focusing on how a radiation source is integrated with a reciprocating print-head assembly or equipped with a shutter mechanism. It details the mounting, shielding and control elements that enable precise exposure of sheet or web copy material during the printing process.
[B41J 11/0022] using convection means, e.g. by using a fan for blowing or sucking air -- B41J 11/0022 pertains to devices or arrangements for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form, such as those used with printers or typewriters. It specifically covers the use of convection means, for example a fan that blows or draws air, to move or position the material during operation.
[B41J 11/00222] Controlling the convection means -- B41J 11/00222 covers systems that regulate the convection or airflow used to move or position copy material in sheet or web form within typewriter-related printing equipment. The classification pertains to devices or arrangements that control fans, ducts, or heating elements to ensure proper handling of paper during operation.
[B41J 11/00224] comprising movable shutters, e.g. for redirection of an air flow -- The entry B41J 11/00224 covers devices used in printing typewriters that support or handle sheet or web material and include movable shutters to redirect airflow. Such shutters can be positioned to control air movement across the copy material during operation, improving handling or drying processes. This falls under the broader category of performing operations in printing.
[B41J 11/0024] using conduction means, e.g. by using a heated platen -- B41J 11/0024 concerns typewriter or printing mechanisms that employ conduction, for example a heated platen, to support or handle sheet or web copy material during printing operations.
[B41J 11/00242] Controlling the temperature of the conduction means -- B41J 11/00242 relates to typewriter or printing devices that handle sheet or web material and includes arrangements for regulating the temperature of the conductive components, such as heating elements or electrical contacts, to ensure stable performance and prevent overheating.
[B41J 11/00244] Means for heating the copy materials before or during printing -- The CPC subclass B41J 11/00244 covers arrangements incorporated in typewriter-style printing machines that heat the paper or other copy material before or during the impression. It includes heating elements, temperature-control devices and associated structures that warm the sheet to improve ink transfer, reduce curl or enable special printing effects. The focus is on means that treat the copy material in situ as part of the printing process.
[B41J 11/0025] Handling copy materials differing in width -- This subclass covers devices or arrangements that adjust, guide, or support sheets, strips or webs whose width varies, allowing typewriters or printing machines to handle different formats; it includes adjustable rollers, side guides or width-selection mechanisms that can be set manually or automatically for each material.
[B41J 11/003] Paper-size detection, i.e. automatic detection of the length and/or width of copy material -- B41J 11/003 covers automatic systems that detect the length and/or width of sheet or web copy material, allowing printing or typewriter machines to recognize paper size and adjust handling, feeding or formatting accordingly. The technology is used to ensure correct processing of various paper dimensions without manual measurement.
[B41J 11/0035] Handling copy materials differing in thickness  B41J11/20  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Platen adjustments for varying the strength of impression, for a varying number of papers or for alignment -> B41J11/20
[B41J 11/004] Platenless printing, i.e. conveying the printing material freely, without support on its back, through the printing zone opposite to the print head -- This subclass refers to printing devices that convey the printing medium without a supporting platen, allowing the sheet or web to move freely through the print zone opposite the print head. It includes mechanisms for handling copy material in sheet or web form where the material is not backed by a rigid support during printing.
[B41J 11/0045] Guides for printing material  curl smoothing  B41J11/0005 ; platens  B41J11/02 ,  B41J11/06 ; guiding webs  B41J15/046 -- The CPC subclass B41J 11/0045 belongs to the hierarchy performing operations → printing → typewriters and relates to devices or arrangements that support or handle copy material in sheet or web form. It specifically covers guides used to smooth curl in printing material, various platens, and mechanisms for guiding webs during the printing process.
[B41J 11/005] Guides in the printing zone, e.g. guides for preventing contact of conveyed sheets with printhead -- The CPC subclass B41J 11/005 covers guides positioned in the printing zone of typewriter-like printing devices that keep conveyed sheets or webs from touching the printhead. It includes any rails, edges, or positioning elements that direct the copy material and maintain proper spacing during printing.
[B41J 11/0055] Lateral guides, e.g. guides for preventing skewed conveyance of printing material -- Lateral guides are devices mounted in printing or typewriter mechanisms to keep sheets or web material aligned as they move, preventing skewed conveyance. They form part of the supporting and handling arrangements for copy material and are classified under CPC code B41J 11/0055.
[B41J 11/006] Means for preventing paper jams or for facilitating their removal -- B41J 11/006 covers features incorporated in typewriter and other printing machines that stop paper from jamming or make jam removal easier. It includes mechanical, pneumatic or electronic devices such as feeders, guides, rollers or sensors designed to keep sheets moving smoothly and to release them quickly when a blockage occurs. This subclass falls under the broader group for devices handling copy material in sheet or web form.
[B41J 11/0065] Means for printing without leaving a margin on at least one edge of the copy material, e.g. edge-to-edge printing -- B41J 11/0065 covers devices or arrangements that allow a printer to place ink or toner right up to the edge of a sheet or web, eliminating the traditional margin on at least one side and enabling edge-to-edge printing. This class falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, typewriters, and handling copy material.
[B41J 11/007] Conveyor belts or like feeding devices -- B41J 11/007 covers conveyor belts and similar feeding mechanisms that move sheet or web material through printing equipment, especially typewriters. These devices are designed to support, transport and position the copy material during the printing operation.
[B41J 11/0075] Low-paper indication, i.e. indicating the state when copy material has been used up nearly or completely -- The entry covers mechanisms that signal when a typewriter's paper supply is nearly exhausted or completely used up, providing a visual, auditory or tactile indication of low-paper condition within the paper-handling system.
[B41J 11/008] Controlling printhead for accurately positioning print image on printing material, e.g. with the intention to control the width of margins -- B41J 11/008 covers methods and devices that control a printhead to position the printed image precisely on paper or other media, especially to adjust margin width. It is used in typewriter-related printing technology to achieve accurate placement of characters and graphics. The classification falls under performing operations, printing, typewriters, and addresses handling of sheet or web material.
[B41J 11/0085] Using suction for maintaining printing material flat  on rotatable drums  B41J13/226 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B41J 11/0085 pertains to devices that use suction to keep printing material flat on rotatable drums, specifically in the context of typewriters and similar printing mechanisms. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office for organizing patent information.
[B41J 11/009] Detecting type of paper, e.g. by automatic reading of a code that is printed on a paper package or on a paper roll or by sensing the grade of translucency of the paper -- The entry covers devices or arrangements used in typewriter printing that identify the type of paper being fed, for example by automatically reading a code printed on a paper package or roll, or by sensing the paper's translucency grade, to ensure appropriate handling of sheet or web material.
[B41J 11/0095] Detecting means for copy material, e.g. for detecting or sensing presence of copy material or its leading or trailing end -- B41J 11/0095 concerns sensors or detecting devices used in printing and typewriter equipment to sense the presence, leading edge or trailing end of sheet or web copy material.
[B41J 11/02] Platens -- Platens are flat, rigid plates that serve as the backing surface for paper or other sheet material as it passes through a printing or typewriter mechanism. By pressing the copy material against an inked form or printing surface, they ensure uniform pressure, proper alignment and consistent image transfer during the operation. This device falls under the CPC class for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form.
[B41J 11/04] Roller platens -- Roller platens are cylindrical pressing elements that support and transport sheet or web material while applying uniform pressure during printing operations. They are used in typewriter-related printing devices to ensure even contact between the inked substrate and the printing surface, improving image transfer and reducing distortion. This class covers their design, mounting, and mechanisms for driving or rotating the rollers.
[B41J 11/053] with sound-deadening devices -- The subclass B41J 11/053 covers typewriter or similar printing devices that incorporate sound-deadening elements to reduce the noise generated during operation. It falls under the broader category of devices for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form, specifically addressing acoustic insulation within the machine's mechanisms.
[B41J 11/057] Structure of the surface -- B41J 11/057 pertains to the specific structure of surfaces involved in devices or arrangements for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form, focusing on aspects like texture, coating, or design that affect functionality.
[B41J 11/06] Flat page-size platens -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Using suction for maintaining printing material flat -> B41J11/0085
[B41J 11/08] Bar or like line-size platens -- In the CPC, B41J 11/08 covers bar-type or line-size platens, which are thin, elongated supports that hold paper or web material during printing or typing, ensuring consistent line spacing and alignment. They are used in typewriters and similar devices to guide sheet movement.
[B41J 11/10] Anvil or like character-size platens -- B41J 11/10 covers an anvil or similar character-size platens used in typewriter mechanisms to support and guide the paper or typed characters during printing operations. These components provide a fixed surface against which typebars strike, ensuring proper alignment, spacing and imprint quality and are considered part of the handling of copy material in sheet form.
[B41J 11/13] Backings or blankets -- B41J 11/13 covers the backings or blankets used in printing presses, including rubber, fabric or other flexible layers that support the printing surface and transfer ink onto paper or web material. It pertains to the components that hold or cushion the copy material during the printing operation.
[B41J 11/14] Platen-shift mechanisms -- B41J 11/14 covers mechanisms that move the platen of a typewriter or similar printing device to advance the paper or web during operation. The classification relates to devices or arrangements for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form, specifically the shift mechanisms that position the platen for line or character spacing.
[B41J 11/16] with balancing means -- This CPC subclass covers typewriter mechanisms that incorporate balancing means to equalize the load on the sheet or web during feeding and transport, improving smooth operation of the copy-handling device. It lies within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, typewriters, and devices for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form.
[B41J 11/18] Platen-impression arrangements -- Platen-impression arrangements are mechanisms that use one or more platens to press a printing surface or typewriter ribbon against a sheet or web of paper, transferring ink or impressions. The class covers designs for supporting, moving, and applying pressure to the material, including adjustable pressure systems and platen configurations.
[B41J 1/12] on side surfaces thereof, e.g. fixed thereto -- The CPC subclass B41J 1/12 covers typewriters that have printing or striking elements mounted on side surfaces of the device, such as fixed side-mounted mechanisms. It pertains to designs where the selective printing mechanism is attached to the sides rather than a central carriage. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 11/20] Platen adjustments for varying the strength of impression, for a varying number of papers, for wear or for alignment -- The CPC class B41J 11/20 covers devices or mechanisms that adjust the platen of a printing or typewriter system to modify the impression force, accommodate different numbers of sheets, compensate for wear, or achieve precise alignment of the copy material. Such adjustments enable consistent print quality and reliable operation across varying printing conditions.
[B41J 11/22] Paper-carriage guides or races -- B41J 11/22 covers guides or races that control the movement of the paper carriage in typewriters, ensuring smooth and accurate paper feeding during printing. These devices are designed to support or handle sheet or web material as it is advanced across the typewriter.
[B41J 11/24] Detents, brakes, or couplings for feed rollers or platens -- The B41J 11/24 subclass covers mechanical elements such as detents, braking devices and coupling mechanisms that control the motion of feed rollers or platens in printing typewriter equipment, ensuring precise handling of sheet or web material during operation. It includes designs that engage, release or lock the rollers or platens to regulate feeding or positioning of the copy medium.
[B41J 11/26] Pin feeds -- B41J 11/26 pertains to pin-feed mechanisms used in typewriter printing devices, describing arrangements that feed individual pins or rods into the type-bar or matrix area to position characters for imprinting on paper or other media.
[B41J 11/27] on or within the platen-rollers -- B41J 11/27 covers devices or arrangements for supporting or handling sheet or web copy material where the supporting elements are placed on or within the platen rollers of printing typewriters. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters and pertains to mechanisms that integrate the platen roller into material handling.
[B41J 11/28] Pin wheels -- The B41J 11/28 entry concerns pin wheels, rotating elements equipped with a series of pins that engage and advance sheet or web material in printing devices such as typewriters. They serve as a support or handling mechanism that moves the copy material incrementally during the printing operation.
[B41J 11/30] Pin traction elements other than wheels, e.g. pins on endless bands -- The CPC subclass B41J 11/30 covers pin-type traction elements used instead of wheels, such as pins mounted on endless bands, for moving sheet or web material in printing or typewriter mechanisms. It falls under devices for supporting or handling copy material within the broader categories of Performing Operations, Printing, and Typewriters.
[B41J 11/32] Adjustment of pin wheels or traction elements, e.g. laterally -- The B41J 11/32 subclass in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers mechanisms that adjust pin wheels or other traction elements, such as lateral adjustments, to control the feeding or positioning of sheet or web material in typewriter-type printing devices. It falls under the broader group for devices or arrangements that support or handle copy material.
[B41J 11/34] Guides coacting with pin feeds -- B41J 11/34 covers guides that work together with pin-feed mechanisms in typewriters and similar printing machines to direct and align sheet or web material during feeding, ensuring proper positioning of the copy for accurate printing.
[B41J 11/36] Blanking or long feeds -- B41J 11/36 covers devices for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form within printing equipment, such as typewriters, and specifically relates to mechanisms that create blanks or feed long continuous strips of paper through the machine.
[B41J 11/38] Manually-operated feeding devices -- B41J 11/38 refers to manually-operated feeding devices for typewriters, encompassing mechanisms that support or handle sheet or web copy material during printing. The classification covers levers, rollers, trays or similar arrangements that position paper for typing without automatic power.
[B41J 1/14] the types or dies being movable relative to the bars or rods -- B41J 1/14 relates to typewriter mechanisms in which the type elements - such as types, dies, or typebars - are designed to move relative to the supporting bars or rods during operation. This subclass covers machines that achieve selective printing by shifting the printable characters rather than using a fixed arrangement.
[B41J 11/40] specially adapted for printing musical scores -- Devices or arrangements for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form that are specially adapted for printing musical scores, such as typewriters or printers equipped with mechanisms to produce staff lines, clefs and other musical notation alongside ordinary text. This subclass covers adaptations that enable accurate placement and alignment of musical symbols during the printing process.
[B41J 11/42] Controlling -- B41J 11/42 pertains to control mechanisms for handling sheet or web copy material in typewriter-type printing devices, encompassing electronic, hydraulic or mechanical systems that regulate feeding, positioning and transport of the media.
[B41J 11/425] for a variable printing material feed amount -- This subclass covers apparatuses and methods that vary the amount of sheet or web material fed to a printing device, enabling precise control of feed rate or length in response to printing conditions. It includes variable feed rollers, sensors and control systems that adjust the feed amount.
[B41J 11/44] by devices, e.g. program tape or contact wheel, moved in correspondence with movement of paper-feeding devices, e.g. platen rotation -- B41J 11/44 covers mechanisms that move control elements such as program tapes or contact wheels in direct correspondence with the motion of paper-feeding components, for example matching the rotation of a platen.
