[B41J 11/46] by marks or formations on the paper being fed -- B41J 11/46 pertains to typewriter or printing mechanisms that handle sheet or web material by sensing or responding to marks, patterns, or other formations on the paper as it is fed, enabling functions such as registration, alignment, or selective feeding.
[B41J 11/48] Apparatus for condensed record, tally strip, or like work using two or more papers, or sets of papers -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supporting or feeding devices for loading rolled-up continuous copy material into printers, e.g. for replacing a used-up paper roll; Point-of-sale printers with openable casings allowing access to the rolled-up continuous copy material -> B41J15/042
[B41J 11/485] Means for selecting a type of copy material amongst different types of copy material in the printing apparatus -- A mechanism incorporated in a printing apparatus that automatically identifies the type of copy material-such as paper, cardstock or film-present in the feed and selects the appropriate handling or printing parameters for that material. This enables rapid switching between different media without manual adjustment.
[B41J 11/50] in which two or more papers or sets are separately fed in the same direction towards the printing position -- This subclass covers machines that feed two or more separate sheets or sets of paper simultaneously, each moving in the same direction toward the printing zone. It relates to typewriter-type printing devices that handle multiple forms in parallel.
[B41J 11/51] with different feed rates -- B41J 11/51 covers typewriter or printing mechanisms that feed sheet or web material at varying rates, enabling different portions of the copy to move faster or slower for functions such as alignment, multi-format handling, or selective printing.
[B41J 11/52] in which one paper or set is moved transversely relative to another -- B41J 11/52 covers mechanisms that move one sheet or a set of sheets sideways relative to another during handling, such as the transverse paper-shift action in typewriters or similar printing devices that reposition copy material across a supporting arrangement. This subclass falls under the broader category of devices for supporting or handling sheet or web material in printing operations.
[B41J 11/53] Devices for holding in place one paper or set during replacement of one or more of the auxiliary papers or sets -- This classification covers mechanisms used in typewriters to secure a single sheet or a group of sheets while an auxiliary paper such as a carbon copy or ribbon is being swapped. It includes clamps, holders or guides that keep the paper stationary during the replacement process.
[B41J 11/54] in which one paper or set is fed towards printing position from the front of the apparatus -- B41J 11/54 covers typewriter or printing mechanisms where a single sheet or a stack of paper is introduced to the printing zone from the front side of the machine. It includes arrangements that guide, support or feed the paper forward into the print position, typically using rollers, guides or feed belts.
[B41J 11/55] with means for adjusting a paper or set -- B41J 11/55 covers typewriter or printing mechanisms that include devices for supporting or handling sheet or web copy material, specifically those equipped with adjustments for positioning or setting the paper. This includes adjustable paper guides, clamps, or rollers that allow the operator to fine-tune paper alignment during operation. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 11/56] specially constructed to facilitate storage or transport of typewriter
[B41J 11/58] Supply holders for sheets or fan-folded webs, e.g. shelves, tables, scrolls, pile holders -- The class B41J 11/58 covers devices that hold or support individual sheets or fan-folded webs of material for typewriters and similar printing equipment, such as shelves, tables, scrolls and pile holders.
[B41J 1/16] with types or dies arranged in stationary or sliding cases or frames or upon flexible strips, plates, bars or rods -- B41J 1/16 pertains to typewriters and selective printing mechanisms where the types or dies are fixed in stationary or sliding cases, frames, or mounted on flexible strips, plates, bars, or rods. This classification covers the specific arrangement and mounting methods of the printing elements.
[B41J 11/60] Erasing or correcting tables -- B41J 11/60 covers devices or arrangements used with typewriters that enable the erasing or correction of printed tables on sheets or web material, allowing users to modify tabular information without re-printing the entire document.
[B41J 11/62] Shields or masks -- B41J 11/62 refers to shields or masks used in typewriter printing, designed to protect portions of the sheet or to define the printing area, and are typically mounted on the carriage or platen as part of devices that support or handle copy material in sheet form.
[B41J 11/64] Applications of scales or indicators -- B41J 11/64 covers the use of weighing devices, scales or indicator mechanisms incorporated into equipment that supports or handles sheet or web material in printing and typewriter operations, enabling measurement-based control, monitoring or feedback of the material during processing.
[B41J 11/66] Applications of cutting devices -- B41J 11/66 covers the use of cutting devices in typewriter or similar sheet-handling equipment, such as mechanisms that trim, separate or perforate printed paper as part of the printing process. It includes arrangements where cutters are integrated into the feed or output path to produce finished sheets of desired size or shape.
[B41J 11/663] Controlling cutting, cutting resulting in special shapes of the cutting line, e.g. controlling cutting positions, e.g. for cutting in the immediate vicinity of a printed image -- The CPC entry B41J 11/663 covers devices or arrangements that control the cutting of sheet or web material in printing systems, especially methods that produce specially shaped cut lines or precisely positioned cuts close to a printed image. It relates to typewriter-style printers and handling of copy material.
[B41J 11/666] Cutting partly, e.g. cutting only the uppermost layer of a multiple-layer printing material -- B41J 11/666 pertains to devices or methods that partially cut a printed sheet, for example removing only the uppermost layer of a multi-layer printing material, within the broader context of handling copy material in printing and typewriter applications.
[B41J 11/68] cutting parallel to the direction of paper feed -- B41J 11/68 covers mechanisms that cut paper or other sheet material in a direction parallel to the feed path, typically used in typewriter or similar printing equipment to separate or trim sheets while they are conveyed.
[B41J 11/70] cutting perpendicular to the direction of paper feed -- B41J 11/70 covers devices that cut paper or web material at a right angle to the direction of feed, such as the perpendicular cutting knives used in typewriter and printer mechanisms. It applies to arrangements that sever sheets or webs while they are moving through the machine, enabling precise sheet separation in copying and printing operations.
[B41J 11/703] Cutting of tape -- B41J 11/703 covers mechanisms for cutting tape used in typewriter or similar printing devices, including tools that slice continuous or pre-cut tape into desired lengths for handling or feeding. It falls under devices for supporting or handling copy material in sheet or web form within the printing and typewriter subclasses.
[B41J 11/706] using a cutting tool mounted on a reciprocating carrier -- The subclass B41J 11/706 covers mechanisms that mount a cutting tool on a reciprocating carrier to cut sheet or web material during handling or processing. It includes devices that move the cutter back and forth to trim, slit or shape copy material in printing and related equipment.
[B41J 1/18] with types or dies strung on wires or rods -- This subclass includes typewriters or printing devices that form characters by arranging individual types or dies on wires or rods, which are strung together to create a selectable line of characters. The mechanism uses these suspended elements rather than a rotating drum or fixed plate for printing.
[B41J 1/20] with types or dies mounted on endless bands or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Using type-belts.  Control circuits therefor.
[B41J 1/22] with types or dies mounted on carriers rotatable for selection -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Drums and wheels in general
[B41J 1/24] the plane of the type or die face being perpendicular to the axis of rotation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Daisy wheel printers and general aspects of the machine  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - With types or dies on spherical, truncated-spherical, or like surfaces -> B41J1/60
[B41J 1/243] Mounting or fixing the carriers -- The B41J 1/243 subclass covers the mechanisms and structures used to mount or fix carriers in typewriters, such as brackets, clamps, and attachment devices that hold ink ribbons or paper carriers in place during operation. It pertains to the design of mounting systems within various types of typewriters and selective printing mechanisms.
[B41J 1/246] Cartridges for the carriers -- B41J 1/246 covers cartridges that are integral parts of typewriter carriers, providing a removable container for the ink-ribbon or other printing medium. These cartridges are designed to be quickly inserted and replaced, ensuring continuous typewriter operation and simplifying maintenance of the selective printing mechanism.
[B41J 1/26] Carriers moving for impression -- This subclass deals with typewriters or selective printing devices in which the carrier-such as a platen, ribbon or other printing medium-is moved to generate each impression. It focuses on the mechanisms that drive the carrier's motion during the impression step, rather than the movement of the printing element itself.
[B41J 1/27] Carriers moving during impression -- B41J 1/27 pertains to typewriter or selective printing devices in which the carrier - such as paper or a printing band - moves relative to the printing element during the formation of an impression. The classification includes mechanisms where the carrier's motion occurs simultaneously with the striking or printing action, enabling continuous or stepped feed during typing.
[B41J 1/28] Carriers stationary for impression, e.g. with the types or dies not moving relative to the carriers -- B41J 1/28 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers typewriters and similar devices where the carrier remains stationary during the printing process, such as systems using fixed types or dies that do not move relative to the carrier.
[B41J 1/30] with the types or dies moving relative to the carriers or mounted on flexible carriers -- This subgroup covers typewriters or selective printing devices in which the type or die elements move relative to the carrier, such as mechanisms where the characters are mounted on a flexible carrier or belt that travels independently of the paper carriage. It includes designs where the printing elements are not fixed to the carrier but shift during operation.
[B41J 13/00] Devices or arrangements specially adapted for supporting or handling copy material in short lengths, e.g. sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In particular selective printing mechanisms comprising devices for the control of the conveyance of sheets, paper sheet guides, rotatable drums for holding sheets by suction or by clamps.
[B41J 13/0009] control of the transport of the copy material -- The code B41J 13/0009 addresses features that manage the feeding and advancing of paper sheets in typewriter-type printing machines. It includes devices such as rollers, clamps, sensors and control circuits that ensure precise positioning and timing of the copy material during operation. These mechanisms are designed to prevent jams and maintain consistent print quality.
[B41J 13/0018] in the sheet input section of automatic paper handling systems -- The code B41J 13/0018 denotes features located in the sheet-input area of automatic paper-handling equipment, such as printers or typewriters, that are specially designed to receive, guide or otherwise manage individual sheets or short lengths of copy material during feeding.
[B41J 13/0027] in the printing section of automatic paper handling systems -- This subclass relates to mechanisms that form part of the printing section of automatic paper handling systems, designed to support, guide, or feed short lengths of paper such as sheets during the printing process of typewriter-like equipment. It includes devices such as sheet feeders, separators, and transport rollers that operate within the printer's feed path.
[B41J 13/0036] in the output section of automatic paper handling systems -- This subclass covers mechanisms located in the output section of automatic paper-handling systems for typewriters, such as conveyors, turners, stackers or guides that receive, transport, align or stack printed sheets after they leave the print zone. It pertains to devices specially adapted for handling short-length copy material in that downstream part of the machine.

[B41J 13/0054] Handling sheets of differing lengths -- This CPC subclass covers devices or arrangements designed to support or handle individual sheets that vary in length, such as in typewriter or printing operations. It includes mechanisms that accommodate short and longer sheets within the same feeding or processing system.
[B41J 13/0063] Handling thick cut sheets, e.g. greeting cards or postcards, larger than credit cards, e.g. using means for enabling or facilitating the conveyance of thick sheets  B41J11/20 ,  B41J13/12  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Platen adjustments for varying the strength of impression, for a varying number of papers, for wear or for alignment -> B41J11/20 - Devices specially adapted for small cards, envelopes, or the like, e.g. credit cards, business cards -> B41J13/12
[B41J 13/0072] Handling wide cut sheets, e.g. using means for enabling or facilitating the conveyance of wide sheets -- B41J 13/0072 covers devices or arrangements for handling wide cut sheets, such as mechanisms that enable or facilitate the conveyance of wide sheets in typewriter printing equipment. It sits within the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy under performing operations, printing, typewriters, focusing on support of short-length copy material.
[B41J 13/0081] Sheet-storing packages, e.g. for protecting the sheets against ambient influences, e.g. light, humidity, changes in temperature -- The CPC subclass B41J 13/0081 relates to sheet-storing packages designed to protect individual sheets used with typewriters or other short-length copy-handling devices from ambient influences such as light, humidity and temperature changes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, typewriters, and devices for supporting or handling copy material in short lengths.
[B41J 13/009] Diverting sheets at a section where at least two sheet conveying paths converge, e.g. by a movable switching guide that blocks access to one conveying path and guides the sheet to another path, e.g. when a sheet conveying direction is reversed after printing on the front of the sheet has been finished and the sheet is guided to a sheet turning path for printing on the back -- This classification covers mechanisms that redirect a sheet at a point where at least two conveying paths converge, using a movable guide that blocks one path and steers the sheet onto another, such as when a sheet is turned to print on its back after the front side is finished.
[B41J 13/02] Rollers -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roller platens -> B41J11/04
[B41J 13/025] Special roller holding or lifting means, e.g. for temporarily raising one roller of a pair of nipping rollers for inserting printing material -- A special roller holding or lifting mechanism that temporarily raises one roller of a nipping-roller pair to allow insertion of printing material such as sheets, used in typewriter-type printing devices for handling short lengths of copy.
[B41J 13/03] driven, e.g. feed rollers separate from platen -- B41J 13/03 covers typewriter feed mechanisms in which the rollers that advance short-length copy material are powered separately from the platen. The classification applies to devices designed to handle sheets by a driven feed system distinct from the printing surface, enabling independent sheet movement during operation.
[B41J 13/036] co-operating with a roller platen -- This subclass covers typewriter mechanisms in which a roller platen works together with other components to guide, support, or advance short sheets of copy material during printing. It includes arrangements that combine the platen's rotating surface with feeding rollers or guide structures to ensure smooth sheet handling.
[B41J 13/042] Front and rear rollers or sets of front or rear rollers each mounted on a separate carrier -- A B41J 13/042 arrangement provides one or more front rollers and one or more rear rollers, each group mounted on its own carrier, to guide and feed short lengths of copy material such as individual sheets within typewriter or similar printing devices. This separate-carrier design improves handling precision and allows independent adjustment of the feeding rollers.
[B41J 13/048] Front and rear rollers both mounted on a common carrier -- A front and rear roller pair that are mounted together on a single carrier, allowing the two rollers to move as one unit when feeding short-length copy material such as sheets through a typewriter or similar printing device. This arrangement supports and transports the material in a coordinated manner.
[B41J 13/054] on the paper apron concentric with the roller platen -- A B41J 13/054 device is a paper-handling arrangement for typewriters that uses a paper apron positioned concentrically around the roller platen, enabling the support and feeding of short-length sheets during printing operations.
[B41J 13/076] Construction of rollers -- B41J 13/076 covers the design and construction of rollers employed in typewriter mechanisms for moving or feeding short-length sheets of paper. It relates to devices that support or handle copy material, focusing on roller geometry, materials, and bearing arrangements that ensure smooth sheet transport.
[B41J 13/08] bands or like feeding devices -- B41J 13/08 covers devices such as bands or similar mechanisms that feed or support short-length copy material, like sheets, in typewriters. These arrangements are designed to handle the material during printing operations.
[B41J 13/10] Sheet holders, retainers , or stationary guides -- Sheet holders, retainers or stationary guides are devices used in typewriter-based printing equipment to support and position short lengths of copy material such as individual sheets, ensuring they are correctly aligned and fed during the printing process.
[B41J 13/103] for the sheet feeding section -- This subclass covers mechanisms that feed individual sheets of paper or other copy material into a printer or typewriter. It includes devices that separate, lift and deliver single sheets from a stack to the printing zone, such as feeders, rollers, pick-up levers and associated control elements. It falls under the B41J series for sheet-handling arrangements.
[B41J 13/106] for the sheet output section -- B41J 13/106 covers devices or arrangements that support, guide, or handle individual sheets as they leave a typewriter, such as rollers, guides, clamps and trays that align, transport and collect printed paper. The subclass pertains to the sheet-output section of typewriters within the broader printing field.
[B41J 13/12] specially adapted for cards, envelopes, or the like -- **Glossary:** - "visting cards" means: "business cards"
[B41J 13/14] Aprons or guides -- B41J 13/14 covers aprons or guides employed in typewriter mechanisms to steady, position or feed individual sheets of copy material during printing operations. These components are designed to support short-length sheets and ensure accurate alignment as the paper moves through the device.
[B41J 13/16] movable for insertion or release of sheets -- B41J 13/16 pertains to typewriter mechanisms that move to insert or release individual sheets of paper. It covers devices or arrangements specially adapted for handling short lengths of copy material by shifting, ejecting or feeding a sheet into or out of the printing area.
[B41J 13/18] concentric with roller platen -- This CPC subclass covers typewriter mechanisms in which the platen is formed by a roller positioned concentrically with another roller, allowing continuous handling of short sheets or copy material. It pertains to devices that support or transport paper in a circular path around a shared axis.
[B41J 1/32] the plane of the type or die face being parallel to the axis of rotation, e.g. with type on the periphery of cylindrical carriers -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - With types or dies on spherical, truncated-spherical, or like surfaces -> B41J1/60
[B41J 13/20] Bails -- Bails are components in typewriter printing devices that hold, guide or support individual sheets or short lengths of paper, ensuring correct alignment for striking and printing. They form part of the mechanisms specially adapted for handling copy material in brief formats.
[B41J 13/22] Clamps or grippers -- B41J 13/22 covers clamps and grippers designed to hold or manipulate short lengths of copy material such as individual sheets in typewriter or printing operations. These devices are used to secure the paper during feeding, positioning or removal, ensuring precise handling in typewriter mechanisms.
[B41J 13/223] on rotatable drums -- B41J 13/223 covers devices or arrangements that support or handle short lengths of copy material, such as sheets, on rotatable drums within typewriter-related printing apparatuses. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters and specifies mechanisms that feed, hold, or transport paper using a drum that rotates during operation.
[B41J 13/226] using suction -- The B41J 13/226 subgroup covers typewriter-style printing devices that employ suction to hold or feed short sheets of copy material, such as vacuum rollers, suction tables or other suction-based arrangements designed to support the paper during operation.
[B41J 13/24] Strips for supporting or holding papers -- B41J 13/24 covers thin strips or bands designed to support, guide or hold individual sheets of paper or similar copy material during typewriter operations, keeping the sheets aligned and preventing them from shifting or fluttering as they pass through the machine. These devices are typically made of flexible materials such as plastic, rubber or fabric and are positioned to engage short lengths of paper for smoother feeding and printing.
[B41J 13/26] Registering devices -- B41J 13/26 relates to registering devices used with typewriters, i.e., mechanisms that count, record or otherwise capture the characters printed on short sheets of paper. Such devices may include keystroke counters, impression registers or electronic loggers that monitor typing activity for verification, accounting or data-storage purposes.
[B41J 13/28] Front lays, stops, or gauges -- B41J 13/28 relates to typewriter mechanisms that control the positioning of the paper at the front of the machine, including front lays, stops and gauges used to align, limit or measure the leading edge of a sheet. These components ensure accurate paper feeding and registration during printing.
[B41J 13/30] Side lays or gauges -- B41J 13/30 covers side lays or gauges used in typewriter-type printing devices to position, align or separate short sheets of copy material, ensuring accurate feeding and spacing during printing operations. These components are specially adapted to support or handle individual sheets as they move through the machine.
[B41J 13/32] Means for positioning sheets in two directions under one control, e.g. for format control or orthogonal sheet positioning -- The B41J 13/32 subclass covers devices that simultaneously control sheet movement in two perpendicular directions, enabling precise format adjustments or orthogonal positioning of individual sheets during printing or typing operations. These mechanisms are designed to handle short lengths of copy material such as single sheets.
[B41J 1/34] Carriers rotating during impression -- The B41J 1/34 classification covers typewriter mechanisms in which the carrier-a paper feed or other printing element-rotates while receiving an impression. It relates to designs where the paper or printing substrate is turned or rolled during each keystroke, enabling continuous or selective printing without linear advancement. This subcategory falls under typewriters with rotating carriers as part of the impression process.
[B41J 1/36] Carriers sliding for impression, e.g. manually operated -- B41J 1/36 refers to typewriter designs in which the carrier that holds the paper or type slides to make each impression, often by manual operation rather than a rotating carriage or automatic feed. The classification covers mechanisms for sliding carriers that position the printing element for selective character printing.
[B41J 1/38] power operated -- It covers typewriters and related printing devices that are powered mechanically or electrically rather than manually, including machines where the striking force is supplied by a motor, solenoid, pneumatic or hydraulic system. The subclass designates power-operated typewriter mechanisms within the printing hierarchy.
[B41J 1/40] Carriers swinging for impression -- B41J 1/40 covers typewriter mechanisms in which the carrier swings to create each impression, a specific form of selective printing. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, and details the motion of the carriage that advances and returns for each character printed.
[B41J 1/42] about an axis parallel to the axis of rotation of the carrier -- B41J 1/42 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate an additional axis arranged parallel to the axis of rotation of the carrier, influencing the motion or positioning of printing elements. This configuration is used to steer or synchronize the carriage or print head relative to the rotating carrier.
[B41J 1/44] Carriers stationary for impression -- The class refers to typewriter designs in which the character carrier stays fixed while the impression is made, usually by a moving hammer or striking element that contacts the paper. It includes arrangements where the paper or the striking element moves rather than the carrier.
[B41J 1/46] Types or dies fixed on wheel, drum, cylinder, or like carriers -- The B41J 1/46 subclass covers typewriters that use types or printing dies mounted on a rotating carrier such as a wheel, drum, cylinder, or similar element. It includes designs where the printable characters are fixed to these moving carriers for selective imprinting.
[B41J 1/48] with a plurality of carriers, one for each character space -- B41J 1/48 covers typewriters that employ several carriers, each dedicated to a single character position, allowing simultaneous or sequential printing across multiple character spaces. The arrangement enables the machine to produce a line of text by moving each carrier independently, rather than using a single moving carriage.
[B41J 1/50] with one or more carriers travelling across copy material in letter-space direction -- This CPC subclass covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms in which one or more carriers, such as a printing head or ribbon, travel across the copy material in the letter-space direction, moving parallel to the line of text. The carrier motion enables sequential character printing as it advances across the paper.
[B41J 15/00] Devices or arrangements specially adapted for supporting or handling copy material in continuous form, e.g. webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In particular selective printing mechanisms comprising devices for feeding web material and devices for tensioning web material
[B41J 15/005] Forming loops or sags in webs, e.g. for slackening a web or for compensating variations of the amount of conveyed web material (by arranging a "dancing roller" in a sag of the web material) -- This CPC subclass covers devices that create deliberate loops or sags in a continuous web of material to slacken it or to compensate for variations in web tension or feed amount, such as arranging a "dancing roller" in a sag of the web. It falls under printing-typewriter equipment for handling web-form copy material.
[B41J 15/02] Web rolls or spindles -- Web rolls or spindles are components used in printing and typewriter equipment to hold, unwind, or wind continuous-form copy material such as paper webs. They provide a stable, rotating support that enables smooth feeding and handling of the web during printing, copying, or related operations.
[B41J 15/04] Supporting, feeding, or guiding devices -- Devices or mechanisms designed to support, feed, or guide continuous-form copy material, such as paper webs, within typewriter-style printing equipment. They ensure smooth handling of the material to maintain precise positioning and tension during the printing operation.
[B41J 15/042] for loading rolled-up continuous copy material into printers, e.g. for replacing a used-up paper roll; Point-of-sale printers with openable casings allowing access to the rolled-up continuous copy material -- This subclass deals with devices that load rolled-up continuous copy material into printers, for example replacing a spent paper roll, and includes point-of-sale printers whose casings can be opened to give access to the roll.
[B41J 15/044] Cassettes or cartridges containing continuous copy material, tape, for setting into printing devices -- The B41J 15/044 subclass covers cassettes or cartridges that hold continuous copy material such as tape, designed to be loaded into printing devices like typewriters. These items are specialized for handling and feeding a web of media in the printing process.
[B41J 15/046] for the guidance of continuous copy material, e.g. for preventing skewed conveyance of the continuous copy material -- B41J 15/046 covers devices or arrangements that guide continuous copy material, such as webs, to keep it moving straight and avoid skew during printing operations on typewriters. The features may include rollers, guides, or other mechanisms that maintain alignment of the material as it is fed through the machine.
[B41J 15/048] Conveyor belts or like feeding devices  B41J11/007  takes precedence -- B41J 15/048 pertains to conveyor belts or similar feeding devices used in typewriters and printing equipment for handling continuous form copy material, such as webs. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO, and it specifies that B41J 11/007 takes precedence when applicable.
[B41J 15/06] characterised by being applied to printers having stationary carriages -- B41J 15/06 covers devices for supporting or handling continuous-form copy material in typewriter-style printers that use a stationary carriage. It includes mechanisms that feed, guide, tension or otherwise manage a web while the print head remains fixed, enabling printing on continuous paper without moving the carriage.
[B41J 15/08] characterised by being applied to printers having transversely- moving carriages -- B41J 15/08 covers devices or arrangements specifically adapted for supporting or handling continuous-form copy material, such as webs, in printers that use a transversely moving carriage. It falls within the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters, focusing on mechanisms that manage the web while the carriage moves side-to-side.
[B41J 15/10] and mounted on the carriage -- B41J 15/10 covers typewriter mechanisms in which the device for supporting or handling continuous-form copy material, such as a web, is mounted directly on the carriage. This arrangement allows the paper to be fed, positioned or trimmed while attached to the moving carriage during printing.
[B41J 15/12] and coupled to the carriage -- In the CPC, B41J 15/12 falls under printing-typewriter equipment and concerns devices or arrangements specially adapted for supporting or handling continuous-form copy material that are mounted on or coupled to the typewriter carriage, such as feed rollers, guides or web-handling units integrated with the moving carriage.
[B41J 15/14] and detached from the carriage -- This subclass covers mechanisms that handle a continuous web of copy material and allow it to be released or detached from the moving carriage of a printing or typewriter device, such as detachable feed or take-up arrangements.
[B41J 15/16] Means for tensioning or winding the web -- A means for tensioning or winding a continuous web is a device that applies controlled pull or wraps the web onto a drum or roll, keeping the material taut and properly positioned during printing or typewriter operations. This ensures consistent feed and alignment of the copy material.
[B41J 15/165] for tensioning continuous copy material by use of redirecting rollers or redirecting nonrevolving guides -- A device for tensioning continuous copy material that uses redirecting rollers or fixed, non-revolving guides to control the web's path and maintain proper tension during printing or typewriting operations. It is classified under devices adapted for handling copy material in continuous form.
[B41J 15/18] Multiple web-feeding apparatus -- Multiple web-feeding apparatus are devices designed to manage and guide continuous-form copy material, such as paper webs, into printing or typing equipment. They ensure precise alignment, tension control, and synchronized feeding of several webs simultaneously, enabling efficient handling of high-volume print jobs.
[B41J 1/52] with copy material moving in the letter-space direction, and the carrier mounting being fixed relative to the machine -- B41J 1/52 pertains to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms where the copy material moves in the letter-space direction while the carrier mounting remains fixed relative to the machine. This classification is part of a detailed system for categorizing inventions and patents, specifically focusing on the mechanical aspects of printing devices.
[B41J 15/20] for webs superimposed during printing -- B41J 15/20 covers devices or arrangements adapted to support or handle continuous-form copy material where multiple webs are superimposed during the printing process. It pertains to typewriter-related printing operations that feed, align, or manage overlapping webs to produce combined images or text.
[B41J 15/22] for feeding webs in separate paths during printing -- The subclass B41J 15/22 covers devices that feed continuous-form material along separate paths during printing, such as mechanisms used with typewriters. It includes arrangements that split a web into distinct feeding lanes to manage copy material more efficiently.
[B41J 15/24] with means for registering the webs with each other -- The CPC subclass B41J 15/24 concerns devices or arrangements specially adapted for supporting or handling copy material in continuous form, such as webs, that incorporate means for registering the webs with each other. It is located under the hierarchy PERFORMING OPERATIONS → PRINTING → TYPEWRITERS.
[B41J 1/54] Types or dies movable on wheel, drum, cylinder or like carriers -- The B41J 1/54 subclass covers typewriters and other selective printing mechanisms that use character-forming dies mounted on a rotating or moving carrier such as a wheel, drum, cylinder or similar element, allowing the dies to be positioned for imprinting. It includes designs of movable type dies and related arrangements for these rotating carriers.
[B41J 1/56] Types or dies on shuttles or like loose carriers -- B41J 1/56 covers the specific types of character-forming elements, such as type dies, that are mounted on shuttles or other detachable carriers in typewriters and similar selective printing devices. It relates to the design and arrangement of these loose carriers that move to strike the inked ribbon and paper.
[B41J 1/58] Types or dies upon arcuate bars -- This subclass covers typewriter or selective-printing mechanisms that employ types or printing dies mounted on curved (arcuate) bars. It includes designs where the characters are formed on such bars, which swing or rotate to strike the paper for rapid character selection.
[B41J 1/60] with types or dies on spherical, truncated-spherical, or like surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. of the golf-ball type
[B41J 17/00] Mechanisms for manipulating page-width impression-transfer material, e.g. carbon paper -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal transfer webs.  Ink donor films (IDF).  Page-wide ribbons.  IDF-identification means.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - In manifolding devices -> B41L
[B41J 17/02] Feeding mechanisms -- Feeding mechanisms are devices that advance page-width impression-transfer material, such as paper or carbon paper, through a typewriter during printing. They ensure continuous, controlled movement of the sheet to achieve consistent line spacing and accurate imprinting. This subcategory falls under printing and typewriter technologies in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B41J 17/04] Feed dependent on the record-paper feed, e.g. both moved at the same time -- B41J 17/04 covers typewriter mechanisms that synchronize the feed of impression-transfer material such as carbon paper with the feed of the record paper, moving both sheets together. The classification addresses devices that link the two feeds so that the carbon copy is positioned correctly as each line is printed, ensuring consistent copying without separate manual adjustments.
[B41J 17/06] "Creep" feed, i.e. impression-transfer material fed slower than the record paper -- B41J 17/06 relates to typewriter mechanisms that feed the impression-transfer sheet, such as carbon paper, at a slower speed than the record paper. This creep feed creates a relative offset between the two sheets, enabling special printing effects or alignment adjustments. The class is part of the CPC group for manipulating page-width impression-transfer material.
[B41J 17/07] electromagnetically controlled -- B41J 17/07 covers typewriter mechanisms that use electromagnetic actuation to control the movement or positioning of page-width impression-transfer material such as carbon paper. The code applies to devices where electric solenoids, motors or other electromagnetic components adjust the width, tension or selection of the transfer medium during printing.
[B41J 17/08] Feed independent of the record-paper feed -- B41J 17/08 pertains to typewriter mechanisms that supply a separate impression-transfer medium, such as carbon paper, without being linked to the main paper feed. It covers designs where the carbon or similar material is fed independently, allowing continuous or selective transfer of impressions across the page width.
[B41J 17/10] electromagnetically controlled -- B41J 17/10 covers typewriter mechanisms that use electromagnetic control to manipulate page-width impression-transfer material such as carbon paper. These devices adjust the material's position or pressure during printing to produce copies. The classification falls under performing operations, printing, typewriters.
[B41J 17/12] Special adaptations for ensuring maximum life -- B41J 17/12 in the Cooperative Patent Classification focuses on specific modifications designed to enhance the durability and longevity of mechanisms that handle page-width impression-transfer materials, such as carbon paper, in typewriters.
[B41J 17/14] Automatic arrangements for reversing the feed direction -- B41J 17/14 covers automatic mechanisms that reverse the direction of the feed in typewriter-based printers that handle page-width impression-transfer media such as carbon paper. The arrangements enable the machine to change the travel of the paper without manual intervention, facilitating bidirectional printing.
[B41J 17/16] Holders in the machine for sheets of impression transfer material -- The B41J 17/16 class covers devices that retain sheets of impression-transfer material, such as carbon paper, within a typewriter-like printing machine. These holders position and secure the sheets so they can be fed past the printing mechanism and receive the transferred characters. They are part of the broader category of mechanisms for manipulating page-width impression-transfer media.
[B41J 17/18] pivotable to and from the platen -- B41J 17/18 relates to typewriter mechanisms that allow the carriage or paper-feeding part to pivot toward and away from the platen. This feature facilitates positioning of page-width impression-transfer media such as carbon paper, enabling precise alignment for double-sided or offset printing.
[B41J 17/20] slidable to and from the platen -- This subclass covers typewriter mechanisms that allow the impression-transfer sheet, such as carbon paper, to be moved toward and away from the platen, enabling insertion, positioning, or removal of the sheet during operation.
[B41J 17/22] Supply arrangements for webs of impression-transfer material -- The class concerns devices and methods that feed continuous webs of impression-transfer material such as carbon paper to typewriters, including reels, rollers, guides and loading mechanisms that maintain proper tension, alignment and supply. It covers arrangements for delivering the material from a supply source to the printing point.
[B41J 17/24] Webs supplied from reels or spools attached to the machine -- B41J 17/24 covers typewriter mechanisms that feed impression-transfer material such as carbon paper as a continuous web from reels or spools mounted on the machine, enabling automatic supply of the material across the page during printing.
[B41J 17/26] Webs supplied from trays or like supports attached to the machines -- This subclass concerns typewriter mechanisms that feed a continuous strip of impression-transfer material such as carbon paper from a tray or similar attached support into the printing area, addressing the supply and handling of the web during operation.
[B41J 17/28] Arrangements of guides for the impression-transfer material -- This classification covers arrangements of guides that direct carbon paper or similar impression-transfer material in typewriter mechanisms, ensuring proper alignment and feeding across the page width. It resides in the CPC subclass for printing typewriters within the performing operations section.
[B41J 17/30] Constructions of guides for the impression-transfer material -- B41J 17/30 covers devices that guide impression-transfer material such as carbon paper during typewriter printing. The classification includes constructions that keep the transfer sheet aligned with the page width, ensuring consistent ink transfer across the line of text. It pertains to mechanisms that manipulate page-width impression-transfer media within the broader printing and typewriter categories.
[B41J 17/32] Detachable carriers or holders for impression-transfer material mechanism -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In general cassettes
[B41J 17/34] Backings for impression-transfer material, e.g. sheets for reducing friction, shields for preventing imprint -- This CPC subclass covers backings for impression-transfer material such as carbon paper, including sheets that reduce friction during printing and shields that prevent unintended imprinting on adjacent surfaces.
[B41J 17/36] Alarms, indicators, or feed-disabling devices responsible to material breakage or exhaustion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reading the IDF
[B41J 17/38] for dealing with the impression-transfer material after use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Peeling-off.  Deleting the information.
[B41J 17/40] for retracting sheets for re-use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Re-use of impression material by using all the remaining ink thereofl
[B41J 17/42] for webs -- B41J 17/42 covers devices that handle continuous strips of impression-transfer material, such as carbon paper, to control its width and movement across a page during printing. It applies to typewriter mechanisms that feed, align or advance such webs to produce uniform impressions.
[B41J 19/00] Character- or line-spacing mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanisms for relative movement in two directions between printing element and medium to be printed and parallel thereto:  **Glossary:** - Character and line spacing: in type-writers generally performed by means of the paper carriage while the print element head is stationary. Character spacing is in the direction of the paper width and line spacing is in the direction of the paper sheet length - Main- and sub-scanning: In modern serial dot printers the character (dot) spacing is generally done by the movement of the print head over the width of a paper sheet (main-scanning) and the line spacing is done by paper movement (sub-scanning). If a drum is continuously rotating during printing, then this movement is called main-scanning. The step-wise movement of the print head after one revolution of the drum is then called sub-scanning - Fast-scanning: see main scanning - Slow-scanning: see sub-scanning
[B41J 19/005] Cable or belt constructions for driving print, type or paper-carriages, e.g. attachment, tensioning means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanical constructions making the movement of carriages possible or easier.  Also including the electrical cables (guiding, attachment).
[B41J 19/02] with retarding devices, e.g. brakes -- This subclass covers mechanisms that control character or line spacing in typewriters and printers by using retardation devices such as brakes to slow or halt movement, ensuring precise placement of printed material. It is situated under Printing within the Performing Operations section.
[B41J 19/04] Sound-deadening or shock-absorbing devices or measures therein -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Adapted for silent return (type-writers) -> B41J19/38
[B41J 19/06] Resilient mounting of mechanism -- The B41J 19/06 subclass covers resilient mounting of the character- or line-spacing mechanism in typewriters, describing structures that attach the mechanism to the frame using springs, elastic elements or other shock-absorbing means to damp vibrations, maintain alignment and ensure smooth, consistent printing while reducing wear.
[B41J 19/08] Buffers, springs or like carriage stops -- The CPC code B41J 19/08 covers components that control the movement of a typewriter carriage, specifically buffers, springs or similar stops that regulate carriage travel during character or line spacing. These mechanisms are part of the printing operation hierarchy under typewriters.
[B41J 19/10] Dash-pots -- B41J 19/10 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to dash-pots used in character- or line-spacing mechanisms of typewriters, which are devices designed to control and dampen the movement for smoother operation.
[B41J 19/12] Gearing made of special material or specially constructed to reduce sound or shock -- B41J 19/12 relates to gearing employed in typewriter character or line-spacing mechanisms that is manufactured from special materials or designed in a way that minimizes noise or shock. It falls under the printing-typewriter subgroup of performing operations.
[B41J 19/14] with means for effecting line or character spacing in either direction -- B41J 19/14 covers typewriter mechanisms that provide means for adjusting line or character spacing in either direction, enabling the user to shift the print position forward or backward to achieve precise spacing of printed text. This subclass falls under printing operations within the broader category of performing operations.
[B41J 19/142] with a reciprocating print head printing in both directions across the paper width -- This CPC subclass covers typewriters with a reciprocating print head that prints in both directions across the paper width, enabling bidirectional printing while handling character- or line-spacing mechanisms.
[B41J 19/145] Dot misalignment correction -- B41J 19/145 covers devices that correct misaligned printed dots in typewriter output, typically by adjusting the impact point or timing to ensure uniform spacing. The classification falls under character- or line-spacing mechanisms for typewriters within the printing operations of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B41J 19/147] Colour shift prevention -- The CPC subclass B41J 19/147 covers mechanisms in typewriters that keep character or line spacing consistent to avoid colour shift during printing. It pertains to devices that control spacing to maintain uniform ink or toner application across the page.
[B41J 19/16] Special spacing mechanisms for circular, spiral, or diagonal-printing apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Serial print head above a rotating drum (spiral printing with main-scanning in rotating direction and sub-scanning in the width direction);  A continuously rotating drum with a print head carriage movement after each drum rotation (circular dot bands).
[B41J 19/18] Character-spacing or back-spacing mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only type-writers.  The carriage of a typewriter is for carrying paper.

[B41J 19/207] Encoding along a bar -- B41J 19/207 covers mechanisms that encode information on a bar to control typewriter operation, such as mechanical patterns or notches that represent characters or spacing settings. The bar is read by a selector to determine which character or line-spacing action is performed, enabling automatic typing or spacing without manual key selection. This sub-class falls under typewriter character- and line-spacing mechanisms within the broader printing and performing-operations hierarchy.
[B41J 19/22] acting by friction or gripping effect -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 19/24] Pawl and ratchet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 19/26] moving a paper or like carriage -- B41J 19/26 covers the mechanisms that move the paper or a similar carriage in typewriters during operation. It includes devices that advance the paper after each character, line or other printing action, facilitating continuous typing.
[B41J 19/28] moving a paper or like web or strip, e.g. over a stationary support -- B41J 19/28 covers mechanisms that move a sheet of paper, web or strip across a fixed support to achieve prescribed spacing of characters or lines in typewriter and printing devices. It relates to the character- or line-spacing functions within the broader printing and typewriter categories of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B41J 19/30] Electromagnetically-operated mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.  Linear motors therefor.
