[B41J 2/1735] with ink supply tank in common containers -- This CPC subclass covers typewriters or selective printing devices that use a common ink-supply tank to feed ink to the printing mechanism. [B41J 2/1742] Open waste ink collectors, e.g. ink receiving from a print head above the collector during borderless printing -- B41J 2/1742 covers open waste-ink collection systems that capture excess ink discharged from a print head, especially in borderless printing where ink may flow beyond the intended area. [B41J 2/17509] Whilst mounted in the printer -- The code B41J 2/17509 refers to typewriter or selective-printing mechanisms in which the printing element remains mounted in the printer during operation, rather than being removable or interchangeable. [B41J 2/17513] Inner structure -- B41J 2/17513 in the Cooperative Patent Classification for the EPO and USPTO pertains to the inner structure of typewriters or selective printing mechanisms, addressing the design and arrangement of core components such as key levers, carriage, and printing-element housings.
[B41J 2/1752] Mounting within the printer -- The B41J 2/1752 subclass deals with the way printing elements are mounted inside a typewriter-type printer, including the attachment of print-heads, ink ribbons, or other marking mechanisms to the printer chassis or carriage. It covers mounting structures, brackets, and positioning methods that secure these components for reliable operation.
[B41J 2/17523] Ink connection -- Ink connection refers to the arrangement that supplies ink from its source to the printing element in typewriters or other selective printing devices, covering ducts, capillary feeds, reservoirs and related components that ensure continuous ink delivery during operation.
[B41J 2/17526] Electrical contacts to the cartridge -- This subclass covers the electrical interface between a typewriter or similar selective printing device and its replaceable ink cartridge, including contact pins, conductive pads and connection arrangements that transmit power or signals to the cartridge. It addresses designs that provide reliable coupling for heating elements or electronic ink-delivery systems within the printing mechanism.
[B41J 2/1753] Details of contacts on the cartridge, e.g. protection of contacts -- The entry covers features that protect or manage the electrical contacts on typewriter or selective printing cartridges, such as shielding, insulation, contact geometry or other measures that ensure reliable connection between the cartridge and the machine. It relates to the design of contact details that prevent damage, contamination or short circuits during the printing process.
[B41J 2/17533] Storage or packaging of ink cartridges -- The entry concerns ways of storing and packaging ink cartridges used in typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms. It includes designs for containers, protective housings and transport systems that preserve cartridge integrity, allow refilling, and prevent leakage or drying of the ink.
[B41J 2/17536] Protection of cartridges or parts thereof, e.g. tape -- The CPC subclass B41J 2/17536 addresses protective measures for typewriter consumables and components, such as cartridges, ribbons or tape, focusing on designs that shield these parts from damage, tampering or premature wear during the printing process.
[B41J 2/1754] with means attached to the cartridge, e.g. protective cap -- B41J 2/1754 pertains to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate a device attached to the cartridge, such as a protective cap, to shield the cartridge when it is not in use.
[B41J 2/17543] Cartridge presence detection or type identification -- The CPC subclass B41J 2/17543 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate features to detect whether a cartridge is present or to identify the cartridge type. It relates to the printing process of such devices, enabling automatic recognition of ink or ribbon cartridges for operational control.
[B41J 2/17546] electronically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Also other data transfer as remaining liquid information
[B41J 2/1755] mechanically -- This subclass relates to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms in which the printing or marking process is carried out mechanically, using mechanical actuation such as lever-driven typebars or gear-driven print heads. It excludes devices that rely primarily on electronic or electro-optical means for marking.
[B41J 2/17553] Outer structure -- The B41J 2/17553 entry concerns the outer structure of typewriters and similar selective printing mechanisms, covering features such as the housing, frame, casing and protective covers that enclose the printing or marking components.
[B41J 2/17556] Means for regulating the pressure in the cartridge -- A means for regulating the pressure inside the ink or type-cartridge of a typewriter or other selective printing device, ensuring consistent ink flow and preventing over- or under-pressurisation during operation. [B41J 2/17559] Cartridge manufacturing -- The B41J 2/17559 class relates to the manufacture of cartridges used in typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms. [B41J 2/17563] Ink filters -- B41J 2/17563 designates ink-filter components used in typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms to clean or condition the ink before it reaches the print head, falling under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, printing and typewriters.

It covers the feeding, storage and delivery arrangements for solid-state ink within these devices.
[B41J 2/17596] Ink pumps, ink valves -- B41J 2/17596 covers ink pumps and ink valves used in typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms, focusing on the components that control the flow of ink to the print head for the intended marking process. This sub-class falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 2/18] Ink recirculation systems -- Ink recirculation systems for typewriters are mechanisms that collect used ink from the printing head and return it to the ink reservoir, maintaining a consistent supply without manual refilling. These systems are designed to improve efficiency and reduce ink waste in selective printing devices.
[B41J 2/185] Ink-collectors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ink-catchers for ink jet printers of the continuous type.  Collecting ink after ejection from printers of the DOD-type.  **Glossary:** - DOD: drop on demand (opposite to ink jet of the continuous type)
[B41J 2/19] for removing air bubbles -- B41J 2/19 is a sub-class within the Cooperative Patent Classification for typewriters and selective printing mechanisms, specifically covering features that remove air bubbles from the ink or printing medium during the printing or marking process. This helps ensure a clean, uninterrupted print by eliminating trapped air that could cause gaps or blemishes.
[B41J 2/195] for monitoring ink quality -- The subclass B41J 2/195 refers to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate means for monitoring ink quality, such as sensors, visual indicators or other devices that detect fading, low density, contamination or other defects in the ink during operation. This feature is integrated into the printing process to ensure consistent mark quality.
[B41J 2/20] for preventing or detecting contamination of compounds -- This subclass concerns typewriters or selective printing devices equipped with features that prevent or detect contamination of the printing compounds such as ink, toner, or other media. It includes mechanisms for monitoring purity, filtering, or cleaning to ensure consistent print quality.
[B41J 2202/00] Embodiments of or processes related to ink-jet or thermal heads -- B41J 2202/00 pertains to devices and methods involving ink-jet or thermal print heads, including their structural designs, material compositions, and the processes used to produce or operate them. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, covering innovations such as nozzle configurations, heating elements, and feed mechanisms that enable precise droplet ejection or thermal transfer for image formation.
[B41J 2202/01] Embodiments of or processes related to ink-jet heads -- This subclass covers designs, manufacturing methods and related processes for ink-jet and thermal print heads used in printing devices such as typewriters. It includes structural features, material choices, actuation mechanisms and control techniques that enable droplet formation and deposition.
[B41J 2202/02] Air-assisted ejection -- B41J 2202/02 covers air-assisted ejection mechanisms used in ink-jet or thermal print heads, where a stream of air assists the discharge of ink droplets. This subclass falls under printing typewriters within the performing operations section.
[B41J 2202/03] Specific materials used -- This subclass concerns the particular materials employed in ink-jet or thermal print heads, including the substrates, conductive or resistive layers, piezoelectric elements, and specialized coatings that influence droplet formation, heating, durability and performance of the head. It focuses on the composition and properties of these components rather than the overall head design or printing method.
[B41J 2202/04] Heads using conductive ink -- The subclass B41J 2202/04 covers heads that employ conductive ink in printing devices such as typewriter-style ink-jet or thermal printers. It includes designs and processes for forming images or characters by using electrically conductive ink within the print head, typically to enable direct writing, pattern formation, or electronic signaling during operation.
[B41J 2202/05] Heads having a valve -- B41J 2202/05 covers printing heads equipped with a valve that controls fluid flow to the discharge zone. The class applies to ink-jet or thermal print heads used in typewriter-type devices, where the valve regulates ink or liquid delivery for precise droplet formation.
[B41J 2202/06] Heads merging droplets coming from the same nozzle -- This subclass covers print-head designs that merge multiple droplets emitted from a single nozzle before they reach the substrate, a technique used in ink-jet and thermal printing to control droplet size, velocity or placement and improve print quality. It falls under CPC B41J 2202/06 within the typewriter-printing hierarchy.
[B41J 2202/07] dealing with air bubbles -- The CPC subclass B41J 2202/07 covers technologies for handling air bubbles in ink-jet or thermal print heads. It includes methods and structures that detect, prevent, or eliminate bubbles to maintain fluid flow and printing quality.
[B41J 2202/08] dealing with thermal variations, e.g. cooling -- B41J 2202/08 covers technologies for managing temperature changes in ink-jet or thermal print heads, particularly methods and devices that provide cooling to limit thermal variation during operation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → printing → typewriters and relates to thermal-head embodiments that require temperature control for reliable printing.
[B41J 2202/09] Ink jet technology used for manufacturing optical filters -- B41J 2202/09 covers the use of ink-jet printing processes to produce optical filter components. It includes methods where ink-jet or thermal heads deposit functional materials that create the filter's spectral properties and the associated apparatus for such manufacturing.
[B41J 2202/10] Finger type piezoelectric elements -- B41J 2202/10 covers finger-type piezoelectric elements, which are thin flexural piezo actuators shaped like a finger and used to generate pressure pulses in ink-jet or thermal printing heads for typewriters and similar devices.
[B41J 2202/11] characterised by specific geometrical characteristics -- B41J 2202/11 covers inventions relating to ink-jet or thermal print heads whose design is defined by particular geometric features such as shape, size, or arrangement of components. The subclass falls under typewriter printing operations and includes both structural embodiments and manufacturing processes that exploit these geometric characteristics.
[B41J 2202/12] with ink circulating through the whole print head -- This CPC subclass concerns typewriter printing devices in which the ink-jet or thermal print head is supplied by a closed ink circuit that circulates ink through the whole head during operation. It covers arrangements where ink is pumped or drawn across the entire head to ensure uniform delivery and removal of waste.
[B41J 2202/13] Heads having an integrated circuit -- The CPC subclass B41J 2202/13 pertains to printing heads that incorporate an integrated circuit, typically used in ink-jet or thermal typewriter devices. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters and addresses embodiments of or processes related to ink-jet or thermal heads.
[B41J 2202/14] Mounting head into the printer -- Mounting head into the printer refers to methods and apparatus for installing an ink-jet or thermal print head within a printer, including positioning, securing, aligning and electrically connecting the head to the carriage or other print mechanism.
[B41J 2202/15] Moving nozzle or nozzle plate -- This subclass relates to designs that move a nozzle or nozzle plate in ink-jet or thermal printheads, enabling precise positioning of the discharge openings relative to the recording medium. It includes mechanisms that translate, pivot or otherwise shift the nozzle array during operation, and is applied to typewriter-style printers and similar printing devices.
[B41J 2202/16] Nozzle heaters -- Nozzle heaters are heating elements incorporated into ink-jet or thermal print heads to raise the temperature of the ink or molten material, thereby influencing its viscosity and facilitating droplet formation and ejection. By precisely controlling the heat applied to each nozzle, they improve print quality, speed, and reliability in printers and typewriters that use such heads.
[B41J 2202/17] Readable information on the head -- B41J 2202/17 covers print heads, especially ink-jet or thermal heads, that carry readable information on their surface, such as markings, codes, bar-codes or other identifiers that convey the head's type, specifications, or operating data. This information is intended to be visually or automatically read to aid setup, maintenance or quality control.
[B41J 2202/18] Electrical connection established using vias -- The code B41J 2202/18 defines methods for forming the electrical interface to ink-jet or thermal print heads by using conductive vias that pass through the substrate to connect drive circuitry with the heating elements or nozzles. It includes the design and fabrication of via-based interconnections in typewriter-style printing devices.
[B41J 2202/19] Assembling head units -- B41J 2202/19 covers methods and apparatus for assembling ink-jet or thermal print head units. It includes steps such as aligning, bonding, and connecting the components that form the functional head. The classification falls under the printing branch of performing operations in the CPC system.
[B41J 2202/20] Modules -- B41J 2202/20 pertains to modules that form part of or are used with ink-jet or thermal printheads, including sub-assemblies, replaceable units, and integrated functional blocks. The classification covers structural or functional combinations of components such as drive electronics, heating elements, nozzles or fluid channels that operate together as a discrete unit within a typewriter-style printing device. It is applied to inventions describing the design, arrangement or interconnection of these modules.
[B41J 2202/21] Line printing -- It covers technology for line printing using typewriter-like devices, especially those employing ink-jet or thermal print heads to produce rows of characters or graphics. It includes mechanisms and methods for moving the print head or paper to achieve line-by-line output.
[B41J 2202/22] Manufacturing print heads -- This class covers methods and apparatus for producing print heads used in ink-jet or thermal printers, including fabrication steps, component assembly, and material treatments. It includes processes such as etching, deposition, bonding, and forming of nozzle arrays and heating elements.
[B41J 2202/30] Embodiments of or processes related to thermal heads -- The CPC subclass B41J 2202/30 covers inventions that describe or process thermal print heads, including their design, construction, and operation. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters and addresses technologies specific to generating heat for ink-jet or thermal printing.
[B41J 2202/31] Thermal printer with head or platen movable -- B41J 2202/31 classifies thermal printers in which the print head or the platen is movable during operation, enabling variable positioning of the print line. This arrangement is used in typewriter-type devices and other printers that require adjustable print zones, often for portable or specialized applications.
[B41J 2202/32] Thermal head for perforating stencil -- The CPC class B41J 2202/32 covers thermal printheads specifically configured to perforate stencil media. Such heads use localized heating elements to create holes in a stencil, enabling subsequent ink transfer in typewriter style printing systems.
[B41J 2202/33] Thermal printer with pre-coating or post-coating ribbon system -- This CPC class B41J 2202/33 covers thermal printers that use a ribbon system in which the printed medium is pre-coating or post-coating. It applies to typewriter-like printing devices with ink-jet or thermal heads, focusing on the ribbon arrangement that adds a coating before or after printing.
[B41J 2202/34] Thermal printer with pre-coating or post-processing -- B41J 2202/34 classifies thermal printers that incorporate a coating step on the print medium either before the thermal imaging process or as a post-processing treatment, typically in typewriter-style devices that use ink-jet or thermal heads to improve print quality, durability or functionality.
[B41J 2202/35] Thermal printing on id card -- B41J 2202/35 pertains to the specific process of using thermal printing technology for creating images or text on identification cards, focusing on the application and methods employed in this specialized printing technique.
[B41J 2202/36] Thermal printing on disk-shaped medium -- B41J 2202/36 classifies methods and apparatus that create printed images on circular or disk-shaped media by using thermal printing techniques. It covers thermal heads, heating elements, drive and positioning systems, and control schemes for depositing heat-activated inks or pigments onto rotating disks or similar round substrates.
[B41J 2202/37] Writing and erasing thermal head -- The B41J 2202/37 entry concerns thermal heads used in typewriter-style printers that can both create and remove marks. It covers devices where heating elements produce visible output and later erase it, often by reversing the thermal effect or using thermochromic media. This classification falls under performing operations-printing-typewriters.
[B41J 2202/38] Test pattern thermal printing -- The B41J 2202/38 entry covers the creation and use of test patterns in thermal printing, detailing methods for generating reference images that verify printer performance, calibrate print head operation, and assess print quality. It encompasses devices or procedures that produce standardized patterns for diagnostic or quality-control purposes.
[B41J 2202/50] Embodiments of processes related to optical heads -- This subclass covers methods for operating or manufacturing optical heads that generate images in printing devices such as ink-jet or thermal printers, including head actuation, droplet formation control, and light-based alignment. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 2203/00] Embodiments of or processes related to the control of the printing process -- B41J 2203/00 covers inventions that implement or manage the control of the printing operation in typewriters, including hardware or software mechanisms that regulate ink flow, impact timing, or printhead behavior. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations - Printing - Typewriters.
[B41J 2203/01] Inspecting a printed medium or a medium to be printed using a sensing device -- B41J 2203/01 covers methods and apparatus for inspecting a printed sheet or a sheet awaiting print by means of a sensing device, such as optical, infrared or tactile sensors, to detect defects, alignment or colour deviations. It is classified under Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters and pertains to the control of the printing process.
[B41J 2203/011] Inspecting the shape or condition, e.g. wrinkled or warped, of a medium to be printed before printing on it -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus for examining the physical condition of a print substrate - such as detecting wrinkles, warping, or other deformations - prior to the printing operation. It relates to the control of the printing process by pre-print inspection of the medium.
[B41J 2/205] for printing a discrete number of tones -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Monochrome gray levels.  Liquids of same hue but with different intensity.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For multi-colour printing -> B41J2/21 - Halftoning by varying the number of within a pixel (dithering) for multi-colour printers -> B41J2/2132
[B41J 2/2052] by dot superpositioning, e.g. multipass doubling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  True half toning by means of superpositioning of droplets of the same colour.
[B41J 2/2054] by the variation of dot disposition or characteristics, e.g. dot number density, dot shape -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Halftoning by a selectable number of adjacent dots of the same colour within a pixel.
[B41J 2/2056] by ink density change -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ejecting liquids of same hue but with different intensity, e.g. light and dark magenta/cyan inks.
[B41J 2/21] for multi-colour printing -- B41J 2/21 covers typewriter-like or selective printing devices that are designed to apply multiple colours in a single operation or successive passes, enabling multi-colour printing on paper or other media. These mechanisms use separate ink sources or colour-changing elements to produce coloured output.
[B41J 2/2103] Features not dealing with the colouring process  per se , e.g. construction of printers or heads, driving circuit adaptations -- A sub-class of typewriters and selective printing mechanisms that concerns elements other than the colouring process itself, such as the construction of print heads, feed or carriage mechanisms, and adaptations of driving circuits. It addresses structural or electronic features that affect how printing is performed without involving ink or pigment application.
[B41J 2/2107] characterised by the ink properties  supplying ink in a solid state  B41J2/17593 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Covering dye-based inks vs pigmented inks.  Inks reactive which each other.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Using solid state inks -> B41J2/17593
[B41J 2/211] Mixing of inks, solvent or air prior to paper contact -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[B41J 2/2117] Ejecting white liquids -- The subclass B41J 2/2117 concerns typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that apply white liquids, such as ink, correction fluid or paint, by ejecting them onto the recording medium during the printing process. It includes devices that spray, jet or otherwise dispense the liquid as part of the marking operation.
[B41J 2/2121] characterised by dot size, e.g. combinations of printed dots of different diameter -- B41J 2/2121 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms whose output is defined by the size of the printed dots, including designs that combine dots of differing diameters to produce the desired marks. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters, focusing on the dot-size characteristics of the printing process.
[B41J 2/2125] by means of nozzle diameter selection -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[B41J 2/2128] by means of energy modulation  control methods or devices therefor, e.g. driver circuits or control circuits  B41J2/04501 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Half-toning or colouring by ejecting an ink droplet of selectable size from nozzles by using different drive power levels.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drive signal shaping, stabilising ejection rates e.g. by temperature measurements -> B41J2/04501
[B41J 2/2132] Print quality control characterised by dot disposition, e.g. for reducing white stripes or banding  methods for local corrections by dot omission, image edge enhancement, or multi-pass mask selection  G06K15/102 ; colour conversion  H04N1/40 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Multi-pass printing; Interlacing, the printed pixels printed in both fast- as slow-scan direction.  Dithering: several non-overlapping dots of different colour (C,M,Y,K) within a pixel to give the impression of one colour.  Preventing banding: white or dark stripes due to systematic dot positioning errors.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods for local corrections by dot omission, image edge enhancement, or multi-pass mask selection -> G06K15/102 - Colour conversion -> H04N1/40  **Glossary:** - Multi-pass: several movements of a print head in the main-scan direction, without a paper feed - Interlacing/interleaving: filling up the lines between the raster lines caused by the distance between two adjacent nozzles. Sometimes also used for filling up the empty pixels in a mask in the fast-scan direction
[B41J 2/2135] Alignment of dots -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Alignment by nozzle drive control adjustment.  Test patterns.
[B41J 2/2139] Compensation for malfunctioning nozzles creating dot place or dot size errors  generating single droplets or particles on demand by pressure, e.g. electromechanical transducers  B41J2/045 -- B41J 2/2139 deals with compensation for malfunctioning nozzles that cause dot placement or size errors in selective printing mechanisms such as typewriters. It addresses correction of single droplets or particles generated on demand by pressure-driven electromechanical transducers.
[B41J 2/2142] Detection of malfunctioning nozzles  generating single droplets or particles on demand by pressure, e.g. electromechanical transducers  B41J2/045 ,  B41J2/05 ; jet deflection sensors  B41J2/125 ; for cleaning purposes  B41J2/16579 -- This subclass covers techniques for detecting malfunctioning on-demand nozzles that produce single droplets or particles, using pressure-based monitoring, electromechanical transducers, jet-deflection sensors, or related cleaning methods.
[B41J 2/2146] for line print heads -- B41J 2/2146 pertains to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that employ line print heads-arrays of printing elements that create an entire line of characters in a single stroke. The code classifies devices whose printing or marking process is defined by this line-head architecture within the broader performing-operations → printing → typewriters hierarchy.
[B41J 2/215] by passing a medium, e.g. consisting of an air or particle stream, through an ink mist -- B41J 2/215 relates to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that form characters by directing a carrier medium-such as an air or particle stream-through an ink mist onto the recording surface. This approach uses a fluid jet to transfer ink particles, enabling marking without direct contact between the print head and the substrate.
[B41J 2/22] characterised by selective application of impact or pressure on a printing material or impression-transfer material -- This sub-group includes typewriters and similar printing devices that form characters by applying impact or pressure only at selected points on a printing material or an impression-transfer medium, such as striking a ribbon or embossing paper.
[B41J 2/225] ballistic, e.g. using solid balls or pellets -- The B41J 2/225 subclass covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that form characters by propelling solid balls or pellets onto the medium, using a ballistic impact process. It is a narrow sub-class of printing typewriters within the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B41J 2/23] using print wires -- This subclass relates to typewriters or selective printing devices that form marks by means of print wires - thin metal filaments that strike an inked ribbon to create characters or symbols. It covers patents where the printing or marking process relies on the motion or arrangement of these wires rather than typebars or other impact elements.
[B41J 2/235] Print head assemblies -- Print head assemblies for typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms comprise the elements that generate the printing or marking impression, such as arrays of pins, hammers, or ink-transfer devices, together with any supporting structures, drive mechanisms and alignment features that position and actuate the print points during operation.
[B41J 2/24] serial printer type -- B41J 2/24 covers serial printer type devices, where characters are printed sequentially by a moving print head in a line or series. It applies to typewriters and other selective printing mechanisms defined by the specific printing or marking process they employ.
[B41J 2/245] line printer type -- Line printer type refers to a class of typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that form an entire line of characters in a single operation, typically using an impact print head such as a rotating drum, chain or bar. These devices are designed for high-speed computer output and produce a continuous line of printed or marked text.
[B41J 2/25] Print wires -- Print wires are the thin metal filaments that strike an inked ribbon to form characters in typewriters or other selective printing devices. The classification covers their design, arrangement and operation as the core elements that convert mechanical motion into printed marks.
[B41J 2/255] Arrangement of the print ends of the wires -- The subclass deals with how the individual print-wire ends or pins are positioned and aligned in typewriter-type or selective printing mechanisms, specifying the spatial arrangement that determines the shape and spacing of printed characters. It covers designs where the configuration of the wire ends is a defining feature of the printing process.
[B41J 2/26] Connection of print wire and actuator -- A B41J 2/26 sub-group covers the means of linking the print wire, or stylus, to its driving mechanism in typewriters and similar selective printing devices. It includes the physical connections, couplings, hinges or mounts that transmit the actuator's motion to the wire for striking the paper.
[B41J 2/265] Guides for print wires -- B41J 2/265 covers devices that guide the print wire in typewriter-style or selective printing mechanisms, ensuring correct alignment and striking of the wire against the inked ribbon. These guides may be fixed or adjustable and are part of the printing process for which the machine is designed.
[B41J 2/27] Actuators for print wires -- This subclass covers the mechanisms that move the print wires in typewriters, such as solenoids, electromagnets or other actuators that cause the wire to strike the ribbon and paper. It includes designs where the actuator is integrated with the printing mechanism to control impact force and speed.
[B41J 2/275] of clapper type -- B41J 2/275 refers to clapper-type typewriters, a subset of selective printing mechanisms that use a hinged clapper to strike an inked ribbon and imprint characters. These devices fall under the printing group within the broader performing operations class.
[B41J 2/28] of spring charge type, i.e. with mechanical power under electro-magnetic control -- B41J 2/28 covers typewriters that use a spring-charged mechanism, where mechanical power stored in a spring is released under electromagnetic control to drive the printing or marking process. This sub-class falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, and specifically addresses devices that combine mechanical and electrical actuation for character imprinting.
[B41J 2/285] of plunger type -- B41J 2/285 covers typewriters that use a plunger-type mechanism to drive the printing element against the paper. The classification applies to designs where the plunger motion directly controls the striking force and timing of the character imprint.
[B41J 2/29] of moving-coil type -- The CPC subclass B41J 2/29 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that employ a moving-coil principle, where a coil driven by an electric current creates magnetic forces to move a printing element and form characters. This subcategory falls under Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters and relates to devices designed specifically for the printing process they perform.
[B41J 2/295] using piezoelectric elements -- B41J 2/295 relates to typewriters and other selective printing mechanisms that generate the printing or marking action using piezoelectric elements, where the piezoelectric effect drives ink ejection or impact. This sub-class distinguishes such devices from those using electromagnetic or purely mechanical drive systems.
[B41J 2/30] Control circuits for actuators -- B41J 2/30 covers electronic or electromechanical control circuits that drive the actuators responsible for striking or moving typebars, hammers or print heads in typewriters and other selective printing devices. The circuits manage timing, power and coordination to achieve accurate character imprinting.
[B41J 23/00] Power drives for actions or mechanisms -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hammer printers -> B41J9/00 - Linear motors -> B41J19/305
[B41J 23/02] Mechanical power drives -- B41J 23/02 pertains to the mechanical power-drive systems used in typewriters, covering arrangements that supply motion to the carriage, platen, paper feed or other typing elements by means such as gears, cams, levers, springs or electric motors, rather than by manual effort.
[B41J 23/025] using a single or common power source for two or more functions -- B41J 23/025 covers typewriter power-drive arrangements that employ a single or shared power source to operate two or more separate functions, such as driving the carriage and controlling the printing mechanism. The classification is used for inventions that combine actions like feed, impact or ribbon movement under one motor or drive system, improving efficiency and reducing the need for multiple independent power units.
[B41J 23/04] with driven mechanism arranged to be clutched to continuously- operating power source -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 2/305] Ink supply apparatus -- B41J 2/305 covers the ink supply apparatus used in typewriters and other selective printing devices, including mechanisms that store, feed, or regulate ink for the printing or marking process. The subclass addresses components such as cartridges, reservoirs, pumps and distribution systems designed to ensure consistent ink delivery to the print head.
[B41J 23/06] by snatch rolls -- B41J 23/06 covers printing typewriters that advance the paper using snatch rolls - a mechanism that intermittently grips and releases the sheet to move it forward for each impression. This sub-group classifies devices where the motion of the paper is driven by these periodic rollers rather than continuous feed systems.
[B41J 23/08] by one-revolution or part-revolution clutches -- B41J 23/08 covers typewriter or similar printing devices in which the drive mechanism uses a clutch that engages for a single revolution or a fraction of a revolution to transfer motion to the print element. The clutch limits power transmission to one-revolution strokes, enabling precise control of hammer or character imprinting.
[B41J 2/31] using a print element with projections on its surface impacted or impressed by hammers -- B41J 2/31 covers typewriters and similar selective printing devices that form characters by striking a print element whose surface carries raised projections. The hammer-driven impact or impression of these projections transfers ink onto the paper, defining each printed mark.
[B41J 23/10] and arrested in selected position -- B41J 23/10 pertains to typewriter power-drive systems that include features for arresting the drive in a selected position, such as locking the carriage or printing element to pause operation or control spacing. The classification covers mechanisms that engage, hold, or release the drive at a predetermined point during printing.

[B41J 2/315] characterised by selective application of heat to a heat sensitive printing or impression-transfer material -- The subclass B41J 2/315 covers typewriters or selective printing devices that create marks by applying heat only to selected areas of a heat-sensitive printing or impression-transfer material. It includes mechanisms where thermal activation produces the image rather than ink or impact.
[B41J 23/16] Mechanisms driven by a spring tensioned by power means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.

[B41J 23/22] for key or like type selection -- B41J 23/22 covers power-driven mechanisms in printing typewriters that select keys or similar elements, enabling rapid, controlled activation of individual typebars or print positions. It relates to the electronic or mechanical drive systems that manage key selection within typewriter printing operations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 23/24] for impression mechanisms -- B41J 23/24 covers the impression mechanisms used in typewriter printing systems. These mechanisms generate the visual imprint of characters on paper, including striking hammers or other devices that transfer ink or impact the medium. The class falls under printing typewriters within performing operations.
[B41J 2/325] by selective transfer of ink from ink carrier, e.g. from ink ribbon or sheet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indirect thermal printing using ribbon between the head and the paper for printing.
[B41J 23/26] for platen or carriage movements, e.g. for line spacing, letter spacing, or carriage return -- B41J 23/26 refers to power-driven devices that move the platen or carriage in typewriters, enabling functions such as line spacing, letter spacing and automatic carriage return. These mechanisms provide the controlled motion required for printing operations.
[B41J 23/28] for type-carriage movements -- B41J 23/28 covers power-driven mechanisms that move the type carriage in typewriters, detailing the actuators and drive systems used to position the carriage during printing operations. It falls under the broader categories of printing, typewriters, and power drives for actions or mechanisms.
[B41J 2/33] from ink roller -- B41J 2/33 includes typewriters or selective printing devices that obtain ink from an ink roller instead of a conventional ribbon, with the roller supplying ink directly to the printing element during operation. The classification applies to mechanisms where a continuously fed roller provides ink to the type-striking surface.
[B41J 23/30] for case shift -- B41J 23/30 covers power-driven mechanisms that shift the type case in typewriters, enabling automatic movement of the type elements during printing. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, specifically addressing powered case-shift drives.

It classifies devices that supply mechanical or electromechanical force to these functions, distinguishing them from the manual key-actuated levers.

[B41J 23/36] and acting on type members -- The subclass B41J 23/36 covers power-driven mechanisms that operate typewriter actions and move the type members, such as electric, pneumatic or hydraulic drives that convert motor energy into the striking motion of the characters. It pertains to devices that provide the force and control for typing operations.
[B41J 23/38] and acting on aligning or case-shift mechanisms -- B41J 23/38 covers power-driven mechanisms that align printed characters or shift between uppercase and lowercase positions in typewriters, including devices that actuate case-shift levers, alignment stops, or other positioning elements during typing.
[B41J 2/34] comprising semiconductors -- This subgroup covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate semiconductor elements in the printing or marking process, such as LED, laser or electronic-ink sources used to form characters. The semiconductor components are integral to generating or transferring the imprint onto the recording medium.
[B41J 2/345] characterised by the arrangement of resistors or conductors -- A subclass of typewriters or selective printing mechanisms where the printing or marking process is defined by the specific arrangement of resistors or conductors within the device's circuitry, influencing how characters are formed on the medium. It belongs to the CPC section on performing operations, under printing and typewriters.
[B41J 2/35] providing current or voltage to the thermal head -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus for supplying electrical power to the thermal print head in typewriters or selective printing devices, including control of current and voltage to generate heat for marking. It falls under CPC B41J for typewriter printing mechanisms.
[B41J 2/355] Control circuits for heating-element selection -- This subclass covers the electronic control circuits that choose which heating element is energized in typewriters or other selective printing devices, ensuring the correct character or line is printed. It includes designs for switching, temperature regulation, and element sequencing within the marking process.
[B41J 2/3551] Block driving -- Block driving is a typewriter mechanism where a group of typebars or printing elements is moved as a single unit to strike the paper, enabling simultaneous printing of multiple characters and increasing printing speed.
[B41J 2/3553] Heater resistance determination -- B41J 2/3553 relates to techniques for measuring or determining the electrical resistance of heating elements used in typewriters or selective printing devices. It includes methods, circuits or apparatus that assess heater condition to ensure proper temperature control during printing.
[B41J 2/3555] Historical control -- Historical control refers to the conventional mechanical devices used in early typewriters to regulate carriage movement, line feed and spacing, such as manual levers, rollers and escapement mechanisms that predate electronic or software-based control systems.
[B41J 2/3556] Preheating pulses -- A preheating pulse is a timed burst of energy applied to the printing element of a typewriter or selective printing mechanism to raise its temperature before the marking strike. This prepares the ribbon, inked surface or thermal head for consistent print quality and reduces lag in the marking process.
[B41J 2/3558] Voltage control or determination -- The B41J 2/3558 subclass covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate means for controlling or measuring the electrical voltage applied to the printing apparatus. It includes circuits, sensors or regulation devices that adjust power to print-heads, ribbons or impact elements to ensure consistent marking performance.
[B41J 2/36] Print density control -- This subclass covers typewriters and selective printing devices that incorporate means for adjusting or regulating the density of printed characters or marks. It includes mechanisms that vary ink transfer, impact force, or exposure to achieve uniform darkness or lightness across output. It is used for controlling print density in typewriter-style printers.
[B41J 2/362] Correcting density variation -- B41J 2/362 covers features in typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that adjust or compensate for variations in ink density across the page, ensuring uniform marking. It includes devices that detect density differences and modify impact force, ink-supply or ribbon positioning to correct the variation.
[B41J 2/365] by compensation for variation in temperature -- This subclass covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate means for compensating temperature-induced variations, ensuring consistent print quality despite changes in ambient or operating temperature.
[B41J 2/37] by compensation for variation in current -- B41J 2/37 relates to typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms that include means for compensating variations in the electrical current during the printing process, thereby maintaining consistent marking quality.
[B41J 2/375] Protection arrangements against overheating -- The subclass B41J 2/375 relates to typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms and defines protection arrangements that prevent overheating of the printing or marking components. It includes thermal sensors, cut-off circuits, cooling devices, heat-dissipating structures and other control features designed to safeguard the machine from excessive temperature.
[B41J 2/38] Preheating, i.e. heating to a temperature insufficient to cause printing -- B41J 2/38 relates to typewriters or selective printing devices that incorporate a preheating stage, where a heating element raises the temperature of the print medium or ribbon to a level below that required for actual ink transfer. The purpose is to improve print consistency, reduce wear, or prepare the material for subsequent printing.
[B41J 2/385] characterised by selective supply of electric current or selective application of magnetism to a printing or impression-transfer material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Print heads only.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Characterised by bringing liquid or particles selectively into contact with a printing material -> B41J2/005
[B41J 2/3855] Electrographic print heads using processes not otherwise provided for, e.g. electrolysis -- The subclass concerns electrographic print heads that employ unconventional printing processes such as electrolysis. It applies to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms where the marking is generated by these electrographic methods.
[B41J 2/39] using multi-stylus heads -- B41J 2/39 relates to typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms that use multiple stylus heads so that several characters or marks can be printed in a single operation, enabling simultaneous imprinting of multiple elements.
[B41J 2/395] Structure of multi-stylus heads -- The subclass B41J 2/395 relates to the structural design of multi-stylus heads used in typewriters or selective printing mechanisms. It covers the arrangement, mounting and mechanical linkages of print heads that contain several stylus elements for simultaneous character formation.
[B41J 2/40] providing current or voltage to the multi-stylus head -- B41J 2/40 covers arrangements for powering a multi-stylus printing head in typewriter-type or selective printing devices, specifying how current or voltage is supplied to each stylus to drive the marking process. It includes circuitry, connectors and control methods that ensure reliable electrical activation of the individual stylus elements during operation.
[B41J 2/405] Selection of the stylus or auxiliary electrode to be supplied -- The B41J 2/405 subclass covers typewriter or selective-printing mechanisms where the system includes a choice of stylus or auxiliary electrode that is provided for the printing or marking operation. It pertains to the selection or provision of the specific writing tip or auxiliary electrode used to transfer ink, impression or other marking material onto the medium. This subclass refines the broader B41J group dealing with typewriters and similar printing devices.
[B41J 2/41] for electrostatic printing -- B41J 2/41 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that employ electrostatic printing, in which a charged pattern attracts toner particles to form an image on the medium. It is a subclass within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 2/415] by passing charged particles through a hole or a slit -- B41J 2/415 pertains to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that form characters by directing charged particles, such as ions or electrons, through a hole or slit onto a substrate, creating the printed or marked image by the particle beam.
[B41J 2/4155] for direct electrostatic printing [DEP] -- **Glossary:** - DEP: Direct Electrostatic Printing, without the intermediate step of a latent image because the toner particles are directly driven through the holes or slits.
[B41J 2/42] for heating selectively -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing by selective heating by applying an electric current to a printing medium.  **Glossary:** - Printing medium: e.g. a paper sheet on which is printed
[B41J 2/425] for removing surface layer selectively from electro-sensitive material, e.g. metal coated paper -- The code B41J 2/425 pertains to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that remove a surface layer from electro-sensitive media, such as metal-coated paper, to create a mark by exposing the underlying material. This function is part of the printing subclass dealing with processes that erase or strip a coating rather than applying ink.
[B41J 2/43] for magnetic printing -- B41J 2/43 relates to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that produce marks by using magnetic ink or magnetic particles. The entry covers devices where the printing process relies on magnetic forces to transfer the marking medium onto a substrate, such as magnetic line printers or similar magnetic-printing equipment.
[B41J 2/435] characterised by selective application of radiation to a printing material or impression-transfer material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Optical print heads as laser print heads
[B41J 2/44] using single radiation source , e.g. lighting beams or shutter arrangements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Characterised by selective application of radiation to a printing material or impression-transfer material for heating selectively by radiation or ultrasonic waves -> B41J2/475
[B41J 2/442] using lasers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Laser characteristics as power, power density.
[B41J 2/445] using liquid crystals -- The subclass B41J 2/445 covers typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms that create markings by employing liquid-crystal technology, such as LCD-driven character displays or variable-opacity elements that control ink or light application. It includes devices where the printing or marking process relies on the optical or electro-optical properties of liquid crystals to form the printed image.
[B41J 2/447] using arrays of radiation sources -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General lay-outs of printers with light array print heads  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Characterised by selective application of radiation to a printing material for heating selectively: -> B41J2/475
[B41J 2/4473] using liquid crystal [LC] arrays -- This subclass covers typewriter or selective printing devices that employ liquid-crystal arrays to form characters or images. The liquid-crystal elements serve as the printing medium, enabling electronic control of the marking process. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 2/4476] using cathode ray or electron beam tubes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[B41J 2/45] using light-emitting diode arrays -- B41J 2/45 relates to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that use light-emitting diode arrays to produce characters or markings. The subclass covers devices in which LEDs serve as the light source for printing, replacing conventional impact or thermal elements and typically includes control circuitry for driving the LED matrix.
[B41J 2/451] Special optical means therefor, e.g. lenses, mirrors, focusing means -- Typewriters or selective printing devices equipped with dedicated optical components such as lenses, mirrors or other focusing elements that aid the printing or marking process, typically to improve alignment, accuracy or image quality. This subclass covers special optical means specifically integrated for the printing operation.
[B41J 2/455] using laser arrays -- B41J 2/455 covers typewriter-like or selective printing devices that create marks by means of laser arrays, where multiple laser beams are coordinated to form characters or graphics directly on the substrate. The classification is used for inventions that employ arrays of lasers rather than single-beam or mechanical printing mechanisms to produce the printed output.
[B41J 2/46] characterised by using glass fibres -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printers using one or more glass fibres as optical waveguide to guide light from a light source to the printing medium.
[B41J 2/465] using masks, e.g. light-switching masks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light-switching mirrors or valves for modulating.  Light slits for focusing light beams.
[B41J 2/4655] using character templates -- This subgroup covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that generate characters by employing reusable character templates or stencils, where the template is pressed against an inked medium to produce the desired imprint.
[B41J 2/47] using the combination of scanning and modulation of light -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light array print heads reciprocating (scanning) over the media to be printed.  **Glossary:** - Light modulation: light source intensity being set on a level corresponding with an image data map
[B41J 2/471] using dot sequential main scanning by means of a light deflector, e.g. a rotating polygonal mirror -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A modulated light beam from, e.g., a laser diode array is directed to a rotating mirror. From there the light beam is directed through a lens and is moved (scanned) over a medium, e.g. an electrically chargeable drum.  Illustrative example of the subject matter classified in this subgroup:  A rotating polygonal scanning mirror for moving (scanning) a laser light beam over an imaging drum.  **Glossary:** - ROS: raster output scanner - Rotating polygon: rotating device having a plurality of mirrored facets - Scanning mirror: moving mirror, e.g. rotating - Main-scanning: here: light beam moving over e.g. a drum - Sub-scanning: drum, belt or paper movement; - Light modulation: light source intensity being set on a level corresponding with an image data map.
[B41J 2/473] using multiple light beams, wavelengths or colours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A plurality of light beams directed to the same scanning mirror (e.g. a light source array).
[B41J 2/475] for heating selectively -- B41J 2/475 covers typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms that create marks by applying heat, such as thermal typewriters that heat selected areas of heat-sensitive paper or ink to form characters. The subclass is limited to devices where heating is the primary means of generating the printed impression.
[B41J 2/4753] using thermosensitive substrates, e.g. paper -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Heating for writing or erasing on print media.
[B41J 2/48] melting ink on a film or melting ink granules -- This subclass covers typewriter-type or selective printing mechanisms that create marks by heating ink so it melts onto a film or into granules, then solidifies to form the printed image. It is part of the printing group within performing operations.
[B41J 2/485] characterised by the process of building-up characters applicable to two or more kinds of printing or marking processes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Matrix printing having means to control the print result e.g. negative print, background shaded, avoiding of wearing out by selective use of print elements. E.g. use of ultra-sonic vibrations therefor.
[B41J 2/49] by writing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reproducing of handwriting.  Signatures.  Pens on carriages (plotters).
[B41J 2/495] by selective printing from a rotating helical member -- B41J 2/495 covers typewriters or other selective printing devices that achieve character selection by means of a rotating helical member, such as a helix-driven type element. The mechanism uses the helix's angular position to determine which character is struck and transferred onto the medium.
[B41J 2/50] by the selective combination of two or more non-identical printing elements -- This CPC subclass covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that form characters by selectively combining two or more different printing elements. It is coded B41J 2/50 and relates to printing processes where non-identical elements are used together to create the printed mark.
[B41J 25/00] Actions or mechanisms not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Selective printers, including type-writers, ink jet and thermal printers.
[B41J 25/001] Mechanisms for bodily moving print heads or carriages parallel to the paper surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. page-width print heads moving during printing for compensating for print element aberrations.
[B41J 25/003] for changing the angle between a print element array axis and the printing line, e.g. for dot density changes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Serial and line print heads.
[B41J 25/005] for serial printing movements superimposed to character- or line-spacing movements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Serial print heads only.  E.g. mechanical alignment parallel to the printing medium  **Glossary:** - Serial print head: reciprocating print head printing during its movement along the width of a medium to be printed (main-scanning) - Line-spacing: movement step in the length direction of the printing material, by means of a medium transport system (sub-scanning)
[B41J 25/006] for oscillating, e.g. page-width print heads provided with counter-balancing means or shock absorbers -- This subclass covers mechanisms that cause a page-width print head to oscillate during printing, including designs that incorporate counter-balancing devices or shock absorbers to control motion and reduce vibration.
[B41J 25/02] Key actions for specified purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.  In general: repeat actions
[B41J 25/04] Back spacing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only for type-writers, in general half-space keys.
[B41J 2/505] from an assembly of identical printing elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Matrix printer print quality adjustment;  Print resolution adjustment;  Interlacing for printers not being of the ink-jet type.  **Glossary:** - Printing elements: e.g. nozzles, electrodes, LED's to form dots on a material to be printed (print media) - Identical printing elements: e.g. an array of nozzles - NLQ: Near letter quality, which means high print quality - Registration/registering: In color printing the method of correlating the overlapping colours on one single position of an image - Print resolution: printing at a predetermined dot density - Dot density: number of dots per unit of surface - Raster: line or grid with a predetermined dot density
[B41J 2/5052] with special adaptations characterised by the ink properties  B41J2/2107  takes precedence -- B41J 2/5052 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that incorporate special adaptations specifically related to the properties of the ink used, such as viscosity, drying time or colour stability. This subclass follows the broader B41J 2/2107 category, which defines the general typewriter printing process, and focuses on how ink characteristics are engineered into the device.
[B41J 2/5054] with special adaptations characterised by dot size  B41J2/2121  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Print heads other than of the ink jet type.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Multi-colour printing characterised by dot size, e.g. combinations of printed dots of different diameter -> B41J2/2121
[B41J 2/5056] using dot arrays providing selective dot disposition modes, e.g. different dot densities for high speed and high-quality printing, array line selections for multi-pass printing, or dot shifts for character inclination  B41J2/2132  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Print quality control characterised by dot disposition, e.g. for reducing white stripes or banding -> B41J2/2132
[B41J 2/5058] locally, i.e. for single dots or for small areas of a character  methods for insertion or deletion of dots, or for character edge smoothing  G06K15/102 -- B41J 2/5058 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that provide locally applied techniques-such as inserting or deleting single dots or smoothing the edges of characters-on small areas of a printed character. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 25/06] Carriage return -- The CPC subclass B41J 25/06 relates to carriage-return mechanisms in typewriters, covering the device or action that returns the paper carriage to the start of the line after printing. It falls under the printing group within the performing operations section.
[B41J 25/08] Case shift -- The B41J 25/08 class defines mechanisms for shifting the character case on typewriters, including manual levers or automatic devices that change between uppercase and lowercase printing. It covers case-shift actions not otherwise specified in other printing-operation subclasses.
[B41J 2/51] serial printer type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printers with a print head reciprocating over the material to be printed in order to print a single line or swath, e.g. geometry aspects as columns/rows of print elements (monochrome) or functional aspects as inclination of characters (matrix printers).  **Glossary:** - Serial printer: printer with a print head reciprocating over the material in order to print a single line or swath - Line head printer: page-width printer printing at least one raster line at the same time
[B41J 25/10] Ink-ribbon adjustment -- The B41J 25/10 subclass defines mechanisms for adjusting the ink ribbon on typewriters, covering devices that shift, tension, or position the ribbon to maintain consistent ink transfer. It includes both manual and automatic adjustment systems that are not specifically addressed in other subclasses of typewriter printing operations.
[B41J 2/512] Adjustment of the dot disposition by adjustment of the arrangement of the dot printing elements of a print head, e.g. nozzles, needles -- B41J 2/512 concerns typewriters or selective printing mechanisms and relates to adjusting the dot disposition by altering the arrangement of the dot-printing elements-such as nozzles or needles-in a print head to modify the printed pattern.
[B41J 25/12] Character spacing -- B41J 25/12 covers features that control the horizontal distance between printed characters on typewriters. It includes mechanisms for adjusting, regulating, or automatically varying spacing to improve legibility and formatting. The code is used for patents describing such spacing arrangements.
[B41J 25/14] Line spacing -- B41J 25/14 relates to mechanisms that set or vary the vertical distance between successive lines of type-written or printed characters, covering line-feed and line-spacing devices used in typewriters and similar printing equipment.
[B41J 2/515] line printer type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Page-width printers printing at least one raster line at the same time.  **Glossary:** - Serial printer: printer with a print head reciprocating over the material in order to print a single line or swath. - Line printer type: page-width printer printing at least one raster line at the same time.
[B41J 25/16] Line spacing and carriage return by a single key -- B41J 25/16 covers typewriter mechanisms that combine line spacing and carriage-return functions into a single key, allowing the operator to advance the paper and return the carriage to the starting position with one actuation. This design is classified under actions or mechanisms not otherwise provided for within the printing and typewriter subgroup.
[B41J 25/18] Tabulating -- B41J 25/18 Tabulating covers the specific mechanisms in typewriters that perform tabulation functions, such as automatic column alignment, tab stops and paper-feed control, which are not addressed by other, more general printing or typewriter subclasses.
[B41J 2/52] Arrangement for printing a discrete number of tones, not covered by group -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangement for multi-color printing, not covered by group B41J2/21 , e.g. applicable to two or more kinds of printing or marking process -> B41J2/525
[B41J 25/20] Auxiliary type mechanisms for printing distinguishing marks, e.g. for accenting, using dead or half-dead key arrangements, for printing marks in telegraph printers to indicate that machine is receiving -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 25/22] for aligning characters for impression -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 25/24] Case-shift mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 2/525] Arrangement for multi-colour printing, not covered by group -- B41J 2/525 covers arrangements in typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that enable multi-colour printing when the configuration is not included in any other subgroup. It pertains to the layout of colour-selection, ink-delivery or printing-head components that allow different colours to be printed in a single operation. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 25/304] Bodily-movable mechanisms for print heads or carriages movable towards or from paper surface -- B41J 25/304 covers mechanisms that physically move the print head or its carriage toward or away from the paper surface. It includes bodily-movable actuators such as levers, cams, or motor-driven assemblies used to adjust the head's distance during printing.
[B41J 25/308] with print gap adjustment mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  With print gap adjustment means on the print head carriage (e.g. for rotation around a guide bar or using a rotatable eccentric bearing).  With print gap adjustment means between the print head and its carriage.  With print gap adjustment means on the printer frame (e.g. for rotation of an eccentric carriage guide shaft).  **Glossary:** - Print gap: Distance between print head nozzle plate and print medium - Guide shaft/rod/bar/rails: Guiding means supporting and guiding a reciprocating print head carriage - Print/printing medium/media: Material to be printed, e.g. a sheet of paper - Carriage/carrier: Print head holder which is movable over the width of a print medium - Platen: Surface supporting the print medium, e.g. flat surface or a rotating drum
[B41J 25/3082] with print gap adjustment means on the print head carriage, e.g. for rotation around a guide bar or using a rotatable eccentric bearing -- B41J 25/3082 covers typewriter printing mechanisms where the print head carriage includes means to adjust the print gap, such as rotating the carriage around a guide bar or using a rotatable eccentric bearing, allowing precise alignment of the typebars with the paper.
[B41J 25/3084] by means of a spacer contacting the matter to be printed -- This CPC subclass covers typewriter-type printing mechanisms that transfer ink or other marking material by a spacer that directly contacts the matter to be printed, rather than by a conventional impact or roller. It is used for actions not otherwise provided for, where the spacer controls spacing, pressure or positioning of the printed mark.
[B41J 25/3086] with print gap adjustment means between the print head and its carriage -- The CPC subclass B41J 25/3086 covers devices that provide means for adjusting the print gap-the distance between the typewriter's print head and its carriage. It pertains to mechanisms that allow precise control of this spacing to ensure consistent character impression during printing.
[B41J 25/3088] with print gap adjustment means on the printer frame, e.g. for rotation of an eccentric carriage guide shaft -- B41J 25/3088 covers printers where the print gap is adjusted by means attached to the printer frame, such as rotating an eccentric carriage guide shaft to change the distance between the print element and the medium.
[B41J 25/312] with print pressure adjustment mechanisms, e.g. pressure-on-the paper mechanisms -- This subclass covers printing and typewriter devices that incorporate mechanisms for adjusting the pressure applied to the paper during operation, such as pressure-on-the-paper systems that vary print force to improve quality or accommodate different media.
[B41J 25/316] with tilting motion mechanisms relative to paper surface -- B41J 25/316 pertains to typewriter-like printing devices that incorporate a tilting motion of the type element or printing carriage relative to the paper surface, enabling adjustment of the imprint angle for effects such as shade or slant.
[B41J 25/32] Impression mechanisms in which a roller co-operates with stationary type-faces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 25/34] Bodily-changeable print heads or carriages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Print head carriages, e.g. for ink jet.  Interchangeable type-writer print heads, as disks.
[B41J 27/00] Inking apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Inking in type-writers and desk-top printers other than of the ink-jet type.
[B41J 27/02] with ink applied by pads or rotary discs -- B41J 27/02 relates to typewriter inking mechanisms that apply ink to the printing elements by means of pads or rotary discs. The ink is transferred from a pad or a rotating disc onto the typebars or other striking members, allowing continuous printing without manual re-inking.
[B41J 27/04] Pads or discs -- The B41J 27/04 subclass covers pads or discs that serve as ink-absorbing or distributing elements in typewriter and other printing mechanisms. These components receive ink from a reservoir and transfer it to the striking element, often being made of felt, sponge or synthetic material and may be replaceable or integral to the device.
[B41J 27/06] Arrangements to ensure maximum life of pads or discs -- The B41J 27/06 subclass deals with typewriter inking apparatus that incorporate features to extend the service life of ink pads or ink discs, such as mechanisms for even ink distribution, protective housings, cleaning cycles, or controlled feeding that reduce wear and depletion. These arrangements aim to maximise the usable lifespan of the consumable inking components.
[B41J 27/08] Arrangements for multicolour work -- B41J 27/08 covers arrangements for multicolour work in typewriter inking apparatus, describing mechanisms that enable the selection or simultaneous application of different ink colors, such as multi-ink ribbons, color-changing cartridges, or switching systems that feed distinct inks to the typebars.
[B41J 27/10] with ink applied by rollers -- B41J 27/10 covers inking apparatus for typewriters or printing devices where ink is transferred to the printing elements by rollers. The subclass includes mechanisms that feed, distribute or apply ink using roller systems, distinguishing it from brush or spray methods. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing and typewriters.
[B41J 27/12] Rollers -- B41J 27/12 covers rollers employed in the inking mechanisms of typewriter printing devices. It pertains to components that transfer or distribute ink onto the ribbon or printing surface as part of the broader inking apparatus category within printing operations.
[B41J 27/14] Arrangements for multicolour work -- B41J 27/14 covers the design of inking apparatus for typewriters that enables multicolour work, including mechanisms for applying different inks in sequence or simultaneously, such as multi-ribbon systems, colour-selection devices and feeding arrangements that produce coloured text or graphics.
[B41J 27/16] with ink deposited electrostatically or electromagnetically, e.g. powdered ink -- B41J 27/16 relates to typewriter inking devices that deposit ink on the page by electrostatic or electromagnetic means, often employing powdered ink. This approach places the ink without direct mechanical contact, enabling precise and controllable printing.
[B41J 27/18] with liquid ink deposited -- B41J 27/18 covers typewriter printing devices that employ an inking apparatus to deposit liquid ink onto the paper during operation. It includes mechanisms that deliver liquid ink to typebars or print heads, enabling continuous or intermittent printing with fluid ink.
[B41J 27/20] with ink supplied by capillary action, e.g. through porous type members, through porous platens -- The subclass B41J 27/20 covers typewriters and similar printing devices that obtain ink by capillary action, using porous type members or porous platens to draw the ink into the printing element. It is a specific ink-supplied printing method within the broader printing operations hierarchy.
[B41J 27/22] with inking discs or sectors -- The subclass B41J 27/22 covers typewriter printing mechanisms that employ rotating inking discs or sector-shaped ink carriers to apply ink to the typebars. It is part of the inking-apparatus group within the printing branch of performing operations.
[B41J 29/00] Details of, or accessories for, typewriters or selective printing mechanisms not otherwise provided for -- This classification covers specific components, accessories and detailed designs for typewriters and selective printing mechanisms that are not provided for elsewhere, including parts for character selection, paper feeding, formatting and other specialized functions.
[B41J 29/02] Framework -- Framework denotes the structural chassis or supporting skeleton of a typewriter or selective printing device, providing rigidity and alignment for the key and printing mechanisms and serving as the base to which other components and accessories are attached.
[B41J 29/023] with reduced dimensions -- B41J 29/023 relates to typewriter or selective printing mechanisms that are designed with reduced dimensions, covering compact accessories, miniature typebars, or other size-reduced features not otherwise specified.
[B41J 29/026] Stackable -- Stackable denotes accessories or mechanisms that allow typewriter parts, paper or related items to be arranged in a piled or nested configuration for easier handling, transport or storage, such as trays, racks or other devices that enable multiple units to be stacked together.
[B41J 29/04] Means for attaching machines to baseboards -- B41J 29/04 covers devices that attach typewriters or other selective printing machines to a baseboard, such as clamps, brackets or anchoring fixtures, to secure the equipment in a fixed position. These accessories provide stability during operation and may be adjustable or removable.
[B41J 29/06] Special supports, platforms or trolleys for supporting machines on tables -- This subclass covers special supports, platforms or trolleys designed to hold typewriters or similar printing machines on tables, providing stable positioning and ease of use for the equipment.
[B41J 29/08] Sound-deadening, or shock-absorbing stands, supports, cases or pads separate from machines -- B41J 29/08 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers sound-deadening and shock-absorbing accessories for typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are not integral to the machine itself. These include stands, supports, cases, or pads designed to reduce noise and vibrations.
[B41J 29/10] Sound-deadening devices embodied in machines -- B41J 29/10 covers sound-deadening devices that are built into typewriters or other selective printing machines. The classification includes any components or accessories designed to reduce noise generated during operation, such as vibration-absorbing mounts, acoustic enclosures, or dampening materials integrated in the machine.
[B41J 29/12] Guards, shields or dust excluders -- B41J 29/12 relates to protective components for typewriters and other selective printing devices, specifically guards, shields or dust-excluders that cover moving or exposed parts. These elements are intended to prevent damage, debris intrusion or accidental contact, thereby enhancing reliability and user safety. The entry falls within the CPC group for details of, or accessories for, typewriters.
[B41J 29/13] Cases or covers -- This group covers protective cases, covers and transport containers specially adapted for typewriters and related selective-printing mechanisms, providing shielding from damage, dust and environmental factors, and includes accessories that are not provided for in any other more specific classification.
[B41J 29/14] Attachments operated by the leg, e.g. the foot, the knee -- B41J 29/14 covers attachments for typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are actuated by a user's leg, such as foot pedals or knee levers. These devices enable the operator to control printing functions without using the hands and are treated as accessories not otherwise listed.
[B41J 29/15] Script supports connected to the typewriter or printer -- The classification B41J 29/15 covers accessories that provide script supports attached to a typewriter or printer, such as brackets, guides, or holders that secure the paper or printing medium during operation.
[B41J 29/16] Auxiliary receptacles for articles, e.g. erasers, pencils -- The B41J 29/16 subclass covers auxiliary receptacles integrated with typewriters that hold supplementary items such as erasers, pencils or similar articles, providing convenient storage or dispensing during operation. It includes designs of compartments, trays, holders or other attachment mechanisms specifically for these accessories.
[B41J 29/17] Cleaning arrangements -- B41J 29/17 refers to cleaning arrangements for typewriters or selective printing mechanisms. This code is used for details or accessories not otherwise provided for. It falls under printing and typewriters.
[B41J 29/18] Mechanisms for rendering the print visible to the operator -- Mechanisms that make printed characters observable to the operator during typing, including visible copy devices, windows, projection or illumination systems that display the impression on the paper or a temporary surface, allowing the user to monitor output in real time.
[B41J 29/19] with reflectors or illuminating devices -- A sub-group of typewriter accessories that incorporates reflectors or lighting elements to illuminate the typing area, improving visibility of the keys or printed line.
