It falls under performing operations, printing, typewriters in the CPC scheme.
[B41J 29/20] Arrangements of counting devices -- The CPC subclass B41J 29/20 covers the design and layout of counting mechanisms used with typewriters or other selective printing devices, including any accessories that incorporate such counters. It addresses how these counting devices are arranged within or attached to the machine for functions such as tallying characters, lines, or pages.
[B41J 29/22] Line counters -- B41J 29/22 covers line counters, devices that record or display the number of lines printed or typed by a typewriter or similar selective printing machine. These mechanisms help users monitor output quantity and manage paper usage.
[B41J 29/24] Word counters -- B41J 29/24 covers devices that count the number of words typed or printed, typically incorporated in typewriters or related accessories, allowing users to monitor text length for drafting, editing, or billing purposes.
[B41J 29/26] Devices, non-fluid media or methods for cancelling, correcting errors, underscoring or ruling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only type writers
[B41J 29/28] Writing or like instruments in holders or guides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. vertical ruling.
[B41J 29/30] Wheels -- B41J 29/30 covers wheels used in typewriter mechanisms such as carriage wheels, platen rollers and other feed wheels that move the typing carriage or advance paper. These components are treated as accessories or detailed parts of typewriters not classified elsewhere.
[B41J 29/32] Type members -- Type members are the individual characters or type elements used in typewriters, including typebars, type sleeves, interchangeable type sets and related accessories that form the printable impression. The classification covers details of these components and any accessories for selective printing mechanisms that are not covered by other categories.
[B41J 29/34] repeatedly actuated -- B41J 29/34 covers details and accessories for typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are repeatedly actuated, such as devices that repeatedly strike keys, feed paper, or perform other cyclic actions not covered by other subclasses.
[B41J 29/36] for cancelling or correcting errors by overprinting -- This CPC subclass deals with devices or methods used in typewriters to correct typing errors by printing over the mistake. It covers mechanisms that overstrike or otherwise cancel the unwanted character, enabling error correction without removing the original imprint. The classification falls under the broader groups of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 29/367] sheet media carrying a pigmented transferable correction layer -- This subclass covers sheets of paper or similar media that carry a pigmented layer intended to be transferred onto printed documents for correction. The layer is applied by pressure or friction to mask errors and restore the original appearance of the text or image.
[B41J 29/373] sheet media bearing an adhesive layer effective to lift off wrongly typed characters -- This subclass covers adhesive-backed sheet media designed for typewriters that can lift off incorrectly typed characters for correction. It includes details of such media and related accessories used in selective printing mechanisms.
[B41J 29/377] Cooling or ventilating arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Using fans or cooling liquid.  Detecting ambient temperature
[B41J 29/38] Drives, motors, controls or automatic cut-off devices for the entire printing mechanism -- The CPC code B41J 29/38 covers drives, motors, control systems and automatic cut-off devices that power or regulate the whole printing mechanism of typewriters or similar selective printing devices. It pertains to the overall motion and operation of the printing unit rather than individual character or line elements.
[B41J 29/387] Automatic cut-off devices -- The B41J 29/387 subclass concerns automatic cut-off devices for typewriters, covering mechanisms that automatically stop the typing stroke, paper feed or ink application when a key is released or the required impression is reached.
[B41J 29/393] Devices for controlling or analysing the entire machine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing and reading test patterns requiring mechanical action.  Battery mounted in the printer.  Wireless communication between the carriage and the printer.
[B41J 29/40] Means for printing fixed, i.e. unchanging, matter in addition to selectable matter -- B41J 29/40 pertains to devices that enable a typewriter or similar selective printing mechanism to add fixed, permanently displayed material-such as forms, logos or pre-printed text-alongside the normally selectable characters. It covers means for printing unchanging matter in addition to variable matter, including related accessories.
[B41J 29/42] Scales and indicators, e.g. for determining side margins -- The B41J 29/42 subclass covers devices that provide scales or visual indicators for typewriters, enabling users to set or check side-margin positions and other printing limits. It includes both built-in mechanisms and detachable accessories that display or measure these margins during operation.
[B41J 29/44] for determining top and bottom margins or indicating exhaust of paper -- B41J 29/44 covers devices or methods used with typewriters to set or indicate the top-bottom margins of the paper, or to signal when the paper is exhausted. It falls under the cooperative patent classification for typewriter accessories in the printing operations subclass.
[B41J 29/46] Applications of alarms, e.g. responsive to approach of end of line -- B41J 29/46 pertains to alarm applications for typewriters or selective printing devices, covering systems that signal conditions such as approaching the end of a line. It includes various sensors, alerts and warning mechanisms integrated with the machine to enhance operation safety and efficiency.
[B41J 29/48] responsive to breakage or exhaustion of paper or approach of bottom of paper -- The B41J 29/48 subclass covers typewriter or selective printing mechanisms that detect and react to paper-related conditions such as a paper break, near-exhaustion, or the approach of the paper's bottom edge, typically by stopping the feed, issuing an alert, or automatically shifting to a standby mode.
[B41J 29/50] Side-stop mechanisms -- Side-stop mechanisms are components that limit the lateral movement of a typewriter carriage or printing head, preventing it from moving beyond a set position during operation. They are used to control line length or align paper and are typically integrated into typewriters or selective printing devices.
[B41J 29/52] Top-and-bottom stop mechanisms -- The B41J 29/52 subclass covers top-and-bottom stop mechanisms, devices that limit the upward and downward travel of typewriter elements such as typebars or print heads to control line spacing and prevent over-travel. These mechanisms are treated as accessories for typewriters that are not otherwise specified in more detailed subclasses.
[B41J 29/54] Locking devices applied to printing mechanisms -- B41J 29/54 covers locking devices that are incorporated into typewriter or selective printing mechanisms to prevent unintended operation. It includes designs for keys, levers or catches that secure the printing elements when not in use. The class falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing and typewriters.
[B41J 29/56] and manually actuated -- B41J 29/56 concerns manually actuated accessories or mechanisms for typewriters that are not covered by other subclasses, such as hand-operated levers, feeds, or control devices that the user directly manipulates to affect printing or paper handling.
[B41J 29/58] and automatically actuated -- The B41J 29/58 entry covers typewriter components or accessories that provide automatic actuation of the printing mechanism, such as systems that trigger keys or hammers without manual input. It applies to selectively printed devices not otherwise covered elsewhere in the typewriter subclass, encompassing mechanisms that initiate typing actions automatically.
[B41J 29/60] in response to failure of power supply -- The CPC subclass B41J 29/60 covers devices, systems or methods that react to a loss of power in typewriters or selective printing mechanisms, providing backup power, automatic shutdown, manual operation or other remedial actions when the main supply fails. It is a narrow entry under Performing operations → Printing → Typewriters, focusing on accessories that ensure continued operation or safe handling of a power-supply failure.
[B41J 29/62] by the absence of paper to lock hammer mechanism -- This subclass concerns typewriter or selective-printing mechanisms that use the absence of paper to lock the hammer. The feature prevents the hammer from striking when no sheet is present, protecting the device and avoiding stray marks.
[B41J 29/64] by a function of the printer to lock the keyboard -- B41J 29/64 covers printer functions that lock the typewriter keyboard, a mechanism that disables typing while the printing unit operates. This accessory prevents keystrokes from interfering with the printing process and is classified under details of typewriters in the CPC.
[B41J 29/66] Locking devices actuated when platen reaches the end of a line -- The entry covers locking mechanisms that engage automatically when a typewriter's platen reaches the end of a line, preventing further movement of the carriage. It pertains to accessories for typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are not covered by other categories.
[B41J 29/68] by completion of a page or predetermined number of lines or exhaustion of paper to lock the keyboard -- Mechanisms that automatically lock a typewriter's keyboard when a page is completed, a predetermined number of lines has been typed, or the paper is exhausted, preventing further keystrokes.
[B41J 29/70] Interlocks between any two-carriage-moving mechanisms, e.g. character-space, back space, tabulation, carriage return or carriage- release mechanisms -- The classification B41J 29/70 pertains to typewriter mechanisms that provide interlocking between any two carriage-moving functions such as character spacing, backspacing, tabulation, carriage return or release, ensuring coordinated operation of these movements.
[B41J 3/00] Typewriters or selective printing or marking mechanisms characterised by the purpose for which they are constructed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In particular bar code printers, flat-bed ink-jet printers, ink-jet printers for printing on bank books, passports or the like, portable printers, hand-held printers, stamp-type ink-jet printers, printers for printing on CDs or DVDs, ink-jet printers for printing on three-dimensional objects, label printers, ink-jet printers for printing on textile, printers for printing on the front and on the back of sheets or continuous material.
[B41J 3/01] for special character, e.g. for Chinese characters or barcodes -- B41J 3/01 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms designed specifically for producing special characters, such as Chinese characters or barcodes, which require unique or complex printing capabilities.
[B41J 31/00] Ink ribbons -- B41J 31/00 covers the supply of ink ribbons used in typewriters and similar printing devices. It includes the structure, composition and methods for providing a continuous or pre-inked ribbon that transfers ink onto paper during typing.
[B41J 31/02] Ink ribbons characterised by the material from which they are woven -- B41J 31/02 covers ink ribbons for typewriters that are distinguished by the material used in their woven structure, such as cotton, polyester or other fibers. These ribbons are designed for printing applications in typewriter devices and fall within the broader class of performing operations, printing, typewriters.
[B41J 31/04] woven from synthetic material -- B41J 31/04 classifies ink ribbons for typewriters that are made from a woven synthetic material rather than traditional cloth or paper, covering their design, manufacture and use in printing operations.
[B41J 31/05] Ink ribbons having coatings other than impression-material coatings -- B41J 31/05 covers typewriter ink ribbons that are treated with coatings other than the usual impression-material layers. These alternative coatings may provide protection, lubrication, anti-static or decorative properties, enhancing ribbon lifespan or handling without forming the printed image itself.
[B41J 31/06] the coatings being directly on the base material, i.e. below impression transfer material -- B41J 31/06 covers typewriter ink ribbons in which the ink or pigment coating is applied directly to the base material, forming a layer beneath the impression-transfer coating. The subclass includes ribbon constructions and processes where the coating resides on the substrate itself rather than on a separate transfer layer.
[B41J 31/08] the coatings being superimposed on impression-transfer material -- This CPC subclass deals with the coatings that are superimposed on impression-transfer material such as typewriter ink ribbons. It includes methods and materials for applying protective or functional layers to the ribbon surface to enhance print quality, durability, or other performance characteristics.
[B41J 31/09] Ink ribbons characterised by areas carrying media for obliteration or removal of typing errors -- Ink ribbons for typewriters that incorporate zones containing a medium-such as correction fluid, carbon filler or similar substance-designed to obscure or remove typing errors. These ribbons allow the user to correct mistakes directly by striking the error area, depositing the obliterating material onto the printed page.
[B41J 31/10] Ink ribbons having arrangements to facilitate threading through a machine -- B41J 31/10 covers ink ribbons designed with features that make threading the ribbon through typewriter or printer mechanisms simpler. These arrangements may include guide holes, notches or pre-formed loops that align with the machine's feed path, reducing manual handling and improving reliability.
[B41J 31/12] Ink ribbons having arrangements to prevent undesired contact between the impression-transfer material and machine parts or other articles -- B41J 31/12 pertains to typewriter ink ribbons that include design elements preventing the impression-transfer material from contacting machine parts or other articles, thereby minimizing smearing, wear, and contamination.
[B41J 31/14] Renovating or testing ink ribbons -- B41J 31/14 covers methods and apparatus for refurbishing, cleaning, repairing or otherwise renewing ink ribbons used in typewriters and similar printing devices, as well as procedures for testing their performance and durability. The classification includes any steps taken to restore used ribbons to operational condition and to evaluate their ink transfer quality.
[B41J 31/16] while fitted in the machine using the ink ribbons -- The CPC subclass B41J 31/16 deals with ink ribbons that are installed in a typewriter while the machine is ready for printing, addressing their placement and handling during operation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 32/00] Ink-ribbon cartridges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Contains general features about ink ribbon cartridges and those with ribbons stored on spools.
[B41J 32/02] for endless ribbons -- B41J 32/02 covers endless-ribbon cartridges used in typewriters, a subset of printing mechanisms that employ a continuous supply of ink-impregnated ribbon rather than a limited strip. The classification addresses the design, construction and handling of such cartridges for sustained printing without ribbon replacement.
[B41J 3/24] for perforating or stencil cutting using special types or dies -- B41J 3/24 relates to typewriters or comparable printing mechanisms that incorporate special accessories for perforating or stencil-cutting, using dedicated dies or punches to produce holes or patterned openings in paper.
[B41J 3/26] for stenographic writing -- B41J 3/26 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are specifically designed for stenographic writing, enabling rapid shorthand transcription. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters in the CPC system.
[B41J 3/28] for printing downwardly on flat surfaces, e.g. of books, drawings, boxes -- B41J 3/28 covers typewriter or selective printing mechanisms that apply ink by printing downward onto flat surfaces such as the pages of books, drawings, or the faces of boxes. The subclass falls under the broader groups of Performing Operations - Printing - Typewriters and addresses devices where the print head moves vertically onto the workpiece.
[B41J 3/283] on bank books or the like -- This subgroup covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms designed specifically for printing on bank books, account ledgers or similar financial record sheets. It includes devices whose construction and operation are adapted to the format and durability requirements of such bookkeeping media.
[B41J 3/286] on boxes -- B41J 3/286 refers to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms specifically designed for printing on boxes, highlighting a specialized application within the broader category of printing and marking devices.
[B41J 3/30] for printing with large type, e.g. on bulletins, tickets -- This subclass covers typewriter-type devices that produce characters of unusually large size, intended for making visible markings on items such as bulletins, tickets, signs, or other documents where readability from a distance is required. It includes mechanisms designed specifically to handle oversized type and related printing or marking operations.
[B41J 33/00] Apparatus or arrangements for feeding ink ribbons or like character-size impression-transfer material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In general, ribbon-sized impression transfer material. But in some cases, also page-width impression transfer material if B41J17/00 does not provide an appropriate entry.
[B41J 33/003] Ribbon spools -- B41J 33/003 relates to devices that hold and supply ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material for typewriters, including spools, reels and the mechanisms that guide the ribbon through the printing element.
[B41J 33/006] Arrangements to attach the ribbon to the spool -- This classification concerns devices or methods used to secure an ink ribbon to its spool in typewriter printing equipment. It includes arrangements such as clamps, brackets, adhesives or mechanical hooks that attach the ribbon to the spool to ensure proper feeding and tension.
[B41J 33/02] Ribbon arrangements -- B41J 33/02 classifies the mechanisms that feed ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material in typewriters, including devices that advance, tension or replace the ribbon to maintain continuous printing. This subclass lies within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 33/04] mounted on moving carriages -- The CPC subclass B41J 33/04 covers typewriter or printing mechanisms in which the ink ribbon or comparable character-size impression-transfer material is mounted on a moving carriage that travels during operation, providing the feed system for the ribbon as the device prints. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters, specifically addressing apparatus or arrangements for feeding the ribbon.
[B41J 33/06] Ribbons associated, but not moving, with typewriter platens, e.g. extending transversely to the length of the platen -- B41J 33/06 classifies typewriter mechanisms in which the ink ribbon is fixed to the platen rather than moving with it, typically extending across the full transverse length of the platen. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, and concerns arrangements for feeding ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material.
[B41J 33/08] and extending parallel to the length of the platen -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Containing in general correcting ribbons on separate holders
[B41J 33/10] Arrangements of endless ribbons -- B41J 33/10 covers apparatus or arrangements for feeding endless ink ribbons or similar impression-transfer material in typewriters, describing mechanisms that continuously supply the ribbon to the print head. Such systems enable uninterrupted printing by automatically advancing the ribbon during operation.
[B41J 33/12] Ribbons carried by coaxially-mounted spools -- This subclass covers mechanisms in typewriter printing apparatus where the ink ribbon is wound on two spools that are mounted coaxially on a common shaft, allowing seamless ribbon feeding and reversal during operation. The design enables continuous supply of character-size impression-transfer material without manual reloading.
[B41J 33/14] Ribbon-feed devices or mechanisms -- This subclass deals with devices that feed ink ribbons or similar impression-transfer material in typewriters, encompassing mechanisms that advance, position, or tension the ribbon during printing. It includes feed rollers, automatic winding systems, ribbon-advancement controls and related apparatus.
[B41J 33/16] with drive applied to spool or spool spindle -- B41J 33/16 covers devices for feeding ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material in typewriters where the driving force is applied directly to the ribbon spool or its spindle. The classification falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, printing, typewriters.
[B41J 33/18] by ratchet mechanism -- The subclass B41J 33/18 covers typewriter devices that feed ink ribbons or other character-size impression-transfer material by means of a ratchet mechanism, detailing the specific arrangements that advance the ribbon stepwise for each keystroke.
[B41J 3/32] for printing in Braille or with keyboards specially adapted for use by blind or disabled persons -- The B41J 3/32 subclass covers typewriters or other selective printing mechanisms that are built to produce Braille output or that incorporate keyboards specially adapted for blind, visually impaired, or otherwise disabled users, enabling tactile printing and accessible operation.
[B41J 33/20] by friction -- Apparatus or arrangements for feeding ink ribbons, or similar character-size impression-transfer material, in typewriters by means of friction. The device uses a friction-driven mechanism to advance the ribbon as each character is printed.
[B41J 33/22] by gears or pulleys -- The classification B41J 33/22 covers typewriter printing mechanisms that move ink ribbons or comparable impression-transfer media using gears or pulleys. It applies to apparatus or arrangements where the ribbon feed is powered mechanically by such rotating elements.
[B41J 33/24] with drive applied directly to ribbon -- This classification covers typewriter mechanisms where the ink ribbon is driven directly by a motor or other drive rather than being moved indirectly. It includes devices that integrate a ribbon-feed system with direct drive to ensure precise ribbon advancement during printing.
[B41J 33/26] by rollers engaging the ribbon -- B41J 33/26 covers typewriter printing mechanisms that advance an ink ribbon or similar character-size impression-transfer material by means of rollers that engage the ribbon. The rollers grip and feed the ribbon to provide continuous printing of characters.
[B41J 33/28] by mechanism pulling or gripping the ribbon -- This subclass covers devices that move the ink ribbon in typewriters by pulling or gripping mechanisms, ensuring proper positioning for each character imprint. It includes mechanisms such as rollers, hooks, or clamps that advance the ribbon in synchronization with typing.
[B41J 33/30] Escapement mechanisms -- The B41J 33/30 class deals with escapement mechanisms used in typewriter ribbon-feeding systems. These mechanisms control the stepwise advancement of ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material in sync with each character imprint, ensuring proper spacing and preventing over- or under-feeding.
[B41J 33/32] Electromagnetic devices -- B41J 33/32 is a CPC subclass that falls under performing operations-printing-typewriters-apparatus or arrangements for feeding ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material. It specifically covers electromagnetic devices such as solenoids, electromagnets and related control circuits used to drive or regulate ribbon-feeding mechanisms in typewriters and comparable printing equipment. The classification addresses the design, operation and integration of these magnetic actuation components.
[B41J 33/34] driven by motors independently of the machine as a whole -- This subclass concerns typewriter or printing apparatus where the ink ribbon or similar impression-transfer material is advanced by a motor that operates independently of the machine's primary drive, allowing separate control of ribbon feeding.
[B41J 33/36] with means for adjusting feeding rate -- B41J 33/36 designates typewriter ribbon-feeding mechanisms that incorporate means for adjusting the feeding rate. It falls under printing operations and covers apparatus or arrangements for feeding ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material where the feed speed can be varied to suit printing conditions.
[B41J 33/38] Slow, e.g. "creep", feed mechanisms -- The code B41J 33/38 falls under the CPC heading for typewriters and concerns mechanisms that advance ink ribbons or similar impression-transfer material at a deliberately slow rate, such as creep or incremental feed systems that move the ribbon only a small distance between characters.
[B41J 33/382] the ribbon being fed only during carriage return -- B41J 33/382 covers typewriter mechanisms that advance the ink ribbon or similar impression-transfer material solely when the carriage returns, rather than continuously during typing. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, focusing on ribbon-feeding arrangements that operate only on carriage return.
[B41J 33/384] and attached to the carriage during writing -- B41J 33/384 pertains to apparatus or arrangements for feeding ink ribbons or similar materials, specifically those that are attached to the carriage during the printing process. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B41J 33/386] the ribbon being fed only by operation of the line spacing mechanism -- B41J 33/386 relates to typewriter printing apparatus that feeds ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material exclusively by the action of the line-spacing mechanism, without any dedicated feed rollers or motorized ribbon drive.
[B41J 33/388] the ribbon being fed only when type impression takes place -- The code B41J 33/388 covers typewriter mechanisms that advance the ink ribbon only at the moment a character is struck, feeding the ribbon on-demand rather than continuously. This timed ribbon feed conserves ribbon material and improves print consistency.
[B41J 3/34] for printing musical scores -- B41J 3/34 classifies typewriter-style or selective marking devices that are specifically engineered to print musical notation, such as staff lines, notes and symbols, onto paper or other media. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, typewriters and covers mechanisms whose purpose is the production of musical scores.
[B41J 33/40] with arrangements for reversing the feed direction -- B41J 33/40 covers typewriter mechanisms that feed ink ribbons or other character-size impression-transfer material and include features that allow the ribbon feed direction to be reversed, enabling reuse of the ribbon and alternate printing of characters.
[B41J 33/42] manually -- B41J 33/42 pertains to apparatus or arrangements for feeding ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer materials in typewriters, specifically those that are operated manually.
[B41J 33/44] automatically -- B41J 33/44 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to apparatus or arrangements for automatically feeding ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer materials in typewriters. This classification is specific to mechanisms that enhance the operational efficiency of printing devices by automating the ribbon feeding process.
[B41J 33/46] and characterised by its application to mechanism in which two spools are driven by pawl-and-rachet mechanism -- B41J 33/46 covers typewriter mechanisms that feed ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer material by using a pawl-and-ratchet system to drive two spools, ensuring coordinated ribbon advance and tension during printing operations.
[B41J 33/48] comprising two pawls and ratchets, one for each spool -- The subclass concerns typewriter ribbon feed mechanisms that use a pair of pawls and ratchets, one for each ribbon spool, to control the advancement and tension of the ink ribbon during printing.
[B41J 33/50] comprising a single pawl or integral double-tooth pawl selectively engageable with two ratchets, one for each spool -- B41J 33/50 covers ribbon-feed mechanisms for typewriters and similar printers that use a single pawl or an integral double-tooth pawl, which can be selectively engaged with two ratchets-one for each spool-to control feed direction and tension.
[B41J 33/51] and characterised by the use of particular reversing control means -- B41J 33/51 covers typewriter ribbon-feeding arrangements that are distinguished by the use of particular reversing control means, allowing the ink ribbon to change direction automatically during printing to ensure continuous impression transfer.
[B41J 33/512] using a pivoted reversing-feeler engaging the external periphery of the wound ribbon -- B41J 33/512 pertains to typewriter or printing equipment that feeds ink ribbons using a pivoted reversing-feeler which engages the external periphery of the wound ribbon to control its movement.
[B41J 33/514] using a pivoted reversing-feeler engaging the interior of the wound ribbon -- This CPC subgroup relates to typewriter ribbon handling devices that employ a pivoted reversing-feeler which engages the inner surface of the wound ribbon to control its movement or tension during printing. The feeler pivots to reverse direction when the ribbon reaches its end, ensuring continuous feed without manual adjustment.
[B41J 33/516] using a reversing-feeler responsive to the tension of the ribbon -- B41J 33/516 covers mechanisms that detect ribbon tension and, using a reversing-feeler, adjust the feed of ink ribbons or similar impression-transfer material in typewriter printing devices. The feeler moves opposite to the feed direction when tension exceeds a set level, preventing breakage or excessive slack.
[B41J 33/518] the reversing-feeler engaging buttons or the like secured to the ribbon near its ends -- B41J 33/518 covers features on ink-ribbon or similar character-size impression-transfer material that aid feeding, specifically reversing-feeler engaging buttons or comparable devices mounted near the ribbon ends. These elements are used in typewriter mechanisms to detect ribbon position and control the reversal or advance of the ribbon during printing.
[B41J 33/52] Braking devices therefor -- B41J 33/52 classifies braking devices used in typewriter-type printing systems to control the motion of ink ribbons or similar character-size impression-transfer media, such as brake rollers, clamps or tension-adjusting mechanisms that stop or regulate ribbon feed.
[B41J 33/54] for ensuring maximum life of the ribbon -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Slow, e.g. "creep", feed mechanisms -> B41J33/38
[B41J 33/56] Ribbon adjusted transversely -- B41J 33/56 covers devices in typewriter printing systems that adjust the ink-ribbon or similar impression-transfer material transversely across the page. It includes mechanisms that shift the ribbon side-to-side to align printed characters with the desired line or column, ensuring proper ink transfer during operation.
[B41J 33/58] Ribbon fed angularly -- The subclass covers typewriter mechanisms that feed ink ribbons or similar impression-transfer material to the printing point at an angle, using arrangements that guide the ribbon angularly to achieve consistent character impressions.
[B41J 3/36] for portability -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tape printers; label printers -> B41J3/4075
[B41J 33/60] responsive to telegraph code or other extraneous signals -- B41J 33/60 covers typewriter mechanisms that feed ink ribbons or similar impression-transfer material in response to external signals such as telegraph code. The provision enables automatic ribbon advancement or selection based on received data, allowing the machine to print characters directly from communications inputs.
[B41J 3/365] Toy typewriters  toy imitations of typewriters  A63H33/3077 -- B41J 3/365 covers toy typewriters, i.e., non-functional or simplified replicas of typewriters intended for play, training or display. These devices imitate the appearance and basic operation of a typewriter but are not designed for real typing work. The classification groups them under printing equipment in the performing-operations section.
[B41J 3/37] Foldable typewriters -- B41J 3/37 covers typewriters that can be folded or collapsed for compact storage or transport, typically featuring hinges or telescoping frames that let the typing mechanism and carriage be reduced in size. The classification applies to portable or handheld models designed to retain full typing functionality while providing a smaller footprint when not in use.
[B41J 3/38] for embossing, e.g. for making matrices for stereotypes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Marking devices having a set of marking elements, for example a set of numerals, wherein each marking element can be selected and used independently from the others in order to carry out the impression operation.
[B41J 3/382] of tapes, e.g. tape cartridges -- This subclass covers typewriter or selective printing mechanisms that use tapes, including tape cartridges, as the medium for storing or delivering characters, symbols or markings to the printing element. It pertains to the feed, handling and positioning of such tapes within the device.
[B41J 3/385] of plates, e.g. metal plates, plastic cards -- The B41J 3/385 subgroup relates to typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are designed to print on plates such as metal plates or plastic cards, typically for imprinting identifying marks, information or images directly onto the plate surface. It covers devices whose purpose is the direct marking of these rigid substrates rather than paper.
[B41J 3/387] with automatic plate transport systems, e.g. for credit cards -- B41J 3/387 pertains to typewriter-style printing or marking devices that incorporate an automatic plate transport system, for example machines that feed and print on credit-card sized plates. The subclass covers mechanisms where the plate moves automatically through the device to apply the intended imprint.
[B41J 3/39] hand-held -- The B41J 3/39 subgroup relates to hand-held typewriters and other selective printing or marking devices that are designed to be operated manually while being held in the user's hand, distinguishing them from larger, stationary or desk-mounted machines.
[B41J 3/407] for marking on special material -- The subclass B41J 3/407 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that are specially constructed to mark on particular or unconventional materials, such as fabrics, plastics, metal sheets or other non-standard substrates, rather than ordinary paper. It addresses the design features and processes enabling effective imprinting on these special media.
[B41J 3/4071] Printing on disk-shaped media, e.g. CDs -- B41J 3/4071 covers devices and methods for applying printable images or data to disc-shaped media such as compact discs, DVDs or Blu-ray discs. It relates to typewriter-like or selective marking mechanisms that are specifically built to record information on rotating, circular substrates.
[B41J 3/4073] Printing on three-dimensional objects not being in sheet or web form, e.g. spherical or cubic objects  B41J3/283 ,  B41J3/286  take precedence; building up a 3D object using individual droplets from jetting heads  B29C64/112 -- B41J 3/4073 pertains to the printing on three-dimensional objects that are not in sheet or web form, such as spherical or cubic items. This classification excludes processes specifically covered under B41J3/283 and B41J3/286, focusing instead on techniques for printing on irregular or non-planar surfaces.
[B41J 3/40731] Holders for objects, e. g. holders specially adapted to the shape of the object to be printed or adapted to hold several objects -- This subclass covers devices that retain the workpiece in a typewriter or similar printing mechanism, such as specially shaped trays or clamps designed to fit a single object or to accommodate multiple items simultaneously. It includes holders tailored to the geometry of the object to be printed.
[B41J 3/40733] Printing on cylindrical or rotationally symmetrical objects, e. g. on bottles -- The CPC subclass B41J 3/40733 pertains to typewriter-style or selective printing mechanisms designed to apply ink or markings onto objects that are cylindrical or have rotational symmetry, such as bottles. It covers apparatuses and methods for positioning, transferring and fixing the print on these curved surfaces.
[B41J 3/4075] Tape printers; Label printers -- Tape printers and label printers are typewriter-like devices that produce characters or graphics on narrow media such as adhesive labels or continuous tape, typically using impact or ink-transfer technology. They are designed specifically for marking, identification or receipt printing where compact, portable output is required.
[B41J 3/4076] printing on rewritable, bistable "electronic paper" by a focused electric or magnetic field -- **Glossary:** - Electrochromism means: the phenomenon displayed by some chemical species of reversibly changing colour when a burst of charge is applied (adjective: electrochromic).
[B41J 3/4078] Printing on textile -- B41J 3/4078 pertains to typewriters and selective printing mechanisms designed specifically for printing on textiles. This classification covers the technical aspects and innovations related to direct printing processes onto fabric or other textile materials, ensuring precision and adaptability in various textile applications.
[B41J 3/413] for metal -- Typewriters or selective printing mechanisms designed specifically for marking metal surfaces, such as metal sheets, plates or components, using methods suited to the hardness and properties of metal. This sub-class covers devices whose purpose is to produce characters or marks directly on metal substrates.
[B41J 3/42] Two or more complete typewriters coupled for simultaneous operation -- This subclass covers arrangements in which two or more complete typewriter mechanisms are mechanically linked to operate at the same time, allowing simultaneous typing on multiple devices for synchronized printing or duplication.
[B41J 3/44] Typewriters or selective printing mechanisms having dual functions or combined with, or coupled to, apparatus performing other functions -- B41J 3/44 covers typewriters or selective printing mechanisms that provide two functions or are integrated with other devices, such as combining typing with printing, embossing, labeling or data entry functions, or being mechanically coupled to equipment that performs a separate operation. The classification groups machines designed to carry out dual-purpose or multifunctional printing tasks.
[B41J 3/445] Printers integrated in other types of apparatus, e.g. printers integrated in cameras -- The code B41J 3/445 covers printers that are built into other devices, such as a printer incorporated within a camera or similar apparatus. It falls under the typewriter and printing hierarchy and focuses on integrated printing mechanisms designed for the specific function of the host equipment.
[B41J 3/46] Printing mechanisms combined with apparatus providing a visual indication -- B41J 3/46 covers typewriters and similar printing devices that incorporate a visual indication system, such as a display or window, which shows the characters to be printed or the printing action. The classification addresses mechanisms where the printing function is integrated with a visual feedback component, enabling the operator to see the output before or during printing.
[B41J 3/50] Mechanisms producing characters by printing and also producing a record by other means -- A typewriter-type device that prints characters on paper while simultaneously generating a complementary record through a different medium, such as inked ribbons, magnetic tape, or electronic storage, falls under CPC B41J 3/50. It covers mechanisms that combine traditional printing with alternative recording methods.
[B41J 35/00] Other apparatus or arrangements associated with, or incorporated in, ink-ribbon mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Impression transfer ribbon material.  Also covering page-width impression transfer material in the sub-groups if B41J17/00 does not provide an appropriate entry.  In general the heating or pre-heating of ink ribbons.
[B41J 35/02] Frames or holders for unwound short lengths of ink ribbons -- The classification B41J 35/02 covers frames or holders designed to support short, unwound lengths of ink ribbon in typewriter printing apparatus. These components keep the ribbon tensioned and positioned for printing and are part of the broader group for typewriter ink-ribbon mechanisms.
[B41J 35/03] the holder being movable to inoperative position, e.g. by swinging upwardly -- B41J 35/03 covers typewriter printing devices in which the ink-ribbon holder can be shifted to a non-working position, typically by swinging it upward, to allow ribbon replacement, maintenance or to prevent printing. The code relates to auxiliary arrangements incorporated in ink-ribbon mechanisms that facilitate this movable holder function.
[B41J 35/04] Ink-ribbon guides -- B41J 35/04 refers to guides for ink ribbons used in typewriter printing devices. These components direct or support the ribbon within the ink-ribbon mechanism to ensure correct positioning during operation.
[B41J 35/06] stationary -- B41J 35/06 refers to stationary components that are part of or attached to the ink-ribbon mechanism of a typewriter, such as fixed guides, supports or housings that keep the ribbon in place during printing.
[B41J 35/08] with tensioning arrangements -- The subclass B41J 35/08 covers typewriter or similar printing devices that include arrangements for tensioning the ink-ribbon, such as mechanisms that keep the ribbon taut during operation. It relates to other apparatus or arrangements incorporated in ink-ribbon systems within the broader category of printing typewriters.
[B41J 3/51] the printed and recorded information being identical -- B41J 3/51 concerns typewriters or selective printing mechanisms in which the information printed on the medium is exactly the same as the information recorded or stored by the device, meaning the output reproduces the input without alteration. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters.
[B41J 35/10] Vibrator mechanisms -- B41J 35/10 pertains to vibrator mechanisms incorporated in ink-ribbon systems of typewriters, i.e., devices that produce oscillatory motion to enhance ink transfer onto the paper. These mechanisms fall under the broader category of apparatus or arrangements associated with ink-ribbon mechanisms in printing typewriters.
[B41J 35/12] adjustable, e.g. for case shift -- B41J 35/12 relates to typewriter mechanisms that provide adjustability for case-shifting functions, especially those incorporated in or associated with ink-ribbon arrangements. It includes devices that enable the user to change the position or timing of the shift to produce upper-case or lower-case characters.
[B41J 35/14] for multicolour work -- B41J 35/14 covers typewriter or similar printing devices that use ink-ribbon mechanisms designed to produce multicolour output, including arrangements that enable different colours to be selected or applied during a single printing operation. It relates to the integration of colour-changing or multi-ribbon systems within the printer's mechanical structure.
[B41J 35/16] Multicolour arrangements -- The class B41J 35/16 concerns typewriter-type printing devices that incorporate ink-ribbon mechanisms capable of producing more than one colour. It covers arrangements such as multi-colour ribbon cartridges, colour-selection devices and other apparatus that enable the printer to switch between or combine different ink colours during operation.
[B41J 35/18] Colour change effected automatically -- The B41J 35/18 subclass covers devices that automatically change the colour of ink in typewriter ribbon mechanisms, usually by switching between separate colour sections or employing pre-programmed sequences. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters, and relates specifically to apparatus integrated with or associated with ink-ribbon systems.
[B41J 35/20] Ink-ribbon shifts, e.g. for exposing print, for case-shift adjustment, for rendering ink ribbon inoperative -- The B41J 35/20 classification covers devices that move the ink ribbon in typewriters, such as mechanisms for exposing the print area, adjusting case shift, or deliberately disabling the ribbon. It relates to apparatus or arrangements incorporated in ink-ribbon systems.
[B41J 35/22] Mechanisms permitting the selective use of a plurality of ink ribbons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Use of correcting ribbons.
[B41J 35/23] with two or more ribbon guides -- B41J 35/23 covers typewriter mechanisms that incorporate two or more ribbon guides within the ink-ribbon system, providing improved ribbon alignment or tension control. These arrangements are part of the broader category of printing apparatus involving typewriters and ribbon mechanisms.
[B41J 35/24] Mechanisms specially adapted for feeding impression-transfer materials of foil form -- Mechanisms specially adapted for feeding foil impression-transfer materials in typewriter-type printing devices, such as those that advance and position thin foil ribbons or foils for impact printing. These mechanisms are part of the foil-feed system and fall under CPC class B41J for typewriters.
[B41J 35/26] Ink-ribbon shields or backings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Avoiding "ghost printing"
[B41J 35/28] Detachable carriers or holders for ink-ribbon mechanisms -- This subclass covers devices that hold or carry ink ribbons and can be removed from a typewriter or similar printing machine. It includes detachable cartridges, reels or frames designed to facilitate ribbon replacement or interchange. The classification is applied to patents describing such detachable ribbon-holding arrangements.
[B41J 35/30] Manifolding or like arrangements -- B41J 35/30 covers manifolding or similar arrangements used in typewriter printing mechanisms, specifically devices that shape, fold or direct the ink-ribbon or related components to control the print line. These structures are part of the auxiliary apparatus associated with ink-ribbon mechanisms, enabling precise positioning or handling of the ribbon during operation.
[B41J 35/32] for producing a plurality of copies along the printing line by a single ink ribbon -- The B41J 35/32 subclass covers devices that generate several copies simultaneously along a printing line by using a single ink ribbon, typically applied to typewriter-type printing mechanisms. It relates to arrangements that incorporate or are associated with ink-ribbon systems to produce multiple impressions in one pass.
[B41J 35/34] using a plurality of separate ink ribbons, e.g. including one hectographic ink ribbon -- B41J 35/34 covers typewriter or similar printing devices that employ multiple separate ink ribbons, such as a standard ribbon together with a hectographic ink ribbon, to provide different colors or functions. The classification relates to the arrangement of ink-ribbon mechanisms within the printing apparatus.
[B41J 35/35] using unwound short lengths of ink ribbons -- The subclass covers typewriter or printing mechanisms that use short, pre-cut lengths of ink ribbon instead of a continuously wound ribbon, enabling simple replacement, reduced waste and compact ribbon storage within the device.
[B41J 35/36] Alarms, indicators, or feed disabling devices responsive to ink ribbon breakage or exhaustion -- A B41J 35/36 device for typewriters monitors the ink-ribbon condition and, when a break or exhaustion is detected, activates an alarm, visual indicator, or disables the paper-feed mechanism to prevent further printing.
[B41J 35/38] Feeding the ink ribbon to waste after use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. deleting the printed text from the ribbon or making the text illegible.
[B41J 3/54] with two or more sets of type or printing elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Selective printing or marking mechanisms with two or more sets or types of printing elements  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Selective printing mechanisms for printing on both faces of the printing material -> B41J3/60
[B41J 3/543] with multiple inkjet print heads  B41J2/17503 ,  B41J2/2103  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ink cartridges; ink cartridges mounted on a common carriage -> B41J2/17503 - Construction of print heads for multi-colour ink-jet printing; multi-colour print heads mounted on a common carriage -> B41J2/2103
[B41J 3/546] Combination of different types, e.g. using a thermal transfer head and an inkjet print head -- B41J 3/546 covers typewriter or selective printing mechanisms that combine multiple printing technologies, such as a thermal transfer head together with an ink-jet print head, to achieve hybrid marking.
[B41J 3/60] for printing on both faces of the printing material -- B41J 3/60 covers typewriter or similar printing mechanisms that are built to print on both faces of a sheet in a single operation, providing double-sided output for forms, labels or stationery. It is classified under Performing Operations → Printing → Typewriters in the CPC hierarchy.
[B41J 3/62] for printing on two or more separate sheets or strips of printing material  -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The cases of two or more cut sheets or at least one cut sheet and at least one strip of printing material that are conveyed simultaneously to or through the printing zone.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Selective printing mechanisms with two or more sets or types of printing elements -> B41J3/54
[B41J 5/00] Common details or accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods or arrangements for sensing record carriers: -> G06K7/00
[B41J 5/02] Character or syllable selected by setting an index -- B41J 5/02 covers typewriter mechanisms in which the operator selects a specific character or group of characters by positioning an index, such as a lever, knob or rotating disc, that aligns the desired type element with the printing point. This subclass pertains to the selection process rather than the actual striking or ink transfer.
[B41J 5/04] Single-character selection -- B41J 5/04 covers typewriter mechanisms that select a single character for printing, including keyboards, levers or selectors that cause one specific character to be struck. The provision relates to common accessories that enable individual character selection in printing operations.
[B41J 5/06] Multiple-character selection -- B41J 5/06 covers typewriter mechanisms that enable the simultaneous selection of more than one character for printing, such as keys or devices that produce ligatures, combined symbols or pre-set character strings in a single operation, together with related accessories that facilitate this function.
[B41J 5/08] Character or syllable selected by means of keys or keyboards of the typewriter type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Keyboards for type-writers.
[B41J 5/10] Arrangements of keyboards -- B41J 5/10 pertains to the layout and organization of keyboards on typewriters, covering the spatial arrangement of keys, grouping of characters, and ergonomic or functional configurations designed to optimize typing performance and user comfort.
[B41J 5/102] Keyboard overlays -- Keyboard overlays are removable covers that fit over the keys of typewriter or printing keyboards to provide alternate labeling, protection or ergonomic features. They may be made of thin plastic or printable material and can display language-specific characters, special functions or tactile guides. The CPC entry B41J 5/102 groups such accessories for typewriter-type input devices.
[B41J 5/105] Constructional details of keyboard frames, e.g. adjusting or fixation means -- This subclass covers structural features of typewriter keyboard frames, such as mechanisms for adjusting the position or securing the frame to a chassis. It includes designs for brackets, bolts, clamps and other fixation devices that enable alignment, tension adjustment or modular assembly.
[B41J 5/107] for special purposes, e.g. Braille, Chinese, multi-language options -- B41J 5/107 relates to typewriter mechanisms designed for special purposes such as Braille output, Chinese character printing or multi-language capabilities. These devices incorporate specific keyboards, typebars or electronic interfaces to handle non-Latin scripts or tactile writing systems.
[B41J 5/12] Construction of key buttons -- B41J 5/12 covers the construction of key buttons for typewriters, detailing the design, materials, shaping and attachment methods of the individual keys and their caps. It concerns the mechanical and ergonomic aspects of key elements that enable the printing operation in typewriter devices.
[B41J 5/14] Construction of key levers -- B41J 5/14 relates to the design and assembly of the key levers of typewriters, covering their shape, mounting method and mechanical integration with the keyboard. It is classified under common details or accessories within the typewriters subclass of printing operations.
[B41J 5/16] Mounting or connecting key buttons on or to key levers -- B41J 5/16 refers to mounting or connecting key buttons on or to key levers in typewriters. This code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system. It falls under printing and typewriters.
[B41J 5/18] Locks -- The CPC subclass B41J 5/18 relates to locking devices used in typewriters, covering mechanisms that secure the keyboard, carriage, platen or paper feed to prevent unauthorized operation or to hold parts in a fixed position during typing, maintenance or transport.
[B41J 5/20] for subsidiary keys, e.g. for shift keys -- This subclass covers devices attached to typewriters that provide subsidiary keys, such as shift keys, which modify the function of the main keys for operations like case shifting.
[B41J 5/22] Interlocks between keys, e.g. without detent arrangements -- Interlock mechanisms that connect typewriter keys, preventing simultaneous or inappropriate key actuation, often by linking the movement of one key to another without using tactile detent features. This includes mechanical or electronic arrangements that coordinate key operation for reliable printing.
[B41J 5/24] with detent arrangements -- B41J 5/24 relates to typewriters that incorporate detent arrangements, i.e., mechanical stops or holding devices that engage when a key is pressed to secure the type element in a set position. These detents are used to control spacing, alignment or to lock moving parts during operation, and are considered common accessories for typewriter mechanisms.
[B41J 5/26] Regulating touch, key dip or stroke, or the like -- The subclass B41J 5/26 covers features that regulate the tactile response of a typewriter, such as the touch pressure, key dip, stroke length or similar characteristics of key operation. It pertains to mechanisms that control how a key is actuated during printing.
[B41J 5/28] Multiple-action keys, e.g. keys depressed by two or more amounts or movable in two or more directions to effect different functions or selections -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. repeat actions for type-writers.
[B41J 5/30] Character or syllable selection controlled by recorded information -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 5/31] characterised by form of recorded information -- B41J 5/31 refers to typewriters and related accessories where the defining feature is the form of the recorded information, such as specific character shapes, fonts, symbols or numeric/ alphanumeric layouts. It covers devices distinguished primarily by how the printed output is formatted.
[B41J 5/32] by printed, embossed, or photographic records, e.g. cards, sheets -- B41J 5/32 covers typewriter mechanisms and accessories designed to work on pre-printed, embossed or photographic media such as cards and sheets. It includes devices that transfer or modify information on these records, for example by imprinting, marking or embossing them.
[B41J 5/34] by strips or tapes -- B41J 5/34 covers typewriter, printer or similar devices that use strips or tapes as the means of supplying ink, characters or other printing material, including ribbon strips, tape cartridges and related feeding mechanisms.
[B41J 5/36] by punched records, e.g. cards, sheets -- B41J 5/36 covers typewriter printing operations in which the characters to be printed are taken from punched records such as cards or sheets, the punches determining the sequence of letters that the machine reproduces. This sub-class falls under printing by typewriters within the broader group of performing operations.
[B41J 5/38] by strips or tapes -- B41J 5/38 covers typewriter or printing mechanisms that produce characters using strips or tapes, such as daisy-wheel or tape-based type elements. The classification includes devices where the print media itself carries the character impressions and related accessories.
[B41J 5/40] by magnetic or electrostatic records, e.g. cards, sheets -- B41J 5/40 includes typewriter or printing mechanisms that produce output on magnetic or electrostatic recording media such as magnetic cards, magnetic strips, or electrostatic sheets. The classification covers the feeding, positioning, impression and handling of these media for data storage or printing purposes.
[B41J 5/42] by strips or tapes -- This subclass relates to typewriter printing methods that form characters by feeding pre-printed paper strips or continuous tape, and includes the associated mechanisms and accessories used for such strip- or tape-based output.
[B41J 5/44] characterised by storage of recorded information -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. font or memory cartridges.
[B41J 5/46] on internal storages -- The CPC subclass B41J 5/46 covers typewriter mechanisms that involve internal storage devices, such as magazines or cartridges that hold printable characters, type elements, or ink ribbons within the machine. It pertains to designs where the storage is built into the typewriter rather than being external, addressing how the stored media are positioned, fed, or replaced during printing operations.
[B41J 5/48] on external storages -- The B41J 5/48 subclass concerns external storage devices associated with typewriter printing mechanisms. It includes any detachable or connected media such as memory modules, magnetic cards, disks or tapes used to store typed data, fonts, settings or program information for the typewriter.
[B41J 5/50] on a single storage -- B41J 5/50 covers typewriter or similar printing mechanisms that use a single storage element for the character set, such as one typewheel, drum or rotating disc holding all symbols. The classification applies to devices where each character is selected from that sole storage unit during the printing operation.
[B41J 5/51] on more than one separate storage, e.g. on additional correction strips or tapes -- This subclass covers typewriter accessories that supply correction media from multiple separate storage units, such as additional correction strips or tapes, rather than a single integrated source. It relates to devices that manage or dispense more than one cartridge or strip to provide repeated correction capability during typing.
[B41J 5/52] characterised by the provision of additional devices for producing a punched or like record, e.g. simultaneously -- B41J 5/52 covers typewriter accessories that include extra mechanisms for creating a punched or similar record while the device is operated, such as simultaneous punching of paper tape or cards during typing.
[B41J 7/00] Type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Index setting -> B41J5/02
[B41J 7/005] Type-selecting actions or mechanisms by unusual means, e.g. for use by physically disabled persons -- The B41J 7/005 subclass covers type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms that employ unconventional methods, such as adaptations for users with physical impairments. It falls under the broader categories of printing and typewriters, focusing on specialized designs that enable disabled persons to operate typewriters more easily.
[B41J 7/02] Type-lever actuating mechanisms -- The B41J 7/02 subclass covers mechanisms that actuate the type lever in a typewriter, converting user input into the selection and striking of individual characters. It includes levers, cams, linkages and other devices that move the type element to the correct position for printing.
[B41J 7/04] Levers mounted on fixed pivots -- B41J 7/04 covers type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms in typewriters that use levers mounted on fixed pivots to move typebars or type elements. The classification includes designs where the lever's rotation about a stationary axis initiates the striking action, often integrated with shift or spacing systems.
[B41J 7/06] and connected to transmission members, e.g. toothed gearing -- The subclass B41J 7/06 covers type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms in typewriters that are linked to transmission elements such as toothed gears. It includes arrangements where the movement of type bars or levers is transmitted via gear trains to achieve the desired character selection. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriter mechanisms.
[B41J 7/08] with pin-and-slot or like loose connections -- B41J 7/08 concerns mechanisms in typewriters that select or actuate type elements using pin-and-slot or comparable loose-connection arrangements. The classification covers designs where pins engage slots to position or release type elements for printing, enabling rapid type selection without rigid mounting. These features fall within the broader category of printing operations in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B41J 7/10] Chain, belt, flexible cable, or like members -- B41J 7/10 covers the use of chains, belts, flexible cables or similar members as transmission elements in typewriter type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms, enabling movement of type bars or other printing components.
[B41J 7/12] U-shaped type-lever on two pivots -- B41J 7/12 pertains to type-selecting mechanisms in typewriters that use a U-shaped lever mounted on two pivots to actuate the appropriate type element. The lever's dual-pivot arrangement transfers the operator's motion to the selected type-bar, enabling precise character imprinting.
[B41J 7/14] Single key-and-type lever -- A single key-and-type lever combines the selection of a character and the striking action in one movable part, so pressing the lever both positions the desired type element and initiates the imprint on the paper. This integrated mechanism simplifies the operation of typewriters and reduces the number of separate components needed for typing.
[B41J 7/16] Type-head pivoted to or rotating on lever -- The B41J 7/16 subclass covers typewriters whose type-heads are attached to a lever that pivots or rotates to bring the character into striking position. This mechanism forms part of the type-selecting system, allowing the individual type element to move via the lever before contacting the inked ribbon and paper.
[B41J 7/18] Levers having moving or variable fulcra to alter the mechanical advantage during the stroke -- B41J 7/18 relates to typewriter mechanisms in which levers have movable or adjustable fulcra that modify the mechanical advantage during the stroke, enabling variable force transmission as the key is pressed.
[B41J 7/20] Levers having moving pivots fixed relative to the lever -- In typewriter mechanisms this subclass includes levers whose pivot points can move while being fixed to the lever body, allowing the lever to alter its motion path yet retain a constant geometric relationship. Such designs are employed for type-selecting or type-actuating functions and improve typing precision.
[B41J 7/22] Type-baskets -- B41J 7/22 covers the type-basket assemblies in typewriters that store, organize and feed individual type slugs to the striking mechanism. It relates to type-selecting or type-actuating systems that move selected characters from the basket to the printing point. The code falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → PRINTING → TYPEWRITERS.
[B41J 7/24] Construction of type-levers -- The entry covers mechanisms in typewriters that select or actuate individual type elements, focusing on the design and construction of the type-lever assemblies that transfer keystrokes to the printing point. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B41J 7/26] Special means, e.g. repulsers, for ensuring return of type- levers -- B41J 7/26 covers mechanisms in typewriter printing that use special devices such as repellers to guarantee the return of type levers after a strike. These assemblies improve reliability and speed by actively resetting the type element to its original position.
[B41J 7/28] Key lever and type member returned independently to rest position -- The B41J 7/28 entry covers a typewriter mechanism in which the key lever and the type-slug (type member) are returned to their rest positions separately after a character is struck. This independent return allows the key to reset without moving the type slug, improving rapid successive typing and reducing wear on the actuation linkage. It falls under type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms within the printing-operations hierarchy.
[B41J 7/30] Preventing rebound or clash of levers or type members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. print sequence control for type-writers.
[B41J 7/32] Type-face selected by operation of sliding members -- This subclass covers type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms in typewriters where the desired type-face is chosen by moving a sliding member that aligns the appropriate character for printing. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under performing operations, printing, typewriters.
[B41J 7/34] Type-face selected by operation of rotary members -- B41J 7/34 pertains to typewriters and the mechanisms that select a typeface by means of rotary components. It covers systems where rotating members, such as wheels or drums, position the desired character for printing, rather than manual key actuation. This subclass falls under printing operations within the broader performing operations group.
[B41J 7/36] Selecting arrangements applied to type-carriers rotating during impression -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. carrousel type carriers for type-writers.
[B41J 7/38] Type movable on carrier for selection -- B41J 7/38 classifies typewriter mechanisms in which the individual type characters are mounted on a movable carrier that can be positioned for selection and striking, covering the type-selecting or type-actuating elements used in printing operations.
[B41J 7/40] Type movable on carrier for impression -- This subclass covers type elements that are mounted on a movable carrier and are displaced to strike the printing surface, such as typebars, type baskets or interchangeable type sleeves that travel to create an impression. It includes mechanisms for moving the type together with its carrier during printing.
[B41J 7/42] Timed impression, e.g. without impact -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. accelerated or delayed impression for type-writers.
[B41J 7/44] with impact -- B41J 7/44 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to typewriters with type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms that operate through an impact method, distinguishing it from non-impact printing techniques.
[B41J 7/46] Rolling contact during impression -- This subclass covers typewriter mechanisms in which the type element contacts the paper while rolling, such as drum or roller typebars that rotate to form the impression. It relates specifically to rolling contact during the impression phase of printing.
[B41J 7/48] Type carrier arrested in selected position by electromagnetic means -- B41J 7/48 covers type-writer mechanisms that keep the type carrier locked in a chosen position by using electromagnetic devices, enabling precise selection of characters during printing. It falls within the printing category under type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms.
[B41J 7/50] Type-face selected by combinations of two movements of type carrier -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. selection over the shortest rotation in type-writers.
[B41J 7/52] by combined rotary and sliding movement -- The CPC entry B41J 7/52 covers type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms in typewriters that use a combined rotary and sliding movement to position the type element, enabling characters to be printed through a hybrid motion of turning and linear travel.
[B41J 7/54] Selecting arrangements including combinations, permutation, summation, or aggregation means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.
[B41J 7/56] Summation devices for mechanical movements -- B41J 7/56 relates to devices that sum or combine several mechanical movements into a single action within type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms, such as the timing or linkage systems used in typewriters to control character selection.
[B41J 7/58] Wedges -- The CPC entry B41J 7/58 covers wedge-based mechanisms used in type-selecting or type-actuating systems of typewriters. It relates to devices where a wedge translates motion to position or fire the appropriate type element, enabling the selection of characters during printing.
[B41J 7/60] Levers -- B41J 7/60 covers levers used in the type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms of typewriters. It includes any lever that transfers motion to typebars, keys, or other actuating elements such as shift, escapement or return levers, regardless of shape or mounting.
[B41J 7/62] Gearing -- The B41J 7/62 subclass concerns gear mechanisms employed in type-selecting or type-actuating systems of typewriters, defining how gears transmit motion to position and strike type elements accurately during printing. It addresses the design, arrangement and interaction of gears that control type selection and timing within the broader printing operation.
[B41J 7/64] Pulley and strand mechanism -- B41J 7/64 covers pulley and strand mechanisms used in type-selecting or type-actuating systems for typewriters, allowing rapid and precise positioning of type elements for printing. The arrangement typically links a rotating drum or lever to a string or belt that moves the chosen character into place.
[B41J 7/66] Movable members, e.g. pins, displaceable according to a code -- B41J 7/66 covers typewriter mechanisms that move pins or similar members according to a coded pattern to select characters for printing. The classification pertains to type-selecting or type-actuating devices that displace these elements based on encoded instructions, enabling the correct letter or symbol to strike the paper.
[B41J 7/68] with means for selectively closing an electric circuit for type presentation -- This subclass covers typewriter mechanisms that select and actuate type elements using a system that can close an electric circuit only for the chosen character. The electrical control is used to present the specific type to the printing point.
[B41J 7/90] Syllable, line, or like type selection -- The B41J 7/90 subclass covers mechanisms in typewriters that select groups of characters such as syllables, whole lines or other predefined blocks for printing, rather than individual keys. It includes arrangements that actuate multiple type elements simultaneously to produce pre-set text strings.
[B41J 7/92] Impact adjustment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type-writers only.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For deciding or adjusting hammer-firing time -> B41J9/46 - For deciding or adjusting hammer- drive energy -> B41J9/48
[B41J 7/94] Character-by-character adjustment -- This CPC subclass covers mechanisms that adjust the position or spacing of each printed character individually, allowing fine control of character alignment in typewriters. It falls under type-selecting or type-actuating mechanisms within the printing group of performing operations.
[B41J 7/96] Means checking correctness of setting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. control of selected characters for type-writers
[B41J 9/00] Hammer-impression mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[B41J 9/02] Hammers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General features.
[B41J 9/04] of single hammers, e.g. travelling along printing line -- B41J 9/04 relates to typewriter printing mechanisms that employ a single hammer which travels along the print line to strike the character ribbon. This arrangement replaces the usual array of fixed hammers with a moving hammer that forms each character in turn.
[B41J 9/06] of stationary hammers, e.g. engaging a single type-carrier -- This subclass covers typewriter mechanisms that use stationary hammers to strike a single type-carrier, producing printed characters without the hammer moving laterally. It includes designs where a fixed hammer is engaged by a lever or other actuation means to emboss the type.
[B41J 9/08] engaging more than one type-carrier -- A type-writer or printing mechanism that simultaneously engages more than one type-carrier, such as multiple ribbons, typebars or type-faces, to produce several characters, colors or effects with a single hammer-stroke. This approach enables multi-character printing, dual-color output or combined imprinting in one operation.
[B41J 9/10] of more than one hammer, e.g. one for each character position -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hammer banks.
[B41J 9/12] each operating in more than one character position -- The B41J 9/12 subclass covers hammer-impression mechanisms in typewriters that are capable of operating in more than one character position. It includes designs where a single hammer can strike different column locations, allowing a single printing element to serve multiple characters or fonts within the same machine.
[B41J 9/127] Mounting of hammers -- The B41J 9/127 subclass covers the ways in which hammers are attached, supported or positioned within type-writer mechanisms. It includes designs for securing the hammer shaft, adjusting its alignment, and methods of fixing the hammer to the printing assembly.
[B41J 9/133] Construction of hammer body or tip -- B41J 9/133 relates to the design and manufacture of the hammer body or its striking tip in typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms. The entry addresses structural features that influence the hammer's durability, weight and impact characteristics for printing characters.
[B41J 9/14] Means for selecting or suppressing individual hammers -- The B41J 9/14 subclass covers mechanisms in typewriter-style printing devices that enable the selection or inhibition of individual hammer strikes, such as key-selection circuits, interlocks, or suppressor devices that control which hammers fire during a print cycle.
[B41J 9/16] Means for cocking or resetting hammers -- B41J 9/16 covers devices that cock or reset the striking hammers of typewriter printing mechanisms. The class includes levers, springs, cams or other arrangements that return the hammer to its ready position after an impression, ensuring rapid successive typing.
[B41J 9/18] Cams -- Cams in typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms are rotating or oscillating components that transform rotary motion into the precise linear motion required to drive the printing hammer. Their shaped profiles control the timing and force of each key strike, ensuring accurate character imprint on paper.
[B41J 9/20] Springs -- B41J 9/20 relates to springs used in the hammer-impression mechanisms of typewriters. It covers coil, leaf or other elastic springs that return the hammer after impact or provide the striking force needed for printing.
[B41J 9/22] Fluid-pressure means -- B41J 9/22 covers the use of fluid-pressure devices, such as pneumatic or hydraulic actuators, to drive the hammer-impression mechanism in typewriters. It classifies the means by which pressurized fluid is employed to generate the striking force that forms characters on paper. This subcategory falls under printing typewriter mechanisms within the broader performing operations group.
[B41J 9/24] Electromagnetic means -- B41J 9/24 designates typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms that are powered by electromagnetic means, covering systems where an electric coil or similar device drives the hammer to strike the paper. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and typewriters.
[B41J 9/26] Means for operating hammers to effect impression -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanisms for quiet printing, mechanical means for print density adjustment.
[B41J 9/28] Cams -- B41J 9/28 covers cams used in typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms, which transfer motion to drive the hammer for printing characters. The code falls under CPC section B41J for printing typewriters, specifically the hammer-impression subgroup.
[B41J 9/30] Springs -- B41J 9/30 covers the use of springs in hammer-impression mechanisms of typewriters, a subclass of printing equipment. The springs provide the return force that drives the hammer back after striking the inked ribbon and paper. This classification falls under performing operations - printing - typewriters.
