[B41J 9/32] arranged to be clutched to snatch roll -- The subclass B41J 9/32 concerns typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms that are arranged to be clutched to a snatch roll, the component that intermittently pulls the paper forward. It covers designs where the hammer's motion is directly linked to the roll's clutching action to ensure synchronized striking and paper feed.
[B41J 9/34] Fluid-pressure means -- B41J 9/34 covers typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms that employ fluid-pressure devices, such as pneumatic or hydraulic systems, to generate the striking force for printing characters, distinguishing them from purely mechanical spring or lever arrangements.
[B41J 9/36] in which mechanical power is applied under electromagnetic control -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preloaded springs.
[B41J 9/38] Electromagnetic means -- The B41J 9/38 subclass concerns typewriter hammer-impression mechanisms that are driven by electromagnetic actuators such as solenoids or coils. It covers designs in which an electric current creates a magnetic field to strike the type-bars, replacing purely mechanical or pneumatic hammer actions. This classification groups typewriters that use electromagnetic means to perform the printing operation.
[B41J 9/40] including an electro-adhesive clutch -- B41J 9/40 covers hammer-impression mechanisms for typewriter printing that incorporate an electro-adhesive clutch. The clutch uses electrically activated adhesive material to engage or disengage the hammer drive, allowing precise control of impact force and timing during the printing process. This sub-class falls under printing-typewriter operations in the CPC.
[B41J 9/42] with anti-rebound arrangements -- B41J 9/42 covers typewriters that use hammer-impression mechanisms equipped with anti-rebound arrangements, which limit the hammer's bounce after striking the ribbon and paper. Such features improve print consistency and reduce ink smearing.
[B41J 9/44] Control for hammer-impression mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Circuits.  General features.  Noise reduction.
[B41J 9/46] for deciding or adjusting hammer-firing time -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flight time adjustment.  Print intensity adjustment by flight time control.
[B41J 9/48] for deciding or adjusting hammer-drive energy -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Print intensity adjustment by drive energy control.
[B41J 9/50] for compensating for the variations of printer drive conditions, e.g. for compensating for the variation of temperature or current supply -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Also compensating for source voltage variations, paper feed variations.
[B41J 9/52] for checking the operation of print hammers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In general: limiting the number of fired hammers.  Detection of the impact.  Preventing overheating.
[B41J 9/54] for checking the breakage of print hammers -- B41J 9/54 covers devices or methods that detect when a print hammer in a typewriter has broken, enabling the machine to halt operation or alert the operator. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, printing, typewriters, hammer-impression mechanisms. The subclass specifically addresses breakage-checking systems for print hammers.
[B41K] STAMPS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hand-operated devices and machines always including a static stamping element carrying a graphical and/or numerical design, e.g. a single letter or number in the form of a raised part forming the stamping image. This raised part has usually elastic properties, e.g. it comprises mainly an elastomeric material. After applying ink on the surface of said stamping form, said inked image is applied by contact pressure onto the surface of a substrate of any dimension, which will form a stamped subject-matter, portable devices or machines as a whole as well as all parts, details and objects specifically adapted for use in such devices or machines, e.g.  feeding and/or handling of substrates to be stamped upon is covered from the in-feed device through the whole machine down to the delivery device.  **Glossary:** - Stamping: always including use of ink or colour
[B41K 1/00] Portable hand-operated devices without means for supporting or locating the articles to be stamped, i.e. hand stamps -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41K 1/003] combined with other articles  combination of writing appliances with stamps  B43K29/005 -- The CPC subclass B41K 1/003 covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that have no means for supporting or locating the items to be stamped, and that are combined with other articles such as writing appliances. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Printing → Stamps, linking the function of stamping with integrated writing tools.
[B41K 1/006] Pocket stamps -- Pocket stamps are small, hand-operated devices that stamp without any built-in support or locating mechanism for the material being marked. They are portable, manually pressed, and used to imprint ink on documents, receipts, or other items.
[B41K 1/02] with one or more flat stamping surfaces having fixed images -- B41K 1/02 covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that have one or more flat surfaces bearing a permanently fixed image, such as a logo, date or serial number, and are used by manually applying pressure without any support or positioning mechanism.
[B41K 1/04] with multiple stamping surfaces -- This CPC subclass covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that have multiple stamping surfaces and provide no means for supporting or locating the articles to be stamped. It is a narrow category within printing, stamps and hand-stamp devices.
[B41K 1/06] with means for locating the image to be obtained -- B41K 1/06 covers portable hand-operated stamps that incorporate a positioning system to align the image being transferred onto the substrate, such as a guide frame or template, to ensure consistent imprint placement.
[B41K 1/08] with a flat stamping surface and changeable characters -- B41K 1/08 covers portable hand-operated stamps that have a flat stamping surface and allow the characters to be changed, such as interchangeable letter or symbol plates, used without any built-in support or positioning aid.
[B41K 1/10] having movable type-carrying bands or chains -- It covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that use movable type-carrying bands or chains to transfer ink onto the stamped surface. These are hand stamps without support or locating means, employing a continuous band or chain of characters.
[B41K 1/12] having adjustable type-carrying wheels -- B41K 1/12 covers portable hand-operated stamps that incorporate type-carrying wheels whose position or diameter can be adjusted, enabling the user to vary imprint size or pressure while stamping objects without any supporting or locating apparatus.
[B41K 1/14] having automatic means for changing type-characters -- B41K 1/14 covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that include an automatic mechanism for changing the type characters used to make the imprint. The class falls under Performing Operations → Printing → Stamps and applies to hand stamps that lack any support or positioning system for the items being stamped.
[B41K 1/16] Numbering devices -- The CPC subclass B41K 1/16 covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that lack any support or positioning system for the items being stamped, specifically devices used to apply numbers or other markings. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and stamps.
[B41K 1/18] for pages -- This subgroup covers portable hand-operated hand stamps intended for imprinting markings on individual pages such as paper, cards or tickets, and it excludes devices that provide support or locating means for the items being stamped.
[B41K 1/20] with means for locating the image to be obtained -- B41K 1/20 covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that incorporate a means to position or locate the image to be stamped, such as alignment guides or positioning frames, allowing precise imprinting without a separate support for the workpiece.
[B41K 1/22] with curved stamping surfaces for stamping by rolling contact -- Portable hand-operated hand stamps lacking any support or locating fixtures and featuring curved stamping surfaces that make contact by rolling, enabling continuous imprinting as the stamp is moved across the material. This subclass falls within the broader group for printing stamps and specifies the use of a rolling-type curvature for the imprinting surface.
[B41K 1/24] Rocking stamps -- B41K 1/24 covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that lack any mechanism for supporting or positioning the material to be marked, specifically rocking stamps that are moved manually to imprint an image.
[B41K 1/26] with stamping surfaces adapted for application to non-flat surfaces -- B41K 1/26 covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that lack any support or positioning mechanism for the items being stamped, where the stamping surface is shaped or configured to apply impressions to non-flat surfaces such as curves or irregular textures. This subclass falls under the broader categories of Performing Operations, Printing, and Stamps within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B41K 1/28] flexible -- B41K 1/28 covers flexible hand stamps that are portable and operated manually, lacking any support or locating mechanism for the items being stamped. These devices consist of a flexible stamping surface attached to a handle, allowing the user to apply inked impressions directly.
[B41K 1/30] for offset or intaglio stamping -- This subclass covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that have no support or locating mechanism and are used for offset or intaglio stamping, such as hand stamps that transfer ink by pressing a raised design onto a surface.
[B41K 1/32] for stencilling -- B41K 1/32 covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that lack any supporting or positioning mechanism for the items being stamped, essentially hand stamps used for applying stencils. These tools are employed to transfer patterns, designs or markings directly onto a surface without the aid of a frame or guide. The classification is part of the broader group dealing with printing and stamp operations.
[B41K 1/34] for multicolour stamping -- B41K 1/34 covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that lack any supports or positioning mechanisms for the material being stamped, specifically those designed to apply multiple colours in a single impression. These hand stamps are classified under printing and stamps in the CPC system.
[B41K 1/36] Details -- B41K 1/36 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers specific details of portable hand-operated stamping devices without means for supporting or locating the articles to be stamped, focusing on components and mechanisms that enable their operation.
[B41K 1/363] Labelling means -- B41K 1/363 covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that apply labels or markings without a supporting fixture, such as handheld stamps used to imprint information, branding or dates onto articles.
[B41K 1/366] Arrangement of counting devices -- The subclass covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that incorporate a counting mechanism. It pertains to the internal arrangement of devices that record the number of impressions made, such as mechanical counters or electronic tally displays integrated into the stamp body.
[B41K 1/38] Inking devices -- Inking devices are portable hand-operated tools that supply ink to hand stamps, enabling users to apply ink without additional support or locating mechanisms. They are designed for quick, manual stamping operations and are classified under printing and stamp technologies.
[B41K 1/40] Inking devices operated by stamping movement -- B41K 1/40 covers portable hand-operated stamping tools that lack any support or positioning mechanism for the items being stamped, where ink is applied through the stamping motion itself. These devices are essentially hand stamps with built-in inking systems activated by the user's stamping action.
[B41K 1/42] with pads or rollers movable for inking -- This subcategory refers to portable hand-operated stamping devices that incorporate ink-applying elements such as pads or rollers that can be shifted or adjusted to ink the stamp surface. It excludes devices with built-in support or positioning systems.
[B41K 1/44] for offset, intaglio, or stencil stamping -- B41K 1/44 covers hand-held stamping tools that lack any support or locating means, intended for applying offset, intaglio or stencil impressions. These devices are used to transfer inked or raised images onto paper, fabric or other substrates by manual pressure.
[B41K 1/46] for multicolour stamping -- B41K 1/46 covers portable hand-operated stamping devices that create multi-colour impressions, intended for use without any built-in support or locating mechanism for the material being stamped. These hand stamps apply several colours in a single operation, typically for small-scale or on-site printing tasks.
[B41K 1/48] with ink ribbons, ink sheets, or carbon tape or paper -- This subclass covers portable hand-operated stamps that use an ink ribbon, sheet, or carbon-based material to transfer an image onto a surface, without any built-in support or positioning mechanism for the items being stamped.
[B41K 1/50] Stamping surfaces impregnated with ink, or made of material leaving a mark after stamping contact -- The code covers portable hand-operated stamping tools whose stamping surfaces are either pre-impregnated with ink or made of a material that transfers a mark when pressed against an item. Such devices lack any mechanism for supporting or locating the articles being stamped.
[B41K 1/52] Ink reservoirs, e.g. integral with stamp handles -- B41K 1/52 designates ink reservoirs that are built into portable hand-operated stamps, which have no built-in support or positioning mechanism. The subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → printing → stamps and covers the supply-detail of hand stamps where the ink container is integral with the stamp handle.
[B41K 1/54] Inking pads -- B41K 1/54 - Inking pads for hand stamps are portable, hand-operated devices that supply ink to stamps without built-in support or locating means for the items being stamped. They enable quick re-inking of handheld stamps in printing and marking operations.
[B41K 1/56] Handles -- B41K 1/56 concerns the handles attached to portable hand-operated stamping devices that lack any support or positioning mechanism for the items being stamped. It covers the shape, ergonomics and material of the grip used to manipulate the hand stamp during printing operations.
[B41K 1/58] Stands or other means for keeping hand stamps or the like within easy reach -- This subclass covers devices that hold portable hand-operated stamps so they can be accessed quickly during stamping work. It includes stands, racks, brackets or similar accessories designed to keep hand stamps or comparable tools within easy reach.
[B41K 3/00] Apparatus for stamping articles having integral means for supporting the articles to be stamped -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41K 3/005] Cheque stamping machines  B41K3/02  -  B41K3/62  take precedence -- The B41K 3/005 subclass covers machines designed specifically for stamping cheques, where the device incorporates built-in supports that hold each cheque in place during the stamping operation, ensuring accurate, repeatable impressions without needing separate feeding or aligning equipment.
[B41K 3/02] with stamping surface located above article-supporting surface -- An apparatus that stamps objects while incorporating a built-in support for the items, where the stamping surface is positioned above the supporting surface. The design integrates the holder and the stamping head in a single machine, enabling precise impression of stamps on articles such as paper, metal or plastic.
[B41K 3/04] and movable at right angles to the surface to be stamped -- This CPC subclass covers stamping machines that incorporate integral means for holding the workpiece while the stamp moves at right angles to the surface to be stamped, enabling precise positioning of articles during the stamping operation.
[B41K 3/06] having type-carrying bands or chains -- The B41K 3/06 subclass covers stamping machines that transfer characters to a workpiece via type-carrying bands or chains rather than individual type pieces. It applies to apparatus that includes an integrated support for the articles being stamped and uses continuous or segmented bands or chain loops to feed the desired imprint. This classification falls under printing-stamps within the broader performing-operations field.
[B41K 3/08] having adjustable type-carrying wheels -- A stamping machine includes integral support for the article and type-carrying wheels whose position can be adjusted to set imprint depth or alignment, enabling precise registration of patterns on various items.
[B41K 3/10] having automatic means for changing type-characters, e.g. numbering devices -- The B41K 3/10 subclass covers stamping machines equipped with automatic mechanisms that change the type characters, such as built-in numbering devices, enabling successive or variable imprints without manual re-setting. It falls within the printing-stamps group of the Performing Operations section.
[B41K 3/102] Numbering devices -- B41K 3/102 covers apparatus that stamps numbers onto articles while providing integral supports to hold the items in place during the stamping operation. The classification includes mechanical, pneumatic or electronic numbering devices used in printing, labeling or similar processes.
[B41K 3/105] actuating means for changing type-characters -- B41K 3/105 covers stamping apparatus that incorporates built-in mechanisms for changing the type characters used to form the imprint. It includes actuating systems such as levers, cams, pneumatic or electric drives that shift, replace or rearrange individual type pieces or blocks without removing the entire stamp.
[B41K 3/107] having movable type-carrying bands or chains -- An apparatus for stamping articles that incorporates movable type-carrying bands or chains as the printing element, with the articles supported by built-in means during the stamping operation. This configuration allows continuous or intermittent transfer of types along the band or chain to imprint patterns onto the workpiece.
[B41K 3/12] with curved stamping surface for stamping by rolling contact -- The B41K 3/12 subclass covers stamping machines that incorporate built-in supports for the items being stamped and use a curved stamping surface that contacts the workpiece in a rolling manner rather than by striking. This design is suited for producing stamps and similar printed articles.
[B41K 3/121] using stamping rollers having changeable characters -- This subclass covers stamping apparatus that uses rollers whose characters can be changed, such as interchangeable type plates, replaceable inserts or adjustable patterns, enabling different impressions without replacing the whole unit. It applies to machines that also provide integral support for the items being stamped.
[B41K 3/122] having type-carrying bands or chains -- Apparatus for stamping articles that employ a continuous type-carrying band or chain to hold the relief elements used during the stamping operation. The band or chain moves the type past the workpiece, allowing repeated impressions without changing individual type pieces. This setup integrates the support for the articles being stamped with the moving type carrier.
[B41K 3/123] having adjustable type-carrying wheels -- B41K 3/123 pertains to stamping machines that include type-carrying wheels whose positions can be adjusted, allowing the spacing or alignment of the imprint to be varied. The adjustment mechanism is integral to the apparatus that supports the workpiece during stamping.
[B41K 3/125] having automatic means for changing type-characters -- B41K 3/125 covers stamping machines that include built-in means to support the workpiece during stamping and feature automatic mechanisms for changing the type or characters being impressed. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and stamps, and relates specifically to apparatus with integral article support and self-acting type-change devices.
[B41K 3/126] Numbering devices -- B41K 3/126 covers devices that apply identification numbers to items during a stamping process. These numbering devices are integrated with stamping apparatus that holds the workpiece, enabling sequential or preset numerals to be impressed simultaneously with the stamp. Typical uses include marking serial numbers on metal parts, tickets, or documents.
[B41K 3/127] actuating means for changing type-characters -- This subclass refers to mechanisms that actuate the replacement or repositioning of type characters in stamping devices where the articles are supported by the apparatus itself. It includes hydraulic, pneumatic or mechanical systems that automatically change the stamping type to produce different impressions.
[B41K 3/128] having type-carrying bands or chains -- Apparatus for stamping articles that incorporates a continuous band or chain bearing the type to be transferred onto the workpiece. The type-carrying band or chain moves relative to the support, allowing variable characters or images to be stamped in a controlled, repeatable manner.
[B41K 3/14] for relief stamping -- B41K 3/14 covers apparatus designed for relief stamping in which the machine incorporates built-in supports that hold the workpiece during the stamping process. The classification falls under printing-stamps and relates to equipment that combines stamping tools with integrated article-holding features.
[B41K 3/16] for intaglio stamping -- B41K 3/16 covers devices designed to stamp items where the machine includes built-in supports that hold the workpiece in place during the stamping operation, particularly for intaglio (recessed) stamping processes. Such equipment typically integrates the support mechanism with the stamping head to ensure precise alignment and pressure.
[B41K 3/18] for offset stamping -- B41K 3/18 covers apparatus for offset stamping, in which the article to be stamped is held by an integrated support and the stamp image is transferred indirectly via a printing plate or similar intermediate surface rather than by direct contact. Such devices are used where precise positioning and repeatable impression are required, often in high-volume printing or labeling operations.
[B41K 3/20] for stencilling -- Apparatus for stamping articles that incorporates built-in means to support the workpiece while applying a stencil pattern, enabling precise imprinting of designs or markings onto the material during the stamping operation.
[B41K 3/22] with means for producing distorted images -- B41K 3/22 covers stamp apparatus that includes built-in support for the items being stamped and features mechanisms to create distorted images on the stamp impression, such as warping, stretching or varying the pattern during printing.
[B41K 3/24] for multicolour stamping -- B41K 3/24 relates to stamping machines that can apply multiple colors in a single operation, featuring built-in fixtures that hold the workpiece while the stamping dies strike. These devices are used for printing or embossing designs in several inks on sheets, labels or other flat articles.
[B41K 3/26] with stamping surface located below article-supporting surface -- This subclass covers stamping machines in which the article rests on a support surface that is positioned above the stamping die, so the stamping surface is located beneath the article-supporting surface. The design integrates the support and stamping functions in a single apparatus, enabling the workpiece to be held while the stamp strikes from below. It is used for producing embossed or imprinted features on items such as cards, labels or metal sheets.
[B41K 3/263] having a stamping surface with changeable characters -- Devices that stamp articles using a stamping surface whose characters can be changed, such as by interchangeable type plates or replaceable rubbers, and that include built-in means for supporting the items being stamped.
[B41K 3/266] having automatic means for changing type-characters, e.g. numbering devices -- This classification covers stamping machines that incorporate built-in supports for the items being stamped and feature automatic systems for changing the type characters, such as numbering mechanisms that adjust the imprint without manual re-setup.
[B41K 3/28] and bearing a positive image -- The B41K 3/28 subclass defines stamping apparatus that includes a positive (relief) image on the stamping element and incorporates an integral system for supporting the workpieces during stamping, ensuring precise, repeatable transfer of the image onto the articles being processed.
[B41K 3/30] and having means for varying the image, e.g. by exchanging stamping plates in succession -- This subclass covers stamping machines that include an integral holder for the article to be stamped and a mechanism for varying the image, for example by exchanging stamping plates in succession. It is part of the printing group within the broader performing-operations class.
[B41K 3/32] with co-operating stamping and counter-stamping members -- The B41K 3/32 subclass covers stamping devices that use a pair of cooperating members-one to strike the article and another to act as a counter-stamp-so the imprint is formed between them. These machines include integrated supports that hold the workpiece in place during the stamping operation, ensuring precise alignment and consistent results.
[B41K 3/34] in the form of indexable cylinders, e.g. of curved or polygonal cross-section, or of movable chains or bands -- B41K 3/34 covers stamping apparatus that incorporates built-in support for the items being stamped and uses interchangeable, indexable cylinders such as curved or polygonal rollers, or movable chains or bands, to feed and position the articles during the stamping process.
[B41K 3/36] with means for deforming or punching the copy matter -- The CPC subclass B41K 3/36 covers apparatus for stamping articles that includes integral means for supporting the material being stamped and incorporates mechanisms that deform or punch the copy matter, such as dies, punches or embossing tools, to imprint or shape the substrate during the stamping process.
[B41K 3/38] separate from the stamping means -- B41K 3/38 classifies apparatus for stamping articles in which the support for the workpiece is built into the machine and is separate from the stamping mechanism. The entry covers devices that hold the article while the stamping means operate independently, such as rollers, plates or fixtures integrated into the stamping system.
[B41K 3/40] for numerical or alphabetical characters -- An apparatus that stamps numerical or alphabetical characters onto items while providing built-in support to hold the articles in place during stamping. The device integrates the stamping tool and the supporting structure in a single unit, enabling precise and consistent marking of characters on a variety of products.
[B41K 3/42] Stamping apparatus with selection mechanisms for successively stamping and delivering lists or other items giving information, e.g. for warehouse administration, washing lists, supermarket guides -- The B41K 3/42 subclass defines stamping devices that incorporate selection mechanisms to automatically stamp and dispense a series of informational sheets or cards, such as warehouse inventory lists, laundry instructions or supermarket guides. It covers apparatus where the articles to be stamped are supported by an integral holder, enabling successive stamping and delivery without manual handling. This falls under the broader categories of printing, stamps and performing operations.
[B41K 3/44] Means for handling copy matter -- An apparatus for stamping articles that includes integral support means for the items to be stamped, equipped with features for moving, feeding, aligning or otherwise handling the copy material during the stamping process. This sub-class covers mechanisms such as conveyors, pushers, guides or holders that manage the workpieces before, during or after stamping. It is part of the broader printing-stamps category within performing operations.
[B41K 3/46] for locating when stationary -- The B41K 3/46 subclass defines apparatus that stamps articles and incorporates integral features for supporting and locating the items while the machine remains stationary, ensuring accurate positioning during the stamping process. This covers devices where the positioning means are built into the stamping equipment itself.
[B41K 3/48] for conveying intermittently to or from stamping station -- The B41K 3/48 subclass defines apparatus that moves articles intermittently to or from a stamping station, where the stamping device incorporates built-in supports for the workpieces. Such conveying mechanisms are designed to operate in sync with the stamping cycle.
[B41K 3/50] for conveying during stamping operation -- B41K 3/50 relates to stamping machines that incorporate means for moving the workpiece while it is being stamped, such as belts, rollers, chains or other feeding mechanisms integrated with the stamping apparatus. The subclass covers devices that convey articles continuously or intermittently through the stamping operation to ensure proper positioning and throughput.
[B41K 3/52] for discharging -- The code B41K 3/52 relates to stamping apparatus that includes built-in means for supporting the workpiece and specifies mechanisms for discharging the stamped articles from the machine. It covers ejection, removal or delivery devices that automatically release the finished product after the stamping operation.
[B41K 3/54] Inking devices -- B41K 3/54 pertains to devices that apply ink to stamping tools or dies, including ink rollers, pads, reservoirs and mechanisms that transfer ink onto the surface to be stamped. These inking devices are integral to apparatuses that stamp articles and ensure consistent impression quality.
[B41K 3/56] using inking pads -- B41K 3/56 relates to stamping processes in which inking pads are employed to apply ink to the stamping surface or to the article being stamped. It includes apparatus or methods that integrate the inking pad within the stamping machine to ensure consistent ink transfer during operation.
[B41K 3/58] using ink ribbons, ink sheets, or carbon tape or paper -- The subclass covers stamping machines that incorporate ink-carrying media such as ribbons, sheets or carbon tape/paper to deposit ink on the article while it is being stamped. It includes the arrangement and feeding of these ink supplies within the stamping apparatus.
[B41K 3/60] using rollers, e.g. rollers with integral ink-supply devices -- This subclass covers stamping machines that incorporate rollers, often with built-in ink supply, to transfer designs onto objects while the workpiece is supported by an integral holder. It falls under the broader groups of performing operations, printing, and stamps.
[B41K 3/62] Details or accessories -- B41K 3/62 pertains to the peripheral components and adjustments of stamping machines that incorporate built-in supports for the items being stamped. It includes guides, holders, feeders, positioning devices and other accessories that aid in aligning, feeding or securing workpieces during the stamping operation.
[B41K 3/625] Arrangement of counting devices -- The B41K 3/625 subclass covers stamping machines that incorporate a counting mechanism to record the number of items stamped or the number of stamping cycles. It relates to the arrangement of such devices within apparatus that also provides integral supports for the articles being stamped.
[B41K 3/64] Stamping mechanisms controlled by feed of copy matter -- The entry covers stamping devices in which the advance of the material to be stamped controls the stamping operation, so that the feed mechanism automatically triggers the stamp as each piece of copy passes the stamping station. It is applied to apparatus that integrates support for the articles with a feed-controlled stamping mechanism, commonly used in printing presses for stamps, postage or serial numbering.
[B41K 3/66] Safety devices, e.g. for preventing extraction of copy matter before completion of stamping operation -- B41K 3/66 covers safety devices in stamping apparatus that prevent the workpiece or copy matter from being removed before the stamping cycle is finished, often built into machines that also provide integral support for the articles being stamped.
[B41K 3/68] Cutting or severing devices -- An apparatus for stamping articles that incorporates built-in means for holding the workpiece during the stamp operation, specifically where the device also includes cutting or severing elements to separate the stamped part from the surrounding material.
[B41K 5/00] Plier-like tools for stamping, or stamping and delivering, tickets or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories specifically adapted for use with said tools, e.g.
[B41K 5/003] Inking devices therefor -- B41K 5/003 covers devices that supply ink to plier-type tools used for stamping or delivering tickets and similar items, ensuring consistent marking during the printing operation.
[B41K 5/006] with means for deforming the copy matter -- This subclass covers plier-type stamping devices that incorporate mechanisms to deform the material being stamped, such as bending or embossing, enabling the production of tickets or similar copies. It is part of B41K 5/00 for stamping and delivering tools within the printing-stamps hierarchy.
[B41K 5/02] with means for varying the image stamped -- B41K 5/02 covers plier-type stamping tools that include mechanisms for changing the image that is stamped on tickets, stamps or similar items, such as replaceable dies, rotating plates or adjustable settings, allowing the imprint to be varied without replacing the whole device.
[B41K 5/023] having type-carrying bands or chains -- This subgroup covers plier-type stamping devices that incorporate a movable band or chain carrying the type characters, used to imprint or dispense tickets or similar items.
[B41K 5/026] having adjustable type-carrying wheels -- The subclass B41K 5/026 concerns plier-type stamping tools that include wheels carrying the printable type and allows those wheels to be adjusted relative to the stamping heads. This adjustment lets the operator align or change the spacing of the type for accurate impression of tickets, receipts or similar items. It falls within the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and stamps.
[B41K 5/04] with devices for collecting counterfoils, or with other means for recording stamping operations -- B41K 5/04 covers plier-type stamping tools used to imprint tickets, receipts or similar items, and includes mechanisms that collect the counterfoils punched out during stamping or otherwise record each stamping event. The classification applies to devices that combine the stamping function with integrated counterfoil handling or stamping-operation monitoring.
[B41K 5/06] for recording on separate tape -- B41K 5/06 covers plier-type stamping tools that also record information onto a separate tape, such as devices used to imprint and issue tickets, receipts or similar documents while simultaneously producing a tape record of the transaction. The classification applies to mechanisms that combine stamping with tape-based data capture.
[B41K 5/08] with counting devices -- Plier-like tools designed for stamping or delivering tickets that incorporate counting devices to record each stamp or issuance. The mechanism tracks the number of tickets processed, aiding in inventory control and fare-collection accuracy. This subclass falls under printing and stamp operations within the CPC.
[B41K 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Portable hand-operated devices without means for supporting or locating the articles to be stamped, i.e. hand stamps; inking devices or accessories therefore -> B41K1/00 - Apparatus for stamping articles having integral means for supporting the articles to be stamped -> B41K3/00 - Plier-like tools for stamping, or stamping and delivering, tickets or the like -> B41K5/00
[B41L] APPARATUS OR DEVICES FOR MANIFOLDING, DUPLICATING OR PRINTING FOR OFFICE OR OTHER COMMERCIAL PURPOSES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Manifolding devices, which comprise assembled sheets or webs and interposed pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries usually in the form of carbon sheets or layers;  carbons and other pressure-sensitive layers or coatings specifically adapted for manifolding;  apparatus or machines for duplicating or printing, which always use a static original in the form of a sheet for duplicating thereof or a static printing form, usually a printing plate, carrying a graphical and/or alphanumerical text or picture forming the duplicating or printing image;  printing or duplicating, always encompassing the use of ink or colour;  printing apparatus or machines exclusively for special uses like office printing or commercial purposes. These special uses encompass in general an almost low number, e.g. up to hundred, of copies or printed sheets;  addressing or like series-printing machines as an example of specific commercial purpose.  Feeding or handling of substrates to be duplicated or printed is covered from the in-feed device through the whole machine down to the delivery device.  Auxiliary folding, collecting or depositing of sheets or webs specifically adapted for said specific apparatus of the present subclass (cf. B41L43/00 ).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sheet materials used for printing purposes -> B41M5/00  **Glossary:** - Manifolding: always encompasses use of pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries, e.g. carbon - Hectograph or gelatine duplicator: transfer of a sheet-like original prepared with special inks including special aniline dyes onto an open pan containing gelatine,afterwards, paper copies are made by pressing a paper containing substrate against the inked and images gelatine surface - Printing or duplicating: after applying ink or colour onto the surface of the original or of the printing form, the inked image is applied by contact pressure onto the surface of a substrate, most common in the form of paper, which will form the copy of printed matter, - Print image or printing image: a coloured design having any shape, for e.g. alphanumerical characters - Offset print or offset printing: §using an intermediate element, most commonly a blanket cylinder, for transfer in one step of the whole inked print image on the printing element, most commonly a printing plate, onto the surface of the substrate to be printed upon - Stencil: sheet like printing plate having cut outs defining by their openings the print or printing image
[B41L 1/00] Manifolding by means of pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for manifolding, e.g. assemblies in the form of pads or books of single sheets or in the form of continuous assemblies of webs,  devices for supplying pressure-sensitive material including carbon gloves,  devices for performing preparatory operations and  devices for performing operations subsequent to manifolding,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 1/02] Devices for preparatory operations, e.g. for bringing together sheets or webs and interposed carbons -- B41L 1/02 covers devices used in printing operations that perform preparatory tasks such as assembling sheets, webs or layers with interposed carbon copies before printing or duplicating. These apparatuses handle feeding, aligning and stacking of multiple media to enable pressure-sensitive or other manifold printing processes.
[B41L 1/04] Devices for performing operations subsequent to manifolding, e.g. for separating single sheets or webs from single form sets, continuous manifold assemblies from carbons -- B41L 1/04 pertains to devices designed for post-manifolding operations in printing processes, such as separating individual sheets or continuous forms from carbon-backed sets, ensuring efficient handling and processing of printed materials.
[B41L 1/06] on single form sets -- B41L 1/06 covers printing or copying devices that produce multiple reproductions from a single form set using pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries. It applies to office or commercial equipment where one master sheet is fed through a manifold to transfer ink onto several copies in one operation.
[B41L 1/08] on continuous manifold assemblies -- B41L 1/08 covers continuous manifold assemblies used in printing equipment that transfers images or text by moving pressure-sensitive layers or intermediary sheets through a printing or duplicating process. The classification applies to devices that keep a steady flow of such layers, enabling high-speed office or commercial printing.
[B41L 1/10] Separate folding or disposition of single webs -- It relates to machines and processes that fold or otherwise position a single web of material, such as paper, in preparation for printing, duplicating or other commercial operations. The classification covers separate folding, reversing, or arranging of an individual web without combining it with other webs, using mechanisms like rollers, guides or pressure-sensitive intermediaries.
[B41L 11/00] Apparatus for directly duplicating from hectographic masters in mirror image, i.e. "wet duplicators" for producing positive copies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flat supports or cylinders or two cooperating rollers for carrying the master sheet,  clay or gelatine is used,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 11/02] with a flat support carrying the masters -- This subclass covers wet duplicator machines that copy hectographic masters placed on a flat support, producing mirror-image positive copies in a single pass. The devices are intended for office or commercial use and fall under CPC code B41L 11/02 within the hierarchy for printing apparatus.
[B41L 11/04] and with carriages for feeding the sheets -- B41L 11/04 covers wet-duplicator machines that copy directly from hectographic masters in mirror image to produce positive copies. The entry specifically concerns devices equipped with sheet-feeding carriages that supply the paper during the duplication process.
[B41L 11/06] for stack duplicating with pressure rollers -- B41L 11/06 covers devices that duplicate hectographic masters by stacking them and passing them through pressure rollers to produce mirror-image positive copies. These wet duplicators use a liquid medium to transfer the image directly onto paper, enabling rapid office or commercial reproduction.
[B41L 11/08] with rotary cylinders carrying the masters -- An apparatus that uses rotary cylinders to hold hectographic masters for wet duplication, producing positive copies in mirror image directly from the master. It is a type of printing device for office or commercial use that duplicates images by transferring ink-moistened pigment from the master to paper. This classification covers the specific configuration where the masters are mounted on rotating cylinders.
[B41L 11/10] with two rollers between which master is stretched -- This subclass covers devices that duplicate hectographic masters by passing the master stretched between two rollers, creating mirror-image positive copies using a wet-duplicator process. It falls under printing apparatus for office or commercial use within the CPC hierarchy.
[B41L 11/12] Driving gear -- B41L 11/12 covers the driving gear that powers wet duplicator machines which copy directly from hectographic masters in mirror image. The gear transmits motion from the motor to the impression roller, ensuring the sheet and master are moved synchronously during the duplication process. It is specific to apparatus used for office or commercial direct duplication.
[B41L 11/14] Constructional features of masters -- B41L 11/14 covers the design and structural aspects of masters used in hectographic duplication, where a wet process creates positive copies from a mirrored master. It addresses features such as material composition, thickness, surface treatment and mounting that affect image transfer.
[B41L 1/12] Severing webs to obtain single sheets or forms, e.g. by cutting, by bursting -- The code refers to mechanisms that separate a continuous web of material into single sheets or forms, typically by cutting or bursting the web. It includes apparatus and processes used in printing or duplicating equipment to produce individual pages from a rolled or fed substrate.
[B41L 1/14] Severing edge perforations from webs -- The B41L 1/14 class includes devices and methods that cut edge perforations into continuous webs, creating lines of weakness that allow easy separation of printed or manifolded sheets. It covers mechanisms that sever these perforations during or after printing, commonly used for tear-off forms, tickets, and other office or commercial paper products.
[B41L 1/16] Carriers or supply devices for pressure-sensitive material, e.g. for carbon sheets -- B41L 1/16 covers carriers or supply devices that hold or feed pressure-sensitive material such as carbon sheets in printing or duplicating equipment. These devices deliver the material to a printing surface by means of pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries, enabling manifolding operations in office or commercial printers.
[B41L 1/18] for carbon webs -- B41L 1/18 covers devices that use carbon webs as the pressure-sensitive medium for manifolding, duplicating or printing in office or commercial equipment. It includes systems where a carbon-coated layer transfers ink by pressure to produce copies, such as carbon-paper or carbon-ribbon mechanisms in duplicators.
[B41L 1/20] Manifolding assemblies, e.g. book-like assemblies -- The CPC subclass B41L 1/20 covers manifolding assemblies used in printing equipment, such as book-like structures that bind or fold printed sheets by means of pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries. These devices form part of apparatus for duplicating or printing for office or commercial purposes, enabling quick and reversible assembly of printed material.
[B41L 1/22] made up of single sheets or forms -- B41L 1/22 pertains to devices that produce multiple copies by using pressure-sensitive layers applied to individual sheets or forms, such as carbonless copy papers and similar office duplicating media. The subclass covers apparatus that manifold prints through single-sheet intermediary layers rather than continuous rolls.
[B41L 1/24] Pads or books -- The CPC class B41L 1/24 refers to pads or books used in pressure-sensitive manifolding devices, where a stack of sheets is transferred by means of a resilient pad or a book of intermediary layers. These components are part of printing or duplicating apparatuses employed in office or commercial settings for rapid sheet-by-sheet reproduction.
[B41L 1/26] Continuous assemblies made up of webs -- B41L 1/26 covers continuous assemblies made up of webs, where two or more web-like substrates are joined in a single unbroken feed for use in printing, duplicating or manifolding operations. The arrangement enables simultaneous handling of multiple layers, often including pressure-sensitive intermediaries, to produce printable sheets or transfer media.
[B41L 1/28] in rolled or wound form -- This subclass covers printing or duplicating equipment that uses pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries supplied in a rolled or wound configuration, such as rolls of carbonless paper, self-adhesive labels, or similar media for office or commercial use.
[B41L 1/30] folded longitudinally -- This subclass concerns devices that fold a sheet or web longitudinally before printing or duplicating, usually employing pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries to transfer images in office or commercial printing equipment. It is part of the apparatus category for manifolding, duplicating or printing.
[B41L 13/00] Stencilling apparatus for office or other commercial use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing apparatus or machines, as a whole as well as parts thereof, using a stencil as the image forming element,  a stencil which is usually a flexible sheet having one or a plurality of cutouts defining the print image; said stencil sheet usually having a plurality of layers often including an adhesive layer in order to fix the stencil onto a carrier as part of a printing apparatus,  printing apparatus, which either dispose of a flat stencil carrier or of a curved or rotary stencil carrier,  main special parts of the stencilling apparatus, like inking units, drive means, drive gears and control means, specifically adapted for use in said apparatus,  auxiliary devices, like means for punching, cutting or severing, specifically adapted for stencilling machines.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Screen or silk-screen printing apparatus -> B41F15/00
[B41L 13/02] with flat stencil carriers -- The B41L 13/02 subclass covers printing or duplicating devices that employ flat stencil carriers, where the stencil is a rigid, planar sheet positioned to receive ink and transfer images onto a substrate. It applies to office and commercial apparatus designed for repeated, accurate imprinting.
[B41L 13/04] with curved or rotary stencil carriers -- This subclass covers stencilling devices for office or commercial use that employ curved or rotary carriers to hold the stencil, allowing continuous or non-linear printing of the pattern. It falls under CPC code B41L 13/04, a subcategory of apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing.
[B41L 13/06] with a single cylinder carrying the stencil -- The CPC subclass B41L 13/06 covers stencilling machines that employ a single cylinder to carry the stencil, typically used in office or commercial settings for printing, duplicating or manifolding documents. It refers to devices where the stencil is mounted on one rotating drum rather than multiple cylinders.
[B41L 13/08] with stencil carried by two or more cylinders, e.g. through the intermediary of endless bands -- B41L 13/08 covers stencil printing apparatus in which the stencil is attached to two or more rotating cylinders, often linked by endless bands, enabling continuous duplication for office or commercial use.
[B41L 13/10] Clips or clamps for securing stencils to stencil carriers -- B41L 13/10 covers clips, clamps or other securing devices that hold a stencil onto its carrier in office or commercial printing equipment. These components ensure the stencil stays flat during manifolding, duplicating or printing operations.
[B41L 13/12] for special purposes, e.g. for reproducing Braille characters -- The subclass B41L 13/12 covers stencilling machines intended for special-purpose printing, notably devices that produce tactile symbols such as Braille characters. It includes apparatus that creates raised-dot patterns on paper or other media for accessibility or other niche applications, falling under printing and manifold apparatus for office or commercial use.
[B41L 13/14] Attachments, e.g. for punching, cutting, severing -- B41L 13/14 covers attachments for stencilling apparatus used in office or commercial printing, such as devices for punching, cutting or severing the generated stencil material. These accessories are intended to modify or enhance the basic printing or duplicating equipment.
[B41L 13/16] Driving gear -- B41L 13/16 covers the driving gear, the component that transmits motion to the printing head or stencil mechanism in office or commercial stencilling devices. It relates to the gear system that powers the movement of the stencil or print carriage within apparatus designed for duplicating or printing.
[B41L 13/18] Inking units -- Inking units are the components of a stencilling machine that store, meter and deliver ink to the print head or stencil, typically comprising reservoirs, pumps and distribution channels to ensure consistent application during office or commercial printing.
[B41L 1/32] folded transversely -- B41L 1/32 refers to devices or processes that fold a sheet transversely-across its width-to create multiple impressions using pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries. It falls under printing apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing in office or commercial settings.
[B41L 1/321] Stacks of sheets attached to a carrier strip or web -- The B41L 1/321 subclass concerns printing apparatus that handle stacks of individual sheets which are permanently or temporarily bound to a carrier strip or continuous web. It relates to pressure-sensitive layer or intermediary based manifolding processes for office or commercial printing.
[B41L 1/323] Uniting several webs, e.g. transversely -- The B41L 1/323 subclass covers devices that unite multiple webs side-by-side, typically by joining them transversely using pressure-sensitive layers or intermediate sheets. It applies to printing or duplicating apparatus where the webs are combined into a single continuous sheet before further processing. This technique is used in office or commercial printing equipment to create larger or composite forms from several narrower web feeds.
[B41L 1/325] longitudinally -- The code refers to printing equipment that forms copies by using pressure-sensitive layers applied in the longitudinal direction of the web or sheet. It includes rollers or belts that transfer a coated layer along the length of the material, enabling continuous duplication for office or commercial use.
[B41L 1/326] Severance lines; Perforations -- B41L 1/326 covers features that create severance lines or perforations in sheets processed by pressure-sensitive manifolding or duplicating equipment, enabling easy tearing or separation of printed forms for office or commercial use. The classification applies to devices and methods that incorporate such line-cutting or hole-punching elements within the printing apparatus.
[B41L 1/328] Removing carbon sheets -- This subclass concerns devices or processes for detaching carbon sheets from multi-layered paper after printing, such as peeling, scraping, blowing or otherwise removing the carbon layer in office or commercial copying operations.
[B41L 1/34] for making masters for hectographic duplicators -- This subcategory covers methods and apparatus for preparing master copies used in hectographic duplication, where a master is produced by applying a pressure-sensitive layer or intermediary to capture the original image for subsequent copying. It falls within the class of devices for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes.
[B41L 1/36] with pressure-sensitive layers or coating other than carbon -- This subclass of B41L deals with apparatus that form copies by pressure-sensitive sheets or coatings that are not carbon based, such as self-adhesive or silicone layers used in office duplicators. It is part of printing apparatus for commercial use, focusing on non-carbon pressure-sensitive media.
[B41L 15/00] Letterpress printing apparatus specially adapted for office or other commercial purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 15/02] with flat printing surfaces, e.g. with flat type-beds, surfaces made of thin sheet material or moulded from plastics or rubber -- A letterpress printing machine designed for office or commercial use that employs flat printing surfaces, such as flat type-beds, made from thin sheet material or molded plastics or rubber, falls under CPC code B41L 15/02. This subclass specifies apparatus where the impression surface is planar rather than cylindrical, targeting high-speed, low-volume printing tasks in business environments.
[B41L 15/04] of composed type locked in chases -- The B41L 15/04 subclass covers letterpress printing machines designed for office or commercial use that employ composed type secured within metal chases. These devices lock the arranged type in a removable frame, allowing rapid set-up and change-over while maintaining precise registration during printing. This configuration is typical of small-scale, high-quality press equipment used for producing forms, labels, and short-run prints.
[B41L 15/06] with curved printing surfaces, e.g. cylinders -- The code B41L 15/06 refers to letterpress printing machines designed for office or commercial use that employ curved printing surfaces such as cylinders. It covers apparatus where the printing plate or type is mounted on a rotating or otherwise curved element to transfer ink onto paper. This subclass falls under the broader category of devices for manifolding, duplicating or printing in a commercial setting.
[B41L 15/08] with stereotypes -- The subclass B41L 15/08 covers letterpress printing machines adapted for office or commercial use that employ stereotypes - reusable metal printing plates - for producing repeated copies, including the mechanisms for handling, feeding and aligning these plates during printing.
[B41L 15/10] for multicolour printing -- This subclass covers letterpress printing machines designed for office or commercial use that can print in multiple colors either simultaneously or sequentially. It includes apparatus with multiple ink stations, color registers or mechanisms for changing inks to achieve multicolour output.
[B41L 15/12] Driving gear -- The B41L 15/12 subclass covers the driving-gear mechanism of letterpress printing machines that are specially adapted for office or other commercial use. It includes gears that transmit power to the printing cylinder or platen, enabling the coordinated movement required for printing operations.
[B41L 15/14] Attachments, e.g. for punching, cutting, severing -- This subclass covers auxiliary devices that can be fitted to a letterpress printing machine for office or commercial use to perform secondary operations such as punching holes, cutting paper or severing sheets. The attachments are designed to integrate with the main printing apparatus and extend its functionality.
[B41L 17/00] Lithographic printing apparatus for office or other commercial purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 17/02] for direct impression printing -- B41L 17/02 covers lithographic printing machines intended for office or commercial use that produce the printed image by direct impression onto the receiving material, without an intermediate transfer step. The subclass includes apparatus where the image-forming element contacts the substrate directly to transfer ink or toner in a single operation. This design is used for on-demand, high-speed printing in business environments.
[B41L 17/04] with flat printing surfaces -- The subclass B41L 17/04 covers lithographic printing machines for office or commercial use that employ flat printing surfaces, such as plates or cylinders, to transfer images onto a substrate. It includes devices where the printing surface remains in a plane during the impression, distinguishing them from rotary or curved-surface systems.
[B41L 17/06] with curved printing surfaces, e.g. cylinders -- The code B41L 17/06 designates lithographic printing machines for office or commercial use that employ curved printing surfaces such as cylinders or rollers. These apparatuses use a curved plate or blanket to transfer ink onto a substrate, enabling continuous or high-speed printing operations.
[B41L 17/08] for offset printing -- The CPC subclass B41L 17/08 covers lithographic printing machines designed for office or commercial use that employ offset printing, where the inked image is transferred from a plate to a rubber blanket before being printed on the final substrate. It falls under apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing in the broader categories of printing and performing operations.
[B41L 17/10] with flat printing surfaces, e.g. co-operating with travelling offset cylinders -- This subclass refers to lithographic printing machines for office or commercial use that employ flat printing surfaces, usually working together with travelling offset cylinders, and is part of the apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing category.
[B41L 17/12] with curved printing surfaces, e.g. forme cylinders -- The subclass B41L 17/12 designates lithographic printing machines for office or commercial use that employ curved printing surfaces, such as forme cylinders, rather than flat plates. It covers apparatus where the curvature of the printing surface is integral to the printing process.
[B41L 17/14] of two-cylinder type, e.g. co-operating forme and impression cylinders -- A lithographic printing machine designed for office or commercial use that employs two cylinders, typically a forme cylinder and an impression cylinder, which work together to transfer inked images onto a substrate. This two-cylinder configuration enables efficient, high-quality printing in compact settings.
[B41L 17/16] of three-cylinder type -- B41L 17/16 covers lithographic printing machines designed for office or other commercial use that employ a three-cylinder configuration. The three cylinders typically include a plate cylinder, an impression cylinder and a transfer or auxiliary cylinder, enabling continuous printing of multiple copies from a lithographic plate. This subclass specifies the mechanical arrangement and operation of such three-cylinder lithographic press systems.
[B41L 17/18] for multicolour printing, e.g. tandem machines -- The CPC subclass B41L 17/18 deals with lithographic printing apparatus intended for multicolour printing, such as tandem machines used in office or commercial environments. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and devices for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes.
[B41L 17/20] without damping means, e.g. using heat-activatable inks, refrigerated printing surfaces -- The CPC subclass B41L 17/20 refers to lithographic printing machines for office or commercial use that operate without conventional damping systems; instead they employ alternatives such as heat-activatable inks or cooled printing surfaces to transfer the image.
[B41L 17/22] Driving gear -- The B41L 17/22 subclass designates the driving-gear components of lithographic printing machines used in office or commercial settings. It covers gears, gear trains and related drive mechanisms that transmit power to the printing cylinder, blanket cylinder or other moving parts of the apparatus.
[B41L 17/24] Attachments, e.g. for punching, cutting, severing -- This CPC subclass covers detachable units that can be fitted to lithographic printers used in offices or other commercial settings to perform secondary actions such as punching, cutting or severing the printed sheet. The devices are designed as accessories mounted on the printing apparatus to provide finishing functions without needing separate equipment.
[B41L 19/00] Duplicating or printing apparatus or machines for office or other commercial purposes, of special types or for particular purposes and not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 19/003] using heat, e.g. wax transfer -- It covers apparatus that duplicate or print using heat-based processes such as wax transfer, where the image is formed by applying heat to transfer wax or similar media onto a substrate. These devices are intended for office or commercial use and are not covered by other more specific categories.
[B41L 19/006] Auxiliary numbering devices -- Auxiliary numbering devices are attachments or integrated mechanisms for duplicating or printing machines that automatically add sequential numbers, counters or other identification marks to each printed sheet. They are used in office and commercial printing equipment to produce numbered forms, invoices, tickets or labels without manual entry.
[B41L 19/02] having forme cylinders carrying a plurality of printing surfaces, or for performing letterpress and lithographic processes selectively or in combination -- B41L 19/02 falls under performing operations - printing - apparatus or devices for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes. It classifies duplicating or printing machines that use forme cylinders bearing several printing surfaces, enabling selective or combined letterpress and lithographic processes.
[B41L 19/04] for printing from selected parts of one or more printing surfaces in one cycle, e.g. line printing -- B41L 19/04 refers to apparatus that prints from chosen portions of one or more printing surfaces in a single cycle, such as line-printing devices. It is a subclass of office or commercial duplicating and printing machines that are specially designed for particular purposes not covered elsewhere.
[B41L 19/06] with co-operating forme and impression cylinders -- B41L 19/06 classifies printing or duplicating machines that employ a cooperating forme cylinder and impression cylinder to transfer inked images onto a substrate, a configuration commonly found in offset presses and other specialized commercial print equipment. This subclass captures apparatus where the two cylinders work together to form the printed copy.
[B41L 19/08] by effecting relative movement of forme and impression cylinders during printing cycle -- B41L 19/08 covers duplicating or printing devices that achieve printing by moving the forme and impression cylinders relative to each other during the printing cycle, a type of apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or commercial purposes.
[B41L 19/10] by projecting and retracting parts of the surfaces of the forme cylinders -- B41L 19/10 covers duplicating or printing machines for office or commercial use that create the image by projecting and retracting sections of the surface of the forming cylinders, allowing variable-depth relief or selective printing on the substrate.
[B41L 19/12] by masking parts of the printing surfaces on the forme cylinders -- The CPC subclass B41L 19/12 covers duplicating or printing machines for office or commercial use in which portions of the printing surfaces on form cylinders are masked to control image areas. It relates to special-type apparatus that deliberately blocks parts of the cylinder surface during printing to produce selective impressions. This subclass falls under apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing, within the broader printing category.
[B41L 19/14] by selective damping of the copy material -- B41L 19/14 pertains to duplicating or printing apparatus designed for office or commercial use, specifically focusing on methods that involve selectively damping the copy material to enhance print quality or efficiency. This technique is applied in specialized machines not covered under broader categories.
[B41L 19/16] by selective tripping of impression cylinders -- The B41L 19/16 subclass covers duplicating or printing machines in which impression cylinders are selectively tripped, meaning they can be engaged or disengaged on demand to control the transfer of ink. This feature is used in special-type office or commercial printers to produce prints only on selected sheets or sections, enabling precise control of the printing process.
[B41L 21/00] Common details of, or accessories for, apparatus or machines for manifolding, duplicating, or printing for office or other commercial purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 21/02] for conveying sheets -- It refers to devices that move sheets of paper or other media within office or commercial printing, duplicating or manifolding machines, including feed rollers, belts, conveyors and other sheet-handling arrangements that transport sheets through the apparatus.
[B41L 21/04] Pins -- Pins are small metal or plastic fasteners used as accessories in office and commercial printing, duplicating or manifolding equipment to secure paper, forms or other media during operation.
[B41L 21/06] Grippers -- Grippers are mechanisms attached to printing or duplicating machines that hold, feed, or release sheets or forms during operation, typically comprising rollers, suction cups, or mechanical clamps, and are used in office or commercial printing equipment
[B41L 21/08] Suction-operated grippers -- Suction-operated grippers are devices that use vacuum to pick up and hold sheets or other items during printing, duplicating or other office/commercial processing. They are commonly integrated into feeding or transport mechanisms of printers, copiers and similar equipment, allowing reliable handling of media without mechanical force.
[B41L 21/10] Combinations of endless conveyors and grippers -- The subclass B41L 21/10 covers systems that combine a continuous (endless) conveyor belt with gripping devices for moving sheets or other media through printing, duplicating or manifolding equipment used in office or commercial environments, emphasizing the integrated mechanical arrangement of transport and gripping.
[B41L 21/12] for conveying webs -- B41L 21/12 covers devices that convey webs in printing, duplicating or manifolding equipment used in office or commercial settings. It includes mechanisms that transport continuous sheets or rolls of material through such machines.
[B41L 23/00] Devices for treating the surfaces of sheets, webs, or other articles in connection with printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 23/02] by damping, e.g. by moistening copy sheets in connection with hectographic printing -- This subclass covers the dampening or moistening of copy sheets during printing, particularly in hectographic processes where adding moisture to the sheets improves ink transfer and image quality.
[B41L 23/04] using friction rollers -- This subclass concerns printing equipment that uses friction rollers to treat the surfaces of sheets, webs or other articles during printing, such as to smooth, feed, or transfer ink and other substances. The rollers make direct contact with the material to modify its surface condition for office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 23/06] using brushes -- B41L 23/06 covers the use of brushes to treat the surface of sheets, webs or other articles during printing processes. It includes brush-based cleaning, conditioning, applying liquids or removing debris from media in devices that manifold, duplicate or print for office or commercial use. This subclass falls under devices for treating printing surfaces within the broader hierarchy of performing operations and printing.
[B41L 23/08] using spray elements -- B41L 23/08 covers the use of spray elements in apparatus that treat the surfaces of sheets, webs or other articles during printing operations. It includes devices that apply liquids, inks, adhesives, powders or coatings by spraying, atomising or misting for surface preparation, conditioning or finishing in office or commercial printing contexts.
[B41L 23/10] using endless bands -- The subclass covers printing equipment that employs continuous loop belts, or endless bands, to treat the surfaces of sheets, webs or other items during printing operations. It includes mechanisms where the moving band contacts the material for cleaning, drying, coating or other surface-treating functions.
[B41L 23/12] using absorbent pads -- Classification B41L 23/12 pertains to printing equipment that treats the surface of sheets, webs or other articles by employing absorbent pads. Such pads may be used to remove excess ink, moisture or other residues, or to apply liquids during the printing process, thereby improving print quality in office or commercial printing devices.
[B41L 23/14] Ducts, containers, or like supply devices for liquids -- The subclass B41L 23/14 covers ducts, containers and similar supply devices that hold or transport liquids used in surface-treatment operations of sheets, webs or other articles during printing. It includes ink, coating or cleaning-fluid reservoirs, channels and feeders that are incorporated into or attached to printing equipment.
[B41L 23/16] Devices for tripping or lifting damping rollers -- B41L 23/16 covers devices that trip or lift damping rollers in printing apparatus. These mechanisms intervene in the handling of sheets, webs or other articles to adjust roller pressure, enable sheet release, or alter the printing register, facilitating precise control of surface treatment during the printing process.
[B41L 23/18] Construction of damping rollers -- B41L 23/18 covers the design and construction of damping rollers used in printing-related equipment that treat the surfaces of sheets, webs or similar articles. It includes features that control vibrations, pressure or motion of the rollers to improve print quality or handling. These rollers are part of devices for manifolding, duplicating or printing in office or commercial settings.
[B41L 23/20] by heat drying, by cooling, by applying powders -- This subclass relates to printing equipment that treats the surface of sheets or webs after ink application by using thermal drying, cooling, or powder application. It includes devices that dry printed material with heated air or ovens, chill it to set ink, or deposit powders such as fixatives or antistatic agents.
[B41L 23/22] Powdering devices, e.g. for preventing set-off -- B41L 23/22 pertains to powdering devices employed in printing operations, such as equipment that applies fine powder to sheets, webs or other articles to prevent set-off after printing. These apparatuses are used in office or commercial printing environments to treat surfaces immediately after ink deposition.
[B41L 23/24] Print-finishing devices, e.g. for glossing prints -- Print-finishing devices are apparatuses that treat printed sheets or other articles after the printing step to improve surface qualities such as gloss, smoothness or durability. They include machines that apply coating, polishing, varnishing or other surface-enhancing processes for office or commercial print outputs.
[B41L 25/00] Devices for damping printing surfaces, e.g. moistening printing surfaces in connection with lithographic printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use.  Usual output of a low number e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 25/005] Pre-damping etching devices -- B41L 25/005 relates to pre-damping etching devices, which apply a controlled amount of moisture to printing surfaces such as plates or blankets before lithographic etching. These apparatuses ensure uniform wetting to improve ink transfer and image quality in office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 25/02] using friction rollers -- The B41L 25/02 subclass includes apparatus that dampen or moisten printing surfaces by means of friction rollers, typically employed in lithographic or similar printing processes to regulate ink transfer and improve print quality. It covers devices where the rollers provide controlled moisture to the substrate.
[B41L 25/04] using brushes -- B41L 25/04 concerns the use of brushes to apply moisture to printing surfaces during lithographic printing, providing controlled dampening that improves ink transfer. It falls within devices for damping printing surfaces in office or other commercial printing apparatus.
[B41L 25/06] using spraying elements -- The CPC subclass B41L 25/06 covers methods and devices that apply spraying elements to moisten or dampen printing surfaces during lithographic printing, facilitating ink transfer and surface preparation. It falls under apparatus for duplicating or printing in office or commercial settings, specifically for damping surfaces. This entry addresses the use of spray mechanisms to achieve controlled moisture application.
[B41L 25/08] using endless bands -- The subclass B41L 25/08 covers lithographic printing apparatus that use continuous, endless bands to dampen or moisten the printing surface, providing a uniform wetting action during plate preparation or printing. Such devices integrate the moving belt as both a transport and moisture-delivery element, improving consistency and efficiency in commercial printing operations.
[B41L 25/10] using absorbent pads -- B41L 25/10 covers devices that employ absorbent pads to damp or moisten printing surfaces, particularly in lithographic printing. These pads are used to control moisture on plates or cylinders, improving ink transfer and print quality.
[B41L 25/12] Ducts, containers, or like supply devices for liquids -- B41L 25/12 covers ducts, containers and similar devices that supply liquids to printing surfaces, primarily for damping or moistening in lithographic printing. The classification pertains to apparatus that deliver or manage liquid on the printing plate to control ink transfer and print quality.
[B41L 25/14] Inking rollers serving also to apply ink repellants -- The B41L 25/14 subclass covers devices that combine inking rollers with a function to apply ink-repellent agents, enabling simultaneous ink transfer and surface treatment. These rollers are used to damp or treat printing surfaces, such as moistening plates in lithographic presses, to improve ink control.
[B41L 25/16] Devices for tripping or lifting damping rollers -- The B41L 25/16 subclass covers mechanisms that raise or disengage damping rollers used to moisten printing surfaces in lithographic and other printing processes. These devices facilitate the temporary removal or repositioning of rollers to control surface moisture during operation.
[B41L 25/18] Construction of damping rollers -- B41L 25/18 covers the design and structure of rollers that dampen printing surfaces, such as those used to moisten plates in lithographic or similar printing processes. The entry includes details of the roller's material, surface texture, internal channels, and mechanisms for delivering a controlled film of liquid to the printing medium. It falls under the broader category of equipment for manifolding, duplicating or printing in office or commercial settings.
[B41L 27/00] Inking arrangements or devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. Less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
