[B41L 27/02] adapted for inking by hand -- B41L 27/02 covers devices or arrangements for printing that are adapted for inking by hand, falling under apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or commercial purposes within the broader class of performing operations and printing.
[B41L 27/04] Ducts, containers, or supply devices or ink-level control devices -- B41L 27/04 covers the ducts, containers and supply devices that deliver or regulate ink in printing apparatus, as well as mechanisms for monitoring and controlling ink level. These components are used in office or commercial printers and duplicators to ensure consistent ink flow and prevent shortages.
[B41L 27/06] Duct blades or like supply devices -- B41L 27/06 covers duct blades or similar devices that supply ink, cleaning fluid, or other liquids to the print zone in printing apparatus. The elements are shaped to direct flow through ducts or channels toward the printing surface, often integrated with rollers or plates. They are used in office or commercial printers to ensure uniform ink distribution during manifolding or duplicating operations.
[B41L 27/08] Troughs or like reservoirs with immersed, or partly immersed rollers -- The subclass B41L 27/08 covers printing apparatus that employ a trough or similar reservoir containing rollers that are immersed or partly immersed to pick up and transfer ink, providing continuous ink-feeding for office or commercial printers.
[B41L 27/10] with ink-ejecting means, e.g. pumps -- The CPC class B41L 27/10 covers printing machines that incorporate ink-ejecting mechanisms, such as pumps, to supply ink to the print zone. It applies to office or commercial devices that manifold, duplicate or print documents where ink is delivered by an active pumping system rather than passive capillary or gravity flow. This subclass refines broader inking arrangements by specifying the use of pumped ink ejection.
[B41L 27/12] Feed or duct rollers -- B41L 27/12 covers feed or duct rollers used in the inking arrangements of printing machines, particularly those that transport ink from a reservoir to the printing plate or cylinder. These rollers are part of apparatus designed for manifolding, duplicating or printing in office or commercial settings.
[B41L 27/14] adjustable for regulating supply -- The code refers to devices that provide a means of adjusting the amount of ink supplied to a printing unit. It includes adjustable reservoirs, valves, pumps or channel structures that let an operator regulate ink flow to maintain consistent print quality in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 27/16] Messenger or other moving transfer rollers -- B41L 27/16 concerns messenger or other moving transfer rollers used in printing apparatus. These rollers convey ink, toner, or a printed medium from one part of the machine to another, facilitating the transfer of images or text onto paper or other substrates in office and commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 27/18] Continuous, e.g. endless-band apparatus -- B41L 27/18 refers to continuous, endless-band printing apparatus in which the printing medium forms a looped or endless band that moves continuously through the machine, allowing uninterrupted inking and image transfer for high-speed office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 27/20] for inking selected parts of printing formes -- B41L 27/20 relates to devices or arrangements that ink only selected portions of a printing forme, allowing selective colour or pattern application in offset, flexographic or other printing processes. The technology is used in office or commercial presses to control ink distribution.
[B41L 27/22] Ink removing or collecting devices -- B41L 27/22 covers devices that remove or collect excess ink from printing surfaces or the print zone, such as ink traps, suction rollers, wipers and other mechanisms that prevent ink buildup during printing. These systems are used in office or commercial printers to improve print quality and reduce waste.
[B41L 27/24] for inking from interior of cylinder -- This subclass covers printing apparatus in which ink is supplied to a printing cylinder from the cylinder's interior, using internal ink channels, reservoirs or pumps that deliver ink through the cylinder wall to the surface, providing uniform inking and minimizing external contamination.
[B41L 27/26] Absorbent pads -- B41L 27/26 covers absorbent pads employed in the inking systems of printing equipment for office or commercial use. The pads serve to receive, retain, and transfer ink to printing cylinders, plates or rollers, facilitating consistent ink distribution during manifolding, duplicating or standard printing operations.
[B41L 27/28] Construction of inking rollers -- This CPC subgroup covers the design and construction features of rollers used to apply ink in printing machines, including materials, surface treatments and mechanisms for delivering ink uniformly. It falls under B41L 27/00 inking arrangements or devices, within the broader categories of printing apparatus for office or commercial use.
[B41L 27/30] Spray apparatus -- The B41L 27/30 class defines spray apparatus used in printing operations, covering devices that atomise or mist ink (or other marking fluids) onto a substrate for office or commercial printing purposes, including the mechanisms that control spray pattern and delivery.
[B41L 27/32] Arrangements for tripping, lifting, adjusting, or removing inking rollers -- Entry B41L 27/32 covers mechanisms that trip, lift, adjust or remove inking rollers in printing devices, enabling control of roller position and tension for efficient ink transfer. The code applies to apparatus used in office or commercial printing systems.
[B41L 27/34] Lifting or adjusting devices -- The B41L 27/34 subclass deals with lifting or adjusting devices used in in-king arrangements for printing apparatus. It covers mechanisms that raise, lower, tilt or otherwise reposition plates, rollers or other components to control ink application in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 27/36] Cams, eccentrics, wedges, or the like devices -- B41L 27/36 covers cams, eccentrics, wedges and similar mechanisms employed in the inking systems of printing apparatus for office or commercial use. These components adjust ink flow, pressure or distribution across printing plates or rollers, enabling precise or variable printing patterns.
[B41L 27/38] fluid-pressure operated -- B41L 27/38 relates to inking arrangements or devices for printing equipment that are powered by fluid pressure, such as pneumatic or hydraulic mechanisms delivering ink to a print cylinder or head. These systems are employed in office and commercial printing apparatus to control ink flow and distribution.
[B41L 27/40] magnetically operated -- B41L 27/40 refers to inking arrangements or devices that are operated by magnetic forces, typically used in printing or duplicating equipment to transfer or apply ink. The classification covers mechanisms where magnetic actuation controls the movement of ink-carrying elements, such as magnetic rollers, plates or brushes, to achieve precise ink deposition for office or commercial printing applications.
[B41L 29/00] Devices for attaching printing elements or formes to supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 29/02] magnetic -- B41L 29/02 refers to magnetic means for attaching printing elements, plates, formes or similar components to a support within printing equipment, enabling quick and secure mounting in office or commercial printing devices.
[B41L 29/04] for attaching printing elements to flat type-beds -- B41L 29/04 covers devices that secure printing elements-such as type, plates, or formes-directly onto flat type-beds in printing presses. The mechanisms enable precise positioning, rapid attachment and detachment, and stable fixation during the printing operation, facilitating efficient setup and repeatable image transfer.
[B41L 29/06] for attaching elements to forme cylinders -- This subclass covers mechanisms that secure printing forms, plates or other printing elements onto forme cylinders in printing machines. It includes clamps, brackets and similar fastening devices that enable precise attachment for office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 29/08] for attaching print formes to flat type-beds -- B41L 29/08 covers devices that secure print formes or other printing elements to flat type-beds in printing machines. These mechanisms attach the formes directly to the bed surface, ensuring precise positioning and stability during the printing process in office or commercial duplicating equipment. It falls under the broader category of apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing.
[B41L 29/10] for attaching non-deformable curved printing formes to forme cylinders -- This subclass concerns devices that attach rigid, curved printing formes to rotating forme cylinders in printing machines. The mechanisms secure the non-deformable formes to the cylinder, ensuring accurate positioning during repeated printing operations for office or commercial purposes.
[B41L 29/12] for attaching flexible printing formes -- B41L 29/12 relates to devices that secure flexible printing formes or elements to a substrate during printing operations. These mechanisms provide reliable attachment of plates, screens or other flexible formes to support structures in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 29/14] Clamping devices -- B41L 29/14 pertains to clamping devices that secure printing plates, cartridges or other printing elements to a support in printing apparatus for office or commercial use. The devices typically use adjustable jaws, hinges or locking mechanisms to maintain precise alignment during operation.
[B41L 29/16] operating automatically during operation of rotary machines to attach the printing formes to the forme cylinders -- B41L 29/16 covers automatic systems that, while a rotary press is running, affix printing formes to the forme cylinders. The classification applies to devices that attach printing elements without manual intervention, enhancing speed and precision in commercial printing operations.
[B41L 29/18] electromagnetic, pneumatic, or hydraulic -- B41L 29/18 relates to devices that secure printing elements or formes to a support by using electromagnetic, pneumatic or hydraulic means. Such mechanisms provide magnetic clamping, air-pressure or fluid-power actuation to hold plates, cylinders or other printing parts in place, allowing precise positioning and rapid release in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 29/20] for adjusting position of leading edges of flexible printing formes circumferentially of forme cylinders -- This CPC entry B41L 29/20 covers devices that adjust the circumferential position of the leading edges of flexible printing formes on form cylinders, enabling precise alignment during manifolding, duplicating or printing operations. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes.
[B41L 29/22] for attaching printing formes to intermediate supports, e.g. adapter members -- B41L 29/22 covers devices that attach printing formes to intermediate supports such as adapters, enabling the forme to be mounted on a different carrier before printing. These devices are used in office or commercial printing equipment that manifolds, duplicates or prints forms, and they facilitate flexible positioning and replacement of the printing elements.
[B41L 3/00] Platens or like sheet supports for manifolding using pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries, e.g. for book-keeping purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Manually operable devices like flat supports, clamps or guides for moving into and holding in registered position with respect to each other of blank paper or blank forms, e.g. business forms, pressure sensitive layers or intermediaries, e.g. carbons, and duplicates.  Also auxiliary devices specifically adapted for use with said platens or supports, technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 3/02] with stationary clamping means for holding the manifolding assembly in registered position, e.g. resilient clamps for holding non-perforated sheets -- This subclass deals with devices used in printing or duplicating operations where the sheet support, such as platens, includes fixed clamping mechanisms that keep the manifolding assembly aligned during operation, for example resilient clamps that hold non-perforated sheets in a registered position.
[B41L 3/04] Bars provided with pins engaging perforations in the elements -- The B41L 3/04 class covers platen-type sheet supports that use bars equipped with pins which engage perforations in the material being processed. These bars lock into pre-drilled holes to hold papers, cards or other sheets in place during manifolding, duplicating or printing operations, primarily for bookkeeping or commercial office use.
[B41L 3/06] with movable clamping or guiding means for the elements of the manifolding assembly -- The CPC subclass B41L 3/06 covers apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing in offices or commercial settings where the platen or sheet support includes movable clamping or guiding mechanisms for the manifolding elements, such as pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries used in bookkeeping and similar tasks.
[B41L 3/08] with non-mechanical means for holding the elements in registered position, e.g. magnetic means -- The CPC subclass B41L 3/08 covers platen-type sheet supports used in printing or duplicating devices where the sheets are held in registration by non-mechanical means such as magnets rather than by physical clamps or pins. These devices are intended for office or commercial manifolding applications.
[B41L 3/10] with means for moving assembled elements step by step relative to platen or support, e.g. for column or line selection -- This subgroup covers printing or duplicating machines that use a platen or similar sheet support together with a mechanism that shifts assembled items incrementally relative to the platen, such as selecting individual columns or lines during bookkeeping or other commercial printing operations.
[B41L 31/00] Devices for removing flexible printing formes from forme cylinders -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 3/12] Auxiliary devices -- The code B41L 3/12 designates auxiliary devices used with platens or similar sheet supports in manifolding, duplicating or printing operations. These devices help position, feed, align or apply pressure to sheets, particularly in pressure-sensitive or bookkeeping printing.
[B41L 3/14] Magazines or storage compartments, e.g. for slips -- This subclass covers magazines or storage compartments built into platen-type printing or duplicating machines that retain individual sheets, slips or forms, especially when a pressure-sensitive layer is used for bookkeeping or invoicing. It includes designs where the compartments feed or retrieve the media during the manifolding process.
[B41L 3/16] Web-feeding arrangements -- B41L 3/16 designates arrangements that feed a continuous web of material into printing or manifold apparatus, including platen or sheet-support systems that guide pressure-sensitive layers for tasks such as bookkeeping.
[B41L 3/18] Displaceable covers, e.g. with windows -- A B41L 3/18 item refers to sheet-support apparatus whose cover can be moved, such as plates or platens that slide, lift or pivot to expose the work area. It includes features like windows or transparent sections that allow observation of the material during manifolding, duplicating or printing operations.
[B41L 3/20] for facilitating manifolding in books -- B41L 3/20 classifies devices that aid the manifolding of sheets into books, such as platens or other sheet supports used with pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries in bookkeeping and similar applications. It falls under printing-related apparatus for office or commercial use that facilitate the formation of bound volumes.
[B41L 3/22] Applications of ink ribbons -- The code B41L 3/22 covers equipment that employs ink ribbons in printing or duplicating operations, particularly platen-type or sheet-support devices that use pressure-sensitive layers or intermediaries for office or commercial purposes such as bookkeeping. It includes the design and use of ribbon-feed mechanisms and related components within manifold or duplicating printers.
[B41L 33/00] Pressing flexible printing formes or sheets or webs of copy material against cylinders, e.g. for smoothing purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use.  Usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 35/00] Cylinders for apparatus or machines for manifolding, duplicating, or printing for office or other commercial purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cylinders and associated devices, e.g. bearings, tripping devices, adjusting devices, guards, covers, heating means, specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 35/02] Forme cylinders -- Forme cylinders are rotating cylinders that hold a printing forme or plate in offset, letterpress or similar printing machines, transferring ink onto the substrate. They are designed for office or commercial printing equipment to ensure even pressure and registration across the printed page. The classification covers their construction, material and mounting features for manifolding, duplicating or printing apparatus.
[B41L 35/04] Registering devices -- B41L 35/04 registers devices for printing cylinders, covering mechanisms that record, align or control the position of the printed sheet or image on the cylinder during manifolding, duplicating or office-type printing operations. These include registration plates, sensors or other devices that ensure accurate registration of successive impressions.
[B41L 35/06] with means for displacing the cylinders -- This subclass covers cylinders used in office or commercial printing, duplicating or manifolding machines that include mechanisms for moving or adjusting the cylinder during operation, allowing precise control of printing pressure, registration or feed. The displacement means may be hydraulic, pneumatic, mechanical or electronic.
[B41L 35/08] with means for displacing the printing formes on the cylinders -- The CPC entry B41L 35/08 covers cylinders used in office or commercial printing, duplicating, or manifolding machines that incorporate mechanisms for moving the printing formes around the cylinder surface during operation. This specification addresses the displacement means integrated into the cylinder design.
[B41L 35/10] Impression cylinders -- Impression cylinders are cylindrical components in printing, duplicating or manifolding machines that receive the inked image from a plate or a blanket and transfer it onto paper or other substrates. Situated within office or commercial printing equipment, they rotate in synchrony with other rollers to ensure uniform pressure and consistent print quality during high-speed production.
[B41L 35/12] Bearings or supports for forme, offset, or transfer or impression cylinders -- The CPC entry B41L 35/12 concerns the bearings, supports and related mounting hardware that hold or guide the forme, offset, transfer or impression cylinders in printing, duplicating or manifolding machines for office or commercial use. It includes shafts, bearing blocks, brackets and other devices that maintain cylinder alignment and facilitate smooth rotation during the printing process.
[B41L 35/14] Means for heating or cooling forme or impression cylinders -- B41L 35/14 covers devices that heat or cool the forme or impression cylinders of printing presses, enabling temperature control of the cylinder surface to improve ink transfer, drying, and dimensional stability during manifolding, duplicating or other commercial printing operations.
[B41L 35/16] Cylinder-tripping devices -- This subclass covers devices that interrupt or release the rotation of cylinders in printing, duplicating or manifolding machines, typically to control paper feed, enable sheet ejection or facilitate maintenance. The mechanisms include trip levers, cams, sensors and other actuators that cause a cylinder to stop or disengage during operation.
[B41L 35/18] Arrangements or dispositions of cylinder bearings, forks, or supports -- The entry concerns how the bearings, forks or support structures for cylinders are arranged or positioned in devices that use rotating cylinders for printing, duplicating or manifolding, typically in office or commercial equipment. It addresses the layout of these cylinder supports to ensure proper operation and alignment.
[B41L 35/20] Eccentric bearings -- Eccentric bearings are adjustable bearing assemblies mounted in printing cylinders that allow the axis of rotation to be shifted relative to the cylinder housing. By offsetting the bearing, the pressure applied to the printing surface can be varied, improving ink transfer and compensating for wear. They are used in office and commercial duplicating and manifolding machines.
[B41L 35/22] Sliding bearings -- The CPC subclass B41L 35/22 relates to sliding bearings employed in the cylinders of duplicating, printing or manifold-type apparatus, providing low-friction support that allows the cylinder to rotate smoothly while maintaining precise axial alignment.
[B41L 35/24] Swinging bearings -- Swinging bearings are rotating supports that allow the cylindrical components of printing, duplicating or manifold machines to pivot or tilt while maintaining alignment and smooth operation. They enable quick adjustment of the cylinder's position for feeding, loading or removing sheet material, improving accuracy and reducing wear in office and commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 35/26] Cylinder lifting or adjusting devices -- The entry covers mechanisms that raise, lower or otherwise adjust the position of cylinders in printing, duplicating or manifolding equipment used in offices or other commercial settings, enabling precise control of pressure and alignment during operation. It is classed under B41L 35/26.
[B41L 35/28] Cams, eccentrics, wedges, or the like -- This subclass covers cams, eccentrics, wedges and comparable mechanical elements that are incorporated into the cylinders of manifolding, duplicating or printing machines. These components shape, drive or adjust the printing surface in office or other commercial equipment.
[B41L 35/30] electrically or magnetically operated -- B41L 35/30 covers cylinders used in office or commercial manifolding, duplicating or printing machines that are driven by electrical or magnetic power. It includes any rotating cylinder that provides the surface for transferring ink or images in printers, copiers or similar equipment where the motion is electrically or magnetically operated.
[B41L 35/32] fluid-pressure operated -- B41L 35/32 relates to fluid-pressure operated cylinders used in apparatuses that fold, duplicate or print documents in office or commercial environments. The cylinders provide the mechanical force to drive paper-handling or printing mechanisms in such machines.
[B41L 35/34] Guards or covers, e.g. for safety purposes, for preventing egress or ingress of foreign matter -- B41L 35/34 classifies protective guards, safety covers and similar devices fitted to cylinders in printing, duplicating or manifold machines, designed to prevent the entry or exit of foreign matter and to protect operators.
[B41L 38/00] Devices for attaching coverings or make-ready devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 38/02] attaching to impression cylinders -- B41L 38/02 relates to devices that attach coverings or make-ready components to impression cylinders in printing presses, enabling the mounting of printing plates, blankets or other media onto the cylinder for subsequent printing operations.
[B41L 38/04] attaching of endless or like continuously-fed coverings -- B41L 38/04 covers mechanisms that secure endless or continuously-fed sheets, films or other coverings to a printing or duplicating machine. It includes devices such as clamps, rollers, suction units or other make-ready fittings that hold the material in place during feeding, alignment or printing operations.
[B41L 39/00] Indicating, counting, warning, control, or safety devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 39/02] Indicating devices, e.g. counters -- B41L 39/02 classifies indicating devices, such as counters, that are integrated with or applied to printing, duplicating or manifolding machines used in office or commercial settings. The code covers mechanisms that display count, warnings, control signals or safety information during the operation of these devices.
[B41L 39/04] Tripping devices or stop motions -- B41L 39/04 covers safety or control mechanisms incorporated in printing or duplicating equipment that intervene to halt motion, such as tripping devices or stop motions. These components are intended to prevent over-travel, jams or other hazardous conditions in office or commercial printing apparatus.
[B41L 39/06] for starting or stopping operation of sheet or web feed -- B41L 39/06 covers devices that start or stop the movement of a sheet or web in printing or copying machines, such as mechanisms that engage or disengage the feed rollers. These control elements are used in office or commercial printing equipment to regulate the timing of material feeding, ensuring precise initiation and termination of the feed cycle.
[B41L 39/08] for starting or stopping operation of cylinders -- B41L 39/08 relates to mechanisms that start or stop the operation of printing cylinders in office or commercial printing equipment. These control devices are used to regulate cylinder motion for timing, safety or process control within manifolding, duplicating or printing apparatus.
[B41L 39/10] for starting or stopping operation of damping or inking units -- B41L 39/10 refers to devices that start or stop the operation of damping or inking units in printing apparatus, providing control over ink flow and unit activation for office or commercial printing systems.
[B41L 39/12] for starting or stopping the machines as a whole -- B41L 39/12 relates to control devices that start or stop a printing machine as a whole, including start-stop switches, interlocks and emergency-stop mechanisms incorporated in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 39/14] Automatic control of tripping devices by feelers, photoelectric devices, pneumatic devices, or other detectors -- B41L 39/14 covers automatic control of tripping mechanisms for printers and similar office equipment using feelers, photo-electric, pneumatic or other detectors, enabling the machine to stop or adjust operation when paper or other conditions are detected. It falls under apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing, specifically devices that indicate, count, warn, control or ensure safety.
[B41L 39/16] Programming systems for automatic control of sequence of operations -- The B41L 39/16 subclass covers software or firmware that programmatically controls the order of steps performed by printing or duplicating equipment, such as sequencing print runs, job scheduling and automated workflow management. It includes programming methods, control algorithms and system architectures that automatically direct the operation of office or commercial printing devices.
[B41L 41/00] Cleaning arrangements or devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 41/02] for forme cylinders -- B41L 41/02 covers cleaning arrangements or devices specifically designed for forme cylinders in printing presses, such as brushes, spray systems or solvent applicators that remove ink and debris to maintain print quality. These solutions are used in office or commercial printing equipment to keep cylinder surfaces clean during operation.
[B41L 41/04] for inking rollers -- B41L 41/04 covers cleaning arrangements or devices specifically designed for inking rollers used in printing apparatuses. It pertains to mechanisms that remove residual ink from roller surfaces to maintain print quality and prevent fouling during office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 41/06] for offset cylinders -- B41L 41/06 covers cleaning arrangements or devices specifically designed for offset cylinders in printing equipment. It includes systems that remove ink, fouling or other contaminants from the cylinder surfaces to maintain print quality and prolong cylinder life in office or commercial offset printing operations.
[B41L 43/00] Auxiliary folding, collecting, or depositing of sheets or webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices specifically adapted for small scale printing apparatus suitable for office use,  usual output of a low number, e.g. less than hundred, of printed subject matter.
[B41L 43/02] Folding -- The B41L 43/02 subclass relates to devices that fold sheets or webs within printing, copying or other office-type equipment, including folding units built into printers, copiers or finishing machines and auxiliary stations that collect and deposit the folded material for subsequent handling.
[B41L 43/04] lengthwise -- B41L 43/04 covers auxiliary mechanisms that handle sheets or webs lengthwise in printing equipment, such as devices that fold, collect or deposit paper along its longest edge for feeding, aligning or stacking in office or commercial printers.
[B41L 43/06] crosswise -- B41L 43/06 crosswise classifies printing apparatus features that handle sheets or webs in a direction transverse to the main feed, such as mechanisms that fold, collect or deposit printed pages side-by-side or in overlapping rows while the material moves across the machine.
[B41L 43/08] Folding-cylinders or drums -- B41L 43/08 covers folding cylinders or drums, components in printing equipment that fold, collect, or deposit sheets or webs after printing. These devices are used in office or commercial presses to produce folded documents such as brochures, flyers, or forms.
[B41L 43/10] Collecting -- The B41L 43/10 subclass covers mechanisms that gather printed sheets or webs after they leave the printing zone, such as collectors, stackers, conveyors, or devices that fold, separate or deposit the output for further handling or storage in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 43/12] and stapling -- B41L 43/12 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers apparatus or devices for performing auxiliary operations such as folding, collecting, or depositing sheets or webs, specifically including stapling functions for office or commercial printing purposes.
[B41L 43/14] Adding inserts -- B41L 43/14 covers devices that automatically insert additional sheets, forms, or other inserts into a stack of printed material during the folding, collecting, or depositing stage of a printing process. The inserts may be blank, pre-printed, or contain supplemental information and are positioned before or after the main sheet.
[B41L 43/16] Depositing -- The B41L 43/16 subclass covers the depositing step in printing equipment, specifically mechanisms that auxiliary-fold, collect, or place sheets or webs after they have been printed, preparing them for further handling or output.
[B41L 45/00] Addressing machines or like series-printing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines using printing plates, separate printing devices and printing surfaces carried by belts or chains,  devices for other operations, e.g. franking or collating, forming part of an addressing machine are included,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 45/02] using printing plates -- The B41L 45/02 subclass pertains to the use of printing plates in addressing machines or similar series-printing devices, facilitating selective ink transfer for office or commercial printing applications.
[B41L 45/04] composed on type-setting machines -- B41L 45/04 refers to features that compose material on typesetting machines, covering mechanisms that arrange characters or images for printing in office or commercial series-printing devices such as addressing machines. This subclass falls under performing operations-printing-apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing.
[B41L 45/06] for addressing combined with other operations, e.g. franking, collating documents -- This CPC subclass covers devices and methods that perform addressing (e.g., printing postal or other address marks) together with additional functions such as franking, collating, or other document handling steps, within office or commercial printing apparatus. It applies to addressing machines or series-printing machines that integrate these combined operations.
[B41L 45/08] with printing surfaces in the form of belts, or carried by chains -- A subclass for addressing machines or similar series-printing devices that employ printing surfaces formed as belts or mounted on chains, allowing the image to be transferred onto a moving belt or chain rather than directly onto paper. It pertains to equipment used in office or commercial settings for manifolding, duplicating or printing operations.
[B41L 45/10] for addressing combined with other operations, e.g. franking, collating documents -- This CPC subclass B41L 45/10 covers addressing functions performed together with other operations such as franking or document collating in address machines or series-printing equipment. It falls under the hierarchy performing operations - printing - apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or commercial purposes - addressing machines or like series-printing machines.
[B41L 45/12] with separate devices for printing additional texts or images, e.g. for printing receipts on blank sheets or webs -- B41L 45/12 covers addressing or series-printing machines that incorporate separate devices for adding extra text or images to a printed product, such as printing receipts, barcodes or logos on blank sheets or continuous webs. It applies to office or commercial equipment that performs this dual-printing function.
[B41L 47/00] Details of addressographs or like series-printing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printing surfaces, devices for handling printing plates, printing mechanisms, devices for text or image selection, mechanisms for conveying copy material, indicating, warning, control and safety devices and auxiliary devices specifically adapted for addressographs,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 47/02] Applications of printing surfaces in addressing machines or like series-printing machines -- B41L 47/02 covers the use of printing surfaces in machines that automatically apply addresses or other data, such as addressing devices and series-printing equipment. It includes the design, arrangement or treatment of the surfaces that receive ink for imprinting on mail pieces or similar items.
[B41L 47/04] of flat or curved plates for relief printing -- This subclass covers the design, handling and use of flat or curved relief-printing plates that carry raised images for ink transfer, as employed in addressographs and similar series-printing machines used in office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 47/06] of flat or curved stencils -- B41L 47/06 covers apparatus that employ flat or curved stencils for series-printing operations such as addressographs. The code applies to devices that manipulate a stencil to transfer ink onto a substrate, typically used in office or commercial environments for repetitive text or image reproduction.
[B41L 47/08] of flat or curved plates for hectographic printing -- B41L 47/08 covers the use of flat or curved plates in hectographic printing, including addressographs and similar series-printing machines that produce multiple copies for office or commercial purposes.
[B41L 47/10] of printing surfaces in the form of belts or chains -- B41L 47/10 pertains to printing apparatus that employ continuous surfaces such as belts or chains as the medium onto which ink or toner is transferred, typically found in office or commercial machines that duplicate or print documents.
[B41L 47/12] Auxiliary devices, e.g. for flattening plates, for assembling plates in predetermined order, for wetting stencils -- In the CPC, B41L 47/12 covers auxiliary devices used with series-printing machines such as addressographs, including mechanisms that flatten printing plates, arrange them in a set order, or wet stencils before printing. These devices are employed in office or commercial printing equipment for manifolding and duplicating.
[B41L 47/14] Devices or arrangements for storing or handling plates -- This subclass covers equipment used to store, transport, feed or organize printing plates in addressograph-type or other series-printing devices, including trays, racks, carousel mechanisms and plate-handling fixtures designed for office or commercial printing apparatus. It is positioned within the broader group for apparatus or devices for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes.
[B41L 47/16] Magazines -- B41L 47/16 pertains to magazines, the removable containers that hold pre-printed forms, address plates or similar media in addressograph-type or related series-printing machines used for office or commercial duplication.
[B41L 47/18] Devices for feeding the plates in their plane -- The subclass B41L 47/18 pertains to mechanisms that transport printing plates while keeping them parallel to the printing plane in addressograph-type or other series-printing machines. It includes plate feeders, conveyors, cam-driven or pneumatic indexes and other devices that ensure correct alignment and continuous feeding during the printing operation.
[B41L 47/20] Devices for feeding the plates otherwise than in their plane, e.g. transversely thereto -- This subclass covers devices that feed printing plates into a machine in a direction other than parallel to the plate surface, such as transverse feeding mechanisms used in addressograph or similar series-printing equipment. It falls under CPC B41L 47/20 within the hierarchy of performing operations-printing-apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or commercial purposes.
[B41L 47/22] with means for presenting plates for repeated printing operations -- This subclass covers apparatuses that automatically present or feed printing plates in manifold or duplicating machines, enabling repeated print cycles without manual plate changes, as found in addressographs and other series-printing equipment.
[B41L 47/24] Mechanisms for conveying copy material through addressographs or like series-printing machines -- The subclass B41L 47/24 pertains to mechanisms that convey copy material through addressographs or comparable series-printing machines, such as belts, rollers, chains or other feeder devices that move cards, envelopes or forms past the printing zone. These mechanisms are employed in office or commercial apparatus designed for manifolding, duplicating or printing multiple items in sequence.
[B41L 47/26] for conveying or positioning single sheetlike articles, e.g. envelopes -- B41L 47/26 covers mechanisms that convey or position individual sheet-like items such as envelopes within printing or duplicating equipment. The code is used for devices that handle single sheets during office or commercial printing processes.
[B41L 47/28] with gauging-rulers or the like, e.g. for facilitating hand-printing of copy material fed from stacks -- The subclass B41L 47/28 covers printing or duplicating devices that incorporate gauging-rulers or similar guides to aid manual hand-printing of copy material drawn from stacked sources. It belongs to the broader group of apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or commercial use.
[B41L 47/30] for conveying webs -- B41L 47/30 covers mechanisms that convey continuous webs of material through printing or duplicating machines, such as rollers, belts or other feed systems, enabling the web to be positioned for printing, copying or other processing in office or commercial equipment.
[B41L 47/32] combined with devices for other purposes, e.g. for cutting, severing, gluing -- This subclass covers printing apparatus that incorporates additional functions such as cutting, severing or gluing, typically found in addressograph or series-printing machines used for office or commercial manifold printing. It lies within B41L 47/ and concerns devices that combine printing with secondary operations to produce finished documents in a single pass.
[B41L 47/34] specially adapted for conveying chains of forms -- B41L 47/34 covers printing or duplicating machines that include mechanisms specially adapted to transport a series of connected forms as a chain through the device. The classification applies to apparatus that moves, aligns and feeds linked sheets or forms continuously for office or commercial printing operations.
[B41L 47/36] for conveying sheets or webs for tabulating purposes -- This subclass covers mechanisms that move sheets or continuous webs in printing or duplicating machines when they are used for tabulating data. It includes feed, transport and alignment devices that ensure precise positioning of paper during the tabulation process. The classification applies to office or commercial equipment designed for forms, invoices or similar sheet-based data collection.
[B41L 47/38] with clamping means for head or margin -- The classification B41L 47/38 covers addressographs and similar series-printing machines that include clamping mechanisms to secure the print head or the paper margin during operation.
[B41L 47/40] with means for automatically reciprocating sheet or web transversely to enable addresses to be printed in columns side by side -- B41L 47/40 pertains to addressograph or similar series-printing machines equipped with mechanisms that automatically move a sheet or web back and forth, perpendicular to its main direction, allowing for the printing of addresses in adjacent columns. This facilitates efficient and organized printing for office or commercial use.
[B41L 47/42] Printing mechanisms -- B41L 47/42 covers printing mechanisms used in addressograph-type and similar series-printing machines that produce multiple identical or sequentially numbered impressions. The classification falls under apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or commercial purposes.
[B41L 47/44] using flat platens -- B41L 47/44 covers the use of flat platens in addressograph-type or similar series-printing machines, where a flat plate holds the printed image while the medium passes over it. It relates to apparatus that manifolds, duplicates or prints for office or commercial purposes, specifically detailing how flat platens are employed in these printing operations.
[B41L 47/46] using line-contact members, e.g. rollers, cylinders -- This subclass covers devices that employ line-contact members such as rollers or cylinders to transfer ink or impression in addressograph or similar series-printing machines, enabling continuous or repeated printing of forms or labels.
[B41L 47/48] with inking or ink-ribbon devices -- B41L 47/48 includes printing or addressograph machines that use an inking system or ink-ribbon to apply ink to the substrate. The devices typically operate in office or commercial environments for manifolding, duplicating or continuous series printing.
[B41L 47/50] using multiple impression-members or -surfaces, e.g. for printing series of addresses with standing context, for printing from selected parts of printing surfaces -- B41L 47/50 relates to printing apparatus that employ two or more impression members or surfaces to produce repeated outputs, such as addressographs that print series of addresses by using multiple plates or by selecting portions of a printing surface. The class covers devices designed for office or commercial use where several impressions are made in a single operation to create manifold printed information.
[B41L 47/52] with movable counter-pressure plates for printing from selected areas of printing surfaces -- The subclass B41L 47/52 covers printing apparatus that employ movable counter-pressure plates to apply pressure only to selected portions of a printing surface, enabling selective imprinting of characters or graphics. It is used in addressograph-type and other series-printing machines for office or commercial applications.
[B41L 47/54] with means for automatically reciprocating printing plate transversely to enable addresses to be printed in columns side by side -- B41L 47/54 pertains to addressograph or similar series-printing devices equipped with an automatic transverse reciprocating mechanism for the printing plate, enabling addresses to be printed side by side in parallel columns.
[B41L 47/56] Indicating, warning, control, or safety devices -- Subclass B41L 47/56 covers indicating, warning, control and safety devices incorporated in addressograph or similar series-printing machines. It includes visual or audible signals, interlocks and other mechanisms that alert users, show operating status or protect against unsafe conditions during printing.
[B41L 47/58] Arrangements or devices for selecting, or for facilitating selection of, text or image to be printed -- The classification covers mechanical or electronic arrangements that enable an operator to choose or aid the choice of specific text or image for printing in addressograph-type or other series-printing machines. It includes selection levers, memory registers, indexing plates or control circuits that determine which content is transferred to the print medium.
[B41L 47/60] Markings applied to printing plates, e.g. code marks, colours, clips, perforations, edge notches, projections -- B41L 47/60 covers the application of markings to printing plates, such as coded symbols, colour codes, clips, perforations, edge notches or other projections that aid registration, identification or handling of plates in addressograph or similar series-printing machines.
[B41L 47/62] Selecting devices, e.g. cams, windows, positions indicators -- B41L 47/62 covers selecting mechanisms such as cams, windows or position indicators that control the arrangement of characters on addressographs and comparable series-printing machines used for office or commercial printing.
[B41L 47/64] Automatic selecting devices with or without overriding manual control, e.g. with scanning-fingers, with presetting controls operable by push-buttons or with program control by punched tapes -- B41L 47/64 covers automatic selecting mechanisms for addressographs or similar series-printing machines, including systems that can be overridden manually. It includes devices that use scanning fingers, preset controls operated by push-buttons, or program control via punched-tape sequences to choose printing characters.
[B41L 49/00] Accessories or attachments for addressographs or like series-printing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories or attachments, e.g. counting, numbering or dating devices specifically adapted for addressographs,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 49/02] Counting, numbering, or dating devices -- B41L 49/02 covers counting, numbering or dating mechanisms that are attached to addressographs or similar series-printing machines, providing automatic sequential numbering, tallying or date imprinting on printed forms for office or commercial use. These devices are accessories that enhance the primary printing apparatus with record-keeping functions.
[B41L 49/04] Devices for applying selection markings to printing plates -- The CPC subclass B41L 49/04 covers devices that apply selection markings to printing plates, indicating which areas of a plate are to be printed, and is used with addressographs or similar series-printing machines.
[B41L 5/00] Autographic registers or like manifolding apparatus using movable strips or webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Portable box-like devices having a writing underlay almost in form of a flat plate for filling out forms, e.g. business forms, by handwriting, and a storage compartment for storing blank paper or blank forms, e.g. business forms, together with pressure sensitive layers or intermediaries, e.g. carbons, and/or for storing duplicates.  Also auxiliary devices specifically adapted for use with said registers, technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 5/02] with means for limiting movements of webs fed by hand -- This subclass covers devices for printing or registering that employ movable strips or webs handled by hand, and it includes mechanisms that limit or control the movement of those webs to maintain proper alignment, spacing or indexing during operation. It is typically applied to office or commercial autographic registers and similar manifolding equipment.
[B41L 5/04] with mechanisms for feeding webs or for arranging web feed -- B41L 5/04 covers printing or manifolding devices that include mechanisms to feed the paper or other web material, or to arrange the way the web is advanced during operation. It applies to autographic registers and similar office or commercial equipment where movable strips or webs are used.
[B41L 5/06] by means of rollers, wheels, or chains transversely -- The B41L 5/06 subclass concerns autographic registers or similar manifold printers that move the web across the printing surface using transverse rollers, wheels or chain mechanisms to advance the material during operation.
[B41L 5/08] by reciprocating mechanisms -- B41L 5/08 covers autographic registers and similar manifold devices that employ movable strips or webs, where the motion of the material is produced by reciprocating mechanisms. The classification applies to office and commercial printing or duplicating equipment that uses back-and-forth movement to advance the recording medium.
[B41L 5/10] with mechanisms for feeding the pressure-sensitive web or webs separately from the other webs, e.g. transversely -- B41L 5/10 covers apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing-such as autographic registers-that include separate feeding mechanisms for a pressure-sensitive web, allowing it to be advanced independently of other webs, often by transverse motion. This enables precise registration of carbonless or similar forms in office or commercial settings.
[B41L 5/12] with means for indicating exhaustion of web supply -- B41L 5/12 pertains to printing apparatus that uses movable strips or webs, such as autographic registers, and incorporates a mechanism to indicate that the web supply is exhausted. It falls within the CPC group for manifolding, duplicating or printing devices for office and commercial use.
[B41L 5/14] with auxiliary means for printing, perforating, or severing the web -- The B41L 5/14 subclass covers manifolding or duplicating machines, such as autographic registers, that incorporate auxiliary devices for printing on, perforating, or cutting a moving web or strip. It includes any additional mechanisms that perform these functions while the web is being fed through the apparatus, typically for office or commercial use.
[B41L 5/16] Accessories, e.g. drawers for storing forms, for money -- This subclass covers accessories for autographic registers or similar manifolding devices that use movable strips or webs, specifically storage drawers intended to hold paper forms, cash or related items in office or commercial environments. It is situated under printing apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing.
[B41L 7/00] Apparatus or machines for duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for duplicating on sheets or webs,  clay or gelatine is used,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 7/02] by passing original and copy-sheet or -web between rollers -- The subclass B41L 7/02 covers duplicating or printing machines in which the original document and the copy sheet or web are conveyed together through a series of rollers, allowing the copy to be produced while both materials pass between the rollers. It applies to office- or commercial-type apparatus that forms copies by this roller-passing arrangement.
[B41L 7/04] with means for guiding original or copy-sheet or -web -- B41L 7/04 covers office or commercial duplicating and printing machines that incorporate mechanisms to guide the original document, copy sheet or printing web through the device, ensuring proper alignment and feed during operation.
[B41L 7/06] with means for severing copy-sheet or -web -- B41L 7/06 designates printing or duplicating devices that incorporate a cutting mechanism to separate the finished copy-sheet or continuous web from the rest of the material, enabling automatic severing of individual pages in office or commercial printing equipment.
[B41L 7/08] with means for moistening or drying -- B41L 7/08 designates office or commercial duplicators and printers that incorporate a system to moisten the printing surface or print medium, or to dry it after ink application. The moistening function is used for processes such as offset printing where a dampening roller prepares the plate, while drying mechanisms accelerate ink set-off for faster handling. This subgroup covers any integrated means for these wet-or-dry operations within the machine.
[B41L 9/00] Apparatus for indirectly duplicating from hectographic originals by means of hectographic intermediaries or transfer surfaces, i.e. "dry duplicators" -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flat supports or rotary cylinders carrying hectographic gelatine paper sheets of webs,  clay or gelatine is used,  technology mostly used until the year 2000.
[B41L 9/02] Containers for clay or gelatin -- A container for clay or gelatin is a receptacle that holds the transfer medium used in dry hectographic duplicators, where the material forms the surface that receives ink from the original and transfers it to copies. It is designed to protect, store and supply the medium within apparatuses for indirect duplication.
[B41L 9/04] with flat supports over which gelatin-paper is stretched -- This CPC subclass B41L 9/04 covers apparatus for indirectly duplicating from hectographic originals by using flat supports on which gelatin-paper is stretched, i.e. dry duplicators that employ a flat gelatin surface to transfer images. It falls under printing apparatus for office or commercial use.
[B41L 9/06] and with carriages for feeding the sheets -- This CPC subclass B41L 9/06 covers apparatus for indirect duplication of hectographic originals that employ dry-duplicator techniques, using hectographic intermediaries or transfer surfaces. The entry specifically includes devices equipped with carriages that feed the sheets through the duplicating mechanism. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and apparatus for manifolding, duplicating or printing for office or other commercial purposes.
[B41L 9/08] and with devices for rolling-in and securing hectographic gelatin-paper webs -- B41L 9/08 refers to equipment that indirectly duplicates documents by means of hectographic intermediaries, specifically machines that roll in and secure gelatin-paper webs used as transfer surfaces in dry duplication. These devices are intended for office or commercial printing applications.
[B41L 9/10] with rotary cylinders carrying sheets of hectographic gelatin-paper -- The B41L 9/10 subclass covers apparatus that duplicates documents indirectly by feeding sheets of hectographic gelatin-paper over rotary cylinders, which transfer the image from a hectographic original onto the paper. It is a type of dry duplicator that uses rotating cylinders to move the gelatin-treated sheets through the duplication process.
[B41M] PRINTING, DUPLICATING, MARKING, OR COPYING PROCESSES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Printing apparatuses -> B41F , B41J - Producing decorative effects -> B44C - Special designs or pictures -> B44F - Printing textiles -> D06P - Photomechanical (UV-photosensitive) recording -> G03F - Xerography -> G03G - Printing printed circuits -> H05K - Printing semiconductor devices -> H10
[B41M 1/00] Inking and printing with a printer's forme -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B41M 1/02] Letterpress printing, e.g. book printing -- B41M 1/02 covers the traditional letterpress method where inked forms press paper to create printed pages, commonly used for book printing and other fine-type reproductions. The process involves setting type or image plates, inking them, and applying pressure with a press to transfer the ink onto the substrate in a single pass. This entry is part of the broader printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes hierarchy.
[B41M 1/04] Flexographic printing -- B41M 1/04 pertains to flexographic printing, a relief process in which flexible, ink-covered plates transfer image onto a substrate through a rotating cylinder. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and specific inking and printing with a printer's forme, and is used chiefly for high-speed production of packaging, labels and similar printed goods.
[B41M 1/06] Lithographic printing -- Lithographic printing, classified under B41M 1/06, covers processes in which a printer's forme is inked and subsequently used to transfer an image onto a substrate. It includes the methods and equipment for preparing the forme, applying ink, and performing the actual print run in lithography. This falls within the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and printing-duplicating-marking-or-copying processes.
[B41M 1/08] Dry printing -- Dry printing refers to a process where a printer's forme transfers ink onto a substrate without using liquid inks, typically using dry ink or powder that is fused to the material. It is employed in printing, duplicating, marking or copying operations where the forme directly produces the image on the final media.
[B41M 1/10] Intaglio printing -- Intaglio printing is a process in which an image is incised or engraved into a printing surface, the recessed lines retain ink while the raised areas remain clean, and the inked pattern is transferred to a substrate under pressure. It falls under the CPC subclass for inking and printing with a printer's forme.
[B41M 1/12] Stencil printing -- B41M 1/12 covers stencil printing, a process where ink is transferred through a pre-formed stencil or mask onto a substrate using a printer's forme. It is used for producing patterns, lettering or images in applications such as screen printing, textile printing and printed circuit boards.
[B41M 1/125] using a field of force, e.g. an electrostatic field, or an electric current -- B41M 1/125 covers printing methods that employ a field of force-such as an electrostatic field or an electric current-to move ink, toner or other marking material from a printer's forme onto a substrate, a technique used in electrophotographic and similar electrostatic printing processes.
[B41M 1/14] Multicolour printing -- The B41M 1/14 class covers multicolour printing processes that use a printer's forme to apply more than one ink colour in a single pass or successive passes, typically involving registration of multiple colour plates.
[B41M 1/16] using different inks which flow into one another to produce iridescent effects -- B41M 1/16 relates to printing processes that employ several inks which flow together on the printer's forme to generate iridescent, colour-shifting effects. It covers the use of blended inks to produce visual changes as the viewing angle varies.
[B41M 1/18] Printing one ink over another -- The code B41M 1/18 covers processes where a second ink is applied over a previously printed ink layer, such as over-printing, registration of multiple colours, or adding a finishing ink on a printed substrate. It is part of the printing, duplicating and marking operations hierarchy.
[B41M 1/20] by applying differently-coloured inks simultaneously to different parts of the printing surface -- B41M 1/20 relates to printing processes in which different coloured inks are applied simultaneously to separate regions of the printing surface, typically using a printer's forme that carries multiple ink zones. This sub-class falls under inking and printing with a printer's forme within the broader categories of printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes.
[B41M 1/22] Metallic printing -- B41M 1/22 covers printing operations in which a metallic ink or alloy is applied using a printer's forme, enabling the transfer of metallic material onto a substrate for creating printed, duplicated, marked or copied images. It falls under performing operations → printing → inking and printing with a printer's forme.
[B41M 1/24] combined with embossing -- The subclass B41M 1/24 covers processes that apply ink to a substrate with a printer's forme while simultaneously producing a raised relief on the same paper or material. It includes combined printing-and-embossing operations where the emboss pattern is created in the same step as the inked print.
[B41M 1/26] Printing on other surfaces than ordinary paper -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-digital printing methods, i.e. "conventional" printing methods, used for printing on other surfaces than orfinary paper, including: metals, organic plastics, horn or similar materials, rubber, glass or ceramic surfaces, wooden surfaces, leather or linoleum.  **Glossary:** - Ordinary paper: sheets made of traditional paper mass
[B41M 1/28] on metals -- B41M 1/28 covers the application of inked printing techniques directly onto metal surfaces using a printer's forme. It includes processes such as stamping, embossing or otherwise transferring ink to metal plates, sheets or components for marking, labeling or decorative purposes.
[B41M 1/30] on organic plastics, horn or similar materials -- The B41M 1/30 subclass concerns printing processes where a printer's forme (type block or mould) is inked and then used to transfer the image onto organic plastics, horn or comparable materials, covering the specific ink-application step for these substrates.
[B41M 1/305] using mechanical, physical or chemical means, e.g. corona discharge, etching or organic solvents, to improve ink retention -- B41M 1/305 covers methods that enhance ink retention on a printer's forme by applying mechanical, physical or chemical treatments such as corona discharge, etching or the use of organic solvents, thereby improving the quality and durability of printed images.
[B41M 1/32] on rubber -- The subclass B41M 1/32 covers printing operations in which rubber forms-such as flexible plates, blankets or sleeves-are used to receive ink and transfer the image to a substrate. It includes methods of inking, applying pressure and drying that are specific to rubber-based printing formes. This falls under the broader categories of printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes.
[B41M 1/34] on glass or ceramic surfaces -- The subclass addresses printing operations where a printer's forme is inked and the image is transferred onto glass or ceramic surfaces, typically using screen, pad or offset techniques for decorative or functional marking. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations - printing - printing, duplicating, marking, or copying processes - inking and printing with a printer's forme.
[B41M 1/36] on pretreated paper, e.g. parchment, oiled paper, paper for registration purposes -- This subclass concerns printing on pretreated paper such as parchment, oiled paper or registration paper using a printer's forme. It falls under inking and printing with a forme within broader printing and performing-operations categories, emphasizing the treated substrate rather than the ink or image.
[B41M 1/38] on wooden surfaces, leather, or linoleum -- The CPC subclass B41M 1/38 covers inking and printing with a printer's forme when the substrate is a wooden surface, leather or linoleum. It falls under printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes in the performing operations hierarchy, addressing methods and apparatuses for transferring ink onto these non-paper materials.
[B41M 1/40] Printing on bodies of particular shapes, e.g. golf balls, candles, wine corks -- Printing on bodies of particular shapes, such as golf balls, candles or wine corks, is covered by CPC B41M 1/40. The subclass deals with methods and apparatus for applying inks, pigments or other markings onto three-dimensional objects with defined contours, using techniques like pad printing, transfer printing, ink-jet or similar processes designed for irregular surfaces.
[B41M 1/42] Printing without contact between forme and surface to be printed, e.g. by using electrostatic fields -- The CPC class B41M 1/42 covers non-contact printing techniques in which the image is transferred to a surface without the printing forme touching it, typically using electrostatic fields to attract ink or toner onto the substrate. It includes methods such as electrostatic and electrophotographic processes that create prints without direct mechanical contact.
[B41M 2205/00] Printing methods or features related to printing methods -- B41M 2205/00 pertains to inventions that concern the way a print is produced, including particular printing techniques, process steps, or functional features that affect the printing operation. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and printing, duplicating, marking, or copying processes.
[B41M 2205/02] Dye diffusion thermal transfer printing (D2T2) -- The code B41M 2205/02 covers dye-diffusion thermal transfer printing (D2T2), a process where heat causes a dye to move from a donor layer onto a receiving substrate, producing high-resolution images on labels, textiles or packaging. It is placed under performing operations, printing, printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes and printing methods or features related to printing methods.
[B41M 2205/04] Direct thermal recording [DTR] -- B41M 2205/04 covers direct thermal recording, a printing process that creates images by applying heat directly to a thermally sensitive medium without using ink or toner, often employed in receipt printers and label makers. This method forms marks through temperature-induced color change in the recording material.
[B41M 2205/06] relating to melt (thermal) mass transfer -- B41M 2205/06 covers printing or marking techniques in which the image-forming material is transferred in a melted, thermally fluid state, typically by heating the material so it flows onto a substrate and solidifies. It includes thermal-transfer, hot-metal and other melt-based mass-transfer processes used in printing, duplicating, marking or copying.
[B41M 2205/08] Ablative thermal transfer, i.e. the exposed transfer medium is propelled from the donor to a receptor by generation of a gas -- Ablative thermal transfer refers to printing methods where heat causes the transfer medium on a donor sheet to generate gas, propelling the material onto a receptor to form an image. This process is classified under printing, duplicating, marking or copying operations.
[B41M 2205/10] Post-imaging transfer of imaged layer -- The B41M 2205/10 subclass covers printing processes in which an image-bearing layer is formed on a first medium and then transferred to a second substrate after the imaging step. It includes methods such as post-exposure transfer of polymer or ink layers, and other techniques that move the completed image from the original carrier to the final product.
[B41M 2205/12] Preparation of material for subsequent imaging, e.g. corona treatment, simultaneous coating, pre-treatments -- B41M 2205/12 covers processes that prepare a substrate for later imaging, such as applying surface treatments, coatings, or corona discharge before printing. It includes methods that modify the material's surface energy or apply functional layers to improve ink adherence or image quality.
[B41M 2205/14] Production or use of a mask -- The B41M 2205/14 subclass relates to techniques and devices for producing or employing masks in printing operations, such as stencils, plates or other patterned holders that define where ink or other media are applied. It addresses the creation, preparation, handling and use of these masks within various printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes.
[B41M 2205/16] Correction processes or materials -- The B41M 2205/16 subclass relates to techniques and substances employed to correct printed output, including processes for removing, covering, or repairing errors on printed media. It covers corrective inks, powders, solvents, or other materials, as well as methods such as erasing, masking, or re-printing to restore the intended appearance of a printed image. This falls under printing methods and features within the broader printing operations hierarchy.
[B41M 2205/18] Erasure -- The CPC subclass B41M 2205/18 covers erasure mechanisms used in printing, duplicating, marking or copying operations. It includes methods for removing or correcting printed marks, such as ink-jet cleaning, laser de-inking, mechanical wiping or chemical dissolution of deposited material.
[B41M 2205/20] Stability against chemicals, e.g. grease -- This subclass concerns printing processes or media that are designed to resist degradation caused by chemicals such as greases, oils, solvents or similar substances, often by using protective coatings, chemically resistant materials or specific treatments to preserve print quality.
[B41M 2205/22] improving gradation of image -- B41M 2205/22 covers printing techniques that enhance the gradation of an image, such as methods for improving tonal transitions, density control, or shading quality during the printing process. It pertains to any measure that yields smoother or more accurate color or tone reproduction.
[B41M 2205/24] Reactive compound reacting in image receiving layer other than leuco dyes or mordants -- The code B41M 2205/24 refers to printing processes that employ a reactive compound in the image-receiving layer, where the compound forms an image by reaction but is not a leuco dye or mordant. It covers the chemical formulation of the receiving layer and the corresponding method of developing the image.
[B41M 2205/26] Donor or receiver with registry means -- The subclass refers to printing processes where either the donor element that supplies ink or the receiver surface that receives the image includes a registration system-such as marks, pins, sensors or other means-to ensure precise alignment of successive impressions or colors, especially in multi-color or multi-layer printing.
[B41M 2205/28] Storage stability -- The B41M 2205/28 subclass covers aspects of how printed media, inks, toners or other printing consumables retain their properties during storage. It includes measures of shelf-life, resistance to drying, clumping, chemical change or other deterioration that could affect print quality when the material is kept before use. This classification is used to protect inventions that improve storage stability of printing supplies.
[B41M 2205/30] Thermal donors, e.g. thermal ribbons -- Thermal donors, such as thermal ribbons, are consumables used in heat-activated printing processes where a heated print head transfers ink from the ribbon onto a substrate. The entry covers the ribbons themselves, their composition, and features that affect the transfer efficiency and image quality in thermal transfer printing. This includes ribbons for label, barcode and other specialized printing applications.
[B41M 2205/32] Thermal receivers -- Thermal receivers are media that develop an image when heated by a printhead, including heat-sensitive papers, ribbons or other substrates used in direct-thermal and thermal-transfer printing. The class covers their structure, materials and any features that affect how they absorb or react to thermal energy during the printing process.
[B41M 2205/34] Both sides of a layer or material are treated, e.g. coated -- This subclass covers printing or surface-treatment methods in which a layer or substrate is processed on both faces, such as applying coatings, functional layers or patterns to each side, either simultaneously or in separate steps, to provide dual-sided functionality or appearance.
[B41M 2205/36] Backcoats -- The subclass B41M 2205/36 concerns backcoats applied to a printed substrate after the primary coating, providing a protective or functional layer. It includes the methods and materials used for applying, drying and curing such backcoats in printing processes.
[B41M 2205/38] Intermediate layers -- The B41M 2205/38 classification covers intermediate layers used in printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes, such as undercoats, interleaves, adhesives or functional films applied between the substrate and the printed image to improve adhesion, ink acceptance, durability or other performance characteristics. It pertains to the materials and methods for applying these layers within the overall printing operation.
[B41M 2205/40] Cover layers -- Cover layers are additional material layers applied over a printed surface to protect, strengthen, or modify its appearance or functionality. In printing processes they may be films, laminates, varnishes or other coatings that seal the image, improve durability, add gloss, or provide specific properties such as waterproofing or chemical resistance.
[B41M 2205/42] Multiple imaging layers -- The B41M 2205/42 subclass covers printing processes that build an image by repeatedly applying separate imaging layers, such as successive ink, pigment, or coating layers to create a final picture. It includes methods where each layer is deposited, cured or fixed before the next is added, enabling multi-colour or multi-material effects on a substrate.
[B41M 3/00] Printing processes to produce particular kinds of printed work, e.g. patterns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Digital or non-digital printing processes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rapid manufacturing and prototyping of 3D objects by additive depositing -> B29C64/00
[B41M 3/001] using chemical colour-formers or chemical reactions, e.g. leuco dyes or acids -- B41M 3/001 concerns printing methods that generate images or patterns by applying chemical colour-formers such as leuco dyes, acid-developed inks or other reactive compounds, and then inducing a chemical reaction to develop the desired hue.
[B41M 3/003] on optical devices, e.g. lens elements; for the production of optical devices  production by thermal imaging means  B41M5/265 -- This classification entry pertains to printing processes used specifically for creating optical devices such as lens elements, focusing on methods that employ thermal imaging techniques for their production. It is part of a broader category dealing with specialized printed work and falls under the cooperative patent classification system shared by the European Patent Office (EPO) and the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO).
[B41M 3/005] Colour cards; Painting supports; Latent or hidden images, e.g. for games; Time delayed images -- The B41M 3/005 subclass covers coloured cards, painting supports and related media that contain latent or hidden images, such as those used in games, puzzles or novelty items, including designs that become visible after a time-delayed process.
[B41M 3/006] Patterns of chemical products used for a specific purpose, e.g. pesticides, perfumes, adhesive patterns; use of microencapsulated material; Printing on smoking articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. printing adhesive patterns, printing silver ink patterns.
[B41M 3/008] Sequential or multiple printing, e.g. on previously printed background; Mirror printing; Recto-verso printing; using a combination of different printing techniques; Printing of patterns visible in reflection and by transparency; by superposing printed artifacts -- B41M 3/008 relates to printing processes in which successive or multiple impressions are applied to the same material, such as printing over a previously printed background, mirror or recto-verso printing, and the combination of different techniques to create patterns visible by reflection, transparency or superposition.
[B41M 3/02] Maps -- A B41M 3/02 classification covers printing processes specifically adapted for producing maps, including the techniques, equipment and steps required to render geographic information accurately on paper. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, printing, and printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes for particular printed works such as patterns. This entry is used by the EPO and USPTO to identify patent documents related to map-making printing technologies.
[B41M 3/04] Music -- B41M 3/04 covers printing processes specifically designed for producing music, such as the imprinting of musical notation, scores or related symbols onto paper or other substrates. It falls under the broader category of printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes used to create particular kinds of printed work.
[B41M 3/06] Veined printings -- B41M 3/06 pertains to veined printings, a subset of printing processes that generate vein-like or linear patterns on a substrate. It covers techniques for producing such decorative lines, often used in textiles, paper or other materials to create patterned effects.
[B41M 3/10] Watermarks -- B41M 3/10 relates to the formation of watermarks in printed or duplicated items, describing techniques for embedding translucent designs or identifying marks into paper or other substrates during printing, marking or copying processes, primarily for security, branding or decorative purposes.
[B41M 3/12] Transfer pictures or the like, e.g. decalcomanias -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of making (printing) the picture to be transferred.
[B41M 3/14] Security printing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods which concern the security printing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Security documents -> B42D25/29
[B41M 3/142] using chemical colour-formers or chemical reactions, e.g. leuco-dye/acid, photochromes -- B41M 3/142 covers printing processes that develop colour through chemical colour-formers or reactions, such as leuco-dye/acid systems or photochromic compounds.
