[B41N 7/00] Shells for rollers of printing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shells for rollers of printing machines, which are not printing cylinders or blankets.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Damping rollers (i.e. mechanical aspects as such) -> B41F7/26 - Construction of inking rollers -> B41F31/26
[B41N 7/005] Coating of the composition; Moulding; Reclaiming; Finishing; Trimming -- B41N 7/005 classifies the processes applied to shells used on printing-machine rollers, covering the application of protective or functional coatings, moulding of the shell material, reclaiming or recycling of used shells, subsequent finishing operations such as polishing or surface treatment, and the trimming or cutting required to achieve final dimensions. It addresses all steps that prepare or refurbish roller shells for printing use.
[B41N 7/02] of leather -- B41N 7/02 covers shells made of leather that are fitted onto rollers of printing machines. These leather sleeves protect the roller surface and provide a replaceable bearing for printing plates or foils.
[B41N 7/04] for damping rollers -- B41N 7/04 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers shells for rollers of printing machines that are designed as damping rollers, used to absorb vibration and improve stability during printing operations. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, printing plates or foils, and pertains to roller components that dampen mechanical noise and movement.
[B41N 7/06] for inking rollers -- B41N 7/06 refers to shells designed for inking rollers used in printing presses. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, printing plates or foils, and specifically covers protective or functional shells that house the roller surface that applies ink to the substrate.
[B41N 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- B41N 99/00 is a catch-all group within the printing plates or foils subclass that gathers inventions dealing with plate or foil technologies not specifically covered by any other defined subgroup. It includes novel materials, processes or applications for printing plates that fall outside the existing detailed categories.
[B41P] INDEXING SCHEME RELATING TO PRINTING, LINING MACHINES, TYPEWRITERS, AND TO STAMPS -- B41P classifies systems and methods for indexing and cataloguing printed outputs, encompassing the arrangement of printed pages, lining-machine products, typewriter output and stamp designs. It includes mechanical or electronic schemes that aid in the retrieval, organization or reference of such printed material.
[B41P 2200/00] Printing processes -- B41P 2200/00 covers the methods and techniques used to produce printed material, including the preparation, transfer, and fixing of images or text onto a substrate. It encompasses all stages of the printing operation, from image creation to final output, and relates to processes employed by various printing, lining, typewriter and stamp devices.
[B41P 2200/10] Relief printing -- Relief printing involves transferring an inked image from a raised surface onto a substrate, typically using plates, blocks, or screens that carry the design in relief. The process includes applying ink to the raised areas and pressing them onto paper or other materials to create the printed image. It falls under the CPC class B41P 2200/10, which groups printing methods related to relief techniques.
[B41P 2200/11] Stereotype printing -- Stereotype printing is a process that creates a solid printing plate by casting a copy of a composed surface, such as type or an image, onto a mould made from paper, metal or polymer. The resulting plate reproduces the original layout exactly and can be used repeatedly in offset or relief printing operations. This technique is classified under CPC B41P 2200/11 within the broader group of printing processes.
[B41P 2200/12] Flexographic printing -- Flexographic printing, classified under B41P 2200/12, refers to a relief-plate process where flexible printing plates transfer ink onto paper, film, or other substrates via rotating cylinders. The method is commonly used for high-speed production of packaging, labels, and other print runs that require rapid drying and the ability to print on a variety of materials.
[B41P 2200/13] Offset printing -- Offset printing is a lithographic process where an inked image is transferred from a printing plate to a rubber blanket and then onto paper, enabling high-volume, consistent reproduction of text and graphics for newspapers, books, magazines and commercial forms.
[B41P 2200/20] Lithography -- B41P 2200/20 designates lithographic printing processes within the CPC's printing operations class. It covers methods that use a planar, chemically treated plate to transfer ink to a substrate, a technique employed in commercial, industrial, and artistic print production.
[B41P 2200/21] Dry offset printing -- Dry offset printing is a printing process in which an image is first transferred from a printing plate to a rubber blanket and then onto the final substrate, using inks that dry rapidly without the need for extensive drying equipment. The method is commonly employed for high-speed commercial jobs such as newspapers and magazines, and falls under CPC class B41P 2200/21 within the printing operations hierarchy.
[B41P 2200/22] Wet offset printing -- B41P 2200/22 refers to wet offset printing, a process where a liquid inked image is first transferred from a printing plate to a rubber blanket and then onto paper. This technique is widely used for high-volume commercial printing such as newspapers, magazines and books.
[B41P 2200/30] Heliography -- B41P 2200/30 covers heliography, a printing process that forms images by exposing a photosensitive surface to ultraviolet light through a mask to produce a direct photographic print. It is classified under the CPC group for printing processes within the broader scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2200/31] Pad printing -- B41P 2200/31 covers pad printing, a technique where a flexible silicone or polymer pad transfers ink from an etched cliché onto a substrate, enabling high-resolution images on irregular or three-dimensional surfaces. The process is used for marking medical devices, electronics, promotional items and other products requiring durable, precise decoration.
[B41P 2200/312] multicolor -- The B41P 2200/312 multicolor class refers to printing processes that employ two or more colours in a single operation, including methods for colour registration, colour separation and the use of multicolour printing machines. It is used for indexing schemes covering printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2200/40] Screen printing -- B41P 2200/40 designates the screen-printing process, a printing technique in which ink is forced through a fine mesh stencil onto a substrate such as fabric, paper, or plastic. The method is widely used for producing decorative patterns, graphics, and markings on textiles, ceramics, and other surfaces. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, printing, indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps, and printing processes.
[B41P 2200/41] Offset printing -- B41P 2200/41 relates to offset printing, a method in which an image is transferred from a plate to a rubber blanket and then onto the printing substrate, enabling high-volume production of items such as newspapers, magazines and commercial printed materials.
[B41P 2200/42] Mimeographic printing, e.g. stencilling -- Mimeographic printing uses a stencil to transfer ink onto paper, allowing rapid duplication of text or simple graphics. It is a low-cost, low-volume printing method often employed for forms, labels, and small-run publications. The classification covers machines, techniques and materials specific to this stencilled process.
[B41P 2213/00] Arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses -- This CPC subgroup (B41P 2213/00) relates to the design of mechanisms that actuate or drive printing presses, including hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or mechanical drive systems, couplings, gearings and torque-conversion arrangements. It covers the technical solutions used to power press cylinders, rollers or other moving elements in printing machines.
[B41P 2213/10] Constitutive elements of driving devices -- The CPC subclass B41P 2213/10 covers the basic components that make up the drive systems of printing presses, including motors, gear trains, couplings, clutches, and related control elements that transmit power to the press. It falls under the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, specifically addressing arrangements for actuating or driving such equipment.
[B41P 2213/11] Motors -- The code B41P 2213/11 refers to motors in printing presses. It is a subclass of arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B41P 2213/112] Fluid motors -- The CPC entry B41P 2213/112 classifies fluid-powered motors used to actuate or drive printing presses, encompassing hydraulic or pneumatic devices that convert fluid pressure into rotary or linear motion for operating press mechanisms. It falls under arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses within the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/114] Hydraulic motors -- B41P 2213/114 covers hydraulic motors used to power printing presses. These devices convert fluid pressure into rotary motion to drive printing cylinders or other press components, forming part of the actuation arrangements for printing machinery.
[B41P 2213/116] Linear hydraulic motors -- Linear hydraulic motors are hydraulic power units that produce rotary motion in a straight-line configuration, providing smooth, controllable drive for printing presses. In CPC B41P 2213/116 they are classified under arrangements that actuate or drive printing machines, linking hydraulic actuation technology with press operation.
[B41P 2213/118] Rotary hydraulic motors -- The B41P 2213/118 entry concerns rotary hydraulic motors employed to power or drive printing presses. It classifies hydraulic drive units that convert fluid pressure into rotary motion for the actuation of printing equipment, including mechanisms that transmit torque to press-rollers or other moving parts.
[B41P 2213/12] Pneumatic motors -- This CPC subclass B41P 2213/12 covers pneumatic motors used to actuate or drive printing presses. It is situated within the hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and indexing schemes for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, and specifically concerns air-powered drive arrangements for press mechanisms.
[B41P 2213/122] Linear pneumatic motors -- Linear pneumatic motors are compact actuators that convert compressed air into rotary or linear motion, providing precise, controllable force for driving printing press mechanisms such as feeders, rollers or indexing devices. They are valued for rapid response, low friction and easy speed adjustment, making them suitable for high-speed printing operations where reliable, clean actuation is required.
[B41P 2213/124] Electric motors -- B41P 2213/124 pertains to electric motors employed to actuate or drive printing presses. It includes motor types, mounting arrangements and power-transfer configurations that directly power press cylinders or drive mechanisms in printing, lining, typewriter and stamp equipment.
[B41P 2213/126] Rotary electric motors -- B41P 2213/126 covers rotary electric motors that serve as the power source for driving printing-press mechanisms, fitting within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, printing, and indexing schemes for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps. It relates specifically to motor arrangements that actuate or drive press components in a rotary fashion.
[B41P 2213/128] Linear electric motors -- This subclass covers linear electric motor mechanisms used to actuate or drive printing presses, converting electrical energy into linear motion for feed, transport, or drive functions in printing equipment. It belongs to the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps within the performing operations section.
[B41P 2213/13] Solenoids -- The CPC subclass B41P 2213/13 covers solenoids employed to actuate or drive printing presses. It relates to hydraulic-pneumatic or electromagnetic devices that convert electrical energy into linear motion for controlling print-run mechanisms. This falls within the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/20] Gearings -- This classification covers gear mechanisms used to transmit power or motion within printing presses. It includes the design and arrangement of gears that drive the press rollers or other moving parts, enabling precise control of paper feed and printing speed.
[B41P 2213/202] Helical gears -- The CPC subclass B41P 2213/202 refers to arrangements that use helical gears to actuate or drive printing presses, fitting within the broader category of printing operations and indexing schemes for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/204] Bevel gears -- B41P 2213/204 covers the use of bevel gears in arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses. It pertains to mechanical transmission devices that change the direction of rotation within the indexing schemes of printing, lining, typewriter and stamp machinery.
[B41P 2213/206] Planetary gears -- B41P 2213/206 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to planetary gear assemblies employed to actuate or drive printing presses. These gear trains provide compact, high-torque transmission for moving print cylinders, rollers or other press components. The subclass falls under PRINTING - INDEXING SCHEME RELATING TO PRINTING, LINING MACHINES, TYPEWRITERS, AND TO STAMPS.
[B41P 2213/208] Harmonic drive -- B41P 2213/208 covers harmonic drives used as actuation or drive arrangements in printing presses. The classification falls under the printing indexing scheme for machines such as presses, liners, typewriters and stamps, focusing on gear systems that provide high reduction ratios and smooth motion.
[B41P 2213/25] Couplings -- B41P 2213/25 classifies the mechanical couplings used to connect drive shafts or other power-transmission elements in printing presses, enabling the transfer of motion and torque between actuating components. It includes rigid, flexible and magnetically linked devices that synchronize and drive printing units.
[B41P 2213/252] Synchroclutches -- B41P 2213/252 covers synchroclutches used to synchronize and control the drive of printing presses. The class pertains to arrangements for actuating or driving printing equipment, ensuring coordinated motion between the press and auxiliary mechanisms. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/254] Devices allowing two gears to engage with each other -- The subclass B41P 2213/254 covers mechanisms that permit two gears to mesh, providing a means of transmitting motion within printing-press drive or indexing systems. It includes gear-engagement devices used to actuate or drive presses in the printing industry.
[B41P 2213/256] in a fixed angular position -- The B41P 2213/256 subclass covers arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses in which the drive element is positioned at a fixed angular orientation relative to the press mechanism. It includes devices that maintain a constant angular relationship to ensure consistent power transmission during operation. This classification falls under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/30] Rotary energy transmitters, i.e. electric energy couplers -- B41P 2213/30 covers rotary energy transmitters used to couple electric power to printing-press drives, such as gear, belt, chain or shaft arrangements that transfer rotational motion from an electric motor to the press. These devices enable precise actuation and speed control of printing operations and are classified under the printing-press actuation hierarchy.
[B41P 2213/302] using pneumatic energy, e.g. pressurised air -- The entry covers mechanisms that employ pressurised air or other pneumatic power to move or control printing press components. It includes designs for driving cylinders, actuators and related control systems that convert pneumatic energy into motion for printing operations.
[B41P 2213/304] using hydraulic energy -- This subclass covers arrangements that use hydraulic power to actuate or drive printing presses, employing hydraulic fluid to provide the force for sheet feeding, impression or platen movement. It pertains to hydraulic-energy based mechanisms within printing equipment.
[B41P 2213/40] Auxiliary devices or processes associated with the drives -- B41P 2213/40 covers auxiliary devices or processes that support the drive mechanisms of printing presses, such as additional motors, gearings, clutches, or control systems that enhance actuation or power transmission. It falls under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, specifically arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses.
[B41P 2213/42] Vibration-dampers for machine parts -- Vibration-dampers for machine parts are devices installed in printing press mechanisms to reduce unwanted oscillations and resonances during operation. By absorbing and dissipating kinetic energy, they improve print quality, protect components, and enhance the reliability of drives and actuating systems.
[B41P 2213/44] Noise reduction -- B41P 2213/44 covers features that reduce noise generated by the actuation or drive systems of printing presses. The subclass falls under arrangements for driving printing presses within the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps. It addresses acoustic mitigation in press mechanisms.
[B41P 2213/46] Lubrication -- This classification covers lubrication arrangements used to reduce friction and wear in the actuation or drive systems of printing presses, including feed rollers, cylinders and other mechanical components. It relates to the provision, type and application of lubricants that ensure reliable operation of press machinery.
[B41P 2213/70] Driving devices associated with particular installations or situations -- Driving devices specifically designed for particular printing press installations or operating situations, covering mechanisms that actuate or power presses in unique configurations, such as specialized gear, motor arrangements, or control systems tailored to a given machine layout.
[B41P 2213/71] Inching drive mechanism, i.e. to obtain stepwise movement -- B41P 2213/71 covers arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses that use an inching drive mechanism to achieve stepwise movement of the press. This subclass falls under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps and relates to devices that control the press in incremental, controlled steps.
[B41P 2213/72] Driving devices for monocolour presses -- B41P 2213/72 covers mechanisms that power or actuate monocolour printing presses, including motors, gears, hydraulic or pneumatic systems specifically designed to drive a single-colour printing unit. It falls within the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/73] Driving devices for multicolour presses -- B41P 2213/73 defines devices that drive the various mechanisms of multicolour printing presses, including systems that control paper feed, ink distribution and cylinder speed to achieve colour registration. It is part of the CPC hierarchy for printing operations under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/732] with means for deviating torque over the printing units -- B41P 2213/732 designates arrangements for actuating or driving printing presses that include means for deviating torque over the printing units, a subcategory of the indexing scheme that covers printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps. This code specifies torque-control mechanisms within the press drive system.
[B41P 2213/734] each printing unit being driven by its own electric motor, i.e. electric shaft -- The entry relates to printing presses in which each individual printing unit is powered by a separate electric motor, providing an electric shaft drive for that unit. This arrangement falls under the CPC subclass B41P 2213/734, which covers indexing schemes for printing and related machinery. It specifies a decentralized motor drive rather than a common drive system.
[B41P 2213/80] Means enabling or facilitating exchange of cylinders -- The code designates devices that make it easier to remove, replace or reposition cylinders in a printing press, such as quick-change couplings, indexing plates, or hydraulic/pneumatic mechanisms that assist cylinder exchange during press operation. These arrangements improve set-up speed and reduce downtime when changing printing forms.
[B41P 2213/802] axially -- The CPC subclass B41P 2213/802 pertains to printing presses and specifies arrangements for actuating or driving the press where the motion is applied axially, i.e., along the axis of the shaft or cylinder that moves the printing elements. It falls under the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2213/804] radially -- The B41P 2213/804 subclass defines printing-press drive arrangements in which power is transmitted radially to the cylinder, platen or other moving element, for example by radial gears, belts, cams or similar mechanisms that act around the axis of rotation.
[B41P 2213/90] Register control -- B41P 2213/90 Register control refers to devices or systems that manage the position of a printed sheet relative to the printing cylinder, ensuring correct alignment during operation of printing presses. It includes mechanisms for adjusting and maintaining register while the press is actuated or driven.
[B41P 2213/91] for sheet printing presses -- This subclass concerns the mechanical arrangements that actuate or drive sheet-fed printing presses, covering the drive systems and control mechanisms used to move the press components during operation. It is part of the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2215/00] Screen printing machines -- The CPC subclass B41P 2215/00 covers machines designed for screen printing, i.e., equipment that transfers ink through a fine mesh onto substrates such as textiles, paper, or plastic. These devices are part of the broader performing-operations-printing group and relate to the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2215/10] characterised by their constructional features -- B41P 2215/10 covers screen printing machines that are defined primarily by their constructional features, such as the design of the screen frame, squeegee mechanism, and material handling components. It falls under the printing operations class and relates to equipment used for applying inks through a mesh onto substrates.
[B41P 2215/11] Registering devices -- B41P 2215/11 relates to registration devices used in screen-printing machines, encompassing mechanisms that align or position the screen, stencil, or substrate to ensure accurate and repeatable print placement during operation.
[B41P 2215/112] with means for displacing the frame -- B41P 2215/112 classifies screen-printing machines that incorporate a mechanism for moving the screen frame during operation, allowing the printed pattern to be displaced relative to the substrate. This falls under the CPC printing section, specifically the indexing scheme that covers printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2215/114] with means for displacing the article -- The subclass B41P 2215/114 covers screen-printing machines that incorporate devices for moving the printed item during operation, such as conveyors, rollers or other displacement mechanisms, ensuring continuous or stepped positioning of the substrate while the ink is applied.
[B41P 2215/12] Screens -- B41P 2215/12 covers the screens employed in screen-printing machines, referring to the mesh or stencil that holds the image to be printed and allows ink to pass through onto the substrate. The classification pertains to printing operations and indexes equipment related to printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2215/13] Devices for increasing ink penetration -- B41P 2215/13 covers devices that enhance the penetration of ink into the substrate in screen-printing processes, such as suction or vacuum units, pressure rollers, and other mechanisms that force ink through the mesh onto the printed surface. These inventions are intended to improve ink transfer efficiency and print quality.
[B41P 2215/132] by increasing pressure above the screen -- This subclass covers screen-printing machines that apply ink by raising pressure on the side of the screen opposite the substrate, using a pressure plate, roller, pneumatic or other means to force the ink through the mesh. The technique improves ink transfer and registration in printing operations.
[B41P 2215/134] by increasing pressure beneath the screen -- This CPC subgroup covers screen-printing processes that transfer ink by raising the pressure on the underside of the screen, using a pressurized backing or chamber to push the screen toward the substrate. The method improves ink deposition and allows fine control of print quality, and it falls within B41P 2215 for screen-printing machines.
[B41P 2215/14] Devices or methods for reducing snap effect -- B41P 2215/14 covers devices or methods that mitigate the snap effect in screen-printing machines, a sudden tension release that can cause print distortion or equipment wear. It includes systems such as tension regulators, dampening mechanisms and control strategies that maintain steady frame movement and ink transfer during operation.
[B41P 2215/50] Screen printing machines for particular purposes -- Screen printing machines for particular purposes are specialized equipment that apply ink through a mesh screen onto substrates for specific applications such as textiles, electronics or signage. They often include adaptations like automatic feeding, multi-color stations or oversized frames.
[B41P 2215/55] for printing compact discs -- B41P 2215/55 designates screen-printing machines specifically adapted for printing on compact discs. It falls under the printing hierarchy, within the indexing scheme that covers printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, and pertains to equipment that deposits ink or coating onto disc surfaces using a screen-printing process.
[B41P 2215/56] for printing ceramic tiles -- B41P 2215/56 designates screen-printing machines specially adapted for applying ink or pigment patterns onto ceramic tiles, employing a mesh stencil to transfer the image onto the tile surface. It is situated under Performing Operations → Printing → Indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2217/00] Printing machines of special types or for particular purposes -- B41P 2217/00 categorises printing machines that are designed for special or dedicated applications rather than general-purpose printing. The class includes equipment such as high-speed presses, security-feature printers, embossers and other devices tailored for particular materials, formats or functions.
[B41P 2217/10] characterised by their constructional features -- B41P 2217/10 covers printing machines that are distinguished primarily by their specific constructional features, such as unique frame configurations, drive arrangements or material handling elements. It applies to special-type or purpose-specific printers whose design details set them apart from conventional printing equipment.
[B41P 2217/11] Machines with modular units, i.e. with units exchangeable as a whole -- Printing machines built from modular components that can be removed and replaced as complete units, allowing the machine to be reconfigured for different printing tasks or maintenance. The classification covers designs where whole modules-such as feeding, impression, or finishing sections-are interchangeable, enabling flexible adaptation of the equipment.
[B41P 2217/12] Machines with reversible printing units -- B41P 2217/12 refers to printing machines equipped with reversible printing units, where the unit can be turned or repositioned to allow printing on opposite sides or to change the print direction. Such machines are designed for special or specific printing tasks that require this flexibility.
[B41P 2217/13] Machines with double or multiple printing units for "flying" printing plates exchange -- B41P 2217/13 covers printing machines that use two or more printing units and incorporate a flying plate-exchange system, allowing plates to be automatically removed and replaced while the machine runs. These machines are designed for high-speed, continuous printing with minimal downtime.
[B41P 2217/14] Machines with constructions allowing refurbishing, converting or updating existing presses -- The CPC subclass B41P 2217/14 covers printing presses designed with features that facilitate refurbishment, conversion or updating of existing machines. It includes mechanisms, modular components or adaptable structures that allow the press to be re-engineered for new functions or improved performance.
[B41P 2217/15] Machines with cylinders only supported on one side, e.g. cantilever construction -- B41P 2217/15 covers printing machines in which the cylinders are supported on only one side, such as cantilever constructions, used for special or purpose-specific printing operations. These machines fall under the indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2217/50] Printing presses for particular purposes -- B41P 2217/50 covers printing presses that are engineered for specific uses, such as particular paper sizes, substrates or specialised printing techniques. The subclass groups machines whose construction or operation is tailored to unique production requirements, setting them apart from general-purpose presses.
[B41P 2217/51] for printing individualised books -- B41P 2217/51 refers to printing machines of special types intended for printing individualised books, such as on-demand or customized single-copy production. The code falls under the performing operations-printing hierarchy and the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2217/52] for printing a long repeat length on webs -- It covers printing machines designed to produce long repeat-length patterns on continuously moving webs, allowing extended sequences without frequent repeats, commonly used in decorative, security or textile printing. Such devices fall under the CPC subclass for special-purpose printing machines.
[B41P 2217/53] for printing on the interior of hollow articles -- B41P 2217/53 is a subgroup of the CPC class for printing machines of special types, specifically covering techniques and equipment for applying printed marks to the interior surfaces of hollow articles such as bottles, tubes or containers. It includes methods, devices and processes that target the inner walls of these objects.
[B41P 2217/55] for printing compact discs -- The CPC subclass B41P 2217/55 defines printing machines of special types that are configured to print compact discs. It is located within the performing operations - printing hierarchy and the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2217/56] for printing ceramic tiles -- B41P 2217/56 covers printing equipment or processes specifically designed for applying images or patterns onto ceramic tiles. It is classified under printing machines of special types within the broader performing-operations and printing categories.
[B41P 2217/60] Means for supporting the articles -- This subclass covers devices that hold or secure printed material or substrates during operation of specialized printing machines, such as fixtures, frames, or supports that position articles for printing. It pertains to mechanisms that maintain correct alignment and stability of items being printed in machines designed for particular purposes.
[B41P 2217/61] internally, e.g. for mugs or goblets -- B41P 2217/61 pertains to printing machines of special types or for particular purposes that apply ink internally, for example to the inner surfaces of mugs, goblets or similar containers. This entry falls under the printing hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B41P 2217/62] externally, e.g. for bottles -- The subclass B41P 2217/62 covers printing machines specially adapted for applying markings to the exterior of objects such as bottles. It includes equipment designed to print directly onto the outer surface of containers for labeling, decoration or identification purposes.
[B41P 2219/00] Printing presses using a heated printing foil -- B41P 2219/00 covers printing presses that apply a heated printing foil, a process used for foil-stamping, embossing or metallic decoration of paper and similar substrates. The subclass is part of the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, grouping technologies that rely on heat-transferred foil.
[B41P 2219/10] Driving devices for the reciprocating die -- B41P 2219/10 designates the driving devices that move a reciprocating die in heated-foil printing presses, covering mechanisms such as cams, levers, hydraulic or pneumatic actuators that provide the necessary back-and-forth motion for foil transfer during printing.
[B41P 2219/11] Hydraulic or pneumatic motors -- The B41P 2219/11 subclass covers printing presses that employ heated printing foil and are powered by hydraulic or pneumatic motors, falling under the broader scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps within the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B41P 2219/12] Electric motors -- This CPC subclass B41P 2219/12 covers electric motors that drive printing presses using a heated printing foil. It is part of the performing-operations group, under printing and the indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps, specifically for foil-based presses.
[B41P 2219/13] Gearings -- B41P 2219/13 pertains to gearings incorporated in printing presses that use a heated printing foil, detailing the transmission elements that drive and coordinate the foil-heating mechanism within the machine. This subclass falls under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, specifically within the broader categories of performing operations and printing.
[B41P 2219/132] Cams or eccentrics -- B41P 2219/132 classifies cams or eccentrics employed in printing presses that use heated printing foil, covering devices that translate rotational motion into the precise intermittent movements required for foil transfer in the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2219/134] Knee-lever -- The B41P 2219/134 "Knee-lever" classifies printing presses that use a knee-lever mechanism to apply pressure while a heated foil transfers foil onto the printed surface. It falls under the performing-operations branch for printing, specifically the indexing scheme covering printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, and relates to presses that employ heated foil for embellishment.
[B41P 2219/20] Arrangements for moving, supporting or positioning the printing foil -- The CPC entry B41P 2219/20 covers the mechanisms that move, support or position a heated printing foil within printing presses. It relates to arrangements that guide, hold or adjust the foil during the printing process, ensuring proper alignment and transfer of the image onto the substrate.
[B41P 2219/21] Supports for the unwinding roll -- The CPC subclass B41P 2219/21 covers components that support the unwinding roll in heated-foil printing presses, such as mandrels, rollers or other structures that hold and guide the foil as it is fed from the roll. These items are used to ensure smooth delivery of the foil onto the printing press during the heating and transfer process.
[B41P 2219/22] Guiding or tensioning the printing foil -- B41P 2219/22 covers devices or methods that guide or apply tension to a heated printing foil during operation of printing presses, ensuring precise foil placement and consistent contact with the substrate. This is part of the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps within the broader performing operations and printing categories.
[B41P 2219/23] Winding up the printing foil -- The B41P 2219/23 subgroup covers mechanisms or processes for collecting and winding the heated printing foil after it has been used in a foil-stamping or foil-printing press. It includes devices that guide, tension, and roll the foil into spools or reels to prepare it for reuse or disposal. This classification falls under indexing schemes for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2219/30] Printing dies -- This subclass addresses printing dies, the patterned tools employed to transfer heated foil onto a substrate in foil-stamping and related processes. It includes the design, fabrication and use of such dies for creating printed images, decorations or security features on paper, plastics, metal or other materials.
[B41P 2219/31] Heating means -- Subclass B41P 2219/31 covers the heating means employed in printing presses that use a heated printing foil, including any heating elements, plates, rollers or other devices that apply thermal energy to the foil for image transfer.
[B41P 2219/32] with means for attaching printing elements to the holder -- The code B41P 2219/32 covers printing presses that employ a heated foil and incorporate means for attaching printing elements to a holder. It belongs to the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps within the broader category of performing operations.
[B41P 2219/33] Supports for printing dies -- B41P 2219/33 covers supports for printing dies in heated foil printing presses, a subcategory of the CPC index for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps. These devices hold and position dies during foil transfer to ensure accurate image imprinting.
[B41P 2219/40] Material or products to be decorated or printed -- This CPC subclass B41P 2219/40 covers materials or products that are intended to receive decoration or printing, particularly those processed with heated foil printing presses. It falls under the performing operations branch, within printing and the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps.
[B41P 2219/41] Web-like material -- The code B41P 2219/41 covers web-like material employed in printing presses that apply heated printing foil. It refers to continuous flexible sheets or rolls that serve as the substrate for foil-stamping processes, enabling rapid, uniform transfer of metallic or pigmented foils onto printed products.
[B41P 2219/42] Sheet-like material -- A sheet-like material refers to the substrates-such as paper, card, polymer or metal foil-employed in printing presses that use a heated foil to transfer images or designs onto a workpiece. The classification covers foils, laminates and other flat media specifically designed for hot-foil printing processes.
[B41P 2219/43] Three-dimensional articles -- B41P 2219/43 pertains to the use of heated printing foil in printing presses for producing three-dimensional articles such as embossed or raised surfaces. The classification falls under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps within the broader performing operations category.
[B41P 2219/432] Pencils -- B41P 2219/432 classifies pencils that are employed in printing presses using heated printing foil, falling under the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps. This subclass is part of the CPC hierarchy for performing operations in printing and deals specifically with the tools used to apply or mark foil on printed material.
[B41P 2219/434] Books, e.g. edge-gilding -- This subclass covers printing presses that apply heated foil to the edges of books to create decorative edge-gilding. It includes methods and apparatus for bonding metallic or other foil strips to book edges during or after binding, typically using heat and pressure.
[B41P 2219/50] combined with existing presses -- The CPC subclass B41P 2219/50 covers printing presses that employ a heated foil and are combined with an existing press unit, detailing how a foil-transfer module is integrated into a conventional printing machine to enable simultaneous or successive printing operations.
[B41P 2219/51] Converting existing presses to foil printing presses -- B41P 2219/51 covers the conversion of conventional printing presses into foil-printing machines, including the addition of heating elements, foil-feeding systems and related adjustments that allow the press to transfer metallic or colored foils onto substrates.
[B41P 2219/60] Using more than one printing foil -- This subclass pertains to printing presses that employ more than one heated foil during the stamping process, allowing foils to be applied sequentially or together to produce multicolour or layered impressions.
[B41P 2227/00] Mounting or handling printing plates -- The CPC subclass B41P 2227/00 covers techniques and apparatus for mounting, handling, positioning or transporting printing plates in printing operations. It includes devices and methods that secure plates to presses, adjust their alignment, and facilitate plate exchange or replacement during the printing process.
[B41P 2227/10] Attaching several printing plates on one cylinder -- The classification B41P 2227/10 covers the method of mounting or handling printing plates in which several plates are attached to a single cylinder, allowing simultaneous impression of multiple images or colors.
[B41P 2227/11] in axial direction -- The B41P 2227/11 subclass pertains to the axial handling of printing plates during mounting or positioning. It covers mechanisms or methods that move plates along their longitudinal axis, facilitating precise alignment in printing operations.
[B41P 2227/12] in circumferential direction -- The B41P 2227/12 subclass deals with mounting or handling printing plates by moving them in a circumferential direction, such as around a rotating cylinder or other circular guide. It covers methods and apparatus that index plates along a curved path within printing presses.
[B41P 2227/20] Means enabling or facilitating exchange of tubular printing or impression members, e.g. printing sleeves, blankets -- This subclass covers devices or methods that enable quick removal, replacement, or swapping of tubular printing members such as sleeves or blankets on printing presses. It includes mechanisms like removable clamps, hinged carriers or automatic loading systems that facilitate exchange of these impression elements.
[B41P 2227/21] Means facilitating exchange of sleeves mounted on cylinders without removing the cylinder from the press -- The CPC subclass B41P 2227/21 covers mechanisms that enable sleeves mounted on printing cylinders to be exchanged while the cylinder stays in the press, facilitating quick removal or replacement of printing plates without dismounting the cylinder and improving printing efficiency.
[B41P 2227/30] Detecting the correct position of printing plates on the cylinder -- The CPC subclass B41P 2227/30 pertains to methods and devices that detect the proper positioning of printing plates on a rotary cylinder, ensuring accurate alignment during plate mounting in offset lithography and related printing operations.
[B41P 2227/40] Adjusting means for printing plates on the cylinder -- B41P 2227/40 pertains to devices that adjust printing plates on a cylinder to ensure correct alignment during printing operations. The classification falls under the indexing scheme for printing, covering mounting and handling of plates in presses.
[B41P 2227/41] axially -- This subclass covers methods or devices for mounting or handling printing plates where the movement or alignment is performed along the plate's longitudinal axis, such as axial feeding, positioning, or rotating of plates in printing presses.
[B41P 2227/42] circumferentially -- The CPC subclass B41P 2227/42 covers devices or methods for mounting or handling printing plates by moving them around their periphery, such as rotating or rolling plate holders that position plates circumferentially for printing operations. This falls under the broader scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps within the performing-operations hierarchy.
[B41P 2227/43] diagonally -- This subclass deals with methods or devices for mounting or handling printing plates in a diagonal orientation or along a diagonal path, applied to printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamp production where such positioning aids registration or access.
[B41P 2227/50] Devices for storing printing plates -- The CPC class B41P 2227/50 covers devices that store, hold, or transport printing plates used in plate-based printing processes. It includes holders, racks, trays and other apparatus that keep plates organized and protected before, during or after printing.
[B41P 2227/60] Devices for transferring printing plates -- B41P 2227/60 covers devices used to move, mount or otherwise handle printing plates during the printing process, such as plate cylinders, racks, and transfer mechanisms. These tools facilitate accurate placement of plates on printing machines, ensuring proper alignment and registration.
[B41P 2227/61] using the sheet feed system -- The CPC subclass B41P 2227/61 covers techniques and apparatus that employ a sheet-feed system to supply paper to a press while mounting or handling printing plates, including mechanisms for loading, advancing, aligning or delivering individual sheets to the printing area. This includes any feed arrangement that integrates with plate-handling operations to improve sheet positioning and throughput.
[B41P 2227/62] Devices for introducing printing plates -- B41P 2227/62 covers devices that introduce printing plates into press units, enabling precise placement and secure handling during printing operations. It falls under the CPC group for printing equipment within the Performing Operations section.
[B41P 2227/63] Devices for removing printing plates -- This CPC subclass covers apparatus designed to detach or lift printing plates from presses, such as plate-removal arms, conveyors or pneumatic systems, facilitating plate turnover during printing operations. It falls under the printing indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2227/70] Forming the printing surface directly on the form cylinder -- B41P 2227/70 covers processes in which the image-bearing surface of a form cylinder is created directly on the cylinder itself, without using a separate plate. Techniques include applying photosensitive layers, laser or electron-beam engraving, or other direct-forming methods that produce the printing surface on the cylinder for offset or flexographic printing.
[B41P 2231/00] Inking devices -- This class relates to devices that apply ink to printing elements in machines such as printers, typewriters, lining equipment or stamp presses. It includes rollers, brushes, ink-ribbons and other mechanisms that distribute, regulate or transfer ink onto the form or print head.
[B41P 2231/10] Axially segmented ducter rollers -- B41P 2231/10 relates to axially segmented ducter rollers used in printing presses, where the roller surface is divided into longitudinal sections that form individual ink ducts, allowing precise ink distribution across the width of the printed material.
[B41P 2231/12] Axially segmented ink blades -- The CPC code B41P 2231/12 covers axially segmented ink blades, which are ink-applying elements divided into longitudinal sections to improve uniform ink distribution on printing plates, typewriter ribbons or stamp surfaces. It falls under the printing hierarchy, specifically the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2231/20] Recovering printing ink -- The CPC subclass B41P 2231/20 covers methods and apparatus for recovering printing ink from printing, lining, typewriter or stamp equipment. It includes processes that collect, filter, clean or otherwise reclaim ink for reuse or proper disposal, and is part of the indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2231/21] by using mixtures of ink and water or repellant -- B41P 2231/21 designates inking devices that apply mixtures of ink with water or a repellant to regulate ink flow and film formation during printing. The code belongs to the CPC printing section, within the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, and concerns methods of delivering ink to the substrate.
[B41P 2233/00] Arrangements for the operation of printing presses -- B41P 2233/00 covers the structural and functional arrangements that control how printing presses operate, including mechanisms for feeding, advancing, aligning, and synchronising printed material, as well as automation and monitoring systems that optimise press performance. This subclass falls under the broader indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2233/10] Starting-up the machine -- B41P 2233/10 relates to arrangements for starting up a printing press, covering control sequences, power-on procedures and initial positioning of the machine to prepare it for operation. It falls under the broader categories of printing and performing operations within the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2233/11] Pre-inking -- B41P 2233/11 covers pre-inking arrangements in printing presses, where ink is applied to type, plates or other impression elements before the printing cycle. It includes devices and mechanisms that store, meter, or transfer ink to the printing surface in preparation for operation.
[B41P 2233/12] Pre-wetting -- Pre-wetting is the step of applying a controlled amount of moisture to the paper or substrate before it contacts the inked printing plate. This moistening improves ink absorption, reduces feathering and enhances the sharpness of printed images, especially in offset and gravure processes. The technique is part of the arrangement of operations for printing presses.
[B41P 2233/13] Pre-registering -- B41P 2233/13 covers arrangements in printing presses that perform pre-registration of the print image, i.e., mechanisms that align or position the substrate or printing plate before the impression to ensure accurate registration. Such devices are part of the indexing scheme for printing equipment, facilitating correct placement of successive prints.
[B41P 2233/20] Safety devices preventing damage -- The CPC subclass B41P 2233/20 covers safety devices incorporated in printing-press operations that protect the machine or the printed material from damage, such as overload protection, jam detection, emergency stop mechanisms and other preventative controls. It falls under Performing Operations → Printing → Indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps → Arrangements for the operation of printing presses.
[B41P 2233/21] by cutting the web in case of web break -- This subclass covers arrangements for operating printing presses that respond to a web break by cutting the web. It falls under the broader category of printing, indexing schemes for printing, and related equipment, and specifies the method of web interruption handling during press operation.
[B41P 2233/22] by deviating the web in case of web break -- B41P 2233/22 concerns devices that divert a web when a break is detected, redirecting it away from the press to avoid damage and maintain operation. The arrangement typically includes sensors, rollers or guide mechanisms that automatically change the web path upon rupture.
[B41P 2233/23] by preventing the web winding up around cylinders -- B41P 2233/23 covers arrangements that keep the web of material from winding around the cylinders of a printing press, including guides, tension-control systems or detection devices that prevent snarling during operation.
[B41P 2233/30] Measuring or controlling the consumption of ink -- This subclass covers devices or methods for measuring or regulating the amount of ink used during operation of printing presses, including sensors, flow meters and control systems that adjust ink-feed to maintain the desired ink transfer. It is located under arrangements for the operation of printing presses in the CPC indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2233/50] Marks on printed material -- B41P 2233/50 relates to the creation of marks on printed material, covering methods, devices and arrangements that apply indicia, codes or identification directly onto sheets, labels, stamps or other printed items during the printing process. It is classified under performing operations for printing within the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2233/51] for colour quality control -- The subclass B41P 2233/51 addresses arrangements for colour quality control in printing presses, covering devices, sensors and procedures that monitor, measure and adjust the colour output to maintain consistent colour reproduction during operation.
[B41P 2233/52] for registering -- The subclass B41P 2233/52 covers arrangements that provide a registering function for printing presses, ensuring precise alignment of the printing material during operation. It relates to indexing schemes used to control the position of sheets, forms or stamps in printing, lining and typewriter equipment.
[B41P 2235/00] Cleaning -- The B41P 2235/00 subclass covers cleaning procedures for equipment used in printing and related fields, including lining machines, typewriters and stamp-producing devices. It addresses methods and devices for removing ink, dust, debris or other contaminants to maintain operational quality and prevent damage. This falls under the broader categories of Performing Operations and Printing in the CPC system.
[B41P 2235/10] characterised by the methods or devices -- B41P 2235/10 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to cleaning methods or devices specifically related to printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps. This code is part of a detailed indexing scheme that addresses various aspects of maintenance and operation for these types of equipment.
[B41P 2235/12] using laser energy -- The subclass B41P 2235/12 covers the use of laser energy to clean printing-related equipment such as printing presses, lining machines, typewriters or stamp devices. It includes methods where laser radiation removes ink, toner, dust or other residues from surfaces without mechanical contact. This falls under the broader CPC group for performing operations and cleaning within printing technology.
[B41P 2235/14] using ultrasonic energy -- B41P 2235/14 pertains to the use of ultrasonic energy for cleaning in the printing field, including lining machines, typewriters and stamp equipment; it covers ultrasonic methods that remove contaminants from surfaces or components during or after printing operations.
[B41P 2235/20] Wiping devices -- This CPC subclass B41P 2235/20 covers wiping devices used to clean printing, lining, typewriter or stamp mechanisms. The devices are designed to remove ink, debris or moisture from rollers, plates or other parts during operation, improving print quality and equipment longevity. It falls within Performing Operations, Printing, indexing schemes relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, and the cleaning category.
[B41P 2235/21] Scrapers, e.g. absorbent pads -- B41P 2235/21 covers cleaning scrapers such as absorbent pads used with printing, lining and typewriter equipment and stamp applications; these devices remove residual ink, debris or moisture from surfaces during or after operation. The classification falls under performing operations - printing - indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps.
[B41P 2235/22] Rollers -- Rollers used for cleaning in printing equipment, including those that apply cleaning agents, remove ink or debris, and maintain the surface of printing cylinders or plates. This subclass covers designs, materials and mechanisms of such rollers intended to keep printing processes free of contamination.
[B41P 2235/23] Brushes -- B41P 2235/23 covers brushes employed for cleaning components in printing equipment, including lining machines, typewriters and stamp devices. The classification falls under performing operations, printing, indexing scheme relating to these machines, and cleaning.
[B41P 2235/24] using rolls of cleaning cloth -- This subclass covers methods or devices for cleaning printing equipment by employing rolls of cleaning cloth, typically applied to rollers, plates or other surfaces in printing, lining or typewriter mechanisms. It falls under cooperative patent classification B41P 2235/24 within the printing and performing operations area.
[B41P 2235/242] Unwinding the cleaning cloth -- The entry B41P 2235/242 covers mechanisms that feed a cleaning cloth from a roll by unspooling it for use in printing equipment. It addresses the unwinding process that supplies the cloth to cleaning stations in printers, linings or typewriter mechanisms.
[B41P 2235/244] Rewinding the cleaning cloth -- B41P 2235/244 covers the step of rewinding the cleaning cloth used in maintaining printing, lining, typewriter or stamp machines. It falls under the indexing scheme for cleaning operations within the broader printing classification and details how the cloth is taken up after use.
[B41P 2235/246] Pressing the cleaning cloth against the cylinder -- This subclass relates to the step of pressing a cleaning cloth against a printing-cylinder to remove ink film or residue. It is part of the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps, situated under performing operations and printing in the CPC hierarchy.
[B41P 2235/26] Spraying devices -- B41P 2235/26 covers spraying devices used for cleaning printing, typewriter or stamp machines. It includes apparatus that atomises liquids or gases to remove ink, dust or residues from rollers, plates or other printing components. The classification pertains to cleaning operations within the broader printing and indexing scheme.
[B41P 2235/27] Suction devices -- B41P 2235/27 pertains to suction devices employed for cleaning purposes in printing equipment, including presses and related machinery. The classification covers mechanisms that withdraw waste material, ink or debris from printing zones to maintain product quality and machine performance.
[B41P 2235/30] Recovering used solvents or residues -- The code B41P 2235/30 covers methods and apparatus for recovering used solvents or residues generated during printing operations, such as cleaning of printing presses or related equipment. It includes processes that separate, collect, or recycle these liquids or solid residues to enable reuse or proper disposal.
[B41P 2235/31] by filtering -- B41P 2235/31 relates to cleaning steps in printing where ink, solvent or other fluids are purified by passing them through a filter. It falls under the printing and related machinery group, specifically the cleaning sub-category.
[B41P 2235/32] by distilling -- The code B41P 2235/32 covers cleaning procedures for printing, typewriter or stamp equipment that employ distillation to recover or purify cleaning solvents. It pertains to separating contaminants from the cleaning fluid by heating and condensing the vapour, allowing the cleaned liquid to be reused.
[B41P 2235/33] by burning -- The B41P 2235/33 subclass covers cleaning procedures for printing, indexing and related equipment that employ burning as the method of removing residues or contaminants. It falls under the broader category of performing operations in printing, specifically within the indexing scheme for printing, lining machines, typewriters and stamps.
[B41P 2235/40] Cleaning restricted areas on cylinders -- The subclass B41P 2235/40 covers methods and devices for cleaning specific, confined zones on printing cylinders, such as the areas that contact ink, impression surfaces, or other restricted sections during printing, typewriter, or stamp production processes. It focuses on targeted cleaning of these limited cylinder regions.
[B41P 2235/50] Selection of materials or products for cleaning -- B41P 2235/50 pertains to the selection of materials or products used for cleaning printing equipment, lining machines, typewriters or stamps. It addresses the choice of cleaning-agents, wipes, solvents or other consumables intended to maintain or restore functional surfaces within the printing process.
[B41P 2251/00] Details or accessories of printing machines not otherwise provided for -- The CPC group B41P 2251/00 covers details or accessories of printing machines that are not specifically covered elsewhere in the classification. It includes any supplementary parts, attachments or auxiliary devices used with printing equipment that do not fall under more narrowly defined categories. This subgroup falls under Performing Operations → Printing → Indexing scheme relating to printing, lining machines, typewriters, and stamps.
[B41P 2251/10] Pumps -- In CPC class B41P 2251/10, pumps refers to devices that supply or circulate fluid within printing machines, especially where the pumps are not covered by other specific subclasses. These pumps deliver ink, cleaning liquids or other fluids to print heads, rollers or related components and are treated as accessories of printing equipment.
[B41P 2251/11] Membrane pumps -- The B41P 2251/11 class covers membrane pumps that serve as auxiliary components for printing equipment, including those used in lining machines, typewriters or stamp devices, when the pump's function or design is not specifically addressed elsewhere in the classification. It falls under the indexing scheme for printing-related machines and their accessories.
[B41P 2251/112] Peristaltic pumps -- B41P 2251/112 designates peristaltic pumps employed as auxiliary components in printing equipment. These pumps are covered as details or accessories of printing machines not otherwise specified in the classification.
[B41P 2700/00] Manifolding, printing or duplicating for office purposes -- This CPC subclass deals with office-type printing operations such as the formation of sheets, duplication and copying of documents, and the handling of paper or similar media in office equipment like typewriters, duplicators and small printing devices. It falls under the broader category of printing within performing operations.
[B41P 2700/10] Hectographic line duplicators -- The B41P 2700/10 class includes office-type hectographic line duplicators, machines that reproduce text or simple graphics by transferring ink from a prepared master sheet to multiple copies in a single line-by-line process, commonly used for forms, labels and low-volume document runs.
[B41P 2700/12] with single master plates -- The B41P 2700/12 subclass covers office-type printing and duplication processes that employ a single master plate to produce copies, focusing on techniques used in manifolding, indexing, and related printing operations. It pertains to systems such as lining machines, typewriters and stamp printers that create duplicated outputs from one original plate.
[B42] BOOKBINDING -- B42 covers the technical field of bookbinding, including methods and machines for assembling printed sheets, stitching, adhesive bonding, and applying covers or protective layers. It also includes related equipment such as binding presses, sewing stations, and finishing devices used to produce finished volumes.
[B42B] PERMANENTLY ATTACHING TOGETHER SHEETS, QUIRES OR SIGNATURES OR PERMANENTLY ATTACHING OBJECTS THERETO -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines, devices and methods for permanently attaching together all kinds of flat or folded sheets, e.g. quires or signatures, to obtain book sections, book blocks or fillings, booklets, pads or blocks.  The permanent attachment can be performed by continuous or discontinuous stitching or otherwise, e.g. by adhesive or with binding elements like clips or finger, claw, or ring-like elements passing through the sheets. Usually, continuous stitching unites several quires or signatures to a book block with a single piece of thread. Discontinuous stitching usually uses wire staples to unite a single set of flat or folded sheets, usually comprising already a back or cover sheet, to a block or booklet.  The binding of collated flat sheets is usually performed by block stitching in a binding margin, the binding of folded sheets is usually performed by saddle stitching along the fold.  Machines, devices and methods for permanently attaching objects to sheets, quires or signatures, e.g. map sections or extensions thereto.  **Glossary:** - Sheet: Piece of paper or cardboard, usually rectangular, flat and printed - Quire: Set of a predetermined number of flat or folded sheets, usually 24 or 25 flat sheets or 12 folded sheets comprising 48 print pages - Signature: Printed sheet that consists of a number of print pages of a book, so layed out, that they will fold, usually with several folds, and bind together as a section of a book
[B42B 2/00] Permanently attaching together sheets, quires or signatures by stitching with filamentary material, e.g. textile threads -- This class covers methods and devices for permanently binding sheets, quires or signatures by stitching them together with filamentary materials such as textile threads, a technique used in bookbinding to create a lasting bond.
[B42B 2/02] Machines for stitching with thread -- B42B 2/02 covers machines that stitch sheets, quires or signatures together using filamentary thread, as employed in bookbinding to create a permanent attachment of paper components. The classification pertains to equipment that feeds material and forms thread stitches to bind pages into a unified volume.
[B42B 2/04] with straight needles -- B42B 2/04 relates to the stitching step in bookbinding where sheets, quires or signatures are sewn together using straight needles that pass filamentary thread through the material. The subclass is part of the permanent attachment of sheets by stitching, under Performing Operations - Bookbinding.
[B42B 2/06] with curved needles -- B42B 2/06 relates to bookbinding processes that permanently join sheets, quires or signatures by stitching with filamentary material, such as textile threads, using curved needles. This subgroup specifies the use of curved-needle stitching within the broader category of permanently attaching pages.
[B42B 2/08] with devices for forming safety knots or with tying mechanisms -- B42B 2/08 covers bookbinding methods where sheets, quires or signatures are permanently stitched using filamentary material and includes devices that form safety knots or incorporate tying mechanisms to secure the stitch. These mechanisms help prevent stitches from loosening during handling or use.
[B42B 2/10] Hand-stitching tools -- Hand-stitching tools are implements used to manually sew together the sheets, quires or signatures of a book. They include needles, awls, stitching frames and other devices that guide filamentary material such as thread through the work pieces, allowing permanent attachment in traditional bookbinding.
[B42B 4/00] Permanently attaching together sheets, quires or signatures by discontinuous stitching with filamentary material, e.g. wire -- This subclass covers bookbinding processes that permanently join sheets, quires or signatures using discontinuous stitching with filamentary materials such as wire. The method creates spaced stitches rather than continuous lines, providing structural support while allowing flexibility.
[B42B 4/02] Rotary type stitching machines -- Rotary type stitching machines are equipment that permanently joins sheets, signatures or other elements by discontinuous stitching with filamentary material such as wire, commonly used in bookbinding. The machines feed material past a rotating stitching mechanism that forms the stitches and secures the items together in a continuous, automated process. This technology falls under the CPC subclass B42B 4/02.
[B42B 5/00] Permanently attaching together sheets, quires or signatures otherwise than by stitching -- The CPC subclass B42B 5/00 covers processes for permanently joining sheets, quires or signatures by means other than stitching, such as adhesive bonding, welding, laminating or other mechanical fixation. It includes techniques that create a durable, non-sewn connection for books, documents or similar printed products.
[B42B 5/02] by eyelets -- B42B 5/02 covers the permanent attachment of sheets, quires or signatures by means of eyelets, which are metal or plastic rings inserted into punched holes and then crimped or nailed to secure the paper. The subclass includes methods for forming, inserting and fixing these eyelets without stitching.
[B42B 5/04] by laces or ribbons -- B42B 5/04 covers the permanent joining of sheets, quires or signatures in bookbinding by means of laces or ribbons, rather than stitching, to secure the components together. This method provides a durable attachment while allowing decorative or functional use of the lacing material.
[B42B 5/06] by clips -- B42B 5/06 relates to bookbinding techniques that permanently join sheets, quires or signatures by means other than stitching, specifically using clips to fasten the pages together. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations and bookbinding, and it addresses the use of clip mechanisms to secure bound elements in a durable manner.
[B42B 5/08] by finger, claw or ring-like elements passing through the sheets, quires or signatures -- B42B 5/08 covers bookbinding methods that permanently join sheets, quires or signatures by passing finger, claw or ring-like elements through the material instead of stitching. The technique secures the pages by inserting a protruding element through the paper and locking it in place.
[B42B 5/10] the elements being of castellated or comb-like form -- B42B 5/10 relates to permanent attachment of paper sheets, quires or signatures by means other than stitching, using binding elements that have a castellated or comb-like shape. The classification covers interlocking or ridged components that engage with matching profiles to hold the sheets together.
[B42B 5/103] Devices for assembling the elements with the stack of sheets -- Devices for assembling the elements with a stack of sheets refer to machines or tools that apply adhesive, pressure, heat or other means to permanently join paper sheets, signatures or quire into a single unit, covering binding operations other than stitching.
[B42B 5/106] combined with manufacturing of the elements  manufacturing from wire  per se   B21F1/04 ,  B21F45/16 -- B42B 5/106 covers methods for permanently joining sheets, quires or signatures by means other than stitching, integrated with the manufacture of the joining elements themselves when those elements are produced from wire. It links bookbinding processes to wire-based component production classified under B21F 1/04 and B21F 45/16.
[B42B 5/12] the elements being coils -- B42B 5/12 refers to book-binding methods that use coiled elements, such as spiral or helix wires, to permanently join sheets, quires or signatures. It covers the preparation, insertion and securing of the coils that form the binding, distinguishing this technique from stitching or other attachment means.
[B42B 5/123] Devices for assembling the elements with the stack of sheets -- B42B 5/123 covers apparatus that arrange and hold a stack of sheets, signatures or other elements together for permanent attachment in bookbinding without using stitching. The devices may include guides, feeders, clamps or conveyors that assemble the elements before adhesive, welding or other binding processes.
[B42B 5/126] combined with manufacturing of the elements  manufacturing from wire  per se   B21F3/04 ,  B21F45/16 -- This classification code, B42B 5/126, pertains to methods for permanently attaching sheets or signatures in bookbinding that involve combining the binding process with the manufacturing of elements from wire. It specifically references techniques related to wire forming and manipulation as detailed in codes B21F3/04 and B21F45/16.
[B42B 7/00] Permanently attaching objects, e.g. map sections, to sheets -- B42B 7/00 falls under the bookbinding subclass dealing with permanently attaching objects to sheets, such as affixing map sections, plates, or other items to a page. The code covers methods and devices that create a lasting bond between the object and the sheet, ensuring durability in the finished product.
[B42B 9/00] Devices common to machines for carrying out the processes according to more than one of the preceding main groups -- B42B 9/00 covers devices that are shared by different book-binding machines and can perform the various actions required to permanently attach sheets, quires or signatures, such as stitching, gluing or clamping. These common components are used across multiple main groups within the permanent-attachment operations of the bookbinding field.
[B42B 9/02] for opening quires or signatures -- This subclass pertains to devices that open or separate bound groups of sheets such as quires or signatures in bookbinding operations. The equipment is used in machines that handle multiple processing steps, including the permanent attachment of sheets, and is applicable where the same apparatus serves more than one main function.
[B42B 9/04] for conveying downwardly-open signatures -- B42B 9/04 relates to bookbinding machinery that transports signatures whose open side faces downward, facilitating their handling in operations such as gluing, stitching or gathering. The subgroup includes conveyors, rollers or similar devices that guide these downward-open signatures through the binding line.
[B42B 9/06] for pasting -- B42B 9/06 covers devices used in bookbinding machines for pasting, i.e., applying adhesive to permanently join sheets, quires, signatures or other objects. It falls under performing operations → bookbinding → permanently attaching together sheets, and pertains to equipment common to machines that perform multiple related processes.
[B42C] BOOKBINDING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular operations and machines peculiar to bookbinding for obtaining books as final products:  Some operations and machines similar to bookbinding operations for obtaining related final products:
[B42C 1/00] Collating or gathering sheets combined with processes for permanently attaching together sheets or signatures or for interposing inserts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gathering and collating are typical for bookbinding in order to arrange the printed pages in a predetermined sequence to obtain the book block.  Gathering sheets means associating and registering folded printed sheets in the correct sequence to obtain a folded quire as a section of a book, collating means associating and registering sections, e.g. folded signatures or folded quires, in the proper sequence for the book block.  Gathering covers as well associating and registering flat or folded sheets to a single copy of a publication, e.g. for subsequent stapling.  Collating covers as well examining the associated section or set of sections, after gathering but before attaching, for proper sequence, e.g. by examining step marks on the back of the folded sheets.  Interposing inserts means inserting separately printed flat or folded sheets, e.g. of different paper or print quality for advertisement, into the gathered or collated main publication for becoming a permanent part of the final product.
[B42C 1/10] Machines for both collating or gathering and interposing inserts -- B42C 1/10 refers to machines that simultaneously collate or gather sheets and interpose inserts such as flyers or supplemental pages, allowing the combined preparation of a bound product in one continuous operation.
[B42C 11/00] Casing-in -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Associating a book block or other fillings with a bookbinding case or cover.
[B42C 11/02] Machines or equipment for casing-in or applying covers to pamphlets, magazines, pads, or other paper-covered booklets -- This CPC subclass covers machinery and devices that automatically bind a cover, also known as casing-in, onto printed products such as pamphlets, magazines, pads, or other paper-covered booklets.
[B42C 11/04] Machines or equipment for casing-in or applying covers to books -- B42C 11/04 refers to machines or equipment that case books or apply protective covers during the binding process. It is a subclass of Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Bookbinding → Casing-in.
[B42C 11/045] Folding end sheets, attaching end sheets to book blocks -- B42C 11/045 covers techniques for folding end sheets, also called endpapers, and for joining those sheets to the book block, including folding, gluing, stitching or other attachment methods used in bookbinding.
[B42C 11/06] Machines or equipment for casing-in by welding plastic materials -- The CPC subclass B42C 11/06 covers machines and equipment that apply a plastic casing to a book by welding the material, typically using heat or ultrasonic methods to seal the film around the cover. These devices are used in bookbinding to create a durable, waterproof outer layer, commonly seen in paperback and soft-cover publications.
[B42C 1/12] Machines for both collating or gathering and permanently attaching together the sheets or signatures -- The entry B42C 1/12 covers machines that perform both the collating or gathering of sheets or signatures and their permanent attachment in bookbinding. It includes equipment that integrates gathering, aligning and binding steps, such as those that insert adhesives, stitching or other fastening methods while assembling the sheets for a finished book.
[B42C 1/125] Sheet sorters combined with binding devices -- B42C 1/125 relates to equipment that integrates a sheet-sorting mechanism with a binding device, allowing sheets to be gathered in order and permanently attached in a single operation. Such machines combine collating functions with stitching, gluing or other binding processes for book production.
[B42C 13/00] Bookbinding presses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The machines, devices or equipment covered by this group is particularly used for treating, e.g. shaping, the book obtained from casing-in operations.
[B42C 13/003] Joint-creasing devices -- Joint-creasing devices are mechanisms in bookbinding presses that create a crease or fold along the joint of a cover and text block, aligning and securing the spine during binding.
