They typically consist of blades, rollers or pressure plates that apply localized pressure to form a sharp, uniform bend.
[B42C 13/006] Drying or setting devices for books -- The B42C 13/006 subclass pertains to devices used to dry or set books after the binding process, including heated presses, drying cabinets, and other apparatus that remove moisture or solidify adhesives to ensure proper book structure. These tools are designed specifically for post-binding treatment to improve durability and finish.
[B42C 15/00] Jacketing books -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Applying a protective book jacket to a bound book, typically an attractive detachable paper cover.  Covers as well applying protective adhesive or heat sealable foils and inserting books into protective cases.
[B42C 17/00] Rebinding books -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Replacement of book covers or fillings of bound books.  Reuse of book covers or fillings for bookbinding.
[B42C 19/00] Multi-step processes for making books -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bookbinding processes comprising a plurality of operations which are not fully covered by a single other group of this subclass and machines performing such sequences of operations. The format of the starting printing substrate (single sheets, signatures, webs) determines the steps required to obtain a book block, especially the type of collating operation.  The multi-step processes comprise operations covered by other places of subclass B42C as a process step and may include as well bookbinding steps not covered by this subclass, such as folding ( B65H45/00 , B31F1/00 ), collating ( B65H39/00 ), stitching with thread ( B42B ), cutting ( B26D ) or decorating ( B44 ), as well as particular handling and conveying steps therefor ( B65H , B65G ).  They may further comprise printing steps adapted for obtaining books, for forme printing ( B41F17/02 ), for electrographic printing ( G03G ) or selective printing ( B41J ), including the forme preparation ( B41C ) or the preprocessing of the content ( G06K15/00 , G06F3/12 , H04N1/00 ).
[B42C 19/02] starting with single sheets -- The subclass B42C 19/02 covers the initial stage of bookbinding that begins with individual sheets, detailing operations such as aligning, folding, and securing single leaves before they are assembled into signatures or blocks. It is part of the multi-step processes for making books.
[B42C 19/04] starting with signatures -- B42C 19/04 relates to the first step in book production that deals with handling and preparing signatures before they are bound. It covers methods for aligning, securing and readying groups of pages (signatures) for subsequent stitching, gluing or other binding operations.
[B42C 19/06] starting with webs not provided for elsewhere -- Multi-step bookbinding processes that begin with a web of material, such as paper or board, which is not covered by other specific categories. This includes handling, forming, and preparing the web before it is converted into sheets or sections for later binding operations.
[B42C 19/08] Conveying between operating stations in machines -- This subclass covers devices and methods that transport workpieces, such as sheets or bound sections, from one operating station to another within bookbinding machines, enabling the sequential steps required to manufacture books.
[B42C 3/00] Making booklets, pads, or form sets from multiple webs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Involves associating, registering and cutting one or more webs in multiple layers, usually printed, to obtain sets of flat or folded sheets in a correct predetermined sequence as a single copy of the final product, e.g. of a booklet, for simultaneous or subsequent binding.
[B42C 5/00] Preparing the edges or backs of leaves or signatures for binding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparing the edges or backs of a set of flat or folded sheets or signatures, usually a book block or fillings, prior to associating it with a case or cover.
[B42C 5/02] by rounding or backing -- The B42C 5/02 subclass relates to the bookbinding operation of preparing the edges or backs of leaves or signatures for binding by rounding them or adding a backing material to reinforce the edges before the final assembly. This falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Preparing the edges or backs of leaves or signatures for binding.
[B42C 5/04] by notching or roughening -- B42C 5/04 covers techniques for preparing the edges or backs of leaves or signatures in bookbinding by creating notches or roughening the surface to improve binding grip. The processes may involve cutting, serrating, scoring, or otherwise texturing the material before assembly. This subclass falls under the broader category of performing operations in bookbinding.
[B42C 5/06] by fanning -- B42C 5/06 denotes a bookbinding method that prepares the edges or backs of leaves or signatures for binding by fanning the pages. The technique spreads the sheets to expose their edges for trimming, coating or reinforcement before the final binding step.
[B42C 7/00] Manufacturing bookbinding cases or covers of loose-leaf binders -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bookbinding cases are usually manufactured as a separate unit to be associated with the fillings of a book.  Cases for hard cover books, or covers for loose-leave binders, are typically produced by printing a rectangular sheet of paper, cloth or leather, known as the cover material, and subsequently gluing the cover material to a pair of rigid panels and a spine.  Soft covers, e.g. for books, folders ( B42F7/00 etc.) or suspension files ( B42F15/00 ), are made from foldable sheet material, e.g. as unitary cover from a single printed blank of cover material.
[B42C 7/001] Manufacturing suspended files -- The CPC subclass B42C 7/001 concerns the manufacturing of suspended files, specifically the production of cases or covers for loose-leaf binders used in bookbinding operations.
[B42C 7/002] Manufacturing loose-leaf binders, folders -- B42C 7/002 covers the production of loose-leaf binder cases or covers, including the forming, shaping and assembling of folders and binder shells used to hold removable paper pages. It addresses manufacturing methods, materials and finishing steps specific to bookbinding of such binder enclosures.
[B42C 7/003] Attaching fasteners -- B42C 7/003 covers the step of affixing fasteners-such as rings, clips, or other binding devices-to bookbinding cases or loose-leaf binder covers during their manufacture. It includes methods for securing these components to the case material, whether by stitching, adhesive bonding, crimping or mechanical attachment. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations and bookbinding.
[B42C 7/004] Rounding the back of covers -- The B42C 7/004 subclass covers the operation of rounding the back of a book cover during manufacturing. It refers to shaping the spine area of a case or loose-leaf binder cover into a curved profile to improve handling and aesthetic appearance. This step follows other cover-forming processes within the bookbinding domain.
[B42C 7/005] Creasing the back of covers -- This classification covers processes for creating a crease in the rear portion of book covers or binder cases, typically to facilitate folding or improve stiffness. The method may involve scoring, embossing or indenting the material before assembly and is used in manufacturing bookbinding cases or loose-leaf binder covers.
[B42C 7/006] Folding the edges of covers -- B42C 7/006 relates to the manufacturing of bookbinding cases or loose-leaf binder covers, specifically the step of folding the cover edges to form a finished edge profile. The subclass addresses apparatuses or processes that perform this edge-folding operation within the broader bookbinding domain.
[B42C 7/007] Reinforcing corners of covers -- B42C 7/007 relates to bookbinding processes that reinforce the corners of covers, especially in the production of bookbinding cases or loose-leaf binder covers. The subclass details methods, structures or materials used to strengthen corner areas to improve durability and handling. It is situated under the hierarchy of performing operations → bookbinding → manufacturing bookbinding cases or covers.
[B42C 7/008] Conveying means between operation stations -- This classification covers devices that transport materials or workpieces from one station to another in the process of making bookbinding cases or loose-leaf binder covers. It includes mechanisms such as conveyors, rollers and transfer tables used to move sheets, blanks or partially assembled components between successive operations.
[B42C 7/009] Decorating book covers -- This CPC subclass B42C 7/009 concerns techniques for ornamenting the exterior of book covers, such as applying pigments, embossing, foil stamping or other decorative treatments to protective cases or loose-leaf binder covers. It falls under Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Manufacturing bookbinding cases or covers of loose-leaf binders.
[B42C 9/00] Applying glue or adhesive peculiar to bookbinding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and devices for applying adhesive, e.g. glue or paste,
[B42C 9/0006] by applying adhesive to a stack of sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and devices to apply adhesive to a collated or gathered stack of sheets for attaching them together, for uniting it with a cover, or both. Usually, the adhesive is applied by a roller, whereby the roller is moved along a stack in a rest position or the stacks are continuously moved along a stationary applicator, as in industrial perfect binders.
[B42C 9/0012] with a roller  B42C9/0043  takes precedence -- This subclass deals with the operation of applying glue or other adhesive to a book block with a roller during bookbinding. It falls under the broader category of bookbinding processes and is superseded by the more specific B42C9/0043.
[B42C 9/0018] for binding stacks of sheets one at the time -- This subclass concerns the bookbinding process of binding sheets one at a time to form a stack, typically by applying a suitable glue or adhesive to each sheet before attachment.
[B42C 9/0025] and subsequently applying a cover  casing-in  B42C11/00 -- This classification code, B42C 9/0025, pertains to the process of bookbinding where glue or adhesive is applied specifically for attaching a cover, known as casing-in, which is further detailed in section B42C11/00.
[B42C 9/0031] with continuous flow of stacks of sheets -- B42C 9/0031 covers bookbinding processes that apply glue or an adhesive while a continuous flow of stacks of sheets moves through the binding apparatus, enabling uninterrupted binding of multiple signatures.
[B42C 9/0037] and subsequently applying a cover  casing-in  B42C11/00 -- This subclass covers the step of placing a cover onto a book after the glue or other adhesive has been applied, commonly referred to as casing-in. It relates to the later stage of bookbinding where the outer casing is bonded to the text block.
[B42C 9/0043] manually  bookbinding presses  B42C13/003 -- The classification code B42C 9/0043 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the EPO and USPTO, pertains to manually operated bookbinding presses, a specific tool or method within the broader category of applying glue or adhesive peculiar to bookbinding.
[B42C 9/005] and applying a cover  casing-in  B42C11/00 -- It pertains to the operation of adhering a protective cover or casing to a bound product as part of the glue-application process in bookbinding, falling under the broader class of applying adhesives specific to bookbinding. This is coded B42C 9/005 and links to the covering step defined in B42C11/00.
[B42C 9/0056] applying tape or covers precoated with adhesive to a stack of sheets  covers therefor  B42D3/002 -- The CPC subclass B42C 9/0056 covers the method of applying adhesive-precoated tape or covers to a stack of sheets in bookbinding, including the resulting covers. It is nested under the broader categories of performing operations and bookbinding.
[B42C 9/0062] the tape being fed from a roller -- The CPC subclass B42C 9/0062 addresses book-binding processes in which adhesive tape is supplied from a roller to the workpiece. It covers devices and methods that feed the tape directly from a rotating roll to apply glue or adhesive during binding operations.
[B42C 9/0068] on a single stack of sheets -- B42C 9/0068 covers the step of applying glue or adhesive to a single-stack of sheets during bookbinding, where the adhesive is applied directly to the sheets as a unit before further processing. This falls under the cooperative classification for performing operations in bookbinding.
[B42C 9/0075] with a continuous flow of stacks of sheets -- A method of applying glue or adhesive in bookbinding in which the sheets to be bound are supplied as a continuous flow of stacked sheets, allowing the adhesive to be applied while the material moves steadily through the process. This approach enables high-speed production of glued-bound books.
[B42C 9/0081] applying adhesive to individual sheets for binding them together  by fusing, welding  B42C9/0093 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B42C 9/0081 pertains to the application of adhesive to individual sheets in bookbinding, specifically through methods such as fusing or welding. This entry is part of a broader category that deals with applying glue or adhesives peculiar to bookbinding processes.
[B42C 9/0087] sheet shingling devices -- Sheet shingling devices are machinery or tools used in bookbinding to align, overlap and bond individual sheets before they are glued together, facilitating the formation of a continuous web or stack that can be trimmed and bound. They are classified under CPC B42C 9/0087, a subgroup dealing with the application of adhesives specific to bookbinding processes.
[B42C 9/0093] by fusing, welding sheets together -- A subclass for bookbinding processes that join sheets by fusing or welding rather than using conventional glue. It covers techniques such as heat, ultrasonic or solvent welding to bond pages, covers or other components during manufacture.
[B42C 9/02] for securing back linings, strips, ribbons or headbands -- This CPC subclass covers bookbinding methods and devices that secure back linings, strips, ribbons or headbands, typically by applying glue or adhesive.
[B42C 9/025] for securing bookmarkers -- This CPC subclass relates to bookbinding techniques that use glue or adhesive specifically to attach bookmarkers, securing them in place within a bound volume. It falls under the performing operations hierarchy for bookbinding and the application of adhesives.
[B42C 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- B42C 99/00 is a residual group in the bookbinding subclass covering any performing-operations related to bookbinding that are not specified in other groups of the subclass. It captures novel, unusual or unspecified binding processes, materials or devices that fall outside the defined categories.
[B42D] BOOKS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular final and input products of bookbinding processes, including  Particular final and input products of other processes for associating printed sheets, signatures or webs, e.g. processes for interposing inserts, including  Printed matter of special format, i.e. selection, arrangement, size or shape of the substrate, or style, i.e. selection or arrangement of indicia, comprising:  Particular devices for use with the above products or printed matter, comprising  Movable-strip writing or reading apparatus.  **Glossary:** - Binding: Used in the sense of book-binding for permanently connecting a filling to a cover to provide a bound product. Usually, the filling is a book block consisting of a set of printed sheets, quires, or signatures, which are loose or already permanently attached together, or insets which are individually mounted to become a permanent part of the book. Usually, the cover is a bookbinding case comprising a spine and the bound product is a book.
[B42D 1/00] Books or other bound products -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Books as an association of fillings with covers;  Albums as bound products comprising a cover associated with fillings, e.g.  Files with adhesive strips for mounting papers, the files providing a cover with adhesive strips allowing for permanently mounting papers thereto, e.g. insets or loose leaves, individual sheets or sheets already united to subunits, being subsequently mounted in one go or collected in the course of time and optionally modified for being mounted thereto.
[B42D 1/001] with at least two separate sets of sheets  loose-leaf binders with two or more filing appliances  B42F13/0033 -- B42D 1/001 pertains to books or other bound products that feature at least two separate sets of sheets, specifically addressing loose-leaf binders equipped with two or more filing mechanisms. This classification is part of the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books.
[B42D 1/002] Back of leaves or signatures prepared for binding -- The CPC subclass B42D 1/002 covers processes for preparing the rear sections of a book, such as the back of leaves or signatures, before they are assembled and bound into the finished volume. This includes methods for arranging, aligning or treating these components to facilitate binding.
[B42D 1/003] characterised by shape or material of the sheets  B42D1/08  and  B42D1/10  take precedence -- B42D 1/003 relates to bookbinding techniques where the defining feature is the shape or the material of the sheets used. When the claim also falls under the more specific sub-groups B42D 1/08 (sheet shape) or B42D 1/10 (sheet material), those sub-groups take precedence.
[B42D 1/004] Perforated or punched sheets -- B42D 1/004 covers the preparation of individual sheets for books or other bound products that have been perforated or punched, typically to create holes for binding, stitching, or other assembly methods. The classification concerns the techniques and equipment used to produce these pre-punched or pre-perforated sheets before they are incorporated into the final bound item.
[B42D 1/005] having plural perforation lines, e.g. for detaching parts of the sheets -- The CPC subclass B42D 1/005 covers devices or methods for creating multiple perforation lines on a sheet or page, enabling portions to be detached, commonly used in books, pamphlets, or other bound products. It belongs to the performing operations hierarchy for bookbinding.
[B42D 1/006] with at least one foldable or folded sheet -- B42D 1/006 covers book-binding processes in which the product includes at least one sheet that is foldable or already folded, such as pamphlets, brochures, or sections that are assembled into a bound volume. It addresses the handling, arranging and securing of these folded elements within the final book or bound product.
[B42D 1/007] Sheets or sheet blocks combined with other articles -- The classification B42D 1/007 covers book-binding methods in which individual sheets or blocks of sheets are attached to other items such as covers, inserts, or decorative elements to form a finished product. It includes techniques that combine the paper components with non-paper articles during the binding process.
[B42D 1/008] Sheet materials -- The B42D 1/008 subclass deals with sheet materials employed in the manufacture or repair of books and other bound products. It includes various types of paper, cardboard, synthetic or composite sheets, and related substrates that are processed, arranged, or joined during bookbinding operations.
[B42D 1/009] characterised by printed matter not otherwise provided for -- B42D 1/009 refers to bookbinding techniques that involve incorporating printed matter which is not otherwise defined in the patent, such as additional text, images or decorative pages that are added to the bound product. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding and books.
[B42D 1/02] in which the fillings and covers are connected by end papers -- This subclass relates to bookbinding techniques in which the text block and the cover boards are linked by endpapers that are folded and glued to the inside of the covers, creating a single, bound volume. It is part of the CPC group B42D 1/02 under PERFORMING OPERATIONS > BOOKBINDING > BOOKS.
[B42D 1/04] in which the fillings and the spine portions of the covers are secured integrally, e.g. paper-backs ("livres brochès", "Broschüren") -- B42D 1/04 covers binding techniques where the text block and the spine sections of the covers are joined in a single, integral construction, typical of paper-back books such as soft-cover novels and brochures. This method secures the fillings and spine together without separate attachment parts.
[B42D 1/06] in which the fillings and covers are united by other means -- A method of bookbinding in which the internal pages (fillings) and the external covers are joined by means other than traditional sewing or simple gluing, such as adhesive bonding, thermal welding, stapling, or other mechanical or chemical techniques that secure the parts together.
[B42D 1/08] Albums -- B42D 1/08 relates to the manufacture of albums, which are bound collections of pages or sheets assembled for holding photographs, stamps, artwork or similar items. It covers the specific bookbinding steps and techniques used to create a cohesive, durable volume designed for organized display or storage.
[B42D 1/10] Files with adhesive strips for mounting papers -- The B42D 1/10 subclass covers files that incorporate adhesive strips designed for mounting individual papers, allowing sheets to be attached and removed without traditional binding. These products are used in bookbinding and other bound-item applications where temporary or repositionable attachment of pages is required.
[B42D 11/00] Carrying forward or transferring entries from one page to another, e.g. for book-keeping -- B42D 11/00 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers methods and devices for carrying forward or transferring entries between pages, primarily used in book-keeping contexts, ensuring accurate record continuation across multiple pages.
[B42D 12/00] Book-keeping books, forms or arrangements -- B42D 12/00 covers inventions related to bookkeeping books, accounting forms and the way such records are arranged. It includes novel designs, structures, templates or configurations of ledgers, journals, receipts or other documents used for financial or administrative record-keeping, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding and books.
[B42D 12/005] Heading indicators -- Heading indicators are visual markings placed on the edges or surfaces of bookkeeping books, forms or similar documents that denote the title or subject of each section, page or column. They facilitate rapid identification and sorting of records during binding or filing operations.
[B42D 12/02] Book-keeping forms -- The B42D 12/02 class covers book-keeping forms, such as accounting books, registers, and related documents, used in the preparation, organization or management of printed or bound publications. It pertains to the design, layout and handling of these forms within the broader field of book-binding operations.
[B42D 12/025] specially adapted for wages or salaries -- B42D 12/025 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to book-keeping books, forms, or arrangements that are specifically designed for recording wages or salaries. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with bookbinding and books, focusing on specialized financial record-keeping tools.
[B42D 13/00] Loose leaves modified for binding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Loose leaves modified for binding and insets become permanent part of the book or bound product, e.g. a laminated passport data page specially adapted for binding with other passport pages.  **Glossary:** - Binding: Used in the sense of book-binding and of permanently attaching sheets together
[B42D 15/00] Printed matter of special format or style not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Printed matter of special format or style comprises:  Particular features selected for the substrate, usually paper or plastic sheets or cards, e.g. the selected material, the size or shape of the article, the article being foldable or made up of multiple parts;  Particular selection, presentation or arrangement of the indicia, e.g. of guiding marks, as combination of markings, as a form or in a table, to obtain a particular technical effect related the intellectual impression provided by the indicia;  Particular association with devices for use therewith, e.g. with stamps, photographs or security elements.  Particular substrate and indicia features can contribute in combination to provide a printed matter specially adapted for a particular purpose, e.g. a bank-note, a postcard, a letter-sheet or an identity card.  **Glossary:** - Printed matter: Articles bearing visible indicia produced by a graphic process, which effect primarily intellectual impressions, e.g. information, as opposite to esthetical impressions. Usually, the articles are sheet materials, the indicia are characters or patterns and examples for the graphic processes are printing, marking, duplicating, copying or decalcomania.
[B42D 15/0006] Paper provided with guiding marks, e.g. ruled, squared or scaled paper -- This CPC subclass covers paper that is pre-printed with guiding marks such as ruled, squared or scaled lines for use in bookbinding and related printing operations. It is classified under B42D 15/00, which pertains to printed matter of special format or style not otherwise provided for.
[B42D 15/0033] Owner certificates, insurance policies, guarantees -- B42D 15/0033 pertains to the production of owner certificates, insurance policies and guarantees that are incorporated during bookbinding of printed matter of special format or style not otherwise provided for. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations - bookbinding - books - printed matter of special format or style.
[B42D 15/004] Questionnaires, statistics  for manually- or mechanically-operated teaching appliances  G09B3/00 -- This classification code, B42D 15/004, pertains to printed matter of special format or style designed for use in questionnaires and statistical materials, specifically for manually- or mechanically-operated teaching appliances as categorized under G09B3/00. It falls within the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books.
[B42D 15/0046] Forms for staff or material planning; Inventories -- B42D 15/0046 pertains to specially formatted printed documents used in book-binding operations for managing personnel, material requirements and stock levels. It includes forms, worksheets and inventory sheets designed to plan staff assignments, track supplies and record inventory counts, distinct from generic printing formats.
[B42D 15/0053] Forms specially designed for commercial use, e.g. bills, receipts, offer or order sheets, coupons -- This CPC subclass covers pre-printed or specially shaped paper forms used in commercial transactions such as invoices, receipts, offer or order sheets, and coupons. It concerns the manufacture and design of printed matter in specific formats for business purposes.
[B42D 15/006] Shipping forms -- Shipping forms are specialized printed documents used to record and facilitate the transport of goods, such as bills of lading, consignment notes and waybills. The classification covers their design, preparation and any associated binding or formatting features that distinguish them from ordinary printed matter. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, books, and printed matter of special format or style not otherwise provided for.
[B42D 15/0066] Timetables, lists or forms for shooter enlistment, e.g. for use at competitions -- B42D 15/0066 covers printed timetables, lists or forms specifically designed for shooter enlistment, such as those used in shooting competitions. It falls under the bookbinding category of the CPC, dealing with printed matter of a special format or style not otherwise provided for.
[B42D 15/0073] characterised by shape or material of the sheets -- The CPC subclass B42D 15/0073 pertains to bookbinding inventions in which the individual sheets are defined by their shape or by the material from which they are made, such as non-rectangular formats, flexible or rigid substrates, or composite sheet constructions used in specialized binding. It falls under printed matter of special format or style not otherwise provided for.
[B42D 15/008] Foldable or folded sheets -- The CPC subclass B42D 15/008 covers methods, devices and articles that produce or handle sheets designed to be folded or to form foldable structures, primarily for bookbinding and other printed matter formats not otherwise specified. It includes the creation, treatment, and assembly of foldable sheets used in special-format books and related products.
[B42D 15/0086] Sheets combined with other articles -- The subclass B42D 15/0086 covers printed sheets that are integrated with additional items, such as covers, binding elements or other auxiliary components, to form a combined product. It applies to special-format printed matter where the sheets are not provided alone but are part of a composite article.
[B42D 15/0093] Sheet materials -- The CPC subclass B42D 15/0093 covers sheet materials employed in the manufacture or repair of books, such as paper, cardboard, or synthetic sheets that form covers, endpapers, or reinforcing layers. It includes any specially prepared or treated sheets used to give books particular structural or aesthetic properties.
[B42D 15/02] Postcards -- The CPC class B42D 15/02 covers postcards, a specific type of printed matter that is not otherwise provided for and falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books. It addresses the production and characteristics of postcards as a distinct format within the printing and binding processes.
[B42D 15/022] combined with permanently fastened sound-producing or light-emitting means or carrying sound records -- This subclass relates to printed matter such as books that have permanently attached sound-producing or light-emitting devices, or that incorporate built-in sound recordings. It covers items where the audio or illumination components are inseparable from the printed item, for example musical greeting cards, illuminated manuals or books with integrated speakers.
[B42D 15/025] with peel-away layer hiding information  tickets for lottos or bingo games  A63F3/065 -- B42D 15/025 pertains to printed matter with a peel-away layer concealing information, specifically for lottery or bingo game tickets. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO, under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books.
[B42D 15/027] combined with permanently fastened other articles, e.g. photographs -- This subclass deals with bookbinding techniques in which additional items such as photographs are permanently attached to the pages or cover of a book during the binding process. It includes devices and processes for securing these supplementary articles so they become integral parts of the printed matter.
[B42D 15/04] Foldable or multi-part cards or sheets -- B42D 15/04 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the EPO and USPTO, pertains to foldable or multi-part cards or sheets under the category of printed matter with special formats or styles. This classification is specific to items that are designed to be folded or consist of multiple parts, such as greeting cards or brochures.
[B42D 15/042] Foldable cards or sheets  B42D15/045  and  B42D15/08  take precedence -- The CPC subclass B42D 15/042 covers foldable cards or sheets, such as greeting cards, brochures, and similar printed matter that can be folded to form a completed product. It is situated under the hierarchy Performing operations → Bookbinding → Books → Printed matter of special format or style not otherwise provided for, and it is subordinate to the more specific subclasses B42D 15/045 and B42D 15/08.
[B42D 15/045] Multi-part cards or sheets, i.e. combined with detachably mounted articles -- The B42D 15/045 subclass pertains to book-binding techniques that use multi-part cards or sheets combined with detachably mounted articles. It covers products such as greeting cards, promotional sheets or other printed matter where individual pages or sections can be separated, removed or have additional items affixed and later taken off.
[B42D 15/047] with photographs or slides -- B42D 15/047 covers book-binding operations for printed matter of a special format or style that incorporates photographs or slides. It falls under the Performing Operations hierarchy, specifically within Bookbinding and Books, and addresses the integration of visual media into specially formatted printed works.
[B42D 15/08] Letter-cards or letter-sheets, i.e. cards or sheets each of which is to be folded with the message inside and to serve as its own envelope for mailing -- B42D 15/08 classifies letter-cards or letter-sheets, which are individual cards or sheets designed to be folded so the message is enclosed within the same piece that also functions as its own mailing envelope. The entry falls under the bookbinding group for printed matter of special format.
[B42D 15/085] with special means hiding information -- B42D 15/085 covers book-binding designs that incorporate special means for concealing information, such as hidden compartments, secret pages, or covert printing techniques. The classification applies to printed matter of unusual formats where the concealment is an integral part of the binding structure rather than a separate accessory.
[B42D 17/00] Hanging or securing devices for books, newspapers or the like -- B42D 17/00 covers hanging or securing devices used to attach books, newspapers or similar printed materials to shelves, racks or other supports. These devices include straps, clips, hooks and other mechanisms that hold the item in place during storage or display.
[B42D 17/005] Hand-held holders for facilitating the reading of newspapers or the like -- The CPC subclass B42D 17/005 covers hand-held devices that hold newspapers, magazines or similar printed material to aid reading. These holders are designed to be portable and keep the publication open without the need for separate supports. They fall under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding and books.
[B42D 19/00] Movable-strip writing or reading apparatus -- A movable-strip writing or reading apparatus is a device that holds a continuous or segmented strip of paper, allowing the user to write on or read successive sections as the strip is advanced. Typical examples include notepads, legal pads and continuous-form printers where the paper feed is manually or mechanically moved. It falls under bookbinding technologies for producing and handling such strip-based documents.
[B42D 19/005] comprising reel to reel type winding and unwinding mechanisms -- The B42D 19/005 class covers bookbinding equipment in which a movable strip, such as a ribbon or web, is wound onto and unwound from a reel-to-reel system, enabling continuous feeding of the material during binding operations. This includes mechanisms that drive both winding and unwinding of the strip as part of the binding process.
[B42D 25/00] Information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures characterised by identification or security features -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Printing processes to produce identification or security features -> B41M3/14
[B42D 25/20] characterised by a particular use or purpose -- This subclass covers information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features and are designed for a particular use, such as authentication, access control or anti-counterfeiting within bookbinding processes.
[B42D 25/21] for multiple purposes -- B42D 25/21 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures designed for multiple purposes, characterized by their identification or security features. These items are often used in contexts requiring versatile documentation or authentication methods.
[B42D 25/22] for use in combination with accessories specially adapted for information-bearing cards -- B42D 25/22 relates to devices that are meant to be used together with accessories specially adapted for information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures having identification or security features, such as smart cards, RFID tags or security-coded paper. The entry falls under the CPC hierarchy performing operations → bookbinding → books.
[B42D 25/23] Identity cards -- B42D 25/23 relates to identity cards, which are information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features for personal verification. These cards may include photographs, signatures, magnetic strips, barcodes, holograms or other anti-counterfeiting elements.
[B42D 25/24] Passports -- B42D 25/24 covers the production and design of passports, which are information-bearing cards or sheets incorporating identification and security elements such as biometric data, holograms, watermarks, and machine-readable zones. The subclass addresses methods, materials and binding techniques used to create these secure travel documents.
[B42D 25/25] Public transport tickets -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus for printing and issuing -> G07B
[B42D 25/26] Entrance cards -- B42D 25/26 covers entrance cards, which are information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures featuring identification or security elements and are used in bookbinding applications such as library or access passes. The classification falls under Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Books → Information-bearing cards characterised by identification or security features.
[B42D 25/27] Lots, e.g. lottery tickets -- B42D 25/27 relates to the manufacture or handling of lots such as lottery tickets, which are information-bearing sheets or cards incorporating identification or security features. The subclass falls under performing operations for bookbinding and covers methods for preparing, processing, or securing these ticket lots.
[B42D 25/28] for use in medical treatment or therapy -- B42D 25/28 covers information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures used in bookbinding that incorporate identification or security features and are intended for medical treatment or therapy, such as patient identification cards, therapy record sheets or medication information inserts integrated into bound documents.
[B42D 25/285] Gift cards -- Gift cards are printable or pre-printed cards that carry monetary value and are used as prepaid means of payment. They are manufactured as information-bearing sheets with security features such as barcodes, magnetic strips or holograms, and are processed through bookbinding-type operations to ensure durability and tamper-resistance.
[B42D 25/29] Securities -- The CPC entry B42D 25/29 falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Bookbinding - Books and concerns information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features. It covers securities such as stock certificates, bonds or other printed documents designed to verify authenticity and prevent fraud. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO for patent documents related to the manufacturing or processing of such security-enhanced paper items.
[B42D 25/30] Identification or security features, e.g. for preventing forgery -- B42D 25/30 covers the incorporation of identification or security elements into information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures, such as those used in bookbinding, to prevent forgery or counterfeiting.
[B42D 25/305] Associated digital information -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Record carriers for use with machines and with at least a part designed to carry digital markings -> G06K19/00
[B42D 25/309] Photographs -- The CPC subclass B42D 25/309 covers photographs that constitute information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures featuring identification or security elements, applied within bookbinding operations. It classifies photographic sheets used in items such as passports, ID cards or other security-marked documents that are incorporated into books or similar assembled products.
[B42D 25/313] Fingerprints -- B42D 25/313 concerns the incorporation of fingerprint patterns as an identification or security feature on information-bearing cards, sheets or book components. It relates to bookbinding and other operations where such biometric marks are printed, embossed or otherwise applied to protect documents.
[B42D 25/318] Signatures -- The class B42D 25/318 addresses signatures used in books and similar printed items, including handwritten, printed, embossed or other identifiable markings that serve authentication, personalization or security purposes. It covers the application, design and integration of such signatures in the binding process.
[B42D 25/324] Reliefs -- The CPC class B42D 25/324 refers to relief features applied to information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures used in bookbinding, where the reliefs serve identification or security purposes. It falls under Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Books and covers embossed or raised markings that provide authentication or data encoding on such materials.
[B42D 25/328] Diffraction gratings -- B42D 25/328 concerns the incorporation of diffraction gratings into information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures, such as security or identification features printed on book pages or related documents. It addresses methods and devices that embed or apply optical gratings for authentication, anti-counterfeiting or data encoding in books.
[B42D 25/333] Watermarks -- B42D 25/333 covers watermarks, which are designs or patterns integrated into paper, card or sheet material during manufacturing to provide visual or covert identification and security. The method creates variations in thickness or composition that become visible when held to light, aiding authentication of books, documents or other information-bearing substrates.
[B42D 25/337] Guilloche patterns -- B42D 25/337 covers the creation of guilloche patterns-intricate interlaced line designs generated by rotating or overlapping circles-used on information-bearing cards, sheets or book structures for identification or security purposes.
[B42D 25/342] Moiré effects -- B42D 25/342 relates to the use of moiré patterns in information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features. It covers methods of generating, applying or detecting moiré effects to provide visual authentication, anti-counterfeiting or data-encoding functions in printed or embossed media.
[B42D 25/346] Perforations -- B42D 25/346 covers the creation of perforations in information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures, such as those used for identification, authentication or security purposes. The class includes methods, apparatus and resulting features where holes or slots are produced to facilitate handling, separation or verification of the material.
[B42D 25/351] Translucent or partly translucent parts, e.g. windows -- B42D 25/351 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures with identification or security features, specifically focusing on those that include translucent or partly translucent components, such as windows.
[B42D 25/355] Security threads -- Security threads are thin metallic or polymer strips embedded in paper or card stock during manufacture to provide tamper-evident or anti-counterfeit features. They run continuously through the substrate and become visible when held to light, often bearing micro-printing, magnetic or fluorescent elements for verification.
[B42D 25/36] comprising special materials -- The B42D 25/36 subclass pertains to bookbinding operations that incorporate information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures featuring identification or security elements, where the process or product makes use of special materials such as polymers, composites, or antimicrobial agents. This classification falls under Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Books → Information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures characterised by identification or security features.
[B42D 25/364] Liquid crystals -- B42D 25/364 pertains to the use of liquid crystal layers in information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features, for example in bookbinding or similar applications. The classification falls under performing operations - bookbinding - books - information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures characterised by identification or security features.
[B42D 25/369] Magnetised or magnetisable materials -- B42D 25/369 covers the use of magnetised or magnetisable materials in information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features, such as those employed in book-binding applications. The code addresses how magnetic elements are integrated to enable data storage, authentication or anti-counterfeiting functions within these specialised documents.
[B42D 25/373] Metallic materials -- The B42D 25/373 subclass concerns the use of metallic materials as substrates or components in information-bearing cards and sheet-like structures that carry identification or security features. It includes metal foils, alloys, laminates and plating employed to give cards such as passports, ID cards or security tags mechanical strength, durability and anti-counterfeit properties.
[B42D 25/378] Special inks -- The B42D 25/378 subclass covers the use of special inks in information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that carry identification or security features, within the context of bookbinding processes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books, and addresses inks formulated for anti-counterfeiting, authentication, or data-encoding purposes.
[B42D 25/382] absorbing or reflecting infrared light -- It covers technologies for cards or sheet-like structures used in books that incorporate features to absorb or reflect infrared light for identification or security purposes. The classification includes materials, coatings and designs that interact with infrared radiation to enable authentication, anti-counterfeiting or data-reading functions.
[B42D 25/387] absorbing or reflecting ultraviolet light -- The classification B42D 25/387 covers information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures, such as those used in books, that incorporate materials or coatings designed to absorb or reflect ultraviolet light for identification or security purposes, including UV-visible inks, dyes or reflective layers applied during the bookbinding process.
[B42D 25/391] absorbing or reflecting polarised light -- The B42D 25/391 subclass concerns book-binding techniques that employ materials or surface treatments to absorb or reflect polarized light, often to create security features or visual effects on information-bearing cards or sheets. It includes coatings, pigments, metallised layers or structural designs that manipulate polarized light for identification or protective purposes.
[B42D 25/40] Manufacture -- This subclass covers processes and apparatus for producing information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that incorporate identification or security features, such as passports, ID cards, or secure documents, focusing specifically on their manufacture.
[B42D 25/405] Marking -- The B42D 25/405 entry covers techniques for applying identification or security marks-such as serial numbers, barcodes, security inks, holograms or other distinguishing symbols-to information-bearing cards, sheets or book components during bookbinding operations. It includes devices and processes that create, attach or embed such marks to ensure traceability, authenticity or anti-counterfeiting of the finished books.
[B42D 25/41] using electromagnetic radiation -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Marking by removal of material using electromagnetic radiation -> B42D25/435
[B42D 25/415] using chemicals -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Marking by removal of material using chemicals -> B42D25/445
[B42D 25/42] by photographic processes -- The B42D 25/42 subgroup pertains to book-binding operations that create information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures with identification or security features using photographic techniques, such as photolithographic or heat-transfer processes. It covers methods for embedding images, holograms or other photo-based security marks during the manufacturing of books or related printed items.
[B42D 25/425] by deformation, e.g. embossing -- B42D 25/425 refers to methods of forming identification or security features on information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures by deforming the material, such as embossing, to create raised or indented patterns that serve as tactile or visual markers. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books.
[B42D 25/43] by removal of material -- B42D 25/43 relates to bookbinding techniques where portions of a card, sheet or paper are removed to form identification or security features, such as cutting, punching or trimming in the production of books or other information-bearing items.
[B42D 25/435] using electromagnetic radiation, e.g. laser -- B42D 25/435 pertains to the use of electromagnetic radiation, such as lasers, for creating identification or security features on information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures within the context of bookbinding and related operations.
[B42D 25/44] using mechanical means, e.g. engraving -- This CPC subclass (B42D 25/44) concerns the use of mechanical techniques, such as engraving, to apply identification or security features to information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures, typically within the context of bookbinding and related book products.
[B42D 25/445] using chemical means, e.g. etching -- B42D 25/445 pertains to chemical methods, such as etching, used to create or modify identification or security features on information-bearing cards and sheet-like structures. It falls under the bookbinding category of the CPC, covering processes that apply chemical treatments to produce marks, patterns, or micro-structures that serve authentication purposes.
[B42D 25/45] Associating two or more layers -- The subclass B42D 25/45 covers techniques for joining two or more layers of information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that have identification or security features, such as laminating, adhesive bonding, heat-fusing or other methods that create a multi-layer security element. It is used in the production of secure documents, cards and similar items where layered construction enhances durability or anti-counterfeiting performance.
[B42D 25/455] using heat -- This subgroup relates to techniques that employ heat to treat information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures with identification or security features, such as activating thermal inks, embossing, or sealing to create or enhance protective marks during bookbinding or security printing.
[B42D 25/46] using pressure -- The subclass B42D 25/46 relates to the use of pressure in the manufacture or processing of information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that have identification or security features, such as embossing, sealing or other pressure-based operations within bookbinding.
[B42D 25/465] using chemicals or adhesives -- The subgroup B42D 25/465 covers methods that employ chemicals or adhesives in the manufacture or processing of information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that possess identification or security features, such as applying binding agents, sealing layers or securing elements to the substrate. This relates to book-binding operations for security-enhanced cards and documents.
[B42D 25/47] using adhesives -- The B42D 25/47 subclass refers to bookbinding processes that involve the use of adhesives on information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that have identification or security features. It covers methods of applying glue to attach, seal, or reinforce such items during the binding operation. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books.
[B42D 25/475] Cutting cards -- B42D 25/475 covers methods and apparatus for cutting cards that serve as information-bearing or security elements in bookbinding. It includes processes that shape or trim identification or anti-counterfeit cards used within book structures, such as library cards, loyalty cards, or protective inserts.
[B42D 25/48] Controlling the manufacturing process -- The entry B42D 25/48 concerns technical measures for controlling the manufacturing of information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that include identification or security features. It covers systems and methods that monitor, regulate and adjust production parameters-such as temperature, pressure, coating or printing-to ensure consistent quality and compliance with security specifications.
[B42D 25/485] by electronic processing means -- B42D 25/485 covers the electronic processing of information-bearing cards or sheet-like structures that have identification or security features used in bookbinding operations. It applies to methods and devices that read, write, or modify data on such media during the manufacture, personalization, or protection of books.
[B42D 3/00] Book covers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All kinds of covers: hard, soft, removable and permanent protective covers and particular details thereof;  Particular devices for use with covers, e.g. for protecting or reinforcing edges of corners thereof;  Accessories for book covers or books not otherwise provided for, specially adapted for use therewith or for removable attachment thereto, e.g. protective jackets or protective book cases.
[B42D 3/002] Covers or strips provided with adhesive for binding -- Sub-class B42D 3/002 relates to book-binding components that are covers or strips pre-coated with adhesive, intended to be applied directly to a book during the binding process.
[B42D 3/004] Devices for protecting or reinforcing edges or corners of covers  attaching said devices  B42C7/007 ; for loose-leaf binders  B42F13/004 -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification, B42D 3/004 falls under Performing Operations - Bookbinding - Books - Book covers. It covers devices that protect or reinforce the edges or corners of book covers, including methods of attaching such devices, and similar solutions for loose-leaf binders.
[B42D 3/006] Indexing means on book covers  on loose-leaf binders  B42F13/0053 -- B42D 3/006 pertains to indexing means on book covers, specifically addressing features related to loose-leaf binders as detailed in B42F13/0053. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office for organizing patent documents.
[B42D 3/008] applied on the back of book covers -- B42D 3/008 covers techniques for applying materials or treatments to the back side of book covers, including lamination, printing, coating, adhesive bonding and protective layers that enhance durability, appearance or functionality. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding and books, specifically dealing with back-cover modifications.
[B42D 3/02] made of special materials -- This subgroup covers book covers that are produced from special materials, including synthetic polymers, metal laminates, and other non-traditional substances, distinguishing them from ordinary paper or cloth covers. It relates to the manufacture of book covers using these materials in binding processes.
[B42D 3/04] loose -- The CPC subclass B42D 3/04 refers to book covers that are supplied or used in a loose, unattached form, intended to be placed over a book without being permanently bound. It covers removable or replaceable protective coverings for books, distinguishing them from integrated or permanently fixed covers.
[B42D 3/045] Protective cases for books -- This CPC subclass B42D 3/045 covers protective cases for books, such as slipcovers, dust jackets, and other enclosures designed to shield printed material from damage, wear, or environmental exposure. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, bookbinding, books and book covers.
[B42D 3/06] with hinges -- B42D 3/06 covers book-cover structures that incorporate hinges, enabling the cover to open and close around the pages. It includes designs where the hinge is integral to the cover or attached as a separate component, allowing repeated movement without detaching. This subclass falls under the book-binding section of the Performing Operations group in the CPC system.
[B42D 3/08] Ornamented covers -- B42D 3/08 covers ornamented book covers, including decorative elements such as embossing, foil stamping and other artistic treatments applied to the outer surface of a bound volume. This subclass falls under the bookbinding category within the performing operations section of the CPC system, addressing designs that enhance the visual appeal and protection of books.
[B42D 3/10] with locks or closures -- B42D 3/10 covers book cover designs that incorporate mechanisms such as locks, clasps or other closures to secure the contents. It is classified under performing operations, bookbinding, books and the book-covers sub-group in the CPC system.
[B42D 3/12] combined with other articles -- The code B42D 3/12 designates book-cover constructions that are formed together with additional elements, such as protective sleeves, inserts, or other attached articles, resulting in a single integrated covering unit for a book. It covers methods and devices that combine the cover and the supplementary component in one manufacturing step.
[B42D 3/123] incorporating sound producing or light emitting means or carrying sound records -- B42D 3/123 covers book cover designs that integrate audio or visual components, such as built-in speakers, radios, light-emitting devices, or slots for sound records. The subclass falls under the book-binding hierarchy, specifically relating to covers that provide multimedia functionality beyond the printed page.
[B42D 3/126] enabling the book to be positioned upright  for loose-leaf binders  B42F13/402 -- B42D 3/126 pertains to book cover designs that allow books to stand upright in loose-leaf binders, enhancing organization and accessibility. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B42D 3/14] with column markers or line or heading indicators -- B42D 3/14 covers book cover manufacturing steps in which column markers, lines, or heading indicators are applied to the cover material. The feature assists in aligning text or graphics during binding or finishing operations. It falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy of Performing Operations - Bookbinding - Books - Book Covers.
[B42D 3/145] attached to the back of the cover -- B42D 3/145 concerns elements that are fixed to the rear side of a book cover. It includes protective strips, reinforcement plates, decorative panels or other attachments mounted on the back of the cover to improve durability or appearance. The classification is used for patents relating to such back-cover attachments.
[B42D 3/16] with means for holding books open -- This subclass pertains to book covers that incorporate devices for keeping the volume open, such as clasps, springs, or other holding mechanisms, enabling hands-free reading or display. It is part of the bookbinding group under performing operations.
[B42D 3/18] Other accessories -- B42D 3/18 relates to accessories used with book covers that are not covered by other specific headings, such as fasteners, protective sleeves or decorative elements. It is positioned in the bookbinding sub-group of the performing-operations section of the CPC.
[B42D 5/00] Sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheets of a pad, note-pad, tablet or block are united with the intention or option to permanently remove single sheets.  Particular supports or holders for such sheets united without binding, not otherwise provided for.  Combinations of such sheets, supports or holders with other articles, not otherwise provided for.  **Glossary:** - Pad: A pad (GB) or tablet (US) comprises a set of identical stacked sheets, usually glued together at one edge and backed by a piece of cardboard provided with a cover flap. - Note-pad: A note-pad is a united set of sheets with usually identical sheets for writing there upon, either unprinted or provided with guiding marks. - Form set: A form set is a united set of sheets printed with a form comprising spaces for filling in data or text, e.g. by writing or printing. - Block: A block is typically a block calendar or tear-off calendar comprising an ordered set of different sheets.
[B42D 5/001] perforated or punched sheets  B42D5/06  takes precedence -- B42D 5/001 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers perforated or punched sheets used to form pads or blocks, with B42D5/06 taking precedence for more specific cases. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy dealing with bookbinding and books.
[B42D 5/002] having plural perforation lines, e.g. for detaching parts of the sheets -- This subclass covers unbound sheets formed into pads or blocks that include two or more perforation lines, allowing individual sections or strips to be detached from the sheet stack. It falls under the bookbinding group of performing operations.
[B42D 5/003] Note-pads -- The CPC code B42D 5/003 covers note-pads, which are sheets gathered together without full binding, often held by a glue line or perforated edge so they can be torn off individually. These products fall under the bookbinding sub-group for sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks.
[B42D 5/005] Supports for note-pads  B42F9/00  takes precedence; underlays  B43L3/00 -- It covers devices that support or hold loose note-pad sheets, such as stiffened backs, clips or other backing elements that keep the sheets together for writing. The class belongs to the bookbinding group for sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks.
[B42D 5/006] combined with auxiliary devices -- The subclass B42D 5/006 relates to book-binding operations in which loose sheets that are not yet bound into a book are united to form pads or blocks, and the joining step is performed together with auxiliary devices such as clamps, rollers, or other supporting equipment. It covers methods that combine the sheets with these ancillary tools before the final binding stage.
[B42D 5/007] with light emitting devices -- The B42D 5/007 subclass covers sheets that are united without binding to form pads or blocks and incorporate light-emitting devices, such as illuminated pages or displays integrated into unbound paper assemblies. It falls under Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Books → Sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks.
[B42D 5/008] with means for engaging sheet perforations or slots -- B42D 5/008 pertains to book-binding processes that assemble loose sheets into pads or blocks by using mechanisms that engage pre-made perforations or slots in the sheets, such as pins, clamps or interlocking bars. This subclass is part of the CPC hierarchy for performing operations in bookbinding where the sheets are united without conventional binding.
[B42D 5/02] Form sets -- B42D 5/02 - Form sets covers collections of pre-arranged or pre-finished sheets, such as pre-cut, pre-punched or otherwise prepared paper, that are assembled into pads or blocks prior to the final binding of a book. It pertains to the preparation of these sheet groups for subsequent bookbinding operations.
[B42D 5/021] Cheque-books -- B42D 5/021 covers the preparation of sheets that are united without conventional binding to form pads used as cheque-books, including cutting, arranging and securing the pages into a functional block for banking transactions.
[B42D 5/022] Holders for cheque-books; Articles combined with cheque-books -- The CPC subclass B42D 5/022 covers devices that hold or contain cheque-books and articles combined with cheque-books such as holders, cases, or integrated accessories. It falls under performing operations - bookbinding - books - sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks.
[B42D 5/023] Continuous form sets -- B42D 5/023 covers continuous form sets of paper sheets that are united without full binding, forming pads or blocks that can later be cut, perforated, or otherwise processed. The sheets remain linked in a continuous strip, enabling efficient handling in bookbinding and related operations.
[B42D 5/025] Mailer assemblies -- B42D 5/025 covers mailer assemblies, which are pre-formed packages that combine one or more sheets, envelopes, or inserts into a single unit for mailing without using traditional binding. The classification includes designs where the components are fused, sealed or otherwise joined to create a ready-to-send mailer, often used for invoices, promotions or bulk correspondence.
[B42D 5/026] with return letter or return card -- A B42D 5/026 cover is applied to pads or blocks of unbound sheets that include an attached return letter or return card, allowing the recipient to send the document back. This sub-class falls under bookbinding methods that join loose sheets without full binding, and specifies the presence of a detachable return component.
[B42D 5/027] Sheets or cards attached to a carrier strip or web  shipping forms  B42D15/006 -- The subclass B42D 5/027 covers ways of joining individual sheets or cards to a continuous carrier strip or web, creating pads or blocks that are not bound in the traditional sense. It is used for producing shipping forms and similar documents where each sheet is attached to a carrier for easy handling.
[B42D 5/028] Form set holders; Articles combined therewith -- In CPC class B42D 5/028, the focus is on devices that hold a set of unbound sheets-such as pads or blocks-and the accessories that are combined with them. This includes form set holders, clamps, frames or other articles that secure or organize the sheets during handling or processing.
[B42D 5/04] Calendar blocks -- B42D 5/04 covers calendar blocks, which are sets of individual calendar sheets or cards that are pre-punched or pre-perforated and assembled without conventional binding into a pad or block for easy removal and use. These blocks are produced by joining the sheets together, often by adhesives or stitching, to create a reusable or disposable calendar format.
[B42D 5/041] with means for engaging calendar sheet perforations or slots -- B42D 5/041 pertains to the manufacturing of pads or blocks where sheets are not bound but united, specifically focusing on those that include mechanisms designed to engage with perforations or slots in calendar sheets. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European Patent Office and United States Patent and Trademark Office for categorizing inventions related to bookbinding and similar processes.
[B42D 5/042] Diaries; Memorandum calendars -- B42D 5/042 relates to the manufacture or assembly of diaries and memorandum calendars, covering devices, methods or binding techniques that combine pre-printed or blank sheets into a portable, date-organized format for personal or professional use.
[B42D 5/043] Supports for desk-type calendars or diaries -- B42D 5/043 relates to supports designed for desk-type calendars or diaries, falling under the bookbinding subclass that deals with sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks. These devices provide a stable holder or frame that keeps the calendar or diary upright and accessible on a desk.
[B42D 5/045] combined with auxiliary devices -- The subclass B42D 5/045 pertains to processes for uniting loose sheets into pads or blocks in which auxiliary devices-such as clamps, frames, conveyors or support tools-are employed to aid the binding operation. It covers the integration of these supplementary elements with the primary sheet-joining method to improve handling, alignment or efficiency.
[B42D 5/046] Suspension devices therefor -- B42D 5/046 is a CPC subclass within performing operations → bookbinding → books, specifically for sheets that are united without full binding to make pads or blocks. It covers suspension devices that hold or support those sheets during processing, such as mechanisms that keep the stack elevated or separated.
[B42D 5/047] in which the calendar sheet or sheets are combined with other articles -- This subclass covers bookbinding processes where calendar sheets are attached to or integrated with other items, such as bound products, packets or blocks, rather than being left as separate loose sheets. It includes methods for combining the calendar element with additional articles during the formation of pads, blocks or assembled books.
[B42D 5/048] movable-strip-type calendars -- Movable-strip-type calendars consist of a series of thin paper strips attached end-to-end and hinged so each strip can be turned to reveal a new date or month, allowing the calendar to be updated by sliding the strip. They are produced as unbound sheet assemblies for use in book-binding contexts.
[B42D 5/06] Tear-off calendar blocks -- B42D 5/06 designates tear-off calendar blocks, which are sheets loosely joined to form pads or blocks that can be removed one at a time for calendar use. The subclass falls under performing operations, bookbinding, books, sheets united without binding to form pads or blocks, and covers mechanisms and arrangements that facilitate sequential removal of individual pages.
[B42D 5/065] having plural perforation lines, e.g. for detaching parts of the sheets -- This CPC subclass relates to unbound sheet assemblies such as pads or blocks that incorporate several perforation lines to enable the selective detachment of portions of the sheets. It covers designs where multiple perforations are provided for easy removal of individual sections.
[B42D 7/00] Newspapers or the like -- The CPC subclass B42D 7/00 covers the production of newspapers, magazines, flyers and other similar printed periodicals, focusing on the methods and apparatus used to form, fold, and bind continuous sheets into these publications. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and books, and addresses the specific steps involved in creating printed media intended for regular distribution.
[B42D 9/00] Bookmarkers -- Bookmarkers are devices designed to mark a reader's place in a book, typically consisting of a thin strip, tag or decorative element that can be inserted between pages. The CPC code B42D 9/00 covers such items within the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding and books.
[B42D 9/001] Devices for indicating a page in a book, e.g. bookmarkers  B42D9/02  takes precedence; attached to book covers  B42D3/145 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B42D 9/001 pertains to devices designed for indicating a page in a book, commonly known as bookmarkers. This classification specifically excludes bookmarkers that are attached to book covers, which fall under a different code.
[B42D 9/002] permanently attached to the book -- B42D 9/002 covers book designs that include bookmarkers or similar elements that are fixed to the binding and cannot be removed, such as integrated ribbons, elastic bands or sewn-in tabs. The classification addresses any permanently attached feature used for locating pages within the book.
[B42D 9/004] removably attached to the book -- This CPC subclass B42D 9/004 covers devices or elements that can be attached to a book in a detachable manner, such as bookmarks, page markers, or other removable accessories used for locating or marking pages.
[B42D 9/005] clamped on the sheet -- The B42D 9/005 subclass relates to book-binding processes in which a device is clamped onto a sheet during handling, such as when applying adhesives, attaching accessories or performing other operations that require the sheet to be held securely. It covers mechanisms that secure the sheet while the binding work is carried out.
[B42D 9/007] Loose bookmarkers -- B42D 9/007 covers loose bookmarkers, which are detachable devices used to mark a position in a book without being attached to the binding. These items are classified under bookbinding operations and are distinct from integrated page markers.
[B42D 9/008] Devices for indicating a spot on a sheet  B42D9/001  takes precedence -- B42D 9/008 covers devices that mark a selected spot on a sheet, including bookmarks, clips, or other indicators that temporarily or permanently denote a position for reference. These tools are used in book-related operations to facilitate locating a specific page or line. The classification falls under performing operations, bookbinding, books, and bookmarkers.
[B42D 9/02] Automatic bookmarkers -- B42D 9/02 covers devices that automatically place or release bookmarks in books, typically integrated into binding or reading apparatuses. These systems detect page position and insert a marker without manual handling, improving convenience for readers.
[B42D 9/04] Leaf turners -- Leaf turners are devices or mechanisms that facilitate the turning of pages in books, often used in binding, reading or display contexts to enable smooth, controlled page movement. They may include manual or automated tools that grip, lift, or flip individual leaves without damage. This subclass groups such appliances within the broader domain of bookbinding operations.
[B42D 9/06] having an arm reset after each operation -- The B42D 9/06 subclass covers bookmarkers in which the supporting arm automatically returns to its original position after each bookmarking action. It defines mechanisms that reset the arm for repeated use, ensuring the device is ready for the next operation without manual repositioning.
[B42D 9/065] magnetically or pneumatically actuated -- B42D 9/065 covers book-binding or bookmarking devices whose actuating mechanism is driven by magnetic or pneumatic forces, such as clamps, rollers or markers that move automatically when energized by a magnet or air pressure. The subclass falls under performing operations, bookbinding, books and bookmarkers.
[B42D 9/08] having radial arms, one per leaf, operated successively -- B42D 9/08 covers book-binding devices that employ a separate radial arm for each leaf, with the arms arranged around a central point and actuated one after another to bind the pages sequentially. This subclass falls under the broader groups of Performing Operations, Bookbinding, and Books, specifically addressing mechanisms that use successive radial arm movements during the binding process.
[B42D 9/082] actuated by hand -- B42D 9/082 covers bookmarkers that are operated manually, meaning the device is actuated by hand to attach or release a marker within a book. It falls under the bookbinding section of the Cooperative Patent Classification, relating to mechanisms for positioning and retrieving bookmarks.
[B42D 9/084] actuated by a single spring -- The subclass B42D 9/084 covers book-binding mechanisms that are actuated by a single spring, such as spring-loaded clamps, rollers or folding devices used to hold, position or fasten pages. It includes devices where the sole source of motion is one elastic spring, distinguishing them from systems using multiple springs or other power sources.
[B42D 9/086] actuated by a spring for each arm -- The code B42D 9/086 designates bookbinding devices whose individual arms are powered by separate springs, allowing each arm to move independently when the mechanism is activated. This feature is used to open, close, or adjust binding components automatically.
[B42D 9/088] actuated by means other than springs -- The B42D 9/088 subclass covers bookmarker devices that are actuated by means other than springs, such as motor-driven, magnetic, pneumatic or other non-spring mechanisms, used to secure or mark a page in a bound book. This falls under the bookbinding hierarchy of performing operations for books.
[B42F] SHEETS TEMPORARILY ATTACHED TOGETHER -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheets temporarily attached together,with or without involving perforation of the sheets.  Means therefor.  Sheet details for temporary attachment.  Sheets and objects temporarily attached together.  Means therefor.  Filing aspects of albums, especially album sheet details for temporarily attaching objects thereto.  Filing appliances, i.e. Holders for collections of papers, sheets, cards, or units thereof, each paper, sheet, card, or unit being individually insertable and extractable.  Without or with fastening means, e.g. With clamping action, with separate holding means or using perforations.  Adapted for being suspended.  Specially adapted for card-filing.  File cards.  Indexing means; Indexing tabs or protectors therefor, e.g.: book fillings, pads, sheets, papers or cards, having edges cut away to facilitate retrieval of a particular location, tabs integral with, permanently fastened to, or detachably mounted on sheets, papers, cards, or suspension files, to facilitate classifying.  **Glossary:** - Filing appliance: means holders for collections of papers, sheets, cards, or units thereof, each paper, sheet, card, or unit being individually insertable and extractable. This expression may thus include a calendar, an instruction manual, or a letter file.
[B42F 1/00] Sheets temporarily attached together -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheets temporarily attached together and means, other than filing appliances, therefor, which do not involve perforation of the sheets. Usually, the means are repeatedly usable fastening means effecting a gripping action on the sheets, e.g. by a clamping jaws or resilient deformation of a clip.
[B42F 1/003] Devices for applying fasteners, e.g. clips, to bundles of sheets -- The B42F 1/003 subclass covers devices that apply fasteners-such as clips or staples-to bundles of sheets that are only temporarily joined, for example in temporary binding or sheet-handling operations. These tools are designed to secure multiple pages together without permanent adhesion, facilitating easy later separation.
[B42F 1/006] Fasteners comprising two co-operating jaws closed by spring action and that can be manually opened, e.g. clamps -- This subclass covers fasteners made of two cooperating jaws that are held together by a spring and can be opened manually, such as clamps used to temporarily bind sheets together in bookbinding.
[B42F 1/02] Paper-clips or like fasteners -- It covers paper clips and similar temporary fastening devices used to bind sheets of paper without permanent attachment.
