These fasteners are generally metal or plastic, designed to hold sheets together by friction and can be easily removed. The entry falls under sheet-binding operations within bookbinding.
[B42F 1/04] metallic -- B42F 1/04 covers the use of metallic elements for temporarily joining sheets in bookbinding, such as metal staples, clips, wire loops or other metal fasteners that hold pages together before permanent binding is applied.
[B42F 1/06] of flat cross-section -- The code B42F 1/06 covers book-binding techniques that temporarily join sheets of material and produce a flat cross-section of the bound assembly. It includes methods and devices that attach pages together without creating a pronounced spine, such as using wires, staples or adhesives that keep the sheets aligned in a planar shape.
[B42F 1/08] of round cross-section -- B42F 1/08 covers bookbinding methods that temporarily attach sheets by using a round-cross-section element, such as a cylindrical rod or spindle, to hold the pages together before permanent binding.
[B42F 1/10] non-metallic -- B42F 1/10 covers non-metallic means for temporarily joining sheets in book-binding operations, such as adhesives, plastic clips, or polymerized films that hold pages together without permanent fastening.
[B42F 11/00] Filing appliances with separate intermediate holding means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Filing appliances whereby the separate intermediate holding means are detachably mounted to the filing appliance, usually a cover, and can hold or engage parts of the collection, with or without using fastening means. Usually, both, the cover and the separate intermediate holding means are particularly adapted for releasable mutual engagement.
[B42F 11/02] engaging folds -- B42F 11/02 covers devices that engage the folds of sheets that have been temporarily attached, typically in bookbinding filing appliances with separate intermediate holding means. The claim relates to mechanisms that lock or hold the folded sheets together during processing.
[B42F 11/04] magnetic -- B42F 11/04 pertains to filing appliances that use magnetic elements as part of the temporary attachment mechanism for sheets, within the broader category of bookbinding and related processes. This classification is specific to devices where separate intermediate holding means are employed to keep documents or sheets together temporarily.
[B42F 1/12] Means for attaching together sheet corners exclusively -- The CPC subclass B42F 1/12 covers devices that join the corners of sheets without permanent bonding, such as corner clips, staples, pins or other fasteners that temporarily hold sheet corners together for handling, storage or processing. It is part of the broader category for bookbinding operations involving sheets that are temporarily attached.
[B42F 13/00] Filing appliances with means for engaging perforations or slots -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Filing appliances with means for engaging perforations or slots;  Particular parts of, or auxiliary devices or operations for, filing appliances:  Covers for loose leave binders and details thereof.  Locking followers or pressure bars for use therewith.  Content transfer devices.  Converting filing appliances into permanent binders.
[B42F 13/0006] Covers for loose-leaf binders  book covers  B42D3/00 -- B42F 13/0006 pertains to covers designed for loose-leaf binders and book covers, encompassing the specific category of filing appliances that engage with perforations or slots. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European Patent Office and United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions related to performing operations, specifically in the domain of bookbinding and temporary attachment of sheets.
[B42F 13/0013] made of special materials -- This subclass covers filing appliances used in bookbinding to engage perforations or slots in sheets that are temporarily attached together, where the devices are made of special materials such as engineered polymers, composites or treated metals to provide enhanced durability, flexibility or resistance to wear.
[B42F 13/002] with hinges -- B42F 13/002 covers bookbinding filing appliances that temporarily attach sheets together and include a hinged mechanism for engaging perforations or slots, allowing the bound material to open and close like a folder or binder. This subclass pertains to devices where a hinge provides a movable joint to secure or release the attached pages.
[B42F 13/0026] with locks or closures -- B42F 13/0026 covers filing appliances used in bookbinding that engage perforations or slots to temporarily hold sheets together and incorporate locks or closures to secure the attachment.
[B42F 13/0033] with two or more filing appliances  books with two or more sets of sheets  B42D1/001 -- This classification code, B42F 13/0033, pertains to bookbinding methods involving the use of multiple filing appliances that engage perforations or slots for attaching sheets temporarily. It specifically addresses books with two or more sets of sheets, aligning with the broader category of filing systems designed for organized document management.
[B42F 13/004] Devices for protecting or reinforcing covers, e.g. edges or corners  attaching said devices  B42C7/007 -- B42F 13/004 covers devices that protect or reinforce the covers of temporarily bound sheets, such as edge or corner protectors, and the means for attaching these devices to the cover, typically used in filing appliances that engage perforations or slots.
[B42F 13/0046] against ring damage -- The B42F 13/0046 subclass covers filing appliances designed to guard the rings of temporary sheet assemblies, such as ring-bound documents, from damage during insertion or removal. It includes mechanisms that engage perforations or slots while providing protective features that prevent ring distortion or breakage.
[B42F 13/0053] Indexing means on file covers -- Indexing means on file covers are features incorporated into the cover of a filing appliance that engage perforations or slots in temporarily attached sheets, allowing the user to locate, organize, or retrieve specific documents within a bound or loose-leaf collection. This device is classified under bookbinding operations for sheet grouping.
[B42F 13/006] applied on the back of file covers -- This classification covers filing appliances that temporarily bind sheets by engaging perforations or slots, specifically those applied to the back of file covers. It includes mechanisms such as clips, fasteners or adhesives that hold papers together without permanent binding.
[B42F 13/0066] with means for attaching the filing appliance to the cover -- B42F 13/0066 pertains to filing appliances used in bookbinding that incorporate a means for attaching the device to the cover, enabling sheets to be temporarily held together. It is a subcategory of the broader group dealing with temporary sheet attachment within the performing operations of bookbinding.
[B42F 13/0073] removable -- B42F 13/0073 relates to removable filing appliances that engage perforations or slots to temporarily bind sheets together, a technique used in bookbinding operations for handling papers without permanent attachment.
[B42F 13/008] Slots or openings in covers for rings -- The subclass B42F 13/008 covers binding equipment in which the cover of a ring-bound book has slots or openings that receive the rings, allowing the cover to be engaged while the sheets are temporarily attached. It applies to filing appliances that use perforations or slots to secure the rings during the binding process.
[B42F 13/0086] Auxiliary ring supports on covers -- Auxiliary ring supports on covers are components added to the covers of filing appliances that engage perforations or slots in sheets, allowing the rings to hold and temporarily bind the pages. They provide additional reinforcement for the ring mechanism, improving stability and ease of sheet removal and replacement.
[B42F 13/0093] Binding devices co-operating with corner or back cut-outs of the stack -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B42F 13/0093 pertains to binding devices that work in conjunction with corner or back cut-outs of a stack, used for temporarily attaching sheets together. This classification is specific to bookbinding methods involving filing appliances designed to engage with perforations or slots.
[B42F 13/02] with flexible or resilient means -- This subclass refers to filing appliances used in bookbinding where sheets are temporarily joined by flexible or resilient components that engage perforations or slots. It falls under performing operations - bookbinding - sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 13/04] with cords, coils, or chains -- A filing appliance for temporarily joining sheets that uses cords, coils or chains to engage perforations or slots, such as ring-binders or cord-bound folders used in bookbinding to hold loose pages together. This device belongs to the Performing operations → Bookbinding → Sheets temporarily attached together subclass.
[B42F 13/06] with strips or bands -- CPC B42F 13/06 covers filing appliances that secure sheets by means of strips or bands, engaging perforations or slots to hold pages together temporarily.
[B42F 13/08] of metal -- B42F 13/08 pertains to filing appliances made of metal that are designed to engage with perforations or slots in sheets temporarily attached together, as part of the bookbinding process. This classification is specific to metallic components used for securing documents in a non-permanent manner.
[B42F 13/10] of plastics -- B42F 13/10 covers plastic filing devices that engage perforations or slots to temporarily bind sheets together, such as plastic folders, clips, and binders used in bookbinding and document handling.
[B42F 13/12] with pillars, posts, rods, or tubes -- B42F 13/12 refers to filing appliances used in bookbinding that engage perforations or slots and incorporate supporting elements such as pillars, posts, rods or tubes to hold the sheets together temporarily. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and sheets temporarily attached together. It specifies the structural means by which the binding device secures the material during the filing process.
[B42F 13/14] with clamping or locking means -- This subclass covers filing devices that engage perforations or slots in temporarily attached sheets and secure them with a clamping or locking mechanism, such as ring binders or similar bookbinding tools. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, bookbinding, and sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 13/16] with claws or rings -- B42F 13/16 relates to filing appliances in bookbinding that temporarily attach sheets by engaging perforations or slots with claws or rings, enabling quick assembly and disassembly of bound sets.
[B42F 13/165] with flexible or resilient claws or rings  using comb-like elements for permanent binding  B42B5/10 ; using coils for permanent binding  B42B5/12 -- The B42F 13/165 subclass covers filing appliances that bind sheets permanently by means of flexible or resilient claws, rings, or comb-like elements, including coil-based systems, designed to engage perforations or slots in the material.
[B42F 13/18] on two bars relatively movable longitudinally -- B42F 13/18 relates to filing appliances that use two longitudinally movable bars to engage perforations or slots in sheets for temporary binding. The bars slide relative to each other, allowing the sheets to be fastened and released without permanent attachment.
[B42F 13/20] pivotable about an axis or axes parallel to binding edges -- A filing or book-binding appliance that holds sheets together by engaging perforations or slots and allows the assembled block to swing or rotate about an axis or axes parallel to the binding edges. The pivotable feature enables the temporarily bound pages to be opened or folded while remaining engaged.
[B42F 13/22] in two sections engaging each other when closed -- B42F 13/22 pertains to filing appliances designed with a two-section mechanism that interlocks upon closure, specifically for items where sheets are temporarily attached together through perforations or slots. This classification is part of the broader categories of performing operations and bookbinding, focusing on specific binding mechanisms.
[B42F 13/24] wherein one section is in the form of fixed rods -- B42F 13/24 refers to filing appliances employed in bookbinding for temporarily attaching sheets, where the portion that engages perforations or slots is formed as fixed rods. The rods hold the sheets in place without moving parts, enabling efficient alignment during binding operations.
[B42F 13/26] and locked when so engaged, e.g. snap-action -- B42F 13/26 covers filing devices used in bookbinding that engage perforations or slots in sheets and provide a locking action, typically by a snap-action mechanism. These appliances temporarily attach sheets together and remain secured once engaged.
[B42F 13/28] in two staggered sections -- B42F 13/28 pertains to filing appliances designed with mechanisms that engage perforations or slots, specifically arranged in two staggered sections for organizing sheets temporarily attached together. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO under the broader category of bookbinding operations.
[B42F 13/30] having a set of rods within a set of tubes for a substantial distance when closed -- B42F 13/30 designates bookbinding filing appliances in which, when closed, a series of rods are housed within matching tubes for a substantial length, enabling sheets temporarily attached by perforations or slots to be filed and retained.
[B42F 13/32] the nesting portions of the rods and tubes being straight -- This CPC subgroup covers bookbinding filing appliances that use straight nesting portions of rods or tubes to engage perforations or slots in sheets temporarily attached together; the straight sections serve as guides that align and feed the sheets during the binding operation.
[B42F 13/34] with the rods locking in the tubes -- B42F 13/34 covers filing appliances that join sheets temporarily by means of rods that are inserted into and locked within tubular guides, securing the perforated or slotted sheets in place. The mechanism is used in book-binding processes for quick, removable attachment of paper sheets.
[B42F 13/36] Locking followers -- Locking followers are components of filing appliances used in bookbinding to engage perforations or slots in sheets, temporarily securing the sheets together while other operations are performed. They move in coordination with the feed mechanism to hold the sheets in place, preventing slippage.
[B42F 13/38] Expansible cover splines -- Expansible cover splines are removable elements that can be spread or expanded to engage perforations or slots in a cover during bookbinding. They temporarily hold sheets together, allowing alignment and handling before permanent binding.
[B42F 13/40] combined or formed with other articles, e.g. punches, stands -- B42F 13/40 covers filing devices that are combined with other elements such as punches or stands, where the device includes means for engaging perforations or slots to hold sheets together temporarily.
[B42F 13/402] enabling the cover to be positioned upright  for books  B42D3/126 -- B42F 13/402 classifies filing appliances that use perforations or slots to temporarily attach sheets together and provide a mechanism for positioning a book's cover upright. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Sheets Temporarily Attached Together.
[B42F 13/404] with punches -- This subclass covers filing appliances that incorporate punching tools to create perforations or slots for temporarily attaching sheets in bookbinding operations.
[B42F 13/406] with means for holding pencils -- This subclass pertains to filing appliances designed to engage perforations or slots in sheets for temporary attachment and that incorporate a mechanism for holding one or more pencils. Such devices are used in bookbinding and office contexts to combine paper fastening with a built-in writing implement.
[B42F 13/408] with sheet rests or sheet lifters -- B42F 13/408 covers filing appliances used in bookbinding where sheets are only temporarily joined, and which incorporate sheet rests or sheet lifters to support, elevate or guide individual sheets while the perforations or slots are engaged. The features help maintain alignment and prevent damage during the binding process.
[B42F 13/42] Content transfer devices -- B42F 13/42 designates devices that transfer material-such as paper, ink, adhesive or other contents-between sheets that are temporarily joined in a filing or binding process. These content-transfer mechanisms are used in bookbinding appliances that engage perforations or slots to move the material from one sheet to another.
[B42F 15/00] Suspended filing appliances -- Suspended filing appliances are mechanisms that hold loose or temporarily bound sheets in a raised position, allowing easy insertion, removal or reordering without permanent attachment; they are used in bookbinding and filing systems to keep documents accessible while maintaining a temporary, non-permanent connection.
[B42F 15/0005] for record carriers, e.g. for disc records -- B42F 15/0005 pertains to suspended filing appliances designed specifically for record carriers such as disc records, ensuring organized and secure temporary attachment within a filing system.
[B42F 15/0011] for sheets, stacks of temporarily bound sheets -- This subclass covers devices that temporarily bind or hold sheets or stacks of paper, such as suspended filing appliances that keep the pages together without permanent fastening.
[B42F 15/0017] for filing individual loose sheets -- A suspended filing appliance designed to organize and store individual loose sheets in a bookbinding context, where the sheets are temporarily attached together. The device provides a means to file each sheet separately, facilitating easy handling, retrieval, and reassembly during the binding process.
[B42F 15/0023] with single suspension means -- This subclass covers bookbinding methods in which sheets are temporarily attached by a single suspension means, such as one wire loop or thread, that holds the pages suspended within the cover. It is used for temporary or easy-to-open bindings where the pages remain loosely connected.
[B42F 15/0029] suspended in the middle -- The code B42F 15/0029 covers devices that keep sheets or pages suspended in the middle of a binding assembly, allowing temporary attachment while the material remains free-standing. It includes mechanisms such as bars, clips or frames that hold the sheets midway between the covers for filing, access or processing without permanent binding.
[B42F 15/0035] suspended at both extremities -- B42F 15/0035 covers suspended filing appliances used in bookbinding where sheets are temporarily attached together by a binding that hangs from both extremities. The arrangement holds the pages in a suspended position between the top and bottom supports, allowing the sheets to be added, removed or opened without permanent attachment. This classification falls under performing operations, bookbinding, sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 15/0041] suspended at other than the middle and the extremity -- Suspended filing appliances that hold sheets at positions other than the middle or the ends, using mechanisms such as clamps, hooks or supports to temporarily attach pages in a binding system. The code applies to devices that create a non-central, non-extremity suspension point for sheets during bookbinding operations.
[B42F 15/0047] with two ore more of suspension means -- The CPC subclass B42F 15/0047 refers to book-binding devices in which the sheets are temporarily attached using suspended filing mechanisms that employ two or more separate suspension elements, such as hooks, wires or loops, to hold the pages before final binding. This design allows the sheets to be positioned and adjusted while remaining loosely connected.
[B42F 15/0052] for suspending stacks of temporarily bound sheets -- The code B42F 15/0052 relates to devices that hold or support a pile of sheets that are only loosely bound, allowing the stack to be kept upright or positioned for further processing. It includes mechanisms such as clips, brackets, or racks designed to suspend temporarily bound sheets during bookbinding operations.
[B42F 15/0058] with fixed suspension means -- This CPC subclass covers devices that hold sheets together by suspending them from a fixed support, such as a bar or frame, rather than using flexible or movable links. It falls under the bookbinding category of sheets temporarily attached together, specifically addressing suspended filing appliances with fixed suspension means.
[B42F 15/0064] with movable suspension means -- This subclass covers bookbinding or filing devices that hold sheets together by means of a suspension system that can be relocated or adjusted, such as movable arms, hooks or brackets that support the temporary attachment of pages.
[B42F 15/007] Support structures for suspended files  hanging large drawings  B42F15/06 -- This subclass covers devices that hold individual sheets or large-format drawings by suspending them from a supporting frame, rod, rail or similar structure, allowing the pages to be displayed, accessed or stored without permanent binding. It includes mechanisms for temporarily attaching the sheets while keeping them upright and accessible.
[B42F 15/0076] rotatable around a vertical axis -- The subclass B42F 15/0076 concerns suspended filing appliances in which the sheets or folders are temporarily bound together and can rotate about a vertical axis for easier access or organization. It falls under the bookbinding operations that involve handling sheets that are only loosely attached.
[B42F 15/0082] with a fixed support -- The CPC subclass B42F 15/0082 pertains to suspended filing appliances employed in bookbinding where sheets are temporarily attached together on a fixed support, such as a rigid frame or clamp, allowing the pages to be held in place before permanent binding.
[B42F 15/0088] with access from the side -- B42F 15/0088 covers suspended filing appliances in which sheets are temporarily attached together and can be accessed from the side of the device. Such apparatuses typically use a suspension system like rings or clips to hold the sheets and provide side entry for inserting or removing pages without disturbing the rest of the collection. They are used in various book-binding and filing applications where quick side access is required.
[B42F 15/0094] with access from above -- This CPC subclass concerns suspended filing appliances for temporarily attaching sheets together, where the device provides access to the bound material from the upper side. It includes mechanisms such as overhead ring binders or similar systems that allow sheets to be inserted or removed from above.
[B42F 15/02] in concertina form -- B42F 15/02 classifies suspended filing appliances that keep sheets temporarily attached together in a concertina or accordion configuration. It covers devices such as expandable folders or fold-out holders that allow a stack of pages to be held loosely while permitting easy expansion, folding, or retrieval.
[B42F 15/023] joined from above -- B42F 15/023 pertains to methods and devices for joining sheets that have been temporarily attached together by applying the joining element from above the stack, such as using a suspended filing apparatus to insert staples, pins or adhesives through the top side.
[B42F 15/026] joined on the side -- B42F 15/026 covers devices or methods that attach the edges of sheets together along their side, creating a temporary connection for filing or binding purposes. It is used in bookbinding where sheets are suspended without permanent stitching, often employing clips, fasteners or side-mounted strips.
[B42F 15/04] Backs or the like therefor -- B42F 15/04 covers the backs or similar parts of suspended filing appliances used in bookbinding to hold sheets that are temporarily attached together. The code is nested under performing operations, then bookbinding, then sheets temporarily attached together, and finally suspended filing appliances.
[B42F 15/06] for hanging large drawings or the like -- B42F 15/06 covers suspended filing appliances intended for hanging large drawings, plans, maps or similar oversized sheets for display, storage or inspection. These devices support the sheets in a vertical or inclined position without permanent binding, facilitating easy access and replacement within a bookbinding or filing system.
[B42F 15/063] with two sets of rods -- The B42F 15/063 subclass covers suspended filing appliances used in bookbinding that employ two parallel sets of rods to hold sheets together temporarily. The dual-rod arrangement provides a guiding framework for arranging pages before permanent binding, facilitating efficient handling of loose or unbound collections.
[B42F 15/066] for hanging a single drawing, e.g. with self-locking means -- The subclass B42F 15/066 covers devices that suspend a single drawing or illustration, usually by means of a self-locking mechanism that lets the sheet be hung from a binder or similar support. It is used for temporary attachment of individual sheets in bookbinding applications.
[B42F 17/00] Card-filing arrangements, e.g. card indexes or catalogues or filing cabinets -- B42F 17/00 relates to card-filing arrangements such as card indexes, catalogues and filing cabinets in which individual cards or sheets are temporarily attached together for easy storage, sorting and retrieval. The class covers the structure, support means and binding methods that keep the cards together while allowing them to be removed or rearranged.
[B42F 17/02] in which the cards are stored substantially at right angles to the bottom of their containers -- B42F 17/02 covers card-filing arrangements in which the cards are stored essentially at right angles to the bottom of their containers, such as in vertical drawers, rotating racks or similar devices that keep the cards upright rather than lying flat.
[B42F 17/04] the cards being staggered sideways -- The code B42F 17/04 refers to a card-filing arrangement in which the cards are positioned staggered sideways, so each card's edge is offset from the next, making the contents of individual cards more readily visible while the cards remain temporarily attached in a filing system.
[B42F 17/06] with separable or loose card separating means -- This subclass deals with card-filing or catalogue systems in which the individual cards are not permanently bound but can be separated or removed by loose or detachable separating elements such as dividers, tabs or strips.
[B42F 17/08] Construction of the containers, e.g. trays or drawers -- The subclass B42F 17/08 deals with the design and manufacture of containers such as trays or drawers used in card-filing systems, where sheets are temporarily attached together. It covers the construction details, materials and assembly features of these containers that hold indexes, catalogues or other filing cards.
[B42F 17/10] Hinged walls -- The B42F 17/10 classification covers hinged walls or panels used in card-filing systems such as indexes, catalogues or filing cabinets. It includes mechanisms that allow a wall or door to swing open or closed for accessing the temporarily attached sheets or cards. This subclass deals with the design of movable side or front walls that facilitate loading, unloading or viewing of the stored items.
[B42F 17/12] Dividing means -- B42F 17/12 relates to mechanisms that separate or split sheets or cards in temporary binding or card-filing systems such as indexes, catalogues or filing cabinets. The class covers dividing means that enable individual cards or sheets to be removed or positioned without permanent attachment.
[B42F 17/14] Locking followers -- Locking followers are devices that engage with a card or sheet stack to retain the items in a filing arrangement, typically using a spring-biased or latch mechanism to keep the temporary attachment firm.
[B42F 17/16] with card-retaining means -- The CPC subclass B42F 17/16 refers to book-binding or sheet-handling devices that temporarily attach sheets together and incorporate means for retaining cards, such as card-index or catalogue cards within a filing system. It covers arrangements where the card-retaining feature is integrated into the temporary binding mechanism, enabling easy insertion, removal and organization of cards.
[B42F 17/18] in which the cards are stored in a flat position -- The code B42F 17/18 refers to card-filing arrangements in which the cards are stored in a flat position, such as flat file cabinets, index trays or other systems where sheets lie horizontally rather than upright or hanging. It falls under performing operations, bookbinding, sheets temporarily attached together, and card-filing arrangements.
[B42F 17/20] and are pivotable relative to the bottom of their containers -- B42F 17/20 pertains to card-filing systems where the cards or folders can pivot in relation to the base of their storage units, enhancing accessibility and organization.
[B42F 17/22] Connections between the cards and their containers -- This subclass covers the means by which individual cards are joined to the surrounding container in card-filing systems such as indexes, catalogues or filing cabinets. It includes clips, slots, hooks, fasteners and other devices that secure cards within the housing, allowing them to be temporarily attached yet readily removable.
[B42F 17/24] Special adaptations for use of index strips -- B42F 17/24 covers special adaptations for using index strips in card-filing arrangements, such as slots, guides or attachment mechanisms that temporarily bind sheets for quick reference. The classification falls under performing operations, bookbinding and sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 17/26] Stands for the containers -- The classification B42F 17/26 pertains to devices that support or hold containers used in card-filing systems, such as card indexes, catalogues or filing cabinets. It covers stands or similar structures that keep these containers upright and accessible for temporary attachment of sheets.
[B42F 17/28] in the form of endless bands or revolving drums -- B42F 17/28 covers mechanisms that keep sheets, such as index cards, in a continuous loop or on a rotating drum, providing temporary attachment for filing, cataloguing or similar storage. The design enables rapid sequential handling of the cards within filing cabinets or related equipment.
[B42F 17/30] modified for particular uses -- B42F 17/30 covers modifications of card-filing arrangements-such as index-card systems, catalogue cards or filing cabinets-tailored for specific purposes. It includes adaptations that alter the basic sheet-temporarily-attached-together format to meet particular functional, ergonomic or environmental requirements, for example custom-sized cards, specialized slot designs or reinforced temporary bindings.
[B42F 17/32] with means for staggering cards already in the file -- The CPC subclass B42F 17/32 covers card-filing or indexing equipment that incorporates a mechanism to shift or stagger cards already placed in the file, allowing each card to be partially exposed for easier identification or retrieval. It falls under performing operations, specifically bookbinding and temporary sheet attachment.
[B42F 17/34] with card selection means, e.g. telephone number list finders -- The B42F 17/34 subclass covers book-binding arrangements in which sheets are temporarily attached together to form card-filing systems, such as indexes, catalogues or filing cabinets, and includes mechanisms that automatically select a specific card, for example telephone-number list finders. This classification pertains to devices that combine physical card storage with a selection aid to retrieve the desired information quickly.
[B42F 17/343] with sliding movement of selected cards -- This subclass covers card-filing or index systems where sheets are temporarily bound together and a selected card can be moved by sliding it along a guide or track, allowing selective access. It includes mechanisms that enable a chosen card to be shifted relative to the others within the filing cabinet or index.
[B42F 17/346] with pivoting movement of selected cards -- B42F 17/346 pertains to card-filing systems-such as indexes, catalogues or filing cabinets-where selected cards can be pivoted or swung out of the main stack for easier access. The classification falls under performing operations, specifically bookbinding, and the subgroup dealing with sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 19/00] File cards -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cards adapted for filing purposes, e.g. for card filing arrangements
[B42F 19/02] folded -- The B42F 19/02 subclass covers the folding of sheets that are temporarily bound together in the creation of file cards, a specific operation within bookbinding where individual cards are shaped by folds before final assembly.
[B42F 19/04] with receptacles or other holding devices -- B42F 19/04 relates to bookbinding techniques for sheets that are only temporarily joined, such as file cards, where the sheets are held together by receptacles, clips, pockets or other holding devices that allow easy removal or repositioning. This subclass falls under the broader category of performing operations in bookbinding for sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 21/00] Indexing means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indexing means, e.g. edges cut away on book or pad sheets, papers, or cards, to facilitate retrieval of a particular location, especially for classifying.  Indexing tabs integral with, permanently fastened to, or detachably mounted on sheets, papers, cards, or suspension files.  Protectors for indexing tabs.
[B42F 21/02] Tabs integral with sheets, papers, cards, or suspension files -- B42F 21/02 covers sheet, paper, card or suspension-file designs that include tabs formed as an integral part of the material for indexing or retrieval purposes. The classification falls under performing operations, specifically bookbinding, and more narrowly under sheets temporarily attached together and indexing means.
[B42F 21/025] for suspension files -- B42F 21/025 covers indexing means for suspension files, devices that temporarily bind a group of sheets so they can be added, removed or rearranged easily. It includes features such as rings, clips, pockets or other attachments that hold loose pages together within the broader bookbinding subgroup of performing operations.
[B42F 21/04] Tabs permanently fastened to sheets, papers, cards, or suspension files -- B42F 21/04 covers the use of permanently attached tabs on individual sheets, papers, cards or suspension-file leaves to provide identification, handling or indexing functions. The tabs are fixed by stitching, adhesive or other means so they remain integral to each sheet.
[B42F 21/045] for suspension files -- This subclass covers indexing devices employed in suspension-file systems in which sheets are temporarily attached together. It relates to mechanisms that enable individual folders or pockets to be organized, retrieved and secured while hanging from a rod or chain.
[B42F 21/06] Tabs detachably mounted on sheets, papers, cards, or suspension files -- The B42F 21/06 subclass covers detachable tabs that are mounted on sheets, papers, cards or suspension-file folders to provide indexing or labeling functions. These tabs can be attached and removed without permanent binding, allowing users to reorganize or update the contents of temporarily bound documents. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding and sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 21/065] for suspension files -- B42F 21/065 classifies indexing means that create suspension files, a type of temporary sheet attachment used in bookbinding. These devices hold loose or bound sheets together while allowing easy removal and reordering, supporting operations where documents must be organized without permanent binding.
[B42F 21/08] in one of a number of predetermined positions -- The B42F 21/08 subclass covers indexing devices used with sheets that are only temporarily bound together in bookbinding operations, where the index marks are placed in one of several predetermined positions on the material. It relates to methods of locating or identifying individual sheets within a loosely assembled set.
[B42F 21/10] represented by slits -- B42F 21/10 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to indexing means for temporarily attaching sheets together, specifically through the use of slits. This classification is used for inventions involving methods or devices that employ slits as a mechanism for organizing or binding documents.
[B42F 21/12] Sheets, papers, or cards having edges cut away to facilitate indexing, e.g. thumb cuts on books -- This subclass covers sheets, papers or cards whose edges are cut away to create notches that aid indexing, such as thumb cuts in books. It falls under the CPC group B42F for binding operations, specifically within performing operations - bookbinding - sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 23/00] Filing appliances not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- B42F 23/00 covers devices used to file or organize sheets that are temporarily bound together, such as folders, trays or other filing appliances that are not classified in the more specific groups of this subclass. These tools facilitate the handling, storage or retrieval of loose or loosely attached pages in bookbinding operations.
[B42F 3/00] Sheets temporarily attached together involving perforations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheets temporarily attached together and means, other than filing appliances, therefor which involve perforation of the sheets. Usually, the means are repeatedly usable fastening means engaging perforations previously provided in the sheets, e.g. bifurcated pins with bendable legs or rings engaging punch-holes in the sheets.  Details of sheets intended, prepared or modified for being temporarily attached together, usually perforation details.
[B42F 3/003] Perforated or punched sheets -- B42F 3/003 covers the creation of perforated or punched sheets that are temporarily linked for easy separation or assembly, such as loose-leaf paper, booklet blanks, or binder inserts. The classification pertains to methods and devices that form rows of holes or punched patterns to enable sheet removal or attachment without permanent binding.
[B42F 3/006] with edge reinforcing means -- This subclass covers methods and devices that add reinforcement to the edges of sheets temporarily joined by perforations, such as strips, tapes, or other means that strengthen the edge during handling or binding. It is part of the CPC group for bookbinding operations involving temporary sheet attachment.
[B42F 3/02] Attachment by means of bifurcated form -- The code B42F 3/02 covers bookbinding processes where sheets that are only temporarily joined by perforations are attached using a bifurcated form, such as a split or double-ended element that engages the perforations to hold the pages together without permanent binding.
[B42F 3/04] Attachment means of ring, finger or claw form -- B42F 3/04 refers to devices that temporarily join perforated sheets by means of ring, finger or claw-shaped attachments, commonly employed in bookbinding to create removable bindings. These attachment elements engage the perforations to hold the sheets together without permanent fastening.
[B42F 3/06] Attachment means of coiled form -- B42F 3/06 covers attachment means for sheets that are assembled in a coiled configuration, where the sheets are temporarily joined by perforations. The features relate to bookbinding methods that enable the sheets to be linked together yet remain removable, typically using perforated edges or a coiled binding element that secures the papers without permanent stitching.
[B42F 5/00] Sheets and objects temporarily attached together -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sheets and objects temporarily attached together and means for temporarily keeping them attached together;  Filing aspects of albums, especially those of album pages for temporarily attaching objects to the pages.  Usually, the means are repeatedly usable for attaching objects and are integral with, assembled to, or fastenable to, the sheets, e.g. corner-holding slits cut into sheets, filing strips on sheets or corner-holding devices to be applied to album pages.
[B42F 5/005] Albums for record carriers, e.g. for disc records -- B42F 5/005 refers to albums intended for holding disc records, such as record carriers that bind or loosely gather multiple records in a single booklet or sleeve. The subclass is part of the performing-operations branch, under bookbinding and sheets temporarily attached together, and it covers products where the sheets or objects are only temporarily fastened for storage or presentation.
[B42F 5/02] Stamp or like filing arrangements in albums -- This subclass covers the temporary joining of stamps or similar collectible items within albums, using arrangements such as pockets, sleeves, or mounts that hold the sheets without permanent binding. It relates to bookbinding techniques that allow sheets to be added, removed or rearranged while preserving the collection.
[B42F 5/04] with transparent pockets -- This subclass covers bookbinding methods in which sheets are temporarily joined and incorporate transparent pockets that can hold or display additional items such as documents, inserts, or other materials. The pockets are formed as clear sections within the temporary binding.
[B42F 5/06] Corner-holding devices, e.g. for photographs -- B42F 5/06 covers devices that grip or hold the corners of sheets, such as photographs, to keep them in place temporarily. These corner-holding tools are used in bookbinding and other sheet-handling operations to secure pages without permanent binding.
[B42F 7/00] Filing appliances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Filing appliances without fastening means acting on the members of the collection can comprise flexible or rigid holders with a single filing space, e.g. pockets, trays, covers with retention means, e.g. for preventing the collection from slipping out, or with a plurality of connected compartments, e.g. portfolios or multi-compartment cases.
[B42F 7/02] single gussetted pockets -- B42F 7/02 relates to single gusseted pockets used in bookbinding for temporarily attaching sheets together. It falls under performing operations, specifically the bookbinding subgroup for sheets temporarily attached, and includes filing appliances that incorporate a single pocket with a gusset to expand and hold pages.
[B42F 7/025] made of transparent material -- B42F 7/025 covers filing appliances used in temporary sheet binding that are made of a transparent material, such as clear plastic folders, sleeves or binders, allowing the contents to be viewed without removal.
[B42F 7/04] Covers with retention means -- Covers with retention means are protective or enclosing elements employed in temporary sheet binding that incorporate a device such as clips, straps or fasteners to hold the sheets securely in place. They are used in filing appliances to keep attached papers organized and prevent accidental loss.
[B42F 7/06] portfolios or cases with a plurality of compartments -- B42F 7/06 covers filing appliances such as portfolios or cases that provide a plurality of compartments for holding sheets temporarily attached together, enabling organized storage and easy retrieval in bookbinding operations.
[B42F 7/065] made of transparent material -- B42F 7/065 concerns filing appliances employed in bookbinding to temporarily attach sheets, where the appliance is made of a transparent material. These devices enable easy handling and positioning of pages before permanent binding.
[B42F 7/08] expansible -- B42F 7/08 covers filing appliances that are expansible, used in bookbinding to temporarily attach sheets together. These devices allow a stack of pages to be held loosely for handling, inspection or processing before permanent binding. The classification falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and sheets temporarily attached together.
[B42F 7/10] Trays -- Trays are filing appliances used in bookbinding to hold sheets that are temporarily attached together, enabling the sheets to be organized, transferred or processed before the final binding step.
[B42F 7/12] Stacked trays -- Stacked trays are filing appliances used in bookbinding to hold sheets that are temporarily attached together, allowing them to be stored, organised, or processed in a stacked arrangement. They facilitate easy insertion, removal and alignment of the sheet bundles during handling or further operations.
[B42F 7/14] Boxes -- B42F 7/14 covers boxes employed as filing appliances for sheets that are temporarily attached together in bookbinding. The subclass includes designs of containers that hold, protect or organize loose or partially bound pages during handling, storage or transport.
[B42F 7/145] for vertically storing documents -- The B42F 7/145 subclass covers filing appliances designed to store temporarily bound sheets in a vertical orientation. It pertains to bookbinding operations where documents are kept upright for convenient access, retrieval or transport, typically using trays, racks or similar devices.
[B42F 9/00] Filing appliances with devices clamping file edges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Filing appliances whereby the clamping devices or clamping backs comprise clamps with one or two pivoting or sliding jaws and releasable resilient clips allowing to insert and extract individual collection members.
[B42F 9/001] Clip boards -- B42F 9/001 relates to clip boards, devices that temporarily bind sheets by clamping their edges, serving as filing appliances used in bookbinding and other document-handling operations.
[B42F 9/002] combined with auxiliary devices, e.g. pencils -- The B42F 9/002 subclass covers filing appliances that clamp the edges of sheets and incorporate auxiliary tools, such as pencils, to perform additional operations while the sheets remain temporarily attached. This includes devices that integrate a writing implement with the clamping mechanism for marking, labeling or otherwise processing the bound pages.
[B42F 9/004] with illuminating means -- This subclass covers book-binding devices that temporarily hold sheets together by clamping the file edges and incorporates illumination, such as built-in lights, to improve visibility during the filing process. It applies to filing appliances equipped with lighting elements that assist operators in aligning and securing sheets.
[B42F 9/005] with auxiliary filing means -- This subclass covers filing appliances that incorporate auxiliary filing means, such as extra clamps or guides, to assist in holding or aligning the edges of sheets temporarily attached together during bookbinding operations.
[B42F 9/007] with sliding clamping means -- B42F 9/007 refers to filing appliances that secure the edges of a stack of sheets by means of sliding clamping mechanisms, used to temporarily bind the sheets together in bookbinding operations. The classification covers devices where the clamp moves linearly to engage and release the file edges.
[B42F 9/008] with symmetrical generally U-shaped clamps -- The B42F 9/008 subclass covers filing appliances that attach sheets temporarily using symmetrical, generally U-shaped clamps to grip the edges of the material. It is part of the bookbinding group for devices that clamp file edges for handling or temporary binding.
[B42P] INDEXING SCHEME RELATING TO BOOKS, FILING APPLIANCES OR THE LIKE -- B42P designates systems and methods for arranging and retrieving printed material, such as index schemes for books, filing appliances, or similar collections. It includes the structure of tables of contents, cataloguing formats, and other organizational tools that facilitate quick location of information within bound or stored documents.
[B42P 2201/00] Books or filing appliances for special documents or for special purposes -- The CPC code B42P 2201/00 designates books or filing appliances that are specially constructed for handling particular kinds of documents or for specific functional needs, such as archival, legal, medical or secure storage. It includes distinctive binding, materials or organizational features tailored to those special purposes.
[B42P 2201/02] for photographic documents, e.g. prints, negatives -- The subclass B42P 2201/02 covers books, albums or other filing devices specifically designed for photographic documents such as prints and negatives, supporting special handling or storage needs for visual media.
[B42P 2201/04] for securities, e.g. bonds, banknotes, stock certificates -- The CPC entry B42P 2201/04 covers bookbinding or filing appliances specifically designed for handling securities such as bonds, banknotes or stock certificates, i.e., special-purpose documents. It falls under performing operations → bookbinding → indexing scheme relating to books, filing appliances or the like.
[B42P 2201/06] for file cards -- This subclass covers filing appliances designed for use with file cards, such as systems that store or retrieve information on individual index cards. It falls under the broader category of indexing schemes for books and filing devices, particularly those intended for special documents or purposes. The code is B42P 2201/06 in the CPC.
[B42P 2201/08] for stationery, e.g. writing paper, envelopes -- B42P 2201/08 pertains to indexing schemes for books or filing appliances that are designed for stationery, such as writing paper and envelopes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, bookbinding, and indexing schemes relating to books, filing appliances or the like, and addresses special documents or special purposes.
[B42P 2201/10] for large documents, e.g. drawings, blue prints -- The B42P 2201/10 subclass concerns indexing or filing appliances specifically adapted for handling unusually large documents such as drawings, blueprints or other oversized papers, providing means to organize, store or retrieve them within a book-binding or filing system. It falls under the broader category of indexing schemes for books or filing appliances used for special documents.
[B42P 2201/12] for mailing, transporting, dispatching documents -- B42P 2201/12 refers to book-like or filing appliances designed specifically for mailing, transporting or dispatching documents. It falls under the performing operations and bookbinding hierarchy, covering indexing schemes for special-purpose document handling.
[B42P 2221/00] Books or filing appliances with additional arrangements -- B42P 2221/00 refers to books or filing appliances that incorporate additional arrangements such as built-in indexing, labeling, shelving, or other supplementary features beyond the basic structure. It is a subclass under performing operations, bookbinding and indexing schemes relating to books, filing appliances or similar devices.
[B42P 2221/02] with indicating means -- The subclass B42P 2221/02 covers book-binding or filing-apparatus arrangements that incorporate indicating means such as tabs, markers, color codes or other visual cues designed to help locate or identify specific sections, pages or files within the bound item. It falls under indexing schemes for books or filing appliances that provide additional organizational features.
[B42P 2221/04] with indexing means -- This subclass covers books or filing appliances that incorporate indexing means such as tabs, markers or other features facilitating quick location of information within the material. It falls under CPC group B42P 2221/00, the bookbinding indexing scheme category for books or filing devices with additional arrangements.
[B42P 2221/06] with information carrying means, e.g. advertisement -- B42P 2221/06 designates books or filing appliances that incorporate an information-carrying element, such as printed advertisements or other promotional material, as part of the product's additional arrangement. The classification is used for inventions where the item combines its primary function with a means of conveying information.
[B42P 2221/08] with odors or fragrance -- The B42P 2221/08 subclass covers books, filing appliances, or similar items that incorporate an odor or fragrance as part of their design, such as scented pages or scented storage containers.
[B42P 2221/10] with tools for inserting sheets, e.g. labels into pockets -- B42P 2221/10 is a CPC subclass for bookbinding that concerns indexing schemes applied to books or filing appliances with added arrangements. It specifically covers devices equipped with tools for inserting sheets, such as labels, into pockets.
[B42P 2241/00] Parts, details or accessories for books or filing appliances -- This CPC subclass B42P 2241/00 covers inventions relating to components, fittings or accessories for books, filing appliances or similar items, such as hinges, locks, handles, protective covers and other supplemental elements that improve handling, storage or functionality. It is positioned under Performing Operations → Bookbinding → Indexing scheme relating to books, filing appliances or the like.
[B42P 2241/02] Fasteners -- The CPC subclass B42P 2241/02 covers fasteners used as parts, details or accessories for books, filing appliances or similar items. It falls under the indexing scheme for books and filing devices within the broader performing operations-bookbinding category.
[B42P 2241/04] Band- or strip-like fasteners, e.g. tie strings -- The CPC class B42P 2241/04 covers band or strip-like fasteners such as tie strings used as parts, details or accessories for books, filing appliances or similar devices. These elements secure or bind items without being integral to the main structure.
[B42P 2241/06] Handles -- B42P 2241/06 - Handles refers to the parts and accessories that serve as handles for books, filing appliances or similar items, enabling manual gripping, transport or positioning. It falls within the CPC subclass for bookbinding accessories and indexing schemes related to books and filing devices.
[B42P 2241/08] Perforations or slots that can cooperate with filing means as claws, rings or the like -- The entry covers perforations or slots formed in books, filing devices or related accessories that are designed to engage filing mechanisms such as claws, rings or similar means, enabling the items to be securely attached, organized or retrieved within a filing system.
[B42P 2241/10] Means for suspending -- Means for suspending covers devices that attach to books, filing appliances or their accessories to hold them in a hanging or supported position, such as straps, hooks, clips or brackets. These components enable items like index cards, folders or small volumes to be suspended for easier access or organization.
[B42P 2241/12] Means for enabling the device to be positioned upright -- A device accessory that allows a book-binding, filing or storage unit to be set upright, such as a stand, bracket, hinge or support mechanism that stabilises the item in a vertical orientation for display, use or storage.
[B42P 2241/14] Means for connecting adjacent devices -- Devices or components that join two neighboring book or filing apparatuses, such as hinges, clips, fasteners or coupling elements, enabling them to be linked or articulated for binding, shelving or organizational functions.
[B42P 2241/16] Books or filing appliances combined with other articles -- The CPC subclass B42P 2241/16 pertains to books or filing appliances that are joined with other articles, such as integrated storage, decorative or functional components. It falls within the indexing scheme for books, filing appliances and related accessories, covering parts, details or accessories that form a combined product.
[B42P 2241/18] with writing instruments -- B42P 2241/18 covers parts, details or accessories for books or filing appliances that incorporate writing instruments, such as pens or stylus units attached to a book cover or a filing system. The entry is placed in the indexing scheme for books within the broader performing operations and bookbinding categories.
[B42P 2241/20] Protecting -- This CPC subclass (B42P 2241/20) concerns protective parts, details or accessories for books and filing appliances, such as covers, sleeves and similar elements that safeguard the items from damage, wear or environmental effects. It is situated under Performing Operations - Bookbinding - Indexing scheme relating to books, filing appliances or the like. The entry is titled Protecting.
[B42P 2241/22] Sheets or cards with additional means allowing easy feeding through printers -- This CPC subclass relates to sheets or cards equipped with feed-assisting features such as perforations, feed lips or other mechanisms that enable smooth, reliable passage through printers, typically as accessories for books, filing devices or similar document handling systems.
[B42P 2241/24] Means for facilitating stacking or packaging -- The subclass B42P 2241/24 covers devices, components or accessories designed to facilitate the stacking, bundling or packaging of books, filing appliances and similar items, such as trays, brackets, holders or sealing means. It is part of the broader bookbinding operations group and addresses parts that aid in orderly storage or transport of printed or bound material.
[B42P 2241/26] Environmental aspects -- B42P 2241/26 concerns the environmental aspects of parts, details or accessories for books, filing appliances or similar items, such as their material composition, recyclability, or impact on waste management. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, bookbinding, and indexing schemes relating to books and filing appliances. This subclass addresses sustainability issues in the design and manufacture of these book-related components.
[B42P 2241/28] Materials, e.g. biodegradable -- B42P 2241/28 is a CPC subclass under PERFORMING OPERATIONS / BOOKBINDING that covers parts, details or accessories for books or filing appliances where the material itself is specified, such as biodegradable or otherwise environmentally friendly substances used in the construction of these components.
[B42P 2241/30] Easily demountable for recycling -- The code B42P 2241/30 covers parts, details or accessories for books or filing appliances that are designed to be easily demountable for recycling, such as removable covers, binding elements or fasteners that facilitate material recovery.
[B42P 2261/00] Manufacturing -- The CPC subclass B42P 2261/00 pertains to manufacturing operations related to bookbinding, indexing schemes for books, and the production of filing appliances or similar devices. It covers the processes, equipment and techniques used to create these items.
[B42P 2261/02] forming gussets -- The CPC subclass B42P 2261/02 relates to manufacturing steps in bookbinding where gussets-reinforcing folds or pleats are formed in covers, signatures or other components to add strength and flexibility. It covers devices and processes that create these gussets during assembly or finishing.
[B42P 2261/04] Producing books by printing sheets in following order, e.g. for ordering via the Internet -- This CPC subclass relates to a method of producing books where printed sheets are assembled in a specific sequence, often used for on-demand printing such as online orders. The process involves printing individual sheets and then arranging them in the correct order before binding, enabling customization and efficient fulfilment.
[B43] WRITING OR DRAWING IMPLEMENTS -- B43 covers implements for applying marks on surfaces, such as pens, pencils, markers, crayons, styluses and related accessories. The class includes the design, structure, operation and manufacturing processes of these writing or drawing tools.
[B43K] IMPLEMENTS FOR WRITING OR DRAWING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Writing implements: pens, pencils, crayons, chalks or like markers for writing or drawing
[B43K 1/00] Nibs -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 1/003] Capillary nibs -- B43K 1/003 covers capillary nibs, a type of writing implement where the ink is delivered through a fine capillary channel to a tip that contacts the writing surface. These nibs are used in fountain pens and similar devices, providing controlled flow of ink for precise lines. The classification falls under performing operations, writing or drawing implements, implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 1/006] Porous nibs  B43K1/12  takes precedence -- B43K 1/006 pertains to porous nibs used in writing or drawing implements, with a specific note that B43K1/12 has precedence, indicating a more specialized classification for certain types of porous nibs.
[B43K 1/01] with ink reservoirs, e.g. funnel-shaped -- The subclass B43K 1/01 covers writing or drawing implements whose nibs incorporate ink reservoirs, typically shaped like a funnel to hold and feed ink to the tip. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and implements for writing or drawing. This classification is used for patents describing such reservoir-equipped nib designs.
[B43K 1/015] Funnel-shaped reservoirs -- B43K 1/015 concerns funnel-shaped reservoirs that are part of nibs for pens, fountain pens or other drawing implements, designed to collect and channel ink toward the tip for controlled flow during writing or drawing. The class covers the shape and function of such ink-holding chambers.
[B43K 1/02] Split nibs -- The B43K 1/02 subclass covers split nibs, which are writing or drawing implements whose tip is divided into two or more separate points. This design enables the production of lines with varying widths or simultaneous parallel strokes, commonly used in fountain pens and technical pens.
[B43K 1/04] with broadened tips -- The subclass B43K 1/04 refers to nibs whose tips are deliberately widened to produce broader marks when writing or drawing. It includes pens, pencils, styluses and similar implements whose tip geometry is broader than a standard point, affecting line width and stroke characteristics.
[B43K 1/06] Tubular writing-points -- B43K 1/06 covers tubular writing-points, the narrow, tube-shaped nibs used in pens and similar implements to channel ink to the paper. These nibs are typically made of metal or plastic and may include feeding channels or slits to control ink flow, distinguishing them from broader or flat-type nibs.
[B43K 1/08] with ball points -- The subclass B43K 1/08 covers writing implements whose nibs are equipped with a ball-point mechanism. It includes pens and similar devices where a rotating ball transfers ink onto a surface, distinguishing them from fountain-type or felt-tip nibs. This category falls under the broader hierarchy of implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 1/082] Balls -- The CPC code B43K 1/082 covers the spherical "ball" element that forms the writing tip of ball-point pens and similar implements, the component that rotates to transfer ink onto a surface. It also includes balls used in other drawing tools where a rolling tip delivers marking material.
[B43K 1/084] Ball beds -- B43K 1/084 covers writing implement nibs that provide a ball bed, i.e., a recess or seat in which a spherical writing element (ball) is held and allowed to rotate. The classification applies to ballpoint pen tips and similar devices where the ball is supported by a precisely formed cavity.
[B43K 1/086] with resilient supporting means for the ball, e.g. springs -- Nibs for pens in which the ball tip is mounted on a resilient support, such as a spring, so that the ball is held with a degree of give, improving control of its movement and ink delivery.
[B43K 1/088] with an intermediate element between the resilient supporting means and the ball -- A pen nib that includes a component placed between the resilient supporting element (such as a spring or flexible backing) and the rolling ball. The intermediate element can modify ball movement, control ink flow or reduce friction, improving writing performance. This configuration is classified under CPC B43K 1/088.
[B43K 1/10] Wire nibs -- The CPC subclass B43K 1/10 covers wire nibs, the fine, often metal or alloy tips that form the writing point of pens, markers or other drawing implements. These nibs are typically fabricated from a single wire or multiple wires fused together to create a durable, flexible point that transfers ink or pigment to a surface. They are a specific type of implement within the broader category of writing or drawing tools.
[B43K 11/00] Filling devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 11/005] for refilling capillary reservoirs -- The B43K 11/005 subclass covers devices, mechanisms or procedures used to refill the capillary-fed ink reservoirs of writing or drawing implements, such as fountain pens, technical pens and similar tools that deliver ink by capillary action. It includes refill cartridges, syringes, pipettes and other means designed specifically for replenishing these small, narrow reservoirs.
[B43K 1/12] Writing-points comprising fibres -- B43K 1/12 refers to writing points that are made from fibres, such as brush-like or fiber-filled nibs used in pens and other drawing tools. These fibre-based tips deposit ink or pigment while providing flexible, controlled strokes.
[B43K 13/00] Devices for removing nibs -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 13/005] for extracting nibs -- The CPC subclass B43K 13/005 covers devices designed to extract nibs from writing or drawing implements, enabling easy removal and replacement of the tip portion of pens, pencils or similar tools. Such apparatuses are used for maintenance, repair or refurbishment of the nib component.
[B43K 13/02] for cleaning nibs, e.g. ink reservoirs therein -- B43K 13/02 covers devices intended to clean the nibs of writing or drawing implements, such as mechanisms that clear ink reservoirs within the nib. These tools are used to maintain and restore the performance of pens and similar instruments.
[B43K 15/00] Assembling, finishing, or repairing pens -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 15/02] Automatic machines -- B43K 15/02 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to automatic machines used for assembling, finishing, or repairing pens. This classification is specific to machinery that automates processes involved in the manufacturing and maintenance of writing implements.
[B43K 17/00] Continuously-adjustable nibs, e.g. for drawing-pens -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 17/005] continuously-adjustable nibs -- Subclass B43K 17/005 covers nibs for writing or drawing implements whose tip width or shape can be varied continuously by the user, such as pens with screws, sliders or elastic mechanisms that allow on-the-fly adjustment of line thickness. It includes designs that provide incremental or infinite settings rather than fixed-size nibs.
[B43K 17/01] Nibs with ink reservoirs -- B43K 17/01 covers nibs that contain an internal ink reservoir to supply ink continuously to the tip of pens or drawing instruments. The class includes designs where the nib is continuously adjustable and the reservoir may be refillable or integrated into the pen body.
[B43K 17/02] Nibs having more than two legs -- This subclass covers continuously-adjustable nibs that have more than two legs, such as those used in drawing pens. It falls under performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 17/04] Holders with arrangements for drawing dotted lines -- The B43K 17/04 class covers pen or drawing-instrument holders equipped with mechanisms that automatically produce dotted or dashed lines. Such arrangements may involve cam or gear systems that intermittently lift or shift the nib during a continuous stroke, enabling consistent spacing without manual interruption.
[B43K 19/00] Non-propelling pencils -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 19/003] Chalks -- Chalks are non-propelling writing or drawing implements made of calcium carbonate or similar material, used for marking on blackboards, sidewalks, or other surfaces. In the CPC they are classified under B43K 19/003, a subcategory of implements for writing or drawing within performing operations.
[B43K 19/006] with single short leads -- This subclass covers non-propelling pencils that employ a single short lead, such as mechanical pencils where a solitary, short lead is placed directly in the barrel without a feeding or propelling mechanism. It falls under writing implements for drawing and related operations.
[B43K 19/02] Pencils with graphite -- The CPC subclass B43K 19/02 pertains to non-propelling pencils that use graphite as the marking core. It falls under implements for writing or drawing, specifically pencils with graphite, and includes various types of graphite pencils used for drawing or writing.
[B43K 19/04] Pencils with metallic writing-core -- This CPC subclass B43K 19/04 covers non-propelling pencils whose writing core is metallic rather than graphite, designed for writing or drawing. It falls under Performing Operations → Writing or Drawing Implements → Implements for Writing or Drawing → Non-propelling pencils.
[B43K 19/06] the writing-core being made from substance wearing off during use -- The B43K 19/06 subclass covers writing implements whose core material is designed to wear away during use, such as pencils whose core consists of a substance that gradually erodes as it writes. This includes non-propelling pencils where the core itself is the consumable writing medium that diminishes with each stroke.
[B43K 19/08] the writing-core being made from wear-resistant substances -- The CPC subclass B43K 19/08 covers non-propelling pencils whose writing core is made from wear-resistant substances such as ceramic, metal or hardened polymer composites, rather than conventional graphite. It pertains to writing-or-drawing implements whose core material is designed to retain shape and durability during use.
[B43K 19/10] equipped with ball-point -- B43K 19/10 covers non-propelling pencils that incorporate a ball-point tip, allowing ink delivery while retaining the form of a traditional pencil. These implements combine the handling characteristics of pencils with ball-point writing technology.
[B43K 19/12] Slate pencils -- B43K 19/12 covers slate pencils, a type of non-propelling pencil designed for marking on slate surfaces such as school blackboards. These implements consist of a thin rod of graphite or similar pigment that leaves a removable mark on the slate, allowing repeated writing and erasing. They are commonly used in educational settings for temporary handwriting or drawing.
[B43K 19/14] Sheathings -- Sheathings are protective coverings or housings applied to non-propelling pencils, such as wooden or mechanical pencils, that encase the writing core. The sheath may be removable or integral, providing lead protection, enhancing grip, and preventing breakage during use.
[B43K 19/145] with paper sheaths -- The code B43K 19/145 designates non-propelling pencils that are supplied with a paper sheath surrounding the graphite core. The sheath protects the writing tip, improves grip and keeps the pencil clean, and is typical of disposable school-type pencils.
[B43K 19/16] Making non-propelling pencils -- B43K 19/16 covers the manufacturing of non-propelling pencils, which are writing implements that lack an internal mechanism to advance the lead. The subclass details processes such as shaping, binding, and finishing the graphite core and wooden or synthetic barrel to produce a standard, manual-feed pencil. This falls under the broader category of implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 19/18] Making pencil writing-cores -- This classification covers processes for producing the graphite or similar cores used in non-propelling pencils, including mixing, shaping, drying and binding of the writing material before it is encased in a wooden or other holder.
[B43K 21/00] Propelling pencils -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 21/003] with lead sections or lead cartridges -- The subclass B43K 21/003 refers to propelling pencils that use lead sections or lead cartridges as the writing element, encompassing mechanical pencils with replaceable or pre-packaged lead cores for continuous use.
[B43K 21/006] Pencil-barrels -- The CPC subclass B43K 21/006 covers the barrel of propelling pencils, i.e., the hollow or solid housing that contains the refillable lead and any internal feed or clutch mechanism of a mechanical pencil. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 21/02] Writing-core feeding mechanisms -- B43K 21/02 covers the mechanical arrangements that advance a writing core, such as lead or graphite, within a propelling pencil. The classification lies under performing operations for writing or drawing implements, specifically implements for writing or drawing that propel pencils, and includes feed mechanisms, lead-advance devices and related control systems.
[B43K 21/027] with sliding tubelike writing-core guide -- The CPC subclass B43K 21/027 covers propelling pencils that incorporate a sliding, tube-like guide to hold and advance the graphite or other writing core. The guide moves within the pencil barrel, allowing smooth extension and retraction of the core while maintaining alignment for consistent marks. This design is a specialized feature of implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 21/033] with automatic feed by pressure during use of pencil -- B43K 21/033 covers pencils that automatically advance lead when the user applies pressure while writing or drawing. The mechanism feeds new lead without manual adjustment, ensuring continuous marking.
[B43K 21/04] with the writing-cores brought into position by gravity -- The CPC entry B43K 21/04 covers implements for propelling pencils in which the writing-cores are positioned by gravity, such as mechanical pencils that allow the lead to fall into place without springs or other active mechanisms. This subclass falls under writing or drawing implements that use gravity to move the core into the writing position.
[B43K 21/045] with writing-cores automatically replaced from magazines -- A propelling pencil in which the lead cartridge is supplied by an internal magazine that automatically feeds a fresh writing-core when the previous one is exhausted, eliminating the need for manual replacement.
[B43K 21/06] with the writing-cores fed by means sliding in longitudinally-slotted casings -- B43K 21/06 classifies pencils whose writing cores are supplied through a longitudinally-slotted casing that allows the core to slide into position. It applies to mechanical or refillable pencils where the feed mechanism uses a longitudinal slot to advance the core toward the tip. This is a subset of implements for writing or drawing focused on core-feeding designs.
[B43K 21/08] with the writing-cores fed by screws -- B43K 21/08 covers propelling pencils where the writing core is fed by a screw mechanism. The pencil uses a rotatable threaded barrel or screw to advance the lead, allowing precise control of feed without push-buttons or springs.
[B43K 21/085] with a threaded propelling shank -- The subclass concerns propelling pencils in which the lead is advanced by rotating a threaded shank that moves longitudinally within the barrel. Turning the shank engages the threads, driving the lead forward for continuous writing or drawing. This design is used in mechanical pencils where a screw-type mechanism provides controlled feed of the writing element.
[B43K 21/10] with separate writing-core remnants ejecting-bar -- This CPC subclass covers propelling mechanical pencils that feature a separate ejector bar designed to expel spent graphite or other writing-core remnants from the barrel. The mechanism allows the user to clear the pencil without disassembly, improving convenience and preventing clogging.
[B43K 21/12] with means preventing overwinding -- A propelling mechanical pencil that advances the lead by a thrust mechanism and incorporates a feature that blocks further winding once the correct tension is reached, thereby preventing overwinding. This subclass falls under implements for writing or drawing, specifically pencils with means to avoid excessive winding.
[B43K 21/14] with writing-cores automatically replaced from magazines -- This subclass covers propelling pencils designed so that exhausted writing cores are automatically replenished from a magazine of spare cores, allowing continuous use without manual core insertion.
[B43K 21/16] with stepwise feed of writing-cores -- The B43K 21/16 subclass covers propelling pencils that advance the writing core in discrete steps, typically using a mechanism that feeds the graphite incrementally as the pencil is used. This includes designs where each stroke causes a controlled, stepwise movement of the core to maintain a consistent tip length.
[B43K 21/18] having ratchet means -- The subclass B43K 21/18 covers propelling pencils that incorporate a ratchet mechanism, allowing the lead to be advanced in a controlled, stepwise fashion while preventing backward movement. It falls under implements for writing or drawing, specifically those that are propelled by internal mechanisms.
[B43K 21/20] with writing-cores automatically replaced from magazines -- A propelling pencil that automatically supplies new writing cores from a built-in magazine, eliminating manual insertion of leads. The device stores multiple cores and feeds them into the tip as each one is used up, providing continuous writing without user intervention. This mechanism falls under the CPC class for implements that replace their writing cores automatically from a reservoir.
[B43K 21/22] Writing-cores gripping means, e.g. chucks -- The subclass B43K 21/22 covers mechanisms that grip the writing core of a propelling pencil, such as chucks or other retaining devices that hold replaceable lead or cartridge while permitting its advancement or retraction during use.
[B43K 21/24] Assembling, finishing, or repairing propelling pencils -- B43K 21/24 relates to the manufacturing processes for propelling (mechanical) pencils, covering operations such as assembling the barrel and mechanism, applying final finishes, and repairing or refurbishing the pencil after use. It includes methods and apparatus for joining components, surface treatment, and maintenance of the internal lead-advancing system.
[B43K 21/26] Automatic machines -- B43K 21/26 covers automatic machines that propel pencils, such as mechanisms that feed, advance, or dispense pencils without manual handling, typically used in manufacturing, vending, or drawing devices. This subclass falls under implements for writing or drawing, specifically propelling pencils.
[B43K 23/00] Holders or connectors for writing implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:
