[B43K 23/001] Supporting means  bracelets with means for attaching a writing instrument or a memo pad  A44C5/0046 -- B43K 23/001 covers supporting structures such as bracelets that incorporate a mechanism for attaching a writing instrument or a memo pad. These devices function as holders or connectors for writing implements, enabling the user to carry and access the tool while wearing the bracelet.
[B43K 23/002] with a fixed base -- B43K 23/002 covers holders or connectors for writing implements that incorporate a fixed base, meaning the support does not move relative to the instrument. Examples include pen clips, drafting-instrument brackets and other stationary mounts that secure pencils, pens or similar tools to a surface or holder.
[B43K 23/004] Holders specially adapted for assisting handicapped or disabled persons to write -- B43K 23/004 designates holders or connectors for writing implements that are specially adapted to help handicapped or disabled users write. These devices include specially shaped pen clips, brackets or supports that enable easier grip and control for persons with reduced motor abilities. The code falls under the CPC section for implements for writing or drawing, specifically holders or connectors.
[B43K 23/008] Holders comprising finger grips -- The subclass B43K 23/008 refers to holders or connectors for pens, pencils or similar writing and drawing implements that incorporate finger grips to enhance user control and comfort. These devices are classified under Performing Operations → Writing or Drawing Implements → Implements for Writing or Drawing → Holders or Connectors for Writing Implements.
[B43K 23/012] Holders for attachment to finger tips -- This subclass concerns devices that secure writing or drawing implements directly to a finger, such as grips, clips or mounts that attach pens, pencils or markers to the fingertip for enhanced control and precision during use. It falls under holders or connectors for writing implements within the broader group of implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 23/016] Holders for crayons or chalks -- B43K 23/016 defines devices that secure crayons or chalks for use in writing or drawing, such as cases, sleeves, clamps or other holders that keep the implements in place during operation or storage. It is a subcategory of holders or connectors for writing implements.
[B43K 23/02] with means for preventing rolling -- This classification covers holders or connectors for writing implements that include features to stop them from rolling, such as clamps or brackets designed to keep pens, pencils, or other drawing tools stationary during use.
[B43K 23/04] enabling the writing implement to be set upright -- B43K 23/04 covers devices that allow a pen, pencil or similar writing implement to be positioned vertically, typically by means of a holder or connector that supports it in an upright orientation. Such mechanisms facilitate storage, display, or use of the implement in a standing position.
[B43K 23/06] Means for connecting two or more writing implements -- This subclass comprises devices that join two or more writing tools so they can be used together, including multi-pen holders, clip-on connectors, interchangeable shafts and similar mechanisms. It covers mechanical, magnetic or clamp-type arrangements that secure pencils, pens, brushes or other drawing implements in a single assembly for simultaneous operation.
[B43K 23/08] Protecting means, e.g. caps -- This subclass covers protective devices for writing or drawing implements, such as caps that cover, seal or safeguard the tip of pens, pencils or similar tools. The caps may be detachable, hinged or integral to a holder.
[B43K 23/10] for pencils -- B43K 23/10 covers devices that hold or connect pencils, such as grips, clips or shafts that secure a pencil to a holder or other tool. These components are designed to position the pencil for writing or drawing and belong to the class of implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 23/12] for pens -- The B43K 23/12 class covers holders, clips, brackets or other connectors designed specifically for pens, facilitating their attachment to surfaces, pockets, or other devices for writing or drawing. It includes pen clips, pocket retainers and similar accessories that secure pens during use or storage.
[B43K 23/122] with means for preventing choking -- B43K 23/122 pertains to holders or connectors for pens, pencils or other writing implements that include safety features designed to prevent choking, for example devices that enclose the tip or lock the implement when not in use.
[B43K 23/124] comprising an air passage -- This subclass covers holders or connectors for pens, pencils or similar writing tools that incorporate an internal air passage. The passage provides ventilation, pressure equalisation or cooling for the implement while it is mounted. It is classified under CPC B43K 23/124.
[B43K 23/126] with clips -- B43K 23/126 covers holders or connectors for writing implements that incorporate clips for attaching the implement to the holder. The classification applies to designs where a clip mechanism secures pens, pencils, brushes or similar tools within a holder, allowing the implement to be clipped on or off quickly. It falls under the broader group of implements for writing or drawing that function as holders or connectors.
[B43K 23/128] with spring means -- B43K 23/128 classifies holders or connectors for writing implements that incorporate spring means to maintain or release the implement, such as spring-loaded caps, retractable pen clips, or spring-biased holders. The spring element provides biasing force for secure attachment or easy removal.
[B43K 24/00] Mechanisms for selecting, projecting, retracting or locking writing units -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 24/02] for locking a single writing unit in only fully projected or retracted positions -- B43K 24/02 pertains to mechanisms that lock a single writing unit so it remains only in its fully projected or fully retracted position, preventing intermediate states. It falls under the broader category of devices for selecting, projecting, retracting or locking writing implements.
[B43K 24/023] with a deformable barrel -- B43K 24/023 covers writing or drawing implements whose barrel is deformable to select, project, retract or lock the writing unit, such as pens with flexible or collapsible barrel sections that change shape to engage the tip.
[B43K 24/026] with a sleeve sliding on the forward end of the lead -- The CPC class B43K 24/026 covers devices for writing instruments where a sleeve slides over the forward end of the lead to control its extension or retraction. It relates to mechanisms that select, project, retract or lock the writing tip, such as pencils or mechanical pens with a movable protective sleeve.
[B43K 24/03] operated by flicking or tilting -- This subclass covers devices in writing or drawing tools where the writing element is released, extended or locked by a flicking or tilting action. It includes mechanisms that let the user select or project the tip through a quick motion, commonly seen in retractable pens or mechanical pencils.
[B43K 24/04] operated by means sliding in longitudinally-slotted casings -- B43K 24/04 covers mechanisms that select, project, retract or lock writing units by sliding them within longitudinally-slotted casings, as used in pens, pencils and other drawing implements.
[B43K 24/06] operated by turning means -- B43K 24/06 covers implements for writing or drawing in which the selection, projection, retraction or locking of a writing unit is performed by a turning mechanism. The turning means may be a knob, dial, lever or rotary cam that moves the writing element into or out of the grip or locks it in place. This subgroup falls under mechanisms for selecting, projecting, retracting or locking writing units.
[B43K 24/08] operated by push-buttons -- Subclass B43K 24/08 covers implements for writing or drawing that use push-buttons to select, project, retract or lock the writing unit, such as click-action ballpoint pens, button-operated mechanical pencils and other devices in which a button controls the movement or fixation of the tip.
[B43K 24/082] placed on the side -- The subclass B43K 24/082 covers writing or drawing implements that have a side-mounted mechanism for selecting, projecting, retracting or locking the writing unit when it is not in use. It includes pens, pencils and similar tools whose tip is stored laterally to protect it and keep the implement compact.
[B43K 24/084] with saw-like or analogous cams -- The B43K 24/084 subclass covers writing or drawing implements that use saw-like or analogous cam profiles to control the selection, projection, retraction or locking of the writing unit. These cam mechanisms actuate the movement of the tip or nib to enable discrete positioning or secure retention during use.
[B43K 24/086] with heart-shaped cams, balls -- A mechanism that uses heart-shaped cams together with ball components to control the selection, projection, retraction or locking of a writing or drawing unit, such as a pen tip or pencil lead. This design provides a specific cam profile and ball detent arrangement for precise actuation of the writing implement.
[B43K 24/088] with spreading spring means -- B43K 24/088 relates to writing or drawing implements that use a spreading spring to control the selection, projection, retraction or locking of the writing unit. The spring expands to spread and lock the tip in place or to release it for use.
[B43K 24/10] for selecting, projecting and locking several writing units -- B43K 24/10 classifies mechanisms that let a user select, project and lock multiple writing or drawing units within a single implement, such as multi-pen retractable pens or devices that position several nibs.
[B43K 24/12] operating by means sliding in longitudinally-slotted casings -- The class concerns writing implements whose operating mechanism moves the tip by sliding parts within a longitudinally-slotted casing. The sliding action is used to select, project, retract or lock the writing unit, as seen in many retractable pens and similar devices.
[B43K 24/14] operated by turning means -- A subclass for writing or drawing implements whose moving, projecting, retracting or locking actions are produced by a rotating component. It includes mechanisms where turning a knob, barrel, cap or similar part engages or releases the writing unit, such as twist-to-extend pencils, rotary caps that lock pens, or other turn-driven selection devices.
[B43K 24/143] with two-way threads -- This CPC subclass covers writing or drawing implements that use a two-way threaded mechanism to select, project, retract or lock the writing unit. The threads allow synchronized movement in opposite directions, enabling secure positioning of the tip.
[B43K 24/146] with cam control -- This subclass covers writing or drawing implements where a cam mechanism is used to select, extend, withdraw or lock the writing tip. It includes pens, pencils or similar devices that employ cam-controlled movement for the writing unit.
[B43K 24/16] operated by push-buttons -- This subclass covers writing implements whose tip is selected, extended, retracted or locked by a push-button mechanism, such as pens, pencils or markers that use a button to engage or release the writing unit. It includes the internal mechanisms that translate the button actuation into movement of the tip for use or storage.
[B43K 24/163] with one push-button for each writing unit -- B43K 24/163 covers devices that provide a separate push-button for each individual writing tip, allowing the user to select, extend or lock a specific pen or stylus without affecting the others. Such mechanisms are used in multi-pen holders, drafting tools, or electronic stylus arrays where individual unit control is required.
[B43K 24/166] selection by gravity with a pendulum or the like -- B43K 24/166 covers devices that use gravity, often with a swinging pendulum, to choose or feed a writing or drawing implement from a set. The mechanism typically lets the desired unit drop or swing into position while others remain held, enabling automatic selection without manual handling.
[B43K 24/18] and for feeding the writing-cores -- B43K 24/18 relates to devices that supply or advance the writing-core-such as an ink cartridge, lead, or similar element-in pens, pencils or other drawing implements, using a feeding or advancing mechanism.
[B43K 24/183] with push-buttons -- The B43K 24/183 subclass covers mechanisms that select, project, retract or lock writing or drawing units by means of push-buttons, forming part of the hierarchy for implements used in writing or drawing.
[B43K 24/186] with a revolving barrel -- This subclass covers pens, pencils or similar writing implements where the writing element is housed in a barrel that rotates to advance, retract or lock the tip. The revolving barrel may also be used to select different colors or types of writing units.
[B43K 25/00] Attaching writing implements to wearing apparel or objects involving constructional changes of the implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 25/02] Clips -- B43K 25/02 covers clips that attach writing or drawing implements to garments or other objects, where the attachment involves a structural modification of the implement. The classification applies to devices that secure pens, pencils, crayons or similar tools to clothing, belts, straps or comparable carriers.
[B43K 25/022] attached to a pen cap -- B43K 25/022 pertains to devices in which the writing implement is incorporated into, or permanently attached to, a pen cap, such as caps that form part of the pen's tip or holder. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, specifically writing or drawing implements that are attached to objects through constructional changes.
[B43K 25/024] detachably secured to the writing tool -- This CPC subclass covers writing or drawing implements that can be fixed to clothing or other objects with a detachable attachment, including any constructional modifications required for the attachment. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 25/026] combined with other objects -- B43K 25/026 covers writing or drawing implements that are integrated with or attached to other objects, such as garments, accessories or tools, where the implement is built into the combined item and may involve structural modifications to accommodate the attachment.
[B43K 25/028] combined with means for propelling, projecting or retracting the writing unit -- It covers writing or drawing implements attached to clothing or other objects that include a mechanism to propel, project or retract the writing unit, such as a built-in spring or motor that extends the tip for use and withdraws it afterwards.
[B43K 27/00] Multiple-point writing implements, e.g. multicolour -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 27/003] only one holder being used either for the pencil or the pen -- B43K 27/003 covers multiple-point writing implements, such as multicolour pens, in which a single holder is designed to accommodate either a pencil or a pen but not both at the same time. The subclass is applied when the holder is interchangeable for one type of writing element.
[B43K 27/006] Pen or pencil barrels -- The code B43K 27/006 classifies the barrel component of pens and pencils, i.e., the outer casing that houses the ink reservoir or lead and may include grips, refill mechanisms, and decorative or ergonomic features. It pertains to writing implements where the barrel forms the main structural and functional body of the device.
[B43K 27/02] Combinations of pens and pencils -- B43K 27/02 covers devices that combine two or more writing implements, such as pens and pencils, in a single unit, allowing simultaneous or interchangeable use of different colors or lead types. These multiple-point writing implements are designed for convenience and versatility in drawing or writing tasks.
[B43K 27/04] Combinations of pencils -- This subclass covers writing implements that combine two or more pencils, such as pencils joined in a single holder or devices providing multiple leads of different colors for multi-color drawing. It includes mechanically united or encapsulated pencil sets used to produce simultaneous or sequential marks of varying hues.
[B43K 27/08] Combinations of pens -- B43K 27/08 covers multiple-point writing implements that combine two or more pens in a single device, allowing simultaneous or selectable use of different ink colours or types. The classification pertains to multicolour or mixed-ink pens designed for writing or drawing.
[B43K 27/12] of ball-point pens -- This subclass deals with ball-point pens that have more than one writing point, such as pens delivering several colours simultaneously or sequentially. It covers designs where separate ink reservoirs feed distinct ball-tips within a single barrel.
[B43K 29/00] Combinations of writing implements with other articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 29/001] with compasses -- B43K 29/001 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to writing implements combined with compasses, detailing items that integrate both writing and drawing tools for precision tasks.
[B43K 29/002] with chance games, e.g. dices, lottery numbers -- B43K 29/002 covers writing or drawing implements that are combined with chance-game elements such as dice or lottery-number generators. These devices integrate a writing function with components used for random selection or gaming.
[B43K 29/003] with optical equipment, e.g. magnifier, viewer -- The CPC subclass B43K 29/003 relates to writing or drawing implements that are combined with optical devices such as magnifiers, viewers or other sight-enhancing equipment. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and combinations of writing implements with other articles. This subclass covers tools where the optical component is integrated to aid the user's view while writing or drawing.
[B43K 29/004] with more than one object -- This subclass covers writing or drawing tools that combine two or more functional objects in a single device, such as pens with multiple nibs, multi-color markers, or combined pen-stylus configurations.
[B43K 29/005] with sound or noise making devices, e.g. radio, alarm -- B43K 29/005 covers writing or drawing implements that are combined with sound-producing devices such as radios, alarms or similar noise-making equipment. [B43K 29/02] with rubbers -- B43K 29/02 pertains to writing or drawing implements that incorporate rubber components, such as rubber grips, caps, or erasers attached to pens, pencils or similar tools. [B43K 29/04] with blotters -- B43K 29/04 covers writing or drawing implements that incorporate a blotting element, such as pens or pencils provided with an attached blotter for drying or ink absorption. The subclass includes devices where the blotter is integral or detachable and is used together with the writing tip.
[B43K 29/05] with applicators for eradicating- or correcting-liquid -- B43K 29/05 covers writing or drawing implements that integrate applicators for eradicating or correcting liquid, such as pens equipped with built-in eraser or correction-fluid dispensers. The classification groups these hybrid tools that combine standard writing functions with liquid-based correction features.
[B43K 29/06] with sharpening devices -- This subclass covers writing or drawing implements that incorporate a sharpening mechanism, allowing the tip of the tool to be reground while attached. It includes pencils, pens, crayons or similar articles provided with built-in or integrally attached sharpeners, as well as combined units where a sharpening device forms part of the writing implement.
[B43K 29/08] with measuring, computing or indicating devices -- This group includes writing or drawing tools that incorporate measuring, computing or indicating devices, such as pens or styluses equipped with digital scales, electronic distance meters or display units that provide real-time information to the user. The devices are integrated with the implement or attached as a combined article, allowing simultaneous marking and data acquisition.
[B43K 29/087] for indicating time, e.g. with calendars or watches -- The CPC subclass B43K 29/087 covers writing implements that are combined with devices for indicating time, such as pens or pencils integrated with calendars, watches, or other time-display mechanisms. It includes any combination where the primary function of the tool is writing or drawing while simultaneously providing a time-reading feature.
[B43K 29/0875] with calendars -- The subclass B43K 29/0875 covers writing implements that incorporate a calendar, such as pens, pencils or crayons with an integrated date-display or calendar mechanism. These devices combine the function of a writing or drawing tool with a built-in means of indicating days, weeks or months, providing convenient date reference while writing.
[B43K 29/093] with calculators -- Devices that combine a writing or drawing implement with a calculator, such as pens, pencils or styluses that incorporate a small electronic calculator, display or input keys, enabling users to perform calculations while writing. The subclass covers any arrangement where the calculator functions are built into the barrel, cap, tip or other part of the implement.
[B43K 29/10] with illuminating devices -- This subclass covers writing or drawing tools that incorporate an illumination device, such as pens, pencils or markers equipped with LEDs, fiber-optic lights or other light sources to light the writing surface. It includes combinations where the lighting function is integral to the implement rather than a separate accessory.
[B43K 29/12] with memorandum appliances -- B43K 29/12 classifies devices that combine writing or drawing implements with memorandum appliances, such as integrated notepads, clipboards, or pockets that hold paper, forms, or other record-keeping items. It covers any arrangement where the writing tool is permanently or detachably joined to an article used for taking or storing brief notes.
[B43K 29/16] with lighters -- This subclass covers articles that combine a writing or drawing implement with a lighter, such as pens or pencils that incorporate a small lighter or lighters that include a built-in stylus.
[B43K 29/18] with hand tools, e.g. erasing knives -- B43K 29/18 relates to combinations of writing or drawing implements with hand tools, for example erasing knives that incorporate a cutting edge to remove marks while the implement is in use. The class covers devices where the erasing or cutting function is integrated into pen-like or brush-like tools.
[B43K 29/185] with cheque protectors -- The CPC subclass B43K 29/185 covers writing implements that are combined with cheque protectors, devices attached to or incorporated in pens or pencils to shield the printed amount on a cheque from alteration or removal. It falls under the broader group for combinations of writing tools with other articles, specifically those designed to enhance security of financial documents.
[B43K 29/20] with other articles having storage compartments -- B43K 29/20 covers writing implements that are combined with other articles incorporating a storage compartment, such as pens or pencils integrated with holders that keep ink cartridges, erasers, or other small items. It relates to hybrid devices where the writing tool and a built-in storage feature are merged into a single product.
[B43K 3/00] Nib holders -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 3/005] Nib pinchers -- Nib pinchers are small hand tools designed to grasp and manipulate pen nibs during assembly, repair or replacement. They typically feature slender, tapered jaws that securely hold the delicate metal tip without damage, facilitating precise alignment in fountain-pen manufacturing and maintenance. This implements for writing or drawing falls under the CPC subclass for nib holders.
[B43K 3/02] with ink guards -- B43K 3/02 refers to nib holders that incorporate an ink guard, a protective element that limits ink flow and prevents excess ink from leaking onto the writing surface. Such devices are used in pens or brushes to improve control and reduce mess.
[B43K 3/04] with retractable nibs -- The subclass B43K 3/04 covers nib holders that incorporate a retractable nib, allowing the writing point to be extended for use and withdrawn into the holder when not needed. This feature is applied to pens or drawing instruments to protect the nib and enable convenient storage.
[B43K 31/00] Writing implement receptacles functioning as, or combined with, writing implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supporting means for writing implements -> B43K23/00 - Desk sets penholders -> B43M99/00
[B43K 31/005] Distributors for leads, cartridges and the like -- This subclass covers devices that hold and feed writing media such as lead sticks, ink cartridges, or similar consumables to a writing instrument. The distributors may be integral to pens, mechanical pencils, or other implements and can be detachable or built-in. They are designed to deliver the consumable to the tip for use.
[B43K 5/00] Pens with ink reservoirs in holders, e.g. fountain-pens -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 5/005] Pen barrels -- The CPC class B43K 5/005 designates the barrel of pens that contain an ink reservoir, such as fountain pens. It covers the cylindrical housing that holds the ink supply, supports the nib and may include features like grip sections, decorative elements, and mechanisms for refilling or sealing the reservoir. This classification groups the structural and material aspects of pen barrels used in ink-based writing implements.
[B43K 5/02] Ink reservoirs -- The B43K 5/02 class defines ink reservoirs that supply ink to writing implements such as fountain pens. It includes both integrated and removable containers designed to hold ink within the holder of the pen and feed it to the nib.
[B43K 5/025] with multiple colours -- B43K 5/025 covers fountain-type pens that contain two or more separate ink supplies, allowing the user to select or switch between different colours while writing. It includes pens with multiple reservoirs, cartridges or chambers each holding a different coloured ink and a mechanism for choosing the colour.
[B43K 5/03] specially adapted for concentrated ink, e.g. solid ink -- Pens with ink reservoirs specially adapted to handle highly concentrated or solid inks, employing mechanisms such as heating elements, melt-release chambers or pressure-driven feeders to dispense the viscous medium onto the writing tip. These designs enable continuous writing with inks that are not liquid in ordinary fountain-pen form.
[B43K 5/04] flexible -- B43K 5/04 classifies pens that contain an ink reservoir within a holder and whose barrel or nib is designed to be flexible, allowing the writing tip to bend or flex during use. The subclass covers flexible fountain-type pens and similar implements.
[B43K 5/06] with movable pistons -- B43K 5/06 covers fountain pens and similar writing implements that have an internal ink-reservoir in a holder and use a movable piston to draw ink into the reservoir. The piston is manually actuated to refill the pen without needing a separate cartridge.
[B43K 5/08] with ink-supplying valves -- B43K 5/08 covers fountain-type pens that incorporate an ink-supplying valve to regulate the flow of ink from the reservoir to the nib. The valve typically activates by pressure or capillary action, improving control and preventing leakage. These devices fall under implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 5/10] with reserve ink chambers -- This subclass covers pens that incorporate a secondary ink reservoir or reserve chamber which supplies ink to the writing tip when the primary supply is exhausted, typically found in fountain-type pens and similar implements with built-in ink storage.
[B43K 5/12] with ink-level inspection means -- This subclass covers fountain-type pens that incorporate a mechanism for checking the amount of ink remaining in the reservoir. The ink-level inspection means may be a window, gauge or other visual indicator built into the pen holder.
[B43K 5/14] Exchangeable ink cartridges -- B43K 5/14 covers pens whose ink supply is provided by cartridges that can be removed and replaced, such as the refillable units used in fountain pens and other writing instruments. The class includes designs for the cartridge, its sealing and loading mechanisms, and methods of coupling the cartridge to the holder.
[B43K 5/145] with valves -- The CPC subclass B43K 5/145 refers to fountain-type pens that include a valve to regulate ink flow from the reservoir to the nib. It covers pens with built-in pressure- or capillary-type valves that control the delivery of ink during writing. This entry is part of the hierarchy for implements for writing or drawing that have ink reservoirs in holders.
[B43K 5/16] with retractable nibs -- This subclass includes fountain-type pens or similar ink-reservoir writing implements whose nib can be withdrawn into the pen holder, typically by a button, twist or slide mechanism, allowing the writing point to be concealed for protection or convenient storage.
[B43K 5/165] with detachable means, e.g. caps, co-operating with the writing element -- This subclass covers fountain pens that incorporate a removable component such as a cap or sleeve that cooperates with the writing tip to protect or close the ink reservoir. The detachable means may seal the nib, prevent drying, or aid ink flow.
[B43K 5/17] with closing means -- B43K 5/17 designates pens that incorporate a closing mechanism to protect the ink reservoir when not in use. This includes caps, twist closures, retractable nibs or other means that seal the reservoir, preventing ink leakage and drying. The class applies to fountain-style pens and similar reservoir-based writing implements.
[B43K 5/18] Arrangements for feeding the ink to the nibs -- B43K 5/18 classifies devices that deliver ink from a reservoir to the nib of a fountain-pen, including internal channels, capillary tubes, feed plates and other mechanisms that control ink flow to ensure consistent writing performance.
[B43K 5/1809] Feed bars -- B43K 5/1809 covers feed bars, the thin conduits that channel ink from the reservoir of a pen-such as a fountain-pen-to the writing point. The classification falls under implements for writing or drawing, specifically pens with ink reservoirs in holders.
[B43K 5/1818] Mechanical feeding means, e.g. valves; Pumps -- The B43K 5/1818 subclass pertains to fountain-pen designs that incorporate mechanical feeding systems such as valves or small pumps to move ink from the reservoir to the writing tip. It covers the internal mechanisms that control ink flow, ensuring consistent delivery during writing. This subclass is part of the broader group of implements for writing or drawing, specifically pens with ink reservoirs in holders.
[B43K 5/1827] Valves -- The subclass B43K 5/1827 covers the valves used in pens with ink reservoirs in holders, such as fountain pens, focusing on mechanisms that regulate ink flow from the reservoir to the writing tip, including feed, pressure-control and suction devices.
[B43K 5/1836] automatically closing -- The CPC subclass B43K 5/1836 covers pens with ink reservoirs in holders, such as fountain-pens, that feature an automatic closing mechanism for the pen tip or cap, ensuring the writing point is sealed when not in use. This includes designs where the closure is driven by spring, gravity, or pressure-activated systems to protect the ink and prevent drying.
[B43K 5/1845] opened by actuation of the writing point  nibs with ball points with resilient supporting means, e.g. acting as a valve  B43K1/086 -- This CPC subclass deals with pens that have ink reservoirs in a holder, such as fountain pens, where the ball-point nib is opened by a mechanical actuation of the writing point. The opening mechanism uses a resilient supporting element that functions like a valve to release ink when pressure is applied.
[B43K 5/1854] mounted on ink-cartridges -- B43K 5/1854 covers pens that contain an ink reservoir within a holder, such as fountain-pens, where the pen is designed to be mounted directly on replaceable ink cartridges. This sub-class falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 5/1863] opened by actuation of the rear-side of the pen -- A fountain-pen type in which the ink-reservoir is accessed by a mechanism activated from the rear side of the pen, causing the pen to open for ink refill or loading. This actuation method distinguishes it from other reservoir-opening designs within pens with ink reservoirs in holders.
[B43K 5/1872] mounted on ink-cartridges -- B43K 5/1872 refers to pens whose ink reservoir is provided as a removable cartridge that is installed in the pen holder, for example fountain pens that use replaceable ink cartridges. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, implements for writing or drawing, pens with ink reservoirs in holders.
[B43K 5/1881] when the writing point points upwards -- The subclass B43K 5/1881 pertains to pens that have an ink reservoir in a holder and are used with the writing point oriented upward, such as fountain-pens held tip-up during operation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 5/189] Pumps -- The subclass B43K 5/189 covers pumps used in pens with ink reservoirs, such as fountain-pen feed mechanisms that convey ink from the reservoir to the nib by capillary action, pressure, or piston action. It includes designs for ink-feed pumps, pressurised or gravity-fed systems, and related components that regulate ink flow during writing.
[B43K 7/00] Ball-point pens -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 7/005] Pen barrels -- The CPC subclass B43K 7/005 covers pen barrels, the cylindrical body of ball-point pens that encloses the ink reservoir and may incorporate mechanisms for refilling or retracting the tip. It pertains to the main structural component of writing implements.
[B43K 7/01] for low viscosity liquid ink -- B43K 7/01 covers ball-point pens that dispense low-viscosity liquid ink. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for implements for writing or drawing, specifically the subgroup for implements for writing or drawing and ball-point pens.
[B43K 7/02] Ink reservoirs -- Ink reservoirs are the internal containers that store ink supply for ball-point pens, providing a sealed chamber that feeds ink to the writing tip while protecting it from drying or leakage. They can be fixed, refillable or replaceable, and are designed to maintain consistent ink flow under pressure. This component falls under the CPC class B43K 7/02 for implements used in writing or drawing.
[B43K 7/03] pressurised, e.g. by gas -- B43K 7/03 covers ball-point pens that use a pressurised reservoir, typically supplied with gas, to force ink toward the tip and maintain consistent flow regardless of orientation. The classification applies to pens where the pressurising means is integral to the writing implement.
[B43K 7/035] the gas acting on a piston -- B43K 7/035 relates to ball-point pen designs that use gas pressure on a piston to advance ink toward the tip. The gas acts on the piston to pressurise the ink chamber, allowing controlled ink flow during writing.
[B43K 7/04] Arrangements for filling the reservoirs, e.g. arrangements at the ball-point ends -- B43K 7/04 relates to the means used to fill the ink reservoirs of ball-point pens, specifically mechanisms located at the ball-point end that supply ink to the writing tip.
[B43K 7/06] Reservoirs with ink-level inspection means -- The subclass covers ball-point pens whose ink reservoir includes a means for visually inspecting the ink level, such as a transparent window, indicator strip or other viewing mechanism that reveals how much ink remains. This feature is part of the pen's internal refill system and allows users to monitor consumption without disassembly.
[B43K 7/08] Preventing leakage -- The B43K 7/08 entry concerns ball-point pens equipped with features that prevent ink leakage, such as seals, valves or other leak-proof mechanisms. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, implements for writing or drawing, specifically ball-point pens.
[B43K 7/10] Arrangements for feeding ink to the ball points -- This subclass covers mechanisms that supply ink from a reservoir to the rotating ball point of a ball-point pen, such as capillary channels, pumps or porous plugs, ensuring continuous ink flow during writing. It includes designs of ink-feed ducts, chambers and their interaction with the ball point.
[B43K 7/105] Feed bars -- Feed bars are components of ball-point pens that convey ink from the reservoir to the writing tip, often formed as a porous or capillary rod that regulates flow. They are integral to the pen's ink-feeding mechanism and classified under implements for writing or drawing.
[B43K 7/12] with retractable ball points -- B43K 7/12 covers ball-point pens whose writing tip can be retracted into the barrel. The subclass includes pens with a push-button, twist, or other mechanism that withdraws the ball point for storage or transport. It falls under implements for writing or drawing in the CPC hierarchy.
[B43K 8/00] Pens with writing-points other than nibs or balls -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43K 8/003] Pen barrels -- The CPC subclass B43K 8/003 pertains to the barrel of pens whose writing points are neither nibs nor ball-point tips. It classifies the hollow or solid body that encloses the ink-reservoir and connects the grip to the non-nib, non-ball writing element. This code covers the structural design and materials of such pen barrels.
[B43K 8/006] using a spraying system, e.g. airbrushes -- B43K 8/006 covers devices that apply colour or other fluids to a surface by means of a spray mechanism, such as air-brushes, where the implement functions as a pen-type writing or drawing tool but uses a spraying system instead of a traditional nib or ball point. This subclass falls within the broader group of pens with non-nib, non-ball writing points.
[B43K 8/02] with writing-points comprising fibres, felt, or similar porous or capillary material -- The B43K 8/02 class covers pens whose writing points are made of fibrous, felt, or similar porous or capillary materials that draw ink by wicking, rather than using a metal nib or ball. It includes brush-type pens, felt-tip markers and other implements that rely on a porous tip to transfer ink to paper.
[B43K 8/022] with writing-points comprising fibres -- B43K 8/022 refers to pens whose writing point is made of bundled fibres rather than a metal nib or ball. Such fibre-based tips create a capillary action that transfers ink onto a surface, typical of felt-tip or brush pens. This subclass is part of the CPC group for writing or drawing implements.
[B43K 8/024] with writing-points comprising felt -- The CPC subclass B43K 8/024 covers pens whose writing tip is made of felt rather than a nib or ball, intended for applying ink, pigment or other marking media to a surface. It is placed under the hierarchy of writing or drawing implements, implements for writing or drawing, pens with writing-points other than nibs or balls.
[B43K 8/026] with writing-points comprising capillary material -- The subclass B43K 8/026 covers pens whose writing point is formed from capillary material instead of a traditional nib or ball, falling under implements for writing or drawing with non-nib/ball points.
[B43K 8/028] Movable closure or gate -- B43K 8/028 covers pens whose writing points are neither nibs nor balls and that incorporate a movable closure or gate to protect or conceal the tip. The subclass includes mechanisms such as caps, sliding covers, rotating shutters or other actuated elements that can be opened or closed to expose or hide the writing point.
[B43K 8/03] Ink reservoirs -- The B43K 8/03 class relates to the ink-reservoir elements of pens that use writing points other than traditional nibs or balls, such as felt-tip or roller-ball pens. It covers internal chambers, cartridges or refillable containers that store and feed ink to the point.
[B43K 8/04] Arrangements for feeding ink to writing-points -- This subclass covers devices that supply ink to the tip of pens where the point is not a nib or ball, such as fountain-pen feed systems, capillary tubes, reservoirs, or mechanisms that move ink from a cartridge or container to the writing point. It includes internal channels, wicks, pumps, or pressure-regulating arrangements designed to maintain continuous flow during writing.
[B43K 8/06] Wick feed from within reservoir to writing-points -- Pens in which ink is drawn through a wick or similar capillary element from an internal reservoir to the writing point, supplying the tip without using a nib or ball, are covered by this subclass. It includes mechanisms where the feed originates inside the ink chamber and transports fluid to the point of contact.
[B43K 8/08] Wick separate from writing-points -- Pens in which the ink-carrying wick or feed is a distinct component separate from the writing point, allowing the ink to travel to the tip without the point itself serving as the reservoir. This includes fountain-pen-type designs where the feed is independent of the nib or ball.
[B43K 8/10] including compartment for soluble solid material -- The subclass B43K 8/10 covers pens that incorporate a built-in compartment for a soluble solid material, such as an ink tablet or refillable pellet, which dissolves to produce the writing fluid. This feature applies to writing or drawing implements whose points are not traditional nibs or balls.
[B43K 8/12] writing-points or writing-point units being separable from reservoir -- B43K 8/12 covers pens whose writing points are not traditional nibs or ball tips and that can be detached from the ink reservoir. It includes replaceable or interchangeable tip units that separate from the cartridge or barrel, enabling the user to swap or replenish the point without discarding the whole pen.
[B43K 8/14] with coreless tubular writing-points -- B43K 8/14 refers to pens that use a coreless tubular writing point, where the tip is formed from a continuous tube without a separate ink core, and is not a nib or ball. This subclass falls under implements for writing or drawing within the broader category of performing operations.
[B43K 8/143] Arrangements for feeding the ink to the writing-points -- The CPC subclass B43K 8/143 covers devices that supply ink to the writing points of pens whose tips are not nibs or balls, such as felt-tip or gel pens. It includes mechanisms that convey ink from a reservoir to the tip, ensuring continuous flow during use.
[B43K 8/146] Ink cartridges, ink reservoirs -- B43K 8/146 covers ink cartridges and ink reservoirs that store liquid ink for pens with writing points other than nibs or balls. It includes replaceable ink supplies, refillable containers and built-in reservoirs that deliver ink to the tip of the implement.
[B43K 8/16] with tubular writing-points comprising a movable cleaning element -- The CPC subclass B43K 8/16 covers pens whose tubular writing-points incorporate a movable cleaning element that can be shifted to clear the tip of dried ink or residue, distinguishing them from nib- or ball-based implements. This feature is intended to maintain consistent ink flow and extend the useful life of the writing point.
[B43K 8/165] Ink cartridges, ink reservoirs -- This group covers ink-filled cartridges and detachable reservoirs that supply ink to writing implements whose tip is neither a nib nor a ball, such as refillable or disposable pens, including the designs of the cartridge, refill and related feeding mechanisms.
[B43K 8/18] Arrangements for feeding the ink to the writing-points -- It covers mechanisms that deliver ink from the reservoir to the tip in non-nib, non-ballpoint pens, such as flow channels, capillary systems, pumps, or wicks that continuously supply ink to the tip during use. The designs may include gravity-fed or pressurised feed and are intended to maintain consistent ink flow to the writing point.
[B43K 8/20] with roller writing-points -- This sub-class covers pens that use a roller-type writing point rather than a nib or a ball to transfer ink to a surface. The roller, often cylindrical or conical, rotates as the pen moves, depositing a continuous line of ink for writing or drawing. It includes devices such as roller-ball pens where the ink-bearing roller replaces traditional ball-point or nib mechanisms.
[B43K 8/22] with electrically or magnetically activated writing-points -- Pens whose writing point is moved or actuated by an electric or magnetic mechanism, such as electronic stylus tips or magnet-responsive nibs, fall under CPC B43K 8/22. This subgroup covers devices where the point's motion is not produced by a traditional nib or ball but by an electromechanical activation.
[B43K 8/24] characterised by the means for retracting writing-points -- The subclass covers pens whose writing tip can be withdrawn into the barrel by a retracting mechanism, including click, twist or push-button devices where the point is not a traditional nib or ball point.
[B43L] ARTICLES FOR WRITING OR DRAWING UPON; ACCESSORIES FOR WRITING OR DRAWING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  boards, e.g. blackboards or whiteboards, or tablets of large scale and small scale, e.g. portable, having essentially a planar surface specifically adapted to be written upon by manual operation using a writing implement, e.g. chalk sticks or colouring pens  accessories, e.g. underlays, for supporting all kind of substrates upon which writing or drawing operations have to be executed  blotters for writing inks or colours in form of single paper sheets, rolled-up paper, non-paper sheets or rolls and blotting pads forming specific underlays  writing or drawing aids including rulers, templates and all kind of drawing instruments including specifically adapted accessories therefore, e.g. means to prevent slippage during writing or drawing operations or facilitating supports for attachment to hands or arms  writing or drawing accessories, e.g.:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wall mounting or suspension arrangements for blackboards or the like, e.g. easels or stands -> A47B97/00 - Implements for writing or drawing in generale.g. pens, pencils -> B43K - Devices used for both teaching and facilitating writing or drawing -> G09B11/00  **Glossary:** - Writing or drawing: always encompasses applying ink or colour or coloured material
[B43L 1/00] Articles for writing or drawing upon -- **Definition:** This place covers:  blackboards or whiteboards in rigid or flexible form,  slates,  special writing surfaces also including translucent surfaces in cooperation with backing members,  writing boards having additional magnetic action,  chemical details of boards or writing surfaces,  accessories, e.g. illuminating devices.  **Glossary:** - Blackboard: reusable board having a dark coloured writing surface, e.g. adapted to be written upon by use of a stick of chalk - Whiteboard: reusable board having a clear coloured, almost white writing surface, e.g. adapted to be written upon by use of a colour pen
[B43L 1/002] chemical details -- B43L 1/002 refers to the chemical composition, formulation and related properties of articles used for writing or drawing, such as inks, paints, pencils, pens or markers, and the substances that impart colour, fluidity or durability to these implements. It covers the specific reagents, solvents, pigments, binders and additives that define the performance of the writing or drawing medium.
[B43L 1/004] with illuminating devices -- Articles for writing or drawing that incorporate a built-in light source, such as pens, pencils or markers equipped with LEDs or fiber-optic illumination, enabling the user to see the writing surface in low-light conditions.
[B43L 1/006] with luminescent substances -- B43L 1/006 covers articles for writing or drawing that incorporate luminescent substances, such as pens, pencils, or markers designed to emit light in low-light conditions. These items are intended to improve visibility of the written or drawn marks.
[B43L 1/008] with magnetic action -- This subclass covers writing or drawing articles that employ magnetic forces to operate or produce marks, such as magnetic pens, magnetic chalk or other magnet-actuated implements.
[B43L 1/02] Slates -- B43L 1/02 covers slates, flat tablets of stone, metal, plastic or other material that serve as reusable writing surfaces, typically used with chalk or similar marking media. These devices belong to the CPC group for articles for writing or drawing upon, under the broader class of writing implements.
[B43L 1/04] Blackboards -- The B43L 1/04 subclass comprises blackboards, the durable surfaces on which writing or drawing is performed in educational, instructional or presentation environments. It includes boards made of slate, chalk-board material, synthetic panels or similar substrates, together with their basic mounting or support components. This falls under the broader category of articles for writing or drawing upon within the performing-operations hierarchy.
[B43L 1/045] with magnetic action -- B43L 1/045 covers articles for writing or drawing that employ magnetic forces to create or display marks, such as magnetic pens, magnetic chalk and magnetic drawing surfaces. These devices use a magnetic field to attract or move pigment, particles or stylus tips, allowing reusable or erasable marking.
[B43L 1/06] rigid -- The CPC subclass B43L 1/06 designates rigid writing or drawing articles, encompassing implements that retain a solid, non-flexible form such as pencils, pens, styluses and similar tools used for marking or sketching on paper or other media.
[B43L 1/08] flexible -- The code B43L 1/08 covers flexible articles used for writing or drawing, such as pads, roll-up sheets or other pliable surfaces on which ink or graphite can be applied. These items are designed to be bendable or rollable, setting them apart from rigid paper or board implements.
[B43L 1/10] Writing surfaces thereof -- B43L 1/10 covers the surfaces on which writing or drawing is performed, such as the plates, boards, pads or other substrates that receive ink, graphite, chalk or similar media. The subclass includes the design, material composition and surface treatments that affect smoothness, texture or absorbency, enabling the creation of marks with pens, pencils, markers or crayons. It is part of the hierarchy for articles used in writing or drawing.
[B43L 11/00] Non-circular-curve-drawing instruments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  instruments for drawing conic sections, e.g. for drawing ellipses by having a compass, having stencils, having cords or similar devices, having gears, having guides,  instruments for drawing spirals,  instruments for drawing involutes.
[B43L 11/005] pendulum-actuated -- The subclass covers drawing instruments that generate non-circular curves by means of a pendulum mechanism, such as pendulum-actuated pens or styluses used for free-form drafting.
[B43L 11/02] for drawing conic sections -- B43L 11/02 is a Cooperative Patent Classification sub-group that covers non-circular curve-drawing instruments specifically designed for drawing conic sections such as circles, ellipses, parabolas and hyperbolas. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations - writing or drawing implements - articles for writing or drawing upon; accessories for writing or drawing.
[B43L 11/04] for drawing ellipses -- B43L 11/04 refers to non-circular-curve-drawing instruments that are specifically adapted for drawing ellipses. The class lies within the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and articles for writing or drawing up on, covering accessories used to produce elliptical shapes.
[B43L 11/041] by means of a compass -- A non-circular-curve-drawing instrument that uses a compass to generate curves, such as a drafting compass or proportionate-arm device, is classified under CPC B43L 11/041. This subgroup falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and articles for writing or drawing up on, covering tools that produce curved lines by pivoting arms rather than rotating a circular tip.
[B43L 11/043] with stencils -- B43L 11/043 refers to non-circular-curve-drawing implements that incorporate stencils, falling under writing or drawing implements for producing specific shapes. These tools combine a drafting instrument with a template to guide the pen or pencil along predefined curves, allowing precise reproduction of complex outlines.
[B43L 11/045] with cords or like flexible elements -- This subclass covers non-circular curve-drawing implements that employ cords, flexible arms or similar pliable elements to guide the drawing tip. It includes devices such as cord-guided curve guides and flexible-arm drafting tools used for drawing or drafting applications.
[B43L 11/05] with gears -- Devices for drawing non-circular curves such as ellipses that include gearing mechanisms to coordinate the motion of arms or pens, enabling precise draft or artistic rendering of complex shapes.
[B43L 11/055] with guides -- B43L 11/055 pertains to non-circular curve-drawing instruments that feature guides, enhancing precision and control in creating curved lines or shapes.
[B43L 11/06] for drawing spirals -- This subclass covers instruments designed to draw spiral shapes, falling under non-circular-curve-drawing tools within the writing or drawing implements group. It is used in the CPC to classify patents for devices that produce spirals such as spiral rulers or curve-drawing pens.
[B43L 11/08] for drawing involutes -- This subclass concerns instruments specially designed to draw involute curves, a type of non-circular curve often used in gear and engineering drawings. The tools may be mechanical or manual devices that produce precise involutes on paper or other drawing media, and they fall within the broader group for non-circular-curve-drawing implements.
[B43L 1/12] having translucent writing surfaces producing visual impressions by co-operation with backing members -- It refers to articles whose writing surface is translucent and creates visible marks only when it works together with a backing member, such as a colored or reflective layer that shows the impression made by pressure or ink. The combination of the transparent surface and backing produces the visual output.
[B43L 1/123] Flexible writing surface or backing -- The CPC subclass B43L 1/123 covers flexible writing surfaces or backing members that support the act of writing or drawing, such as pliable pads, rolls, sheets or protective layers that can be bent, folded or wound. It includes materials designed to receive ink, graphite or other marking media and to serve as temporary or reusable supports for pens, pencils, brushes or styluses.
[B43L 1/126] withinterposed adhesive substances -- B43L 1/126 designates articles for writing or drawing that incorporate an interposed adhesive layer, such as pads or sheets where a sticky medium separates the writing surface from the substrate. This subclass covers tools and consumables that rely on the adhesive to transfer or receive marks, fitting under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and articles for writing or drawing upon.
[B43L 12/00] Means to prevent slippage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  devices forming part of drawing or writing appliances,  separate devices used for cooperation with writing or drawing appliances,  specific materials, e.g. magnetic.
[B43L 12/02] magnetic -- A magnetic means to prevent slippage is an accessory for writing or drawing implements that uses magnetic force to hold the medium-such as paper, board or drafting sheet-in place while the tool is used. This solution is applied where the surface is ferromagnetic or where a magnetic backing can be attached, ensuring the material does not shift during the operation.
[B43L 13/00] Drawing instruments, or writing or drawing appliances or accessories not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  automatic draughting machines and hand held drawing devices for keeping parallelism, including specific components like:  pantographic instruments for copying, enlarging or diminishing,  devices for drawing in perspective including:  mathematical drawing instruments  contour tracing devices  automated and manual lettering devices  curve rulers or templets:  hatching devices, which means devices generating stepwise movement of drawing equipment.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Straightedges -> B43L7/00 - Circular curve-drawing or like equipment -> B43L9/00 - Non-circular-curve-drawing instruments -> B43L11/00 - Means to prevent slippage -> B43L12/00
[B43L 13/001] Mathematical drawing instruments -- The CPC subclass B43L 13/001 covers mathematical drawing instruments, such as compasses, dividers, protractors, set squares and related accessories that are used for geometric construction, technical drafting or scientific illustration. It includes devices designed to reproduce precise angles, arcs or distances and any ancillary parts that enable accurate measurement or layout in drawing and drafting tasks.
[B43L 13/002] Angle intersecting devices -- A B43L 13/002 device is a drafting accessory that enables the user to establish or copy intersecting angles, such as angle finders, protractors or other tools that align two lines at a prescribed angle for precise drawing or drafting work.
[B43L 13/004] Manual lettering devices -- Manual lettering devices are hand-operated tools that help produce uniform characters for writing or drawing, such as stencils, templates, calligraphy pens and portable lettering guides. They enable precise lettering on paper, boards or similar surfaces without the use of power.
[B43L 13/005] Portable combined drawing-aids including boards -- B43L 13/005 classifies portable combined drawing aids, such as boards that integrate multiple drawing tools or accessories into a single, transportable unit, enabling users to draw, measure or draft without needing separate implements.
[B43L 13/007] Contour tracing devices -- B43L 13/007 covers devices that allow an operator to follow the outline of a drawn or printed shape and reproduce it on another medium, such as tracing pens, stylus-guided mechanisms, or automated contour followers used in drafting, engraving, or graphic-art applications.
[B43L 13/008] Circle transformers -- B43L 13/008 covers circle transformers, i.e., devices that convert rotational motion into controlled linear movement to produce circles or arcs in drafting and drawing, including compasses, beam-circle mechanisms and related accessories not covered elsewhere.
[B43L 13/02] Draughting machines or drawing devices for keeping parallelism -- B43L 13/02 covers draughting machines or drawing devices specifically designed to maintain parallel lines or shapes during drafting, such as pantographs, parallel rule devices, and related apparatus that ensure consistent spacing and alignment while drawing or drafting. It pertains to equipment not otherwise classified elsewhere in the writing or drawing implements group.
[B43L 13/022] automatic  apparatus for recording measured values  G01D ; control systems for drawing machines  G06K15/22 ,  G06F3/13 -- B43L 13/022 covers automatic devices that record measurements and control drawing machines, such as sensors or electronic systems that monitor pen pressure, position or speed and feed the data to a recording unit or to a computer-controlled drawing process. These apparatuses are classified as accessories for writing or drawing implements that are not otherwise specified, integrating measurement (G01D) and control (G06K15/22, G06F3/13) functions for precise, automated drafting.
[B43L 13/024] Drawing heads therefor -- Drawing heads are components of drawing instruments that secure and guide pens, pencils, brushes or similar implements, enabling precise placement and movement during drafting. They may be detachable fixtures, clamps or sockets that can be exchanged to accommodate different tools or accessories.
[B43L 13/026] for writing characters or symbols  manual lettering devices  B43L13/004 -- B43L 13/026 pertains to manual lettering devices used for writing characters or symbols, falling under the broader category of drawing instruments and writing accessories. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B43L 13/028] Hand held or associated with the writing instrument -- The class covers devices that are held in the hand or attached to a pen, pencil or similar writing tool to aid or modify its use, such as grips, holders, caps, or auxiliary mechanisms that are not covered in other specific categories.
[B43L 13/04] Guides for rulers -- B43L 13/04 relates to guides for rulers that are used with drawing or writing instruments. The guides help align, measure or restrain the ruler during use and are classified as accessories for writing or drawing that are not covered by any other specific subclass.
[B43L 13/041] with toothed racks -- This subclass covers drawing or writing devices that incorporate a toothed rack mechanism, such as instruments where a rack engages a gear to incrementally move a stylus, guide paper, or adjust tension. The rack provides precise, stepped motion not covered by other subclasses.
[B43L 13/043] with squares -- B43L 13/043 relates to drawing or writing implements that include an integrated square, such as pens, compasses or drafting tools equipped with a built-in right-angle guide for measuring or laying out straight lines and right angles. The subclass covers accessories where the square feature is a permanent part of the instrument and is not otherwise classified.
[B43L 13/045] with carriages -- The CPC subclass B43L 13/045 covers drawing instruments or writing/appliance accessories that incorporate a carriage, such as drafting frames or mechanisms that move the tip of the tool along a guide. It falls under the hierarchy for articles for writing or drawing, specifically those not otherwise provided for.
[B43L 13/046] with cables -- This sub-group covers drawing or writing instruments and accessories that are equipped with cables, such as powered pens, styluses or other implements that receive electricity or signals through a cord. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, writing or drawing implements.
[B43L 13/048] with T-squares -- This CPC subclass covers drawing or drafting accessories that incorporate T-squares, such as devices that combine a ruler with a drafting-board support. It applies to items not otherwise classified elsewhere within the drawing-instrument group.
[B43L 13/06] with pivoted guide rods -- B43L 13/06 relates to drawing or writing instruments that include pivoted guide rods, which can swing or be adjusted to control the movement of a pen, stylus or other marking element, thereby enhancing precision and stability during drafting or sketching.
[B43L 13/08] Protractor heads -- The B43L 13/08 subclass addresses protractor heads, the detachable or integral rotating parts of drafting instruments that provide angle measurement for drawing or design work. It covers those accessories that are not otherwise specified, used in conjunction with pens, pencils or other writing implements.
[B43L 13/10] Pantographic instruments for copying, enlarging, or diminishing -- Pantographic instruments are mechanical devices that trace a drawing and reproduce it at a different size, allowing copying, enlargement or reduction of images. Classified under B43L 13/10, they belong to the drawing-instrument category for tools that are not otherwise specified.
[B43L 13/12] Multiple writing devices -- B43L 13/12 covers multiple writing devices, i.e. apparatus that combine two or more writing or drawing implements in a single unit such as pens with interchangeable tips, multi-colour markers, or combined pen-pencil tools. It belongs to the CPC section for drawing instruments and accessories not otherwise provided for.
[B43L 13/14] Devices for drawing in perspective -- B43L 13/14 comprises devices that aid in producing perspective drawings, such as sighting frames, perspective-grid tools, or optical aids that help render three-dimensional views on a two-dimensional surface. These tools are classified under writing or drawing implements and accessories not otherwise provided for.
[B43L 13/141] Two-dimensional devices -- B43L 13/141 pertains to two-dimensional devices employed in drawing or writing, such as plotters, drafting tables, and other mechanisms that produce flat images on a surface.
[B43L 13/142] T-squares  T-squares in general  B43L7/02 -- B43L 13/142 pertains to T-squares, specifically addressing T-squares in general under the broader category of drawing instruments and writing or drawing appliances not otherwise provided for. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B43L 13/143] mounted on parallel-motion drawing appliances  parallel devices in general  B43L13/02 -- The subclass B43L 13/143 covers drawing or writing appliances that are mounted on mechanisms providing parallel motion, such as drafting frames, parallel guides or other devices that move pens, pencils or stylus tips simultaneously along parallel paths. It includes accessories that enable such coordinated movement and is situated under drawing instruments not otherwise provided for, within the broader group for parallel-motion drawing devices.
[B43L 13/144] including protractors -- B43L 13/144 covers drawing or drafting tools that incorporate a protractor for measuring and laying out angles, either as a separate device or integrated with pens, pencils or other drawing implements. It falls within the subclass for drawing instruments or accessories not otherwise specified.
[B43L 13/145] Rulers  rulers in general  B43L7/00 -- B43L 13/145 pertains to rulers, specifically addressing general aspects of rulers that are not covered under more specific categories. This classification is part of the broader category of drawing instruments and writing or drawing accessories.
[B43L 13/146] Squares  squares in general  B43L7/02 -- B43L 13/146 pertains to squares, which are tools used for drawing or verifying right angles. This classification includes various types of squares designed for precision work in writing and drawing applications.
[B43L 13/147] Three-dimensional devices -- B43L 13/147 covers three-dimensional devices used as accessories or implements for writing or drawing, such as holders, stands, or supports that give depth or structure to the drawing instrument or its output, when the device is not covered by a more specific subclass. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and accessories for those implements.
[B43L 13/148] using copying or multiple drawing arrangements -- B43L 13/148 covers the use of copying or multi-drawing setups with drawing, writing or drafting instruments. It includes arrangements that enable one device to reproduce or duplicate marks made by another, such as parallel drawing boards, tracing mechanisms or other systems that create multiple copies of a drawing simultaneously.
[B43L 13/149] including grids -- A subclass for drawing instruments, writing or drawing appliances and related accessories that are not covered elsewhere, specifically those equipped with or incorporating grids. It falls under articles for writing or drawing and their accessories within the broader performing-operations group.
[B43L 13/16] free-hand -- The B43L 13/16 subclass groups drawing instruments, writing or drawing appliances and accessories intended for free-hand use, meaning they are employed directly by the hand without templates, guides or mechanised assistance. It covers pens, pencils, markers, stylus-type tools and similar implements not covered elsewhere.
[B43L 13/18] having optical or projecting equipment -- The subgroup B43L 13/18 covers drawing or writing instruments that include optical or projecting equipment to aid the user, such as drafting tools with built-in lenses, magnifiers, light sources, or small projectors that display or illuminate the work surface. These devices combine the function of a writing or drawing implement with visual-assistance features not covered by other, more specific classifications.
[B43L 13/20] Curve rulers or templets -- The B43L 13/20 subclass covers curve rulers and templates, which are curved measuring or guiding devices used in drafting, sketching or technical drawing. These accessories help create consistent arcs and contours and are classified under writing or drawing implements not otherwise provided for.
[B43L 13/201] Stencils for drawing figures, objects -- B43L 13/201 relates to stencils that serve as templates for reproducing predefined shapes, symbols or figures in drawing or drafting work. These accessories are used with writing or drawing implements to transfer repeated patterns onto a surface.
[B43L 13/203] human, animal, or vegetal figures -- B43L 13/203 concerns drawing or writing aids specifically intended for producing human, animal or vegetal figures. It includes templates, stencils, cut-outs and similar accessories that help reproduce these shapes in sketching, calligraphy or technical drawing where the figures are not provided for by other more specific categories.
[B43L 13/205] geometrical figures -- B43L 13/205 pertains to tools and devices used for creating geometrical figures in the context of writing or drawing, encompassing instruments that facilitate precise geometric constructions.
[B43L 13/206] material objects -- B43L 13/206 covers material objects that serve as the surface or medium for drawing, writing or marking, such as paper, board, fabric, plastic sheets or metal plates, when they are not specifically provided for in other CPC groups. It includes items intended to receive marks from pens, pencils, brushes or similar implements.
[B43L 13/208] letters, numbers, symbols  lettering devices  B43L13/004 -- The CPC subclass B43L 13/208 covers devices that assist in producing letters, numbers or other symbols on a surface, such as stencils, templates, lettering guides or mechanised lettering appliances. These tools are used to ensure consistent shape, size and spacing when marking or drawing characters, and are not otherwise classified under more specific writing or drawing implements.
[B43L 13/22] Adjustable curve rulers -- Adjustable curve rulers are drafting tools that can be set to various radii to produce smooth, continuous curves when drawing or drafting. They consist of a flexible or segmented rule whose curvature can be altered and locked into place, allowing precise replication of curved lines and arcs in technical or artistic work. This accessory falls under the CPC class for drawing instruments not otherwise specified.
[B43L 13/24] Devices for generating stepwise movements of drawing equipment, e.g. for hatching -- B43L 13/24 relates to devices that create stepwise or incremental movements of drawing tools, enabling automated hatching, stippling or other repetitive line patterns. The mechanisms control the motion of pens, brushes or similar implements to produce uniform spacing and angles during drawing.
[B43L 15/00] Supports for attachment to hands or arms for facilitating writing or drawing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  bracelets and/or wrist straps possibly including fastening means,  rigid ring-like or flexible supports.
[B43L 17/00] Writing or drawing accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  blotting paper sheets also including hand held use,  reeled blotting paper also including roller blotters,  use of blotting material other than paper.
[B43L 17/02] for blotting-paper sheets -- B43L 17/02 covers accessories for writing or drawing that consist of sheets of blotting paper, used to absorb excess ink from a pen, brush or printed surface. These blotting-paper sheets help prevent smearing and improve the clarity of ink-based work.
[B43L 17/04] hand held -- Hand-held writing or drawing accessories are portable supports, grips or holders that attach to pens, pencils, brushes or similar implements to improve control, ergonomics or functionality while the user works. They are classified under CPC B43L 17/04 within the broader group of accessories for writing or drawing.
[B43L 17/06] for reeled blotting-paper -- This subclass pertains to accessories for writing or drawing, specifically blotting-paper supplied in a reel format. It covers rolled or reeled blotting-paper used to absorb excess ink or moisture during writing or drawing operations.
[B43L 17/08] Roller blotters -- Roller blotters are accessories for writing or drawing implements that consist of a cylindrical roll covered with an absorbent material, used to remove excess ink from rollers or pens and maintain consistent ink flow during printing, drawing or calligraphy.
[B43L 17/10] using blotting material other than paper -- B43L 17/10 classifies the use of non-paper blotting material in writing or drawing accessories, such as pads or supports that absorb ink or graphite. It falls under performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and articles for writing or drawing up on, accessories for writing or drawing.
[B43L 19/00] Erasers, rubbers, or erasing devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B43L 19/0006] motor-driven -- B43L 19/0006 covers motor-driven erasing devices, i.e., electrically powered erasers or rubber tools that remove pencil, charcoal, or ink marks. The class includes portable and bench-type units where a motor drives a rotating or vibrating eraser head.
[B43L 19/0012] Mechanical motors, e.g. springs, spindles -- B43L 19/0012 classifies mechanical driving mechanisms, such as springs or spindles, employed to power erasers, rubbers or other erasing devices. It is situated within the CPC hierarchy for accessories used in writing or drawing, specifically the subgroup for erasing tools. This code is used to index patents that incorporate motorised or spring-loaded eraser mechanisms.
[B43L 19/0018] with fluids -- This subclass covers erasing or rubbing implements that use a fluid medium to aid removal of marks, such as liquid or gel-based erasers, solvent-impregnated rubbers, or devices that dispense cleaning fluid onto a surface. The fluid may be water, oil or other solvents employed to dissolve or loosen ink, graphite or pigment.
[B43L 19/0025] Eraser or rubber material, e.g. comprising hard particles -- This sub-group covers eraser or rubber compositions used in writing or drawing implements that contain hard particles to improve durability or erasing performance. It includes formulations where fillers such as ceramic, glass, or mineral particles are dispersed in the elastomer matrix. The classification applies to materials for pencils, pens, and similar devices.
[B43L 19/0031] comprising magnetic particles therein -- This subclass covers erasers or other rubber-type erasing devices that contain magnetic particles within the material, giving the product magnetic properties for use on magnetic surfaces or to attract and retain magnetic objects.
[B43L 19/0037] Eraser cleaners -- B43L 19/0037 covers cleaning compositions, tools or methods specifically designed to remove debris and restore the performance of erasers and rubber erasing devices used with writing or drawing implements.
[B43L 19/0043] Erasing by electronic or electrolytic means -- B43L 19/0043 covers erasing devices that employ electronic or electrolytic processes to remove markings, such as electrically powered erasers that generate heat, light, or chemical reactions to clear ink or pencil traces. The class groups these technologically advanced alternatives to traditional rubber erasers.
[B43L 19/005] Finger mounted erasers -- Finger-mounted erasers are small erasing devices that attach to a finger, often as a ring or fingertip accessory, enabling users to erase pencil or ink markings directly with the hand. They provide convenient, precise correction for writing and drawing on paper and similar surfaces.
[B43L 19/0056] Holders for erasers -- The code B43L 19/0056 covers devices that secure or contain erasers, such as clips, cases or built-in holders designed for use with pencils, pens or other writing instruments. These holders may be detachable or integral to the implement and keep the eraser readily available during use.
[B43L 19/0062] Magnetic holders -- Magnetic holders are devices that use magnetic force to securely attach writing or drawing implements, erasers and related accessories to metal surfaces or boards. They enable convenient positioning and retrieval of pens, pencils, or erasing tools during use. Such holders are classified under B43L 19/0062.
[B43L 19/0068] Hand-held holders -- Hand-held holders are small devices designed to grip erasers, rubbers or other erasing tools so they can be carried and used conveniently while writing or drawing. They are typically shaped to fit comfortably in the user's hand or attach to a pen, pencil or similar implement, providing easy access to the eraser without requiring a separate grip.
[B43L 19/0075] of the pencil type -- B43L 19/0075 covers pencil-type erasers, which are elongated rubber or polymer devices shaped like a pencil and intended for removing graphite or other marks. These erasers are classified under articles for writing or drawing and are designed to be held and used like a writing implement.
[B43L 19/0081] of the mechanical pencil type -- B43L 19/0081 relates to erasers that are built into or specifically fashioned for mechanical pencils, serving as the erasing component of the writing implement. These rubber or erasing devices are integrated with the pencil to allow convenient correction while drawing or writing.
[B43L 19/0087] Assembling, repairing or finishing erasers -- B43L 19/0087 pertains to the assembly, repair or finishing processes applied to erasers, rubbers or other erasing devices. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, specifically writing or drawing implements and their accessories, and addresses methods or apparatuses for joining, restoring or surface-treating erasers.
[B43L 19/0093] Eraser shields -- Eraser shields are thin protective sheets, often made of transparent plastic or paper, placed between an eraser and the drawing surface to prevent accidental removal of line work. They are used in drafting, illustration and technical drawing to shield specific areas while erasing.
[B43L 19/02] Erasing knives -- Erasing knives are handheld blade tools used to remove or scrape away pencil, ink, or other markings from paper or similar surfaces.
