They are classified under B43L 19/02, a subclass of writing and drawing accessories that includes erasers, rubbers, and other erasing devices.
[B43L 19/04] Fibrous erasers -- Fibrous erasers are flexible, fabric-like erasing tools made from woven or non-woven fibers such as felt, wool, or synthetic cloth, often impregnated with an erasing agent. They are designed to remove graphite, charcoal or similar marks from paper without tearing the surface, and are commonly used as accessories for pencils, pens and drawing instruments.
[B43L 21/00] Blackboard or slate cleaning devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  hand-held devices,  automatic cleaning devices,  auxiliary means for absorbing chalk dust,  wiper holders.
[B43L 21/02] with means for absorbing the chalk dust -- Blackboard or slate cleaning devices that incorporate a feature for capturing chalk dust, such as absorbent pads, brushes with dust-retaining elements, or suction mechanisms, are classified under B43L 21/02. This subclass covers any cleaning tool where the primary function includes means to trap or absorb the particulate residue generated during writing.
[B43L 21/04] Wiper holders -- B43L 21/04 refers to devices that hold wipers used for cleaning blackboards or slates. These holders are classified under performing operations for writing or drawing implements, specifically accessories for cleaning writing surfaces. They ensure the wiper is positioned for easy replacement or use during maintenance.
[B43L 23/00] Sharpeners for pencils or leads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  motor driven sharpeners,  hand-held sharpeners,  sharpeners with gearing, e.g. with cranked handles,  with continuous conveying means for the pencil,  with radial cutting blades,  with rasping surfaces,  with rotating cutting bodies,  only axial movement against cutting blades for sharpening pencils or leads,  mainly rotational movement against cutting blades for sharpening pencils or leads,  cutting blades for shaping the writing point.
[B43L 23/002] with continuous conveying means for the pencil -- This subclass covers pencil sharpeners that incorporate a mechanism to continuously feed the pencil into the sharpening station, allowing successive sharpening without manual repositioning. It includes devices where the pencil is moved along a track or conveyor as it is sharpened.
[B43L 23/004] with radial cutting blades -- This subclass covers pencil or lead sharpeners that use a circular set of cutting blades arranged radially to shave the writing implement. The blades are positioned around the lead and rotate or are fixed in a ring to cut from multiple directions, often in mechanically powered devices.
[B43L 23/006] with rasping surfaces -- B43L 23/006 designates pencil or lead sharpeners that employ rasping surfaces to shape the tip, providing a abrasive action for fine sharpening. It falls under accessories for writing or drawing implements within the CPC hierarchy.
[B43L 23/008] with rotating cutting bodies -- The CPC code B43L 23/008 covers pencil or lead sharpeners that use rotating cutting bodies to shape the writing implement, falling within the broader group of articles for writing or drawing and their accessories. This subclass specifies the mechanism of rotation rather than manual or fixed-blade sharpening.
[B43L 23/02] with gearing -- B43L 23/02 refers to pencil or lead sharpeners that employ a gear transmission to rotate the sharpening blade or carrier. The gearing provides a controlled, often faster or smoother sharpening action compared to manual or direct-drive designs. This classification falls under accessories for writing or drawing implements within the CPC hierarchy.
[B43L 23/04] with cranked handles -- This subclass covers pencil or lead sharpeners that are operated by a cranked handle, a rotary mechanism that advances the cutting element as the handle is turned. It falls under the broader groups for writing and drawing implements and their accessories, specifically within the performing-operations section.
[B43L 23/06] in which the pencils or leads are sharpened by only axial movement against cutting blades -- The CPC entry B43L 23/06 designates pencil or lead sharpeners that sharpen by moving the implement only axially against fixed cutting blades, a subcategory of accessories for writing or drawing implements.
[B43L 23/08] in which the pencils or leads are sharpened mainly by rotational movement against cutting blades -- Sharpeners for pencils or leads that primarily use a rotary motion to bring the pencil against cutting blades, creating a conical point while the blades rotate around the axis of the pencil. The device may include a housing, guide, and waste collection chamber, and is designed for consistent, controllable sharpening of drawing or writing implements. This classification covers mechanisms where rotation, rather than linear scraping, is the main sharpening action.
[B43L 23/085] one of the blades being used for shaping the writing point -- B43L 23/085 covers sharpeners for pencils or leads in which a blade is used to shape the writing point, describing devices that cut and form a fine tip for writing implements as a subset of articles for writing or drawing accessories.
[B43L 25/00] Ink receptacles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  portable ink receptacles,  ink receptacles incorporated in a table or bench,  ink receptacles having:  accessories, e.g.:
[B43L 25/001] with adjustable depth of dipping -- B43L 25/001, within the CPC group for ink receptacles, concerns devices that allow the depth at which the nib or tip is immersed in ink to be varied by an adjustable mechanism.
[B43L 25/002] Caps or closure means for ink receptacles  closure means for containers in general  B65D -- B43L 25/002 pertains to caps or closure mechanisms designed specifically for ink receptacles, addressing the need to securely close and protect ink containers used in writing implements. This classification also considers general closure means for containers as covered under B65D.
[B43L 25/004] Ink receptacles with solid ink; Non reversable receptacles -- Ink receptacles with solid ink are devices that hold a solidified ink block for use in pens, markers or similar writing implements and are designed for single-use, non-refillable operation.
[B43L 25/005] Ink receptacles incorporated in a table or a bench -- B43L 25/005 refers to ink receptacles that are built into a table or bench used for writing or drawing. The entry includes integrated inkwells, reservoirs or refill stations that are permanently mounted in work surfaces for convenient supply of ink.
[B43L 25/007] Portable ink receptacles; Filling devices for receptacles -- Portable ink receptacles are small containers designed for carrying ink for use with pens, brushes or other drawing implements, typically featuring leak-proof seals and easy-to-open lids. The classification also covers devices that dispense or refill these receptacles, such as syringes, squeeze bottles or cartridge loaders.
[B43L 25/008] with wick feed -- Ink receptacles equipped with a wick that draws ink from a reservoir to supply a writing or drawing implement, providing a continuous feed without the need for manual refilling. This arrangement is used in pens, brushes or other devices where a steady ink supply is required.
[B43L 25/02] with separate dipping-cups -- B43L 25/02 covers ink receptacles that incorporate separate dipping cups for loading ink, typically used with pens, brushes or other drawing implements. The design provides a distinct container that can be filled independently of the main reservoir, facilitating easier refilling and reducing contamination of the primary ink supply.
[B43L 25/04] supplied by pressure arrangements -- Ink receptacles that incorporate pressure mechanisms to feed ink to a writing or drawing implement, such as pressurised cartridges, reservoirs or pens in which ink is delivered under controlled pressure. This subclass groups devices where the ink supply is provided by such pressure arrangements.
[B43L 25/06] supplied by tilting the receptacles -- The subclass B43L 25/06 concerns ink receptacles that deliver ink to a writing or drawing implement by tilting the container, allowing the ink to flow from the reservoir into the pen or brush without pumping. It includes mechanisms where the tilt of the receptacle itself acts as the supply method, often found in fountain-pen or brush systems.
[B43L 25/08] with arrangements for dissolving ink powder -- Ink receptacles equipped with built-in systems that dissolve powdered ink to create a liquid supply, allowing the device to generate ink on demand for pens, markers or similar drawing implements.
[B43L 25/10] with means for holding objects -- This subclass covers ink receptacles that incorporate mechanisms for holding objects such as pens, brushes or other writing implements. It falls under B43L 25, the group for accessories for writing or drawing, specifically ink containers equipped with holders.
[B43L 25/12] with pen-wiping means -- The CPC class B43L 25/12 covers ink receptacles that incorporate a mechanism for wiping the pen, such as a built-in eraser or cleaning surface. It falls under the hierarchy for writing or drawing implements, specifically articles for writing or drawing up on, and accessories for those tools.
[B43L 27/00] Ink stands -- **Definition:** This place covers:  supports for a single ink receptacle or a plurality of ink receptacles,  portable supports,  supports fixedly mounted e.g. on a desk or a table,  supports for ink receptacles having means for securing other objects thereon.
[B43L 27/02] having means for securing objects thereon -- B43L 27/02 covers ink stands equipped with devices that lock or attach items such as pens, brushes, or containers to the stand. The subclass includes any securing means-clips, brackets, straps or similar fixtures-integrated into the stand to hold objects in place while in use.
[B43L 27/04] securable to other objects -- Ink stands are accessories that support and position ink containers for pens, brushes or other writing or drawing tools, often designed to be fastened to desks, tables or similar objects for stability and easy refilling. This falls under CPC B43L 27/04, a subcategory of articles for writing or drawing up on and their accessories.
[B43L 3/00] Writing or drawing underlays, e.g. blotting pads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  rigid or flexible sheet-like substrates having a single layer structure or a multilayer structure  support structures for underlays in portable form or fixed to another substrate or device  accessories like casings, illuminating devices, luminescent substances, rain or moisture protection means.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Blotters in general -> B43L17/00 - Clip boards and covers with clamping backs in general -> B42F9/00
[B43L 3/001] with illuminating devices -- A B43L 3/001 underlay is a writing or drawing pad, such as a blotting or drafting pad, that includes an integrated light source to illuminate the surface being worked on. The lighting may be provided by LEDs, electroluminescent panels or other illumination means, enabling better visibility of the material under the pad.
[B43L 3/002] with luminescent substances -- This subclass covers writing or drawing underlays, such as blotting pads, that incorporate luminescent substances to make marks visible in low-light conditions. The luminescent material may be phosphorescent, fluorescent or electroluminescent and is intended to illuminate the surface during or after use.
[B43L 3/004] without blotting means -- B43L 3/004 refers to writing or drawing underlays, such as blotting pads, that are supplied without any built-in blotting means. The classification applies to devices where the underlay is used separately from a blotting element, meaning the implement itself lacks an absorbent or blotting feature.
[B43L 3/005] provided with a casing -- The subclass B43L 3/005 covers writing or drawing underlays such as blotting pads that are equipped with a protective or functional casing, typically to enclose the absorbent material and facilitate handling, storage, or transport of the underlay.
[B43L 3/007] with rain protection means -- B43L 3/007 covers writing or drawing underlays such as blotting pads that incorporate rain-protective features. The subclass is part of the CPC group dealing with articles for writing or drawing and their accessories, specifically those designed to shield the underlay from moisture.
[B43L 3/008] with supporting means  bracelets with means for attaching a writing instrument or a memo pad  A44C5/0046 ; for attachment to shopping trolleys  B62B3/1428 -- This subclass covers underlays employed when writing or drawing, such as blotting pads, that incorporate supporting means like bracelets or brackets for attaching a pen, pencil or memo pad, and may include provisions for securing the underlay to objects such as shopping trolleys.
[B43L 5/00] Drawing boards -- **Definition:** This place covers:  usually portable boards,  pivotable drawing surface,  drawing copying devices using a transparency,  also including means for clamping sheets of paper thereto e.g. by magnets, suction devices.
[B43L 5/002] with pivoting drawing surface -- This subclass covers drawing boards whose work surface is mounted so it can be swiveled or tilted about a pivot point, allowing the artist or drafter to change angle without removing the board. The feature facilitates ergonomic positioning and ease of use during drafting or sketching.
[B43L 5/005] with magnetic action -- B43L 5/005 covers drawing boards equipped with magnetic features that enable the board surface or attachments to attract and hold metal-based drawing sheets, tools or accessories, facilitating stable positioning during drafting work.
[B43L 5/007] drawing copying devices using a transparency -- The B43L 5/007 subclass includes devices that duplicate drawings by placing a transparent sheet on a drawing board, allowing the original image to be reproduced onto a second surface through tracing or illumination.
[B43L 5/02] having means for clamping sheets of paper thereto -- The subclass B43L 5/02 covers drawing boards that include a mechanism for securing one or more sheets of paper to the board. This clamping feature ensures the paper stays fixed while the user draws or writes, improving precision and preventing slippage during use. It is a specific technical characteristic within the broader group of writing and drawing implements.
[B43L 5/022] with magnets -- The subclass B43L 5/022 covers drawing boards that incorporate magnets, enabling the attachment and positioning of paper, drafting sheets or accessories directly on the board surface for improved stability and convenience during drawing or writing tasks.
[B43L 5/025] with paper rolls -- B43L 5/025 pertains to drawing boards equipped with mechanisms for handling paper rolls, facilitating the use of continuous or large sheets of paper for drafting and writing tasks.
[B43L 5/027] with suction devices -- B43L 5/027 covers drawing boards equipped with suction devices that secure paper or drafting material to the board surface, facilitating precise drawing and preventing movement. These devices typically use vacuum or suction cups to hold the work in place.
[B43L 7/00] Writing or drawing aids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  single rectangular shaped straightedges,  T-shaped and L-shaped straightedges,  plural non-adjustable straightedges, e.g. triangles, forming right and/or other angles,  plural straightedges including squares pivotably or slidably movable relative to each other,  accessories, e.g. rollers, holding means,  combined with other drawing aids,  combined with drawing equipment, e.g. for hatching, dotting.
[B43L 7/002] for drawing boards and tables -- It covers devices designed as boards or tables that support drawing or drafting activities, providing a stable surface and often adjustable features to hold paper, drawing sheets, or drafting instruments. These aids are used in art, technical drawing and design work.
[B43L 7/005] combined with other drawing aids -- B43L 7/005 designates drawing aids that are integrated with one or more additional aids, such as a ruler combined with a protractor, template or guide, to assist in producing accurate lines or shapes. The class applies to accessories that function together as a single unit to improve writing or drawing precision.
[B43L 7/007] with holding means -- B43L 7/007 relates to articles for writing or drawing that include a holding means, such as grips, clips or brackets designed to secure the implement to the hand or to a surface. These devices facilitate control and stability during use and may be integral or attachable to pens, pencils, brushes or similar tools.
[B43L 7/02] T-squares -- T-squares are drafting accessories designed to guide a drawing instrument along a straight edge, enabling accurate horizontal or vertical lines on paper or drafting boards. They consist of a rigid ruler-like bar with a right-angled head that rests against the edge of the work surface, providing a stable reference for pens, pencils or other drawing tools. This item falls under the writing or drawing aids category of the CPC hierarchy.
[B43L 7/023] with magnets -- B43L 7/023 covers writing or drawing aids that incorporate magnetic elements, such as magnetic rulers, clipboards, or board accessories that use magnets to hold or position paper. These devices are designed to assist the user in writing or drawing tasks by providing magnetic attachment or positioning functions.
[B43L 7/027] Plural non-adjustable straightedges fixed at right angles -- The entry covers a set of two or more straightedges that are permanently joined at a right-angle junction and cannot be adjusted. These non-adjustable, right-angled straightedges are used as accessories for guiding pens, pencils or other drawing instruments in technical or artistic drafting.
[B43L 7/0275] Triangles -- The CPC subclass B43L 7/0275 covers triangular accessories used as aids in writing or drawing, such as drafting triangles, geometry sets or other triangular devices that provide straight-edge guides, angle reference or support for precise line work.
[B43L 7/033] Plural non-adjustable straightedges forming non-right angles -- Plural non-adjustable straightedges that are used together to create angles other than right angles, serving as drafting or drawing aids for laying out lines and shapes without the ability to change their set angle.
[B43L 7/04] with rollers -- This subgroup covers writing or drawing aids that incorporate rollers as a functional element, such as roller-type pens, drafting tools or accessories that use a rotating cylinder to apply or transfer ink, graphite or other marking material onto a surface.
[B43L 7/08] with arrangements for attaching additional drawing equipment, e.g. for hatching, dotting -- This subgroup includes writing or drawing implements that are provided with means for fastening supplementary drawing attachments, such as hatching or dotting devices, allowing the user to add extra functional elements to the basic instrument.
[B43L 7/10] Plural straightedges relatively movable -- The CPC subclass B43L 7/10 relates to plural straightedges that are relatively movable, employed as writing or drawing aids for aligning, measuring or guiding lines. It includes devices comprising two or more straightedge members that can slide, pivot or otherwise adjust their spacing relative to one another to provide variable widths or angles during drafting or marking.
[B43L 7/12] Square and pivoted straightedges -- The subclass B43L 7/12 covers drafting tools that are straightedges in a square shape or equipped with a pivot joint, allowing the edge to be locked at a set angle. These devices are used as accessories for writing or drawing to produce straight lines and precise angles.
[B43L 7/125] Comprising at least two straightedges remaining parallel -- The subclass B43L 7/125 covers writing or drawing aids that incorporate at least two straightedges that are kept parallel to each other, providing a fixed reference for producing consistent straight lines or evenly spaced marks during use.
[B43L 7/14] Square and sliding straight edges -- B43L 7/14 covers square and sliding straight edges, which are accessories for writing or drawing that provide precise straight lines and right-angle references. These tools can be fixed or movable along a ruler or guide to assist drafting, marking, or measuring tasks.
[B43L 9/00] Circular curve-drawing or like instruments -- **Definition:** This place covers:  compasses in form of beam compasses, having parallel legs or legs formed by flat springs,  dividers,  proportional compasses or dividers,  changeable leg ends for conversion into compasses, dividers, and callipers,  common features of compasses, dividers, and callipers,  e.g. legs, pivots, angle adjustments, mountings for points or writing appliances,  perforated or marked discs or rulers.
[B43L 9/002] with perforated or marked discs -- Instruments for drawing circular arcs or curves that employ a perforated or marked disc to set or indicate the radius, such as compasses, templates or protractors fitted with adjustable discs that bear holes or scale markings for precise curve generation.
[B43L 9/005] with rollers or carriages -- Class B43L 9/005 covers circular-curve or similar drafting instruments that incorporate rollers or a carriage mechanism to guide the drawing implement. These devices use rolling or sliding components to produce smooth, consistent curves for writing or drawing applications.
[B43L 9/007] with perforated or marked rulers -- The CPC subclass B43L 9/007 covers circular or curve-drawing instruments that incorporate perforated or marked rulers, allowing the user to measure or align distances while drawing arcs. These accessories are used with drafting tools such as compasses, templates or similar implements.
[B43L 9/02] Compasses -- B43L 9/02 classifies compasses, the circular-curve-drawing or similar instruments used for drafting, engineering, or artistic purposes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, specifically writing or drawing implements and their accessories, covering tools that produce precise arcs and circles.
[B43L 9/025] Compasses with parallel legs -- Compasses with parallel legs are drafting instruments whose two arms remain parallel while one leg holds a fixed point and the other a writing implement, enabling precise drawing of circles, arcs or similar curves without the point rotating around the hinge.
[B43L 9/04] Beam compasses -- Beam compasses are precision drafting tools consisting of a rigid beam with adjustable legs or arms that hold a drawing point or scribing tip, allowing the user to inscribe circles, arcs, and other curved geometry on paper or metal. They are employed in engineering, architecture and technical illustration for accurate curve creation.
[B43L 9/045] Tape compasses -- B43L 9/045 refers to tape compasses, portable instruments that combine a flexible, graduated measuring tape with a pivoted drawing point to lay out circles and arcs on paper or other surfaces. They are classified under writing or drawing implements and their accessories for drawing.
[B43L 9/06] with legs formed by flat springs -- B43L 9/06 covers circular-curve or similar drawing tools whose supporting legs are made from flat spring metal. These instruments, such as flexible French curves, use the spring-formed legs to hold the curved edge in position while drawing arcs or free-hand curves on paper. It falls under the class for writing or drawing implements and accessories.
[B43L 9/08] Proportional compasses or dividers -- B43L 9/08 covers proportional compasses and dividers, specialised instruments that copy or scale distances and angles by maintaining a constant ratio between pointed ends, used for drawing precise circular curves and related geometrical layouts.
[B43L 9/10] with arrangements for drawing polygons -- The subclass B43L 9/10 covers circular curve-drawing or similar instruments that incorporate mechanisms for drawing polygons, such as templates, linkages or adjustable guides that enable the user to produce regular polygon shapes as part of a writing or drawing tool.
[B43L 9/12] Dividers -- B43L 9/12 covers dividers, which are tools used to scribe or draw circular arcs and curves. They belong to the class of accessories for writing or drawing, specifically instruments that produce circular curves.
[B43L 9/14] with changeable leg-ends for conversion into compasses, dividers, or callipers -- The CPC subclass B43L 9/14 covers circular-curve-drawing tools that feature interchangeable leg ends, allowing the instrument to be converted into a compass, a pair of dividers or a set of callipers. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and articles for writing or drawing up-on, accessories for writing or drawing.
[B43L 9/16] Features common to compasses, dividers, and callipers -- B43L 9/16 relates to the features that are common to compasses, dividers and calipers, covering shared parts or functions of these circular-curve-drawing instruments. It is classified under performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and articles for writing or drawing upon; accessories for writing or drawing.
[B43L 9/18] Legs with toggle joints -- The CPC subclass B43L 9/18 covers legs or support members of drafting or curve-drawing instruments that incorporate toggle-joint mechanisms for locking or adjusting their position. These toggle-joint legs enable quick length or angle changes while maintaining stability during use.
[B43L 9/20] Pivots -- Pivots are the rotating joints or axes that allow circular-curve drawing instruments to swing around a fixed point, enabling the drawing of arcs, circles or other curved lines. The feature includes the bearing, axle or hinge that connects the movable arm to a stationary base or support in drafting tools such as compasses, protractors and similar accessories. It is classified under B43L 9/20 for accessories used in writing or drawing implements that create circular curves.
[B43L 9/22] Leg-angle adjusting-means separate from pivots -- The B43L 9/22 subclass covers circular-curve drawing tools that incorporate a mechanism for adjusting the angle of the instrument's leg independently of the pivot point. This separate leg-angle adjusting means allows fine-tuning of the curve radius without moving the main pivot, improving precision in drafting or drafting-related applications.
[B43L 9/24] Means for mounting points or writing appliances on legs -- B43L 9/24 covers devices that attach or secure writing or drawing tools to the legs of a support such as an easel, drafting table or portable stand. The means may include brackets, clamps, sockets or other mounting elements that fix pens, pencils, brushes or similar implements to a leg, enabling hands-free operation or convenient tool placement.
[B43L 9/245] for mounting centering elements -- B43L 9/245 covers devices for attaching centering elements to circular-curve drawing or similar instruments. The mounting elements ensure precise positioning of the pivot or guide point during the drawing of arcs and circles, and are classified under articles for writing or drawing accessories.
[B43M] BUREAU ACCESSORIES NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Bureau: office, a room in which business, professional or private duties are carried out for non-manual work
[B43M 1/00] Fixing seals on documents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  bureau devices and methods for applying wax or adhesive seals to usual postal envelopes and the like  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for closing envelopes -> B43M5/00
[B43M 1/02] Sealing-wax holders -- B43M 1/02 covers devices designed to hold sealing wax used for fixing seals on documents, including trays, molds, or holders that keep wax in a usable form for imprinting. These accessories facilitate the application of wax seals by securely storing the material and often providing a means to melt or shape it.
[B43M 11/00] Hand or desk devices of the office or personal type for applying liquid, other than ink, by contact to surfaces, e.g. for applying adhesive -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bureau devices and methods for applying liquid, other than ink, by contact to article surfaces, e.g. adhesive, water, paint.
[B43M 11/02] with rollers -- Hand-held or desk-type applicators that use rollers to transfer liquids other than ink, such as adhesives, onto a surface by direct contact. These devices are classified as bureau accessories for applying liquid by rolling action.
[B43M 11/04] with pads -- Hand- or desk-mounted applicators that spread liquids other than ink, such as adhesives, by direct contact with a surface, using a pad or similar contact element. The devices belong to the bureau accessories group and are intended for manual or supported operation in applying the liquid evenly.
[B43M 11/06] Hand-held devices -- B43M 11/06 covers hand-held or desk-type office and personal devices that apply a liquid other than ink by direct contact with a surface, such as small brushes, rollers or applicators used for adhesives or similar fluids. These tools are classified under bureau accessories for applying liquids.
[B43M 11/08] of the fountain-pen type -- B43M 11/08 covers hand-held or desk-mounted applicators of the fountain-pen type that transfer liquids other than ink, such as adhesives, by direct contact with a surface. These devices are classified as bureau accessories for applying liquids and belong to the broader group of writing or drawing implements.
[B43M 11/085] with ball points -- B43M 11/085 relates to hand- or desk-type office or personal devices that apply a liquid other than ink to a surface by direct contact, specifically designs that use a ball-point applicator. These are classified under bureau accessories for writing or drawing implements.
[B43M 13/00] Dispensers without mechanisms for single stamps modified for the application of stamps to articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bureau devices and methods for applying postage or trading stamps to articles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Franking apparatus -> G07B17/00
[B43M 15/00] Drawing-pins, Thumb-tacks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pins, usually with a head, used to fasten paper or other thin objects to a board, a desk and the like.
[B43M 2017/00] Other bureau accessories -- B43M 2017/00 is a CPC subclass for "other bureau accessories" within the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Writing or Drawing Implements → Bureau Accessories Not Otherwise Provided For. It covers miscellaneous items that supplement or support writing and drawing tools on a desk, such as trays, holders, clamps, or other ancillary components that are not separately classified. These accessories are intended to improve the organization, stability or functionality of bureau implements.
[B43M 3/00] Devices for inserting documents into envelopes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bureau devices and methods for inserting documents into usual postal envelopes and the like, said envelopes having openings, closed sides and flaps.
[B43M 3/02] equipped with documents-folding means -- B43M 3/02 covers bureau accessories designed to insert papers into envelopes while simultaneously folding them. Such devices integrate a document-folding mechanism with the feeding system, enabling automatic preparation of letters or forms for mailing.
[B43M 3/04] automatic -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B43M 3/04 pertains to automatic devices for inserting documents into envelopes, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and bureau accessories not otherwise provided for.
[B43M 3/045] for envelopes with only one flap -- B43M 3/045 covers devices that feed documents into envelopes having a single flap, focusing on mechanisms that insert a sheet or stack for sealing. It belongs to the bureau accessories group for writing or drawing implements within the performing operations class.
[B43M 5/00] Devices for closing envelopes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bureau devices and methods for closing usual postal envelopes and the like, mostly by moistening, by heating or by pressuring.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fixing seals on documents -> B43M1/00
[B43M 5/02] Hand devices for both moistening gummed flaps of envelopes and for closing the envelopes -- B43M 5/02 covers hand-operated tools that simultaneously moisten the adhesive strip on an envelope flap and seal the envelope. These devices combine a dampening mechanism with a closing action, typically used in office or mailing settings for efficient envelope preparation.
[B43M 5/025] portable hand tools -- The B43M 5/025 class covers portable hand tools used in the process of closing envelopes, such as small staplers, sealing devices, and manual clipping instruments. These tools are considered bureau accessories that are not otherwise provided for in the broader writing or drawing implements category.
[B43M 5/04] automatic -- B43M 5/04 refers to automatic devices for closing envelopes, encompassing machines that seal envelopes without manual intervention. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, writing or drawing implements, and bureau accessories not otherwise provided for. These systems typically use heat, pressure, or adhesive application to achieve a sealed envelope.
[B43M 5/042] for envelopes with only one flap -- B43M 5/042 covers devices designed to seal envelopes that have a single flap, such as simple adhesive strips, sealing tapes, or fastening mechanisms specifically intended for one-flap envelopes. The class falls under performing operations, writing or drawing implements, bureau accessories not otherwise provided for.
[B43M 5/045] using heat-sensitive adhesive -- This subclass covers devices that close envelopes by applying a heat-sensitive adhesive, typically using a heating element to activate the bonding material. Such apparatus may include rollers, patches or sealing strips that melt the adhesive to seal the flap.
[B43M 5/047] using pressure-sensitive adhesive -- The B43M 5/047 subclass covers devices for closing envelopes that employ pressure-sensitive adhesive, such as self-adhesive strips, flaps or labels applied by pressure alone without the need for water, heat or other activation methods. These are considered bureau accessories specifically designed for sealing envelopes.
[B43M 7/00] Devices for opening envelopes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bureau devices and methods for opening usual postal envelopes and the like.
[B43M 7/001] with abrading or sawing wheels -- A device in the bureau-accessories group for opening envelopes that uses an abrading or sawing wheel to cut or tear the seal, enabling rapid or precise envelope opening in settings such as offices or mailrooms.
[B43M 7/002] Hand tools -- B43M 7/002 covers hand-held devices designed to open envelopes, such as letter openers and similar manual tools. These tools are considered bureau accessories used in writing or drawing operations.
[B43M 7/004] including non-mechanical means -- B43M 7/004 classifies envelope-opening accessories that rely on non-mechanical means such as chemical agents, heat, suction or manual pressure without moving parts, to breach the flap or seal. It covers tools and devices designed for bureaus that open envelopes by these methods rather than by blades, knives or other mechanical actions.
[B43M 7/005] with at least two cutting discs -- This subclass covers envelope-opening devices that use at least two cutting discs. The dual-disc arrangement slices the envelope edge or flap to provide a fast, clean opening, typically as a bureau accessory.
[B43M 7/007] with cutting blades -- B43M 7/007 covers devices used to open envelopes that incorporate cutting blades. It falls under the bureau accessories section of the Cooperative Patent Classification, specifically within the subgroup for writing or drawing implements that perform operations such as opening envelopes.
[B43M 7/008] with a cylindrical cutter -- The B43M 7/008 subclass defines envelope-opening tools that employ a cylindrical cutting element. These devices are considered bureau accessories used for performing operations such as opening mail, and are grouped under writing or drawing implements.
[B43M 7/02] Devices for both opening envelopes and removing contents -- Devices designed to simultaneously open an envelope and extract its contents, typically incorporating a cutting or tearing element combined with a mechanism that lifts or removes the inner paper or material without manual handling. These tools are used in mail processing, parcel handling and automated sorting systems to streamline envelope opening and content retrieval.
[B43M 9/00] Paper-weights -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Objects for keeping loose papers in place on desks.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sheets temporarily attached together, filing appliances, file cards, indexing -> B42F
[B43M 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bureau devices such as desk sets, pen holders, desk-receptacles, paperclip holders, desktop organizers, desktop stands and the like.

[B43M 99/003] with only one penholder -- The subclass includes accessories for writing implements that provide a single penholder, such as a solitary pen stand or a one-pen holder attached to a desk organizer. It covers designs where only one pen can be accommodated and does not fall under any more specific groups within the bureau accessories subclass.
[B43M 99/004] with magnetic holding means -- B43M 99/004 relates to bureau accessories for writing or drawing implements that employ magnetic means to retain or secure the tool, such as magnetic pen or pencil holders, where the item is not covered by other groups of this subclass.
[B43M 99/005] with suction holding means -- A bureau accessory for pens, pencils or similar implements that incorporates a suction-based holding device to attach or retain the implement on a surface, used when the item does not fall under any other more specific groups of this subclass.
[B43M 99/006] with more than one penholder -- This subclass covers bureau accessories for writing or drawing implements that provide more than one penholder, such as multi-pen holders or carriage devices. It is used to organize several pens or pencils within a desk arrangement.
[B43M 99/007] Stands for pens with tubular or porous writing-points -- B43M 99/007 covers stands intended for pens that feature tubular or porous writing points, used for writing or drawing. It falls under the hierarchy Performing operations → Writing or drawing implements → Bureau accessories not otherwise provided for → Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass.
[B43M 99/008] Desk-receptacles for holding writing appliances -- B43M 99/008 covers desk-mounted containers designed to store and organise writing instruments such as pens, pencils or markers. These receptacles form part of bureau accessories and are used when no other subclass provides a more specific classification.
[B43M 99/009] Paperclip dispensers or holders -- Paperclip dispensers or holders are devices designed to store, organize and release paperclips for quick access, typically comprising a receptacle, spring or gravity-fed mechanism, and a guide that allows a single clip to be withdrawn at a time. These accessories are used in offices and similar settings to facilitate the efficient handling of paperclips during document preparation.
[B44] DECORATIVE ARTS -- B44 of the Cooperative Patent Classification groups inventions related to decorative arts, covering processes, tools and apparatus for creating ornamental designs on objects such as ceramics, glass, metal, textiles, wood and other materials. It also includes methods for applying finishes, surface treatments and embellishments that enhance the aesthetic appearance of functional products.
[B44B] MACHINES, APPARATUS OR TOOLS FOR ARTISTIC WORK, e.g. FOR SCULPTURING, GUILLOCHING, CARVING, BRANDING, INLAYING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Artist's machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving or able to be controlled three-dimensionally for making single sculptures or models,  artist's machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving or able to be controlled substantially two-dimensionally for carving, engraving, or guilloching shallow ornamenting or markings,  machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks, e.g. embossing coins,  machines, apparatus or hand tools for branding,  machines or apparatus for inlaying with ornamental structures, e.g. tarsia or mosaic work,  artist's hand tools for sculpturing, kneading, carving, engraving, guilloching or embossing; accessories therefor  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Marking or engraving machining specially adapted for treating particular metal objects or for obtaining special effects or results on metal object by the action of a high concentration of electric current -> B23H9/06 - Working of wood not provided for in subclasses B27B - B27L by burning or charring, e.g. cutting with hot wire -> B27M1/06 - Printing, duplicating, marking or copying processes; colour printing combined with embossing -> B41M1/24 - Machines for ornamenting, producing designs, embossing of leather surfaces -> C14B1/56 - Mechanical treatment for finishing or producing designs or patterns on leather -> C14B15/12  **Glossary:** - Decorative arts: Crafts or arts providing an effect or product meant to be judged by eye, whereby human emotions or intellect may be influenced or affected.Such crafts or arts may comprise techniques such as sculpturing, kneading, carving, engraving, guilloching, embossing, branding, inlaying. - Artistic work: Work performed by craftsmen or artists to provide an effect or product meant to be judged by eye, whereby human emotions or intellect may be influenced or affected.Artistic work may comprise techniques such as sculpturing, kneading, carving, engraving, guilloching, embossing, branding, inlaying.
[B44B 1/00] Artists' machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving, or able to be controlled, three-dimensionally for making single sculptures or models -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Artist's machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving or able to be controlled three-dimensionally for making single sculptures or models  US3975983
[B44B 1/003] having several cutting tools -- A machine or apparatus for artistic work that incorporates multiple cutting tools, allowing three-dimensional shaping of a single sculpture or model, falls under CPC B44B 1/003. It is classified within decorative arts and performing operations for tools such as carving, guilloching or inlaying that can be moved or controlled to produce the work.
[B44B 1/006] using computer control means -- B44B 1/006 covers machines or apparatus for sculpturing, carving, guilloching, branding or inlaying that incorporate computer-controlled movement of tools or work holders, enabling three-dimensional shaping of single sculptures or models through automated or programmed operations.
[B44B 1/02] wherein three-dimensional [3D] copies are made -- B44B 1/02 relates to machines, apparatus or tools for artistic work that produce three-dimensional copies of existing sculptures or models, using movable tool carriers or work holders that can be positioned in three dimensions. The equipment is intended for reproducing finished forms rather than shaping raw material.
[B44B 1/04] having devices for changing, e.g. proportionally enlarging or reducing, the shape from an original pattern -- B44B 1/04 covers machines or apparatus for artistic work that include mechanisms allowing the shape of a work to be altered, such as devices that proportionally enlarge or reduce a pattern transferred from an original model. These devices are integrated into sculpturing, carving, branding or inlaying tools that move three-dimensionally.
[B44B 1/06] Accessories -- The subclass B44B 1/06 concerns accessories for artists' machines or apparatus used in three-dimensional sculpting, carving, branding or similar decorative art operations. It includes auxiliary components, add-on devices, interchangeable heads, clamps, guides or other supplemental parts that support or enhance the primary tool or work holder of the machine.
[B44B 11/00] Artists' hand tools for sculpturing, kneading, carving, engraving, guilloching or embossing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Artist's hand tools for sculpturing, kneading, carving, engraving, guilloching or embossing essentially creating a surface relief onto a workpiece
[B44B 11/02] for substantially two-dimensional [2D] carving, engraving, or guilloching -- This CPC subgroup covers hand-operated tools specifically designed for creating essentially flat or surface-level patterns by carving, engraving or guilloching. It includes implements used by artists to produce two-dimensional decorative motifs on materials such as metal, wood or plastic.
[B44B 11/04] for embossing -- B44B 11/04 covers hand-operated tools designed specifically for embossing, i.e., producing raised patterns on materials such as metal, leather or paper. The classification falls under machines and apparatus for artistic work within the decorative arts field.
[B44B 2700/00] Machines, apparatus, tools or accessories for artistic work -- The B44B 2700/00 group covers machines, apparatus, tools and accessories specifically designed for artistic work such as sculpturing, carving, branding, inlaying and other decorative operations. It falls under the broader sections for performing operations, decorative arts and equipment used in artistic manufacturing.
[B44B 2700/02] Artist's machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving or able to be controlled substantially two-dimensionally for carving, engraving, or guilloching shallow ornamenting or markings -- B44B 2700/02 refers to artist's machines or apparatus that incorporate tools or work holders capable of moving in a substantially two-dimensional manner to carve, engrave or guilloche shallow decorative patterns or markings. These devices are designed for ornamental work such as inlay, branding or surface decoration. The classification falls under decorative-arts machines for artistic work.
[B44B 2700/021] by copying -- This subclass covers machines, apparatus, tools or accessories designed to duplicate or reproduce artistic works, such as copying sculptures, carvings, branding or inlay pieces, using mechanical or automated processes rather than creating original forms.
[B44B 2700/023] for engraving parallel lines of flat surfaces -- B44B 2700/023 covers machines, apparatus, tools or accessories designed to engrave parallel lines on flat-surface materials such as metal, wood, or similar, creating regular linear patterns for decorative or functional purposes. It falls under decorative arts equipment used in artistic work.
[B44B 2700/025] for engraving a surface of revolution -- B44B 2700/025 covers machines, apparatus, tools or accessories specifically designed to engrave the surface of a revolved workpiece, such as cylinders, cones or spheres, by removing material in a patterned or decorative manner. This subclass falls under decorative arts equipment for artistic operations like carving, branding or inlaying, and pertains to devices that perform continuous or indexed engraving on rotationally symmetric surfaces.
[B44B 2700/026] for guilloching -- B44B 2700/026 covers machines, apparatus, tools or accessories specifically designed for guilloching, a decorative engraving technique that produces intricate, repetitive patterns on surfaces such as metal, leather or paper. These devices automate or assist the manual process, often using rotating cutters or stylus systems to create the characteristic interlaced lines.
[B44B 2700/028] Electrical engraving machines -- B44B 2700/028 classifies electrical engraving machines, devices that use electric power to produce decorative cuts, patterns or markings on materials such as metal, wood or glass. These tools fall under the broader group for machines, apparatus or tools employed in artistic work, including sculpturing, carving, branding and in-laying.
[B44B 2700/04] Machines or tools for carving -- B44B 2700/04 designates machines and tools specifically made for carving in artistic or decorative work, such as shaping wood, stone or other substrates by removing material to form a design. It falls under the decorative-arts branch of performing-operations classifications covering equipment for sculpturing, guilloching, branding and inlaying.
[B44B 2700/045] Electrical engraving machines -- Electrical engraving machines are electrically powered devices that cut, incise or mark surfaces to create decorative designs on materials such as metal, wood or stone. They employ motor-driven spindles, electrodes or lasers to achieve precise artistic patterns and are used in ornamental crafting and finishing.
[B44B 2700/06] Machines, apparatus or tools for stippling -- B44B 2700/06 relates to machines, apparatus or tools that create stipple effects on surfaces as part of artistic work, such as devices that apply fine dot patterns to metal, wood, leather or similar materials. It falls under the decorative-arts group for equipment used in sculpting, carving, branding or inlaying.
[B44B 2700/08] Machines, apparatus or hand tools for branding -- The B44B 2700/08 subclass covers devices and hand tools designed to apply a permanent brand or mark to materials, typically by heating a metal stamp or similar element. It includes branding irons, heated plates and related mechanisms for creating decorative or identification marks on surfaces such as wood, leather or metal.
[B44B 2700/09] Machines or apparatus for making models -- B44B 2700/09 covers machines, apparatus and tools specifically designed for producing scale models or prototypes in artistic or decorative contexts. It includes equipment for shaping, assembling or finishing model components such as casting, machining or surface treatment.
[B44B 2700/12] Accessories -- This subclass covers supplementary items used with machines, apparatus or tools employed in decorative arts such as sculpturing, carving, branding, guilloching and inlaying. Accessories include attachments, replaceable parts and consumables that aid or enhance the primary equipment's function.
[B44B 3/00] Artists' machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving or able to be controlled substantially two-dimensionally for carving, engraving, or guilloching shallow ornamenting or markings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Artist's machines or apparatus equipped with tools or work holders moving or able to be controlled substantially two-dimensionally for carving, engraving or guilloching shallow ornamenting or markings  US2561436  GB679787
[B44B 3/001] by copying -- This subclass deals with machines or tools that create decorative patterns by copying an existing design onto a surface. It covers apparatuses that transfer a template or image to a workpiece using controlled movement for engraving, guilloching or shallow ornamentation.
[B44B 3/002] using a pantograph -- B44B 3/002 covers machines or apparatus for decorative arts that use a pantograph to transmit motion, allowing two-dimensional control of tools for shallow carving, engraving, guilloching or other ornamental markings.
[B44B 3/003] using a pendulum -- The B44B 3/003 subclass covers machines for decorative arts that use a pendulum to drive or guide the cutting tool or work holder, creating a two-dimensional oscillatory motion for shallow carving, engraving or guilloching of ornamental patterns.
[B44B 3/004] using a tool controlled by a photoelectric scanning device -- The CPC subclass B44B 3/004 covers machines or apparatus for decorative arts that use a tool whose movement is directed by a photo-electric scanning system, enabling precise two-dimensional control for carving, engraving, guilloching or other shallow ornamentation. This combines automated scanning with tool positioning to produce patterned markings on a workpiece.
[B44B 3/005] characterised by the power drive -- B44B 3/005 refers to decorative-arts machines whose cutting or engraving tools are powered rather than hand-driven. It includes apparatus with motorised drives that move the tool or work holder in two dimensions to produce shallow carving, guilloching, branding or inlay markings. This subgroup is a sub-category of machines for artistic work within the broader decorative-arts classification.
[B44B 3/006] carving or engraving devices having several cutting tools -- This subclass covers machines or apparatus used in the decorative arts that incorporate several cutting tools for carving or engraving. The devices are designed to move or be controlled essentially in two dimensions, enabling shallow ornamentation such as guilloching, branding or inlay work. They include holders and tool arrangements that allow simultaneous or sequential use of multiple cutters.
[B44B 3/007] in layered material -- A B44B 3/007 machine or apparatus is designed for artistic operations such as carving, engraving or guilloching when the workpiece consists of layered material, enabling the tool or work holder to move in two dimensions to treat, remove or mark individual layers of a composite or laminated substrate.
[B44B 3/008] characterised by multi-step processes -- B44B 3/008 covers artistic machines or apparatus whose tool or work holder moves essentially in two dimensions and whose operation is defined by a series of successive steps, each contributing to the formation of shallow decorative markings, carvings, engravings or guilloches. The classification applies when the multi-step sequence is integral to the device's function.
[B44B 3/009] using a computer control means -- B44B 3/009 covers machines for sculpting, carving, guilloching or similar decorative operations that are controlled by computer means, such as CNC or computer-numeric-controlled systems, enabling two-dimensional movement of tools or work holders to produce shallow ornamentation.
[B44B 3/02] wherein plane surfaces are worked -- Machines or apparatus for artistic work whose tool or work holder moves essentially in a plane to shape or decorate flat surfaces, such as carving, engraving or guilloching shallow ornamental markings. This subclass applies to devices that operate on planar surfaces in decorative-arts applications.
[B44B 3/04] wherein non-plane surfaces are worked -- This subgroup covers artists' machines or apparatus equipped with tools that can be guided in two dimensions to carve, engrave, guilloche or otherwise ornament non-plane surfaces such as curved or irregular shapes. The equipment includes work holders and control mechanisms that allow shaping of shallow markings on surfaces that are not flat.
[B44B 3/06] Accessories, e.g. tool or work holders -- B44B 3/06 relates to accessories such as tool or work holders for machines used in decorative-arts operations like carving, engraving, guilloching or shallow ornamentation. These components are mounted on or interchangeable with apparatus whose cutting tools move primarily in two dimensions.
[B44B 3/061] Tool heads -- B44B 3/061 covers the heads of tools mounted on machines used for decorative carving, engraving, guilloching or shallow ornamentation. It includes the design of cutting or shaping elements such as bits, knives or inserts, and their attachment mechanisms that move in two dimensions under operator control. The classification applies to apparatus for artistic work like sculpturing, branding or inlaying.
[B44B 3/063] Tool holders -- Tool holders are components of machines used in decorative arts that secure and position cutting or engraving tools while the workpiece moves in two dimensions, enabling controlled shallow carving, engraving or guilloching. They may be interchangeable and allow precise alignment for consistent ornamentation.
[B44B 3/065] Work holders -- B44B 3/065 covers work holders, i.e., devices that securely grip or support a piece of material while it is being carved, engraved, guilloched or otherwise shallowly ornamented by machines used in decorative arts. The holders allow the workpiece to be positioned and moved in a substantially two-dimensional manner relative to the tool.
[B44B 3/066] Master copy holders -- In CPC class B44B, which covers machines for decorative arts, the sub-group 3/066 defines master copy holders - devices that secure a master pattern, template or model and guide the tool carriage during two-dimensional carving, engraving or guilloching. These holders enable precise replication of shallow ornamental markings on the workpiece.
[B44B 3/068] Tracer heads  B44B3/004  takes precedence -- B44B 3/068 covers tracer heads, the guiding devices fitted to artistic machines that control a cutting or engraving tool as it moves in two dimensions. These heads translate a traced pattern into shallow carving, engraving or guilloching marks on a workpiece.
[B44B 5/00] Machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks, e.g. embossing coins -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks, e.g. embossing coins, embossing dies and accessories for holding work or loading and discharging arrangements  US20070275169  DE2146865
[B44B 5/0004] characterised by the movement of the embossing tool(s), or the movement of the work, during the embossing operation -- B44B 5/0004 covers machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks in which either the embossing tool or the workpiece is moved relative to the other during the embossing process, such as in coin embossing.
[B44B 5/0009] Rotating embossing tools -- The B44B 5/0009 subclass comprises rotating embossing tools used in decorative arts, such as machines that apply raised designs, markings or patterns to surfaces like metal, coin blanks, and other materials. These tools are employed for embossing, branding, or inlaying ornamental features during sculpting, guilloché, carving or similar artistic processes.
[B44B 5/0014] and rotating workpieces -- This subclass covers machines that emboss or impress decorative marks onto workpieces while the pieces are rotated during the operation, such as devices used for coin embossing or other rotating-workpiece marking processes.
[B44B 5/0019] Rectilinearly moving embossing tools -- This CPC subgroup covers machines or tools that emboss patterns or marks by moving the embossing element along a straight, line-moving path relative to the material being treated. It includes devices where the tool slides or is driven in a rectilinear motion to produce raised designs on objects such as coins, plaques, or metal sheets.
[B44B 5/0023] actuated by a lever -- The subclass B44B 5/0023 includes embossing or marking machines that are powered by a lever, in which the lever supplies the force that drives a die or tool against the material. These lever-actuated devices are used in decorative-arts applications such as sculpturing, branding, carving or inlaying.
[B44B 5/0028] cooperating with rotating workpieces -- The code B44B 5/0028 covers machines or apparatus used for embossing decorations or marks where the workpiece rotates in coordination with another moving element, such as in coin-embossing or other rotating-workpiece processes. It falls under the decorative-arts subclass for tools that shape, carve, brand or inlay artistic objects. This entry concerns the cooperative interaction of rotating parts during the embossing operation.
[B44B 5/0033] Oscillating embossing tools -- The code B44B 5/0033 designates machines or apparatus that employ an oscillating tool to emboss decorative patterns or markings on objects, including coins and other metal items. These devices typically produce raised relief by rapid back-and-forth motion of a die or stylus during the embossing process.
[B44B 5/0038] actuated by a lever -- B44B 5/0038 designates machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks, such as coin embossing, that are operated by a lever. These devices belong to the decorative arts subclass for tools used in sculpturing, carving, branding and related artistic processes. The entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations and decorative arts.
[B44B 5/0042] performing a rolling-off motion with respect to the workpiece -- This subclass covers machines or apparatus used in decorative-arts work that emboss or mark a surface by rolling the tool off the workpiece, such as devices for embossing coins. It falls under performing operations in the decorative arts field, specifically machines for artistic work like sculpturing, carving or branding.
[B44B 5/0047] by rolling  B44B5/0061  takes precedence -- This subclass covers machines or apparatus that emboss decorative patterns or marks onto surfaces by rolling the material between patterned rollers. It falls under decorative arts, specifically tools for sculpting, carving and similar artistic work, and is designated B44B 5/0047, with the broader subclass B44B 5/0061 taking precedence.
[B44B 5/0052] by pressing  B44B5/0061  and  B44B5/0076  take precedence -- B44B 5/0052 covers machines or apparatus that emboss decorative marks, such as coin embossing, by applying pressure. It belongs to the hierarchy of performing operations in the decorative arts, specifically tools for artistic work like sculpturing or carving. Sub-classes B44B 5/0061 and B44B 5/0076 are considered more specific and therefore take precedence over this entry.
[B44B 5/0057] using more than one die assembly simultaneously -- The subclass concerns machines that emboss decorative patterns or marks, for example on coins, by operating more than one die assembly at the same time to imprint the workpiece. It is situated under decorative arts, machines or apparatus for artistic work, specifically multiple-die embossing.
[B44B 5/0061] characterised by the power drive -- B44B 5/0061 pertains to machines or apparatus for embossing decorations, marks or designs-such as on coins-where the embossing action is provided by a powered drive system rather than manual force. The classification covers devices that use electric, pneumatic or hydraulic power to generate the required pressure for imprinting patterns.
[B44B 5/0066] producing a vibratory motion -- B44B 5/0066 covers machines or apparatus that produce a vibratory motion to emboss decorations or marks, such as coin striking or other decorative imprinting. The classification falls under decorative arts, specifically tools for artistic work that create patterns or reliefs by vibrating the workpiece or tooling during the embossing process.
[B44B 5/0071] which simultaneously apply a decorative material -- B44B 5/0071 pertains to machines or apparatus that emboss a surface while at the same time applying a decorative material such as paint, enamel or powder, enabling the creation of marked or coloured designs in a single operation.
[B44B 5/0076] having a series of embossing tools each of which can be brought into working position -- The B44B 5/0076 subclass covers machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks that incorporate a series of embossing tools, each of which can be individually positioned into a working state. Such devices enable sequential or selective embossing operations, for example in coin or medal production.
[B44B 5/008] in layered material; connecting a plurality of layers by embossing -- B44B 5/008 concerns machines that emboss layered materials in order to join multiple layers together, such as presses that create a permanent bond while forming decorative or functional relief patterns. The technology is used for applications like multilayered coins, cards, or composite panels where the embossing process simultaneously produces an imprint and unites the layers.
[B44B 5/0085] Portable apparatus for manual operation -- B44B 5/0085 refers to portable apparatus for manual operation used to emboss decorations or marks on surfaces such as coins, metal sheets, paper or similar materials. The devices are hand-driven, compact and intended for onsite or field use rather than fixed industrial installations.
[B44B 5/009] by multi-step processes -- B44B 5/009 covers machines or apparatus that emboss decorative patterns or marks, such as on coins, by employing a sequence of two or more distinct processing steps rather than a single stamping operation. The classification applies to equipment that combines successive actions like forming, pressing, or heating to achieve the final embossed design.
[B44B 5/0095] using computer control means -- B44B 5/0095 covers machines or apparatus for embossing decorations, marks or designs-such as coin embossing-where the operating functions are directed by computer-controlled means, enabling automated or programmable embossing processes.
[B44B 5/02] Dies -- Dies are tools that shape or imprint decorative patterns or markings onto material, commonly used for embossing coins and similar items. They belong to the class of machines or apparatus for artistic work that perform decorative embossing operations.
[B44B 5/022] Devices for holding or supporting work -- The subgroup includes machines or apparatus used in decorative-arts processes that secure the workpiece, such as jigs, clamps, or holders for embossing, carving, stamping or similar operations, providing stability and precise positioning of the material during shaping or marking.
[B44B 5/024] Work piece loading or discharging arrangements -- B44B 5/024 covers arrangements for loading or unloading work pieces in machines that emboss decorations or marks, such as coin embossing equipment. It pertains to the handling mechanisms that feed material into or remove it from the embossing apparatus.
[B44B 5/026] Dies  B44B5/028  takes precedence -- B44B 5/026 pertains to dies used in machines or apparatus for embossing decorations or marks, with a note that B44B 5/028 takes precedence. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B44B 5/028] Heated dies -- Heated dies are temperature-controlled stamping tools used in embossing machines to apply decorative patterns, marks or reliefs onto surfaces such as coins, metal blanks or other workpieces. By heating the die, the process reduces the required force and improves metal flow, allowing finer detail and smoother finishes in artistic and commemorative applications.
[B44B 7/00] Machines, apparatus or hand tools for branding, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines, apparatus or hand tools for branding essentially by using heat to create a desing on the surface of a workpiece, e.g. by partially burning the surface layer
[B44B 7/002] in layered material -- B44B 7/002 pertains to machines, apparatus, or hand tools specifically designed for branding in layered materials. This classification is part of the broader category of Machines, apparatus or tools for artistic work and falls under the specific subcategory of branding equipment.
[B44B 7/005] by multi-step processes
[B44B 7/007] using a computer control means -- B44B 7/007 covers machines, apparatus or hand tools for branding that are operated by computer-control means, such as CNC or digitally programmed devices that apply marks, logos or patterns to material surfaces.
[B44B 7/02] Branding irons -- B44B 7/02 covers machines, apparatus and hand tools used for branding, i.e. applying a mark or emblem by heating a metal iron to imprint onto surfaces such as wood, leather, or livestock. These devices may be portable or stationary and are employed in decorative arts and identification. The classification falls under decorative arts, performing operations, and tools for artistic work.
[B44B 9/00] Machines or apparatus for inlaying with ornamental structures, e.g. tarsia or mosaic work -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Uniting ornamental elements on a support -> B44C1/28 - Uniting ornaments elements to structures -> B44C3/12 - Imitation of mosaic or tarsia work patterns -> B44F11/04
[B44C] PRODUCING DECORATIVE EFFECTS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes and apparatuses for producing decorative surface effects e.g. decalcomanias, for producing ornamental structures e.g. mosaics, and for producing special ornamental bodies, e. g. ornamental plaques so far as the matter produces an effect or mark meant to be judged by the eye and so far as such processes or apparatus are not specifically provided for elsewhere.  Paperhanging  **Glossary:** - Decorative effects: Covers also "information", when used in connection with the expressions "transfer picture" or "decalcomanias",
[B44C 1/00] Processes, not specifically provided for elsewhere, for producing decorative surface effects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  processes for altering locally the surface material, for applying flat material, for applying transfer pictures or the like, for applying ornamental structures, for applying plastic materials and superficially modelling their surface, for removing surface material, for pressing or stamping ornamental designs on surfaces, for inlaying with ornamental structures, for uniting ornamental elements on a support.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Paperhanging -> B44C7/00 - Decorating or marking of tyres -> B60C13/02 - Decalcomanias on ceramics -> C04B41/45 - Decorating textiles -> D06Q1/00
[B44C 1/005] by altering locally the surface material  surface treatment to obtain special artistic surface effects or finishes  B44D5/00 -- B44C 1/005 covers processes that locally alter a surface material or apply a surface treatment to create special artistic effects or finishes, such as texture, color variation, or other decorative modifications. It is a sub-category of producing decorative effects within the broader performing operations and decorative arts hierarchy.
[B44C 1/02] Pyrography -- B44C 1/02 covers pyrography, a process where designs are created on a surface by applying heat or fire to burn the material and produce decorative patterns. This falls under performing operations in decorative arts for producing decorative surface effects, and includes methods not otherwise classified.
[B44C 1/04] Producing precipitations -- The B44C 1/04 entry covers processes that generate decorative surface effects by forming precipitates, such as applying chemical solutions that react to produce solid particles or crystals on a material. It includes methods where the precipitate itself creates the visual pattern, texture or color, and is used where no more specific CPC subclass exists for such decorative treatments.
[B44C 1/10] Applying flat materials, e.g. leaflets, pieces of fabrics -- The CPC subclass B44C 1/10 covers processes for applying flat materials-such as leaflets, sheets of fabric or similar pieces-onto a surface to create a decorative effect. It falls under Performing Operations → Decorative Arts → Producing Decorative Effects and includes methods not specified elsewhere.
[B44C 1/105] comprising an adhesive layer  decalcomania  B44C1/16 ; adhesive tapes  C09J7/20 -- B44C 1/105 pertains to processes for creating decorative surface effects that involve the use of an adhesive layer, specifically addressing techniques related to decalcomania and adhesive tapes. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with producing decorative effects in the decorative arts.
[B44C 1/14] Metallic leaves or foils, e.g. gold leaf -- B44C 1/14 covers processes for applying metallic leaves or foils such as gold leaf to create decorative surface effects. It is classified under performing operations, decorative arts, and producing decorative effects for methods not otherwise specified in the CPC.
[B44C 1/145] Devices for applying metal leaves of foils -- B44C 1/145 covers tools and equipment specifically designed to apply metal-leaf foils onto surfaces in decorative-art processes. The devices may include brushes, rollers, applicator pads or similar implements that transfer thin metallic sheets to create ornamental finishes.
[B44C 1/16] for applying transfer pictures or the like -- B44C 1/16 covers processes for applying transfer pictures, decals or similar decorative images to a surface, typically as part of a decorative finishing operation. The methods may involve heat, pressure, adhesives or other means to transfer the image onto the substrate, enabling the creation of patterned or illustrated surfaces.
[B44C 1/162] Decalcomanias with a transfer layer comprising indicia with definite outlines such as letters and with means facilitating the desired fitting to the permanent base -- B44C 1/162 relates to decalcomania processes in which the transfer layer bears clearly defined outlines such as letters and includes mechanisms that aid accurate positioning and attachment to a permanent base, used for producing decorative surface effects.
[B44C 1/165] for decalcomanias -- This subclass covers processes for applying decalcomania, a method of transferring printed designs onto a surface to create decorative effects. It includes techniques for preparing, laminating and fixing the transferred image onto substrates such as glass, metal or ceramic where the process is not otherwise specified.
[B44C 1/17] Dry transfer -- B44C 1/17 Dry transfer covers processes for applying decorative patterns to a surface by moving an image from a carrier onto the target without using liquids, typically employing pressure, heat or adhesive activation. The method is used to create decorative surface effects in various materials where the pattern is pre-formed on a sheet and then transferred in a single step.
[B44C 1/1704] Decalcomanias provided with a particular decorative layer, e.g. specially adapted to allow the formation of a metallic or dyestuff layer on a substrate unsuitable for direct deposition  B44C1/1716 ,  B44C1/1737  take precedence -- The entry B44C 1/1704 covers processes for producing decalcomania where an intermediate decorative layer is applied to a substrate that cannot receive metal or dye deposits directly, enabling subsequent metallic or dyestuff layers. This subgroup refines and takes precedence over B44C 1/1716 and B44C 1/1737.
[B44C 1/1708] Decalcomanias provided with a layer being specially adapted to facilitate their release from a temporary carrier  B44C1/172 ,  B44C1/1741  take precedence -- B44C 1/1708 covers decalcomania sheets that include a specially formulated release layer to aid removal from a temporary carrier, used in processes for creating decorative surface effects. It falls within the broader hierarchy of Performing Operations - Decorative Arts - Producing Decorative Effects. The classifications B44C 1/172 and B44C 1/1741 are cited as preceding entries.
[B44C 1/1712] Decalcomanias applied under heat and pressure, e.g. provided with a heat activable adhesive -- The entry covers a process for creating decorative surface effects in which a decalcomania is transferred onto a material by applying heat and pressure, typically using an adhesive that becomes active when heated. This method falls within the broader category of decorative arts operations aimed at producing ornamental finishes on surfaces.
[B44C 1/1716] Decalcomanias provided with a particular decorative layer, e.g. specially adapted to allow the formation of a metallic or dyestuff layer on a substrate unsuitable for direct deposition -- B44C 1/1716 covers decalcomania that includes a special intermediate layer applied to a substrate that cannot directly receive metal or dye, allowing a metallic or coloured coating to be formed afterward. The method enables decorative layers to be attached to surfaces otherwise unsuitable for direct deposition.
[B44C 1/172] Decalcomanias provided with a layer being specially adapted to facilitate their release from a temporary carrier -- B44C 1/172 covers decalcomania products that include a specially designed release layer enabling the image to be transferred from a temporary carrier onto a surface, facilitating the application of decorative effects. The classification falls under performing operations in decorative arts for producing decorative surface effects.
[B44C 1/1725] using an intermediate support -- Processes for producing decorative surface effects in which an intermediate support or carrier is employed to transfer, apply or develop a coating, pattern or finish onto the final substrate, such as transfer printing, laminating or applying films that are later removed or bonded to create the decorative result. This approach is used when the decorative material is first placed on a temporary layer before being transferred to the ultimate product.
[B44C 1/1729] Hot stamping techniques -- Hot stamping techniques involve transferring a foil layer onto a substrate by applying heat and pressure, creating metallic or pigmented decorative finishes. The process uses a heated die, foil sheet, and sometimes a release coating, and is employed on paper, plastics, textiles and metal parts to achieve glossy, embossed, or reflective effects.
[B44C 1/1733] Decalcomanias applied under pressure only, e.g. provided with a pressure sensitive adhesive -- B44C 1/1733 covers processes for producing decorative surface effects by applying decalcomania solely through pressure, for example using a pressure-sensitive adhesive.
[B44C 1/1737] Decalcomanias provided with a particular decorative layer, e.g. specially adapted to allow the formation of a metallic or dyestuff on a substrate unsuitable for direct deposition -- B44C 1/1737 covers decalcomania processes where a special decorative layer is applied to enable metallic or dye deposition on substrates that cannot receive direct coating. The layer is formulated to receive the metallic or pigment after transfer, allowing decorative effects on otherwise unsuitable materials.
[B44C 1/1741] Decalcomanias provided with a layer being specially adapted to facilitate their release from a temporary carrier -- This class covers decorative transfer prints (decalcomanies) that include a special release layer designed to ease detachment from a temporary backing during application. It falls under processes for producing decorative surface effects in the decorative arts.
[B44C 1/1745] using an intermediate support -- The subclass covers processes that employ an intermediate support - such as a release film, carrier sheet, or backing material - to apply decorative finishes to a surface. It includes steps where the design is first placed on the support then transferred to the final product, enabling patterns, colors, or textures to be produced efficiently.
[B44C 1/175] Transfer using solvent -- A process for applying a decorative surface effect in which a design or coating is transferred onto a substrate with the aid of a solvent, the solvent dissolving or mobilising the material so it can migrate and adhere to the target surface. This method is classified under producing decorative effects within the broader group of decorative arts operations.
