[B44C 1/1752] Decalcomanias provided with a particular decorative layer, e.g. being specially adapted to allow the formation of a metallic or dyestuff layer on a substrate unsuitable for direct deposition  B44C1/1756 ,  B44C1/1758  take precedence -- B44C 1/1752 covers processes for producing decorative surface effects in which a decalcomania includes a specially adapted intermediate layer that permits the application of a metallic or dyestuff coating onto substrates that cannot receive such layers directly. The entry lies under performing operations - decorative arts - producing decorative effects and takes precedence over the related subclasses B44C 1/1756 and B44C 1/1758.
[B44C 1/1754] Decalcomanias provided with a layer being specially adapted to facilitate their release from a temporary carrier  B44C1/1756 ,  B44C1/1758  take precedence -- B44C 1/1754 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to decalcomanias featuring a specialized layer designed to ease their removal from a temporary carrier, with more specific adaptations detailed under codes B44C1/1756 and B44C1/1758.
[B44C 1/1756] Decalcomanias applied under heat and pressure, e.g. provided with a heat activable adhesive -- The entry B44C 1/1756 covers processes for applying decalcomania, a transferred printed design, to a surface by means of heat and pressure, typically using a heat-activable adhesive. It includes the equipment and steps required to bond the decal to materials such as ceramics, glass, metal or textiles to produce decorative surface effects.
[B44C 1/1758] Decalcomanias applied under pressure only, e.g. provided with a pressure sensitive layer -- The code B44C 1/1758 designates processes for applying decalcomania designs to a surface solely by pressure, such as using a pressure-sensitive layer, within the decorative arts field that creates decorative surface effects.
[B44C 1/18] Applying ornamental structures, e.g. shaped bodies consisting of plastic material -- B44C 1/18 covers processes for applying ornamental structures, such as shaped bodies made of plastic, to a surface to create a decorative effect. It is classified under performing operations in the decorative arts for producing decorative surface effects that are not specifically provided for elsewhere.
[B44C 1/20] Applying plastic materials and superficially modelling the surface of these materials -- This CPC subclass covers processes for applying plastic substances to a surface and shaping them on the surface to create decorative effects. It includes methods for coating, spreading, embossing or otherwise superficially modelling plastics to achieve ornamental finishes, where no more specific subclass applies.
[B44C 1/205] chemical modelling -- B44C 1/205 relates to processes that use chemical modelling to create decorative surface effects, such as simulating or formulating chemical reactions that produce specific patterns, textures, or colors on a substrate. It falls under performing operations in the decorative arts and covers methods not otherwise specified for producing ornamental finishes.
[B44C 1/22] Removing surface-material, e.g. by engraving, by etching -- B44C 1/22 covers processes that remove material from a surface to create decorative effects, such as engraving or chemical etching. It applies to any method of subtractive treatment used to produce patterns, textures or visual contrasts on items like ceramics, glass, metal or plastic.
[B44C 1/221] using streams of abrasive particles -- B44C 1/221 covers processes that employ streams of abrasive particles to create decorative surface effects, such as sand-blasting, abrasive jetting or similar techniques that modify the texture or appearance of a material for artistic or ornamental purposes.
[B44C 1/222] using machine-driven mechanical means -- This subclass covers processes that employ machine-driven mechanical means to create decorative surface effects, such as embossing, stamping, or other mechanically applied designs on articles. The methods are specifically for producing decorative effects where no more detailed subclass applies.
[B44C 1/224] manually -- B44C 1/224 covers manual methods for creating decorative surface effects, such as hand-applied painting, embossing, or other hand-operated techniques. The classification is used for processes not otherwise specified elsewhere in the decorative arts section.
[B44C 1/225] by engraving -- B44C 1/225 covers processes that create decorative surface effects by engraving, including techniques that remove material or incise patterns to form visual designs on various substrates. It falls within the broader category of decorative arts producing decorative effects.
[B44C 1/227] by etching -- This subclass covers processes that produce decorative surface effects by etching, whereby material is removed from a substrate through chemical or mechanical means to form patterns or designs, commonly applied to metal, glass, ceramic or similar decorative-arts materials.
[B44C 1/228] by laser radiation -- B44C 1/228 covers processes that use laser radiation to create decorative effects on surfaces, such as engraving, marking, or colour alteration, within the broader class of decorative arts operations. It applies when the laser method is not otherwise specified in the CPC.
[B44C 1/24] Pressing or stamping ornamental designs on surfaces -- The CPC subclass B44C 1/24 covers processes for applying ornamental designs to surfaces by pressing or stamping, used to create decorative effects on items such as textiles, paper or metal. It falls under performing operations, decorative arts, producing decorative effects and includes methods not otherwise specifically classified.
[B44C 1/26] Inlaying with ornamental structures, e.g. niello work, tarsia work -- Inlaying with ornamental structures, such as niello or tarsia work, involves embedding decorative materials into a surface to create patterned or figurative designs, often by carving or etching a recess and filling it with a contrasting medium.
[B44C 1/28] Uniting ornamental elements on a support, e.g. mosaics -- B44C 1/28 covers processes for attaching decorative elements to a substrate to create a unified ornamental surface, such as mosaics, in which separate pieces like stones, tiles or glass are bonded onto a support. It falls under producing decorative effects for decorative arts.
[B44C 3/00] Processes, not specifically provided for elsewhere, for producing ornamental structures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Superimposing layers, modelling plastic materials, sculpturing, stamping or bending, producing and filling perforations, uniting ornamental elements to structures.
[B44C 3/005] Removing selectively parts of at least the upper layer of a multi-layer article -- The subclass B44C 3/005 covers processes that selectively remove portions of the upper layer of a multilayer article to produce ornamental surface effects, such as by abrasion, etching, laser or other means, while leaving the underlying layers intact. It is used for decorative treatments where only the top coating or layer is patterned or eliminated.
[B44C 3/02] Superimposing layers -- This class covers processes for producing ornamental structures by applying one material layer over another to create decorative effects, such as coatings, prints, laminates, embossing or multi-layer painting. It includes any technique where layers are superimposed to achieve visual or textural enhancement of a surface.
[B44C 3/025] Superimposing layers to produce ornamental relief structures -- A process in which successive material layers are applied, bonded or fused to create raised ornamental patterns on a surface, such as embossing, laminating or layering techniques that generate relief structures for decorative purposes on items like fabrics, wallpapers, panels or other ornamental articles.
[B44C 3/04] Modelling plastic materials, e.g. clay -- The subclass B44C 3/04 covers processes for shaping plastic modelling materials such as clay to create ornamental structures, where the method is not otherwise specified. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations - Decorative Arts - Producing Decorative Effects.
[B44C 3/042] producing a copy from an original structure -- The subclass B44C 3/042 covers processes that duplicate an existing ornamental structure, such as casting, molding, imprinting or other methods used to create a copy of a decorative shape or pattern. It applies to any technique that reproduces the original design for decorative purposes.
[B44C 3/044] Chemical modelling -- B44C 3/044 covers processes that use chemical modelling to create ornamental structures or decorative effects, such as shaping, hardening or sculpting materials through chemical reactions, applied in the decorative arts and not classified elsewhere.
[B44C 3/046] using a modelling surface, e.g. plate -- B44C 3/046 relates to methods of producing ornamental structures in which a modelling surface such as a plate is employed to shape or form the decorative effect. The subclass falls under Performing Operations - Decorative Arts - Producing Decorative Effects and addresses processes not otherwise classified.
[B44C 3/048] applying an additional decorative element, e.g. picture -- This subclass covers processes for adding an extra decorative element such as a picture to an ornamental object or structure. It includes methods of applying printed, painted, embossed or otherwise attached designs after the base decorative work is completed, where the technique is not specified elsewhere.
[B44C 3/06] Sculpturing -- B44C 3/06 designates processes for sculpturing, covering methods of shaping, carving or forming material into three-dimensional ornamental structures used in the decorative arts. It includes techniques not specifically listed elsewhere for producing such decorative effects.
[B44C 3/08] Stamping or bending -- B44C 3/08 classifies processes used to create ornamental structures by stamping or bending material, typically metal, within the decorative-arts field. It covers methods that shape decorative elements through pressure or deformation rather than casting, forging or other techniques, and is applied where no more specific subclass exists for producing decorative effects.
[B44C 3/082] comprising a cutting out operation -- The CPC subclass B44C 3/082, situated in the decorative arts group for producing decorative effects, relates to processes that incorporate a cutting-out operation to form ornamental structures. It covers methods in which material is removed from a workpiece to achieve the desired decorative pattern or shape.
[B44C 3/085] stamping  B44C3/082  takes precedence -- B44C 3/085 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes of stamping for creating ornamental structures, with a note that B44C3/082 takes precedence. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European and United States patent offices to categorize inventions related to decorative arts.
[B44C 3/087] bending  B44C3/082  takes precedence -- B44C 3/087 pertains to processes involving the bending of materials to create ornamental structures, with a note that B44C3/082 takes precedence in classification. This code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO for decorative arts operations.
[B44C 3/10] Producing and filling perforations, e.g. tarsia plates -- B44C 3/10 covers processes for creating and filling perforated decorative structures, such as tarsia plates, where patterns are formed by cutting or punching holes and then inserting material to produce ornamental effects. It pertains to methods not otherwise classified for making ornamental panels with built-in openings that are subsequently filled or treated to achieve a decorative surface.
[B44C 3/12] Uniting ornamental elements to structures, e.g. mosaic plates -- This CPC subclass covers processes for joining ornamental components such as mosaic plates to a base or structure to create decorative surfaces. Methods may include adhesive bonding, mechanical fastening, or other techniques for securing the elements in place.
[B44C 3/123] Mosaic constructs -- This subclass covers processes for creating mosaic structures used as ornamental surfaces, including the laying, fixing and assembling of stone, glass, ceramic, metal or similar pieces into decorative patterns on walls, floors, panels or other substrates. It addresses methods not otherwise specified in the CPC for producing such decorative effects.
[B44C 3/126] Devices for making mosaic constructs automatically -- B44C 3/126 relates to automated equipment that builds mosaic constructions by positioning individual pieces according to a preset design, providing a mechanised process for creating ornamental structures.
[B44C 5/00] Processes for producing special ornamental bodies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  ornamental bodies comprising inserts, mountings for objects, e.g. pictures and of horns on plates, ornamental plaques, e.g. decorative panels and veneers, natural ornaments and imitations thereof, leaded lights
[B44C 5/005] comprising inserts -- The CPC subclass B44C 5/005 covers processes for making ornamental bodies that incorporate inserts, meaning decorative elements such as beads, gems, metal pieces or other materials are placed within or added to a base product during formation. It falls under performing operations in decorative arts, specifically producing special ornamental bodies.
[B44C 5/02] Mountings for pictures -- B44C 5/02 concerns the devices and methods used to mount pictures, photographs or artwork for display. It includes frames, hooks, brackets and other supporting structures that attach or hold decorative images in place, enabling their presentation as ornamental objects.
[B44C 5/04] Ornamental plaques, e.g. decorative panels, decorative veneers -- This CPC subclass deals with processes for producing ornamental plaques such as decorative panels or veneers that are applied to surfaces for visual effect, covering methods and apparatus used to create special ornamental bodies in the decorative arts.
[B44C 5/0407] containing glass elements -- B44C 5/0407 refers to processes for producing ornamental bodies that incorporate glass elements, such as beads, fragments or inserts, within decorative items. It falls under the CPC hierarchy PERFORMING OPERATIONS - DECORATIVE ARTS - PRODUCING DECORATIVE EFFECTS, covering methods that embed glass to achieve visual or functional effects.
[B44C 5/0415] containing metallic elements -- B44C 5/0415 falls under the CPC group for processes that create special ornamental bodies and specifies techniques that incorporate metallic elements into the decorative material. It covers methods such as applying metal powders, foils, or alloys and using metal-based finishes to achieve ornamental effects on surfaces.
[B44C 5/0423] containing fibreglass elements -- B44C 5/0423 covers processes for producing ornamental bodies that incorporate fiberglass elements, such as decorative panels, sculptures or other decorative objects where fiberglass provides strength or unique surface effects. The classification pertains to methods of shaping, curing or finishing these fiberglass-enhanced decorative items within the broader field of decorative arts.
[B44C 5/043] containing wooden elements -- This subgroup covers processes for making ornamental objects in which wooden components are incorporated, detailing how wood is combined with other decorative materials to produce special ornamental bodies. It applies to techniques used in the decorative arts for creating items such as carved or in-laid wooden parts within a larger decorative work.
[B44C 5/0438] containing stone elements -- This subclass covers processes for making ornamental objects that incorporate stone parts, such as embedding or attaching stones to ceramic, metal or other decorative bodies. It includes methods for shaping, fixing, or treating the stone within the finished decorative item.
[B44C 5/0446] bearing graphical information -- Processes for producing decorative objects that carry printed, drawn or otherwise reproduced images, patterns, or symbols on their surfaces, including methods of applying graphics to ornamental bodies such as ceramics, glass, metal or textiles. The entry covers techniques for integrating visual information into the decorative effect of the item.
[B44C 5/0453] produced by processes involving moulding -- B44C 5/0453 covers processes that create decorative bodies by moulding, where a material is shaped within a cavity or press to form an ornamental object. It includes techniques such as casting, injection or compression moulding used to produce special decorative effects.
[B44C 5/0461] used as wall coverings -- This subclass covers manufacturing processes for creating ornamental bodies that are applied as wall-coverings, such as decorative panels, tiles or laminates. It falls under the decorative arts branch of the CPC, focusing on special ornamental bodies used to produce decorative effects on walls.
[B44C 5/0469] comprising a decorative sheet and a core formed by one or more resin impregnated sheets of paper -- The subclass B44C 5/0469 details processes for creating decorative bodies in which a decorative sheet is bonded to a core formed from one or more paper sheets impregnated with resin. It addresses the manufacture of ornamental panels, laminates or similar items where the resin-impregnated paper provides structural support beneath a surface layer. This falls within the broader category of producing decorative effects in the decorative arts.
[B44C 5/0476] with abrasion resistant properties -- This CPC subclass covers processes for manufacturing ornamental objects or surfaces that are designed to be abrasion resistant. It includes methods and treatments that give decorative bodies such as enamel, coating or molded parts enhanced wear resistance while preserving their aesthetic appearance.
[B44C 5/0484] containing metallic elements -- This CPC subclass B44C 5/0484 covers processes that incorporate metallic elements into special ornamental bodies, such as metal-coated, metal-infused or metal-decorated items, to achieve decorative effects. It falls under performing operations, decorative arts, producing decorative effects, and processes for producing special ornamental bodies.
[B44C 5/0492] containing wooden elements -- This CPC subclass covers processes for producing special ornamental bodies that incorporate wooden components, such as carved or assembled wooden parts used in decorative objects. It is situated under performing operations, decorative arts, and producing decorative effects.
[B44C 5/06] Natural ornaments -- The B44C 5/06 class covers processes for producing ornamental bodies that incorporate natural motifs such as leaves, flowers, shells or other botanical or animal forms, typically applied as decorative surfaces or patterns on objects. It includes methods for shaping, arranging, fixing or treating these natural ornaments to create decorative effects on items ranging from ceramics to textiles.
[B44C 5/08] Leaded lights -- B44C 5/08 defines processes for producing leaded lights, i.e., stained-glass panels or decorative windows assembled with lead cames or similar binders. It covers methods of shaping, fitting, soldering and finishing glass pieces to create ornamental glazed bodies for architectural or artistic use.
[B44C 7/00] Paperhanging -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines, apparatus, tools and accessories for paperhanging, for applying adhesives, for applying the paper to the surface to be covered, for finishing operations.
[B44C 7/02] Machines, apparatus, tools or accessories therefor -- B44C 7/02 relates to machines, apparatus, tools or accessories employed for hanging paper, including frames, rollers and devices that position or tension paper in decorative applications. It is part of the decorative arts subclass that addresses producing decorative effects through paper hanging.
[B44C 7/022] Tapestry work -- Tapestry work in this classification covers the design, manufacture and mounting of woven decorative textiles used as ornamental wall hangings. It includes techniques for producing pictorial or patterned effects by interlacing threads on a fabric base, often applied as paper-hanging style decorations.
[B44C 7/025] for cutting wallpaper -- A device or method designed for cutting wallpaper during paperhanging, enabling precise trimming of patterned sheets to fit walls or architectural features. It falls under the decorative arts section of the CPC, addressing operations that produce decorative effects on surfaces.
[B44C 7/027] Machines, apparatus, tools or accessories for removing wall paper  cleaning, walls or wall coverings  A47L11/00 -- B44C 7/027 covers machines, apparatus, tools or accessories specifically designed to strip wallpaper, clean walls or remove other wall coverings, facilitating the preparation of surfaces for new decorative finishes. It falls under Performing Operations → Decorative Arts → Producing Decorative Effects → Paperhanging.
[B44C 7/04] for applying adhesive -- B44C 7/04 concerns the application of adhesive in paperhanging, detailing methods or devices that deposit glue onto paper or similar substrates to affix them to walls or other surfaces as part of decorative-arts processes that create ornamental effects.
[B44C 7/06] for applying the paper to the surface to be covered -- B44C 7/06 covers the process of applying paper or similar sheets to a surface, including the methods, tools and adhesives used to hang wallpaper or other decorative coverings. It falls under Performing Operations → Decorative Arts → Producing Decorative Effects → Paperhanging, and relates to techniques for securing the paper to walls, ceilings or other substrates.
[B44C 7/08] for finishing operations -- B44C 7/08 covers finishing operations in the decorative-arts field of paperhanging, including the final steps that secure, trim, smooth or otherwise complete the application of paper or similar materials to surfaces for decorative effect.
[B44D] PAINTING OR ARTISTIC DRAWING, NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special techniques in artistic painting or drawing and accessories and implements for use in connection with painting or artistic drawing, not otherwise provided for, surface treatments to obtain special artistic surface effects, preserving painting e.g. by varnishing.
[B44D 2/00] Special techniques in artistic painting or drawing, e.g. oil painting, water painting, pastel painting, relief painting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special techniques in artistic painting or drawing, e.g. oil painting, water painting, pastel painting, relief painting and use of kits including kits for drawing or painting, relief painting, use of stencil in painting or drawing operations.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Imitating brush strokes -> B44F11/02
[B44D 2/002] Kits for drawing or painting -- Kits designed for artistic drawing or painting that combine multiple supplies such as pigments, brushes, pens, canvases, paper or other media into a single package, enabling users to perform a complete painting or drawing activity.
[B44D 2/005] Relief painting  imitating brush strokes  B44F11/02 -- B44D 2/005 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers techniques for creating relief paintings that mimic brush strokes, a method used to add texture and depth to artistic works. This classification is specific to the decorative arts and falls under special painting or drawing techniques not covered elsewhere.
[B44D 2/007] using stencils in artistic drawing or painting operations -- The B44D 2/007 subclass concerns artistic painting or drawing techniques that employ stencils to apply or mask media, allowing reproducible patterns, shapes or designs in oil, water, pastel, relief or similar media. It includes the preparation, positioning, use and removal of stencils during the creative process.
[B44D 3/00] Accessories or implements for use in connection with painting or artistic drawing, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories or implements for use in connection with painting or artistic drawing, not otherwise provided for, e.g. devices for cleaning hand tools after use, palettes, paint boxes, implements for stirring or mixing paints, paint cans, brush holders, containers for storing residual paint or paint brushes, paint roller trays, covers or lids for paint cans, implements or apparatus for removing or dissolving dried paints, stretching frames for canvases, implements or apparatus for special techniques, e.g. painting lines, egg painting , lamps for baking lacquers, painter belts, cord line chalkers.
[B44D 3/003] Methods or devices for colour determination, selection or synthesis, e.g. use of colour tables -- The subclass covers tools and techniques that enable a painter or draftsman to identify, select or create specific colours, such as colour charts, mixing guides or devices that generate colour formulations for use in painting or artistic drawing. It falls under accessories or implements for painting or artistic drawing within the decorative arts field.
[B44D 3/006] Devices for cleaning paint-applying hand tools after use  removing dry paint from surfaces  B44D3/16 ; apparatus for dissolving dry paints  B44D3/24 ; devices for cleaning brushes  A46B17/06 ; cleaning in general  B08B -- B44D 3/006 covers devices used to clean paint-applying hand tools after use, including apparatus for removing dry paint from surfaces, dissolving dried paints, and cleaning brushes.
[B44D 3/02] Palettes -- The CPC subclass B44D 3/02 covers palettes, which are accessories or implements used in painting or artistic drawing that are not otherwise specified elsewhere. It falls under the hierarchical path Performing Operations → Decorative Arts → Painting or Artistic Drawing, Not Otherwise Provided For.
[B44D 3/04] Paint boxes -- Paint boxes are containers, either portable or stationary, used to store, protect and organize paints, pigments, brushes and related art supplies for artists. They are classified as accessories for painting or artistic drawing under CPC code B44D 3/04.
[B44D 3/06] Implements for stirring or mixing paints -- B44D 3/06 covers devices used to stir or blend paints and other artistic media, such as palette knives, mixing sticks, or mechanical agitators designed for artists. These implements are intended to improve uniformity of colour and consistency during painting or drawing.
[B44D 3/08] for liquid or semi-liquid paints -- The B44D 3/08 subclass covers accessories or implements designed specifically for applying liquid or semi-liquid paints in painting or artistic drawing. This includes tools such as brushes, rollers, pens, sprayers and other devices that facilitate the spread, control or manipulation of fluid pigments. It falls under the broader category of decorative arts equipment for painting not otherwise provided for.
[B44D 3/10] Sieves -- Sieves are mesh or perforated tools used in painting and artistic drawing to separate unwanted particles from pigments, inks, or other media, ensuring a uniform consistency and removing debris. They may be handheld or mounted and are employed to filter paints, grind pigments, or clean equipment, aiding in achieving smooth application and precise coloration.
[B44D 3/105] spatulas -- Spatulas are hand-held tools used in painting or artistic drawing to mix, spread, or apply paints, inks, pigments or varnishes. They typically consist of a flat, flexible blade attached to a handle, allowing artists to manipulate media on surfaces or palettes. This subclass covers any such implements not otherwise listed in the CPC.
[B44D 3/12] Paint cans -- Paint cans are containers intended to store liquid pigments, varnishes or other coating substances, typically with a sealing lid and an opening for dispensing the contents. They serve as accessories in painting or artistic drawing when no more specific container type is covered.
[B44D 3/121] Paint cans equipped with permanently attached brush holding means -- A paint container that incorporates a built-in mechanism for holding a brush, allowing the brush to remain attached to the can throughout use and storage, thus streamlining application and reducing the need for separate brush holders.
[B44D 3/122] having separate compartments for the different paint compounds -- This CPC sub-class B44D 3/122 covers accessories or implements used in painting or artistic drawing that provide separate compartments for different paint compounds, allowing the user to keep colors distinct during application. It falls under the decorative arts group for performing operations.
[B44D 3/123] Brush holders independent from paint can, e.g. holders removably attached to paint can -- The subclass B44D 3/123 covers brush-holding accessories that are separate from the paint can, such as detachable brackets or supports that can be mounted on or removed from a paint container, providing a way to store or position brushes without being built into the can itself.
[B44D 3/125] Containers for storing paint brushes and the like, separate from the can used in painting operation -- B44D 3/125 designates containers intended for holding paint brushes and similar implements apart from the paint can used during the painting operation, falling under the decorative-arts subclass for accessories in painting or artistic drawing.
[B44D 3/126] Paint roller trays -- B44D 3/126 covers paint roller trays, the shallow containers that hold paint or cleaning fluid for use with rollers during decorative painting tasks. These accessories are designed to facilitate even application and efficient cleaning of rollers in artistic or maintenance painting.
[B44D 3/127] Covers or lids for paint cans  covers or lids for containers of general type  B65D -- B44D 3/127 pertains to specialized covers or lids designed for paint cans, which are also applicable to general containers. This classification is part of the broader category of accessories and implements used in painting or artistic drawing that are not covered elsewhere. It intersects with B65D, which deals with closures for various types of containers.
[B44D 3/128] Wiping bars; Rim protectors; Drip trays; Spill catchers -- B44D 3/128 covers auxiliary devices such as wiping bars, rim protectors, drip trays and spill catchers used with painting or artistic drawing equipment. These items are intended to remove excess paint, protect the edges of tools and containers, and collect runoff to keep the work area clean.
[B44D 3/14] Holders for paint cans -- B44D 3/14 is a classification code within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO, specifically addressing holders designed for paint cans, which are accessories or implements related to painting or artistic drawing.
[B44D 3/16] Implements or apparatus for removing dry paint from surfaces, e.g. by scraping, by burning -- B44D 3/16 covers tools and devices intended to strip dry paint from surfaces, such as scrapers or burners that physically or thermally remove the coating. These implements are classified under accessories for painting and artistic drawing in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B44D 3/162] Scrapers -- Scrapers are accessories used in painting or artistic drawing to remove, spread, or smooth paint, ink, or other media on a surface. They are employed for surface preparation, correction of imperfections, or creating textured effects, and are not covered by more specific tool categories within the decorative arts classification.
[B44D 3/164] comprising blades -- B44D 3/164 covers accessories or implements used in painting or artistic drawing that incorporate one or more blades, such as palette knives, painting spatulas, scraper blades and similar blade-type tools for applying, mixing or removing media.
[B44D 3/166] by heating, e.g. by burning -- This subclass relates to accessories or implements used in painting or artistic drawing that operate by heating, for example tools that burn the surface to produce texture or colour effects.
[B44D 3/168] by electrically heating  air heaters using electric energy supply, the air being in direct contact with the heating medium,  F24H3/0405 -- B44D 3/168 pertains to accessories or implements for painting or artistic drawing that involve electrically heating air heaters. These heaters use an electric energy supply and have the air in direct contact with the heating medium, as specified by F24H3/0405.
[B44D 3/18] Boards or sheets with surfaces prepared for painting or drawing pictures -- B44D 3/18 covers boards, panels, or sheets whose surfaces have been treated or primed to receive paint or drawing media, such as primed canvas, prepared wooden panels, paperboard and similar substrates that are marketed as ready-to-use for artistic work. These items are classified as accessories or implements specifically prepared for painting or artistic drawing. It falls under the decorative arts section for painting or artistic drawing not otherwise provided for.
[B44D 3/185] Stretching frames for canvases -- B44D 3/185 covers devices used to stretch and secure canvas material on a frame for painting. The entry includes frames, hoops, or similar apparatus designed to tension canvas before or during artistic use. It falls under accessories for painting or artistic drawing not otherwise provided for.
[B44D 3/22] Implements or apparatus for special techniques, e.g. for painting lines, for pouring varnish -- Implements or apparatus for special painting techniques such as drawing precise lines or pouring varnish, used as accessories in painting or artistic drawing not covered elsewhere. These devices enable controlled application of liquids or fine-detail line work.
[B44D 3/225] Instruments or apparatus for painting lines -- B44D 3/225 covers devices and tools specifically designed to apply paint in the form of lines, such as line-drawing brushes, pens, rollers or other marking implements used in painting or artistic drawing. It falls under the decorative-arts group for accessories or implements for painting not otherwise provided for.
[B44D 3/24] Lamps for baking lacquers -- B44D 3/24 covers lamps designed for baking lacquers, used as accessories in painting or artistic drawing processes. These devices provide controlled heat to cure or dry lacquer coatings on artworks and are classified under decorative arts, painting or artistic drawing.
[B44D 3/38] Cord line chalkers -- B44D 3/38 covers cord-line chalkers, which are accessories used in painting or artistic drawing to hold and dispense chalk or similar marking material along a cord or string for creating straight or curved guide lines. These devices are classified under decorative arts, specifically painting or artistic drawing, when no other provision applies.
[B44D 5/00] Surface treatment to obtain special artistic surface effects or finishes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  special artistic surface effects or finishes e.g. by using chemical or electrochemical processes, crackle finishes, faux painting techniques
[B44D 5/10] Mechanical treatment -- B44D 5/10 designates mechanical treatment methods used to modify a surface for special artistic effects or finishes, such as rubbing, polishing, or sandblasting, in the context of decorative painting and other artistic drawing processes.
[B44D 7/00] Preserving paintings, e.g. by varnishing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preservation and also restoration of paintings.
[B44F] SPECIAL DESIGNS OR PICTURES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special designs or pictures produced by creating an effect or product meant to be judged by eye, whereby human emotions or intellect may be affected.  Designs are arrangements or patterns of elements or features of an artistic or decorative work, e.g. an ornamental pattern.  Pictures are visual representations or images of a subject or scene (e.g. a person, object or landscape) projected, painted, drawn or otherwise produced upon a surface.  The designs or pictures are characterised as special by particular technical features used to create the patterns or images or by characteristic features of patterns or images which provide the special effects, especially:  designs or pictures characterised by special or unusual light effects, e.g. produced by reflected or transmitted light, or characterised by colour effects;  designs characterised by outlines or by irregular areas, e.g. mottled patterns;  designs imitating three-dimensional effects, natural patterns or artistic work.  This subclass also covers processes for creating such designs or pictures insofar as they are not covered by other places.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Advertising or display means using special optical effects -> G09F19/12  **Glossary:** - Design: The arrangement or pattern of elements or features of an artistic or decorative work, especially for producing an effect or product meant to be judged by eye, whereby human emotions or intellect may be influenced or affected. - Picture: A visual representation or image of a subject or scene (e.g. a person, object or landscape) projected, painted, drawn or otherwise produced upon surface
[B44F 1/00] Designs or pictures characterised by special or unusual light effects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Designs or pictures produced by reflected or transmitted light e.g. as optical images and illusions and moiré and flickering effects, or characterised by colour or iridescent effects as colour shifting or rainbow effects  **Glossary:** - Moiré: A secondary and visually evident superimposed pattern created, for example, when two identical (usually transparent) patterns on a flat or curved surface (such as closely spaced straight lines drawn radiating from a point or taking the form of a grid) are overlaid while displaced or rotated a small amount from one another
[B44F 1/02] produced by reflected light, e.g. matt surfaces, lustrous surfaces -- B44F 1/02 covers designs or pictures whose visual effect is created by reflected light, including matte or lustrous surfaces that produce special or unusual light effects. It falls under the decorative-arts category for special designs or pictures characterized by such light-based appearances.
[B44F 1/04] after passage through surface layers, e.g. pictures with mirrors on the back -- B44F 1/04 covers designs in which light is transmitted through or reflected by surface layers to produce special visual effects, such as pictures that incorporate mirrors on the back or other transparent/reflective coatings. The classification is applied to decorative artworks where the appearance relies on the passage of light through the material rather than surface pigmentation alone.
[B44F 1/045] having mirrors or metallic or reflective layers at the back side -- This subclass covers decorative designs or pictures that achieve special light effects by incorporating mirrors, metallic or other reflective layers on the back side. The reflective backing creates visual contrast or sparkle, distinguishing the design within the broader category of special or unusual light-effect artworks.
[B44F 1/06] produced by transmitted light, e.g. transparencies, imitations of glass paintings -- B44F 1/06 classifies designs or pictures whose visual effect relies on transmitted light, including transparencies and imitations of glass paintings, where the artwork is viewed through light passing through the medium. This falls under decorative arts, special designs or pictures, and covers items that produce distinctive light effects by illumination from behind.
[B44F 1/063] Imitation of leaded light -- B44F 1/063 classifies decorative designs that imitate the visual effect of leaded glass lighting, covering patterns, pictures or ornamental elements that simulate the characteristic bright, segmented glow of leaded light.
[B44F 1/066] comprising at least two transparent elements, e.g. sheets, layers  layered products of glass and synthetic resin containing decorations or patterns  B32B17/10247 ; window units comprising two or more parallel glass panes  E06B3/66 -- This subclass covers decorative designs or pictures that create special or unusual light effects by using at least two transparent elements, such as laminated sheets or layers of glass combined with synthetic resin, or window units made of parallel glass panes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, decorative arts, and special designs or pictures.
[B44F 1/08] characterised by colour effects -- B44F 1/08 pertains to designs or pictures that achieve their visual impact primarily through colour effects, such as illumination, colour change, or special lighting arrangements, rather than shape or pattern alone. It is used for decorative items whose appearance depends on these colour-based visual treatments.
[B44F 1/10] Changing, amusing, or secret pictures -- B44F 1/10 covers designs or pictures that change appearance, create amusement, or reveal hidden images, often through optical, mechanical or light-dependent effects such as lenticular, flip-book, or secret-picture mechanisms.
[B44F 11/00] Designs imitating artistic work -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Designs imitating artistic work, e.g. oil paintings, imitation of mosaic or tarsia-work patterns, imitation of ceramic patterns
[B44F 11/02] Imitation of pictures, e.g. oil paintings -- B44F 11/02 covers decorative designs that replicate the appearance of artworks, such as oil-painting imitations, applied to textiles, carpets, or similar surfaces. It falls under the decorative arts hierarchy for special designs or pictures that mimic artistic works, guiding patent classification for fabric-based ornamental imitations.
[B44F 11/04] Imitation of mosaic or tarsia-work patterns -- This CPC subgroup B44F 11/04 covers designs that imitate mosaic or tarsia-work patterns, used in decorative objects or surfaces. It classifies ornamental designs where the appearance reproduces the look of assembled colored pieces such as stone, glass or ceramic tessellations rather than the actual material composition.
[B44F 11/06] Imitation of ceramic patterns -- B44F 11/06 classifies designs that copy the appearance of ceramic patterns, covering ornamental motifs, textures or decorative effects that mimic those traditionally found on pottery or tiles. It is situated under Performing Operations → Decorative Arts → Special Designs or Pictures → Designs imitating artistic work. This subclass includes reproductions of glaze effects, slip-decorated surfaces and other ceramic-style ornamentation applied to a variety of objects.
[B44F 1/14] Iridescent effects -- A design or picture that produces an iridescent effect, where colours shift or shimmer when viewed from different angles or under varying light, falls under CPC B44F 1/14. It is classified within the decorative-arts group for special designs that rely on unusual light phenomena to create a changing visual appearance.
[B44F 3/00] Designs characterised by outlines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Designs or pictures characterised by outlines
[B44F 5/00] Designs characterised by irregular areas, e.g. mottled patterns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Designs or pictures characterised by irregular areas, e.g. mottled, stochastic, orthogonally ambiguous, random patterns
[B44F 7/00] Designs imitating three-dimensional [3D] effects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  designs imitating three-dimensional effects  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group:
[B44F 9/00] Designs imitating natural patterns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  designs imitating natural patterns, e.g. wood grain, stone surface, horn, ivory, crystalline structures, pearl and mother-of-pearl effects, metallic or oxidised metallic surfaces, leather
[B44F 9/02] wood grain effects -- The CPC entry B44F 9/02 falls under Performing Operations / Decorative Arts / Special Designs or Pictures and addresses designs that imitate natural patterns, specifically wood-grain effects used to give a realistic timber appearance to surfaces such as paper, fabric, or other materials.
[B44F 9/04] of stone surfaces, e.g. marble -- This subclass covers decorative designs that imitate natural stone patterns such as marble on surfaces, including methods for applying or producing stone-like appearances on building and ornamental elements.
[B44F 9/06] of horn, ivory, or meerschaum surfaces -- B44F 9/06 covers decorative designs applied to surfaces made of horn, ivory or meerschaum, especially when those designs imitate natural patterns such as wood grain, animal hide or mineral textures. It falls under the decorative-arts group for special designs or pictures.
[B44F 9/08] of crystalline structures, pearl effects, or mother-of-pearl effects -- This CPC subclass deals with decorative designs that imitate the visual characteristics of crystalline structures, pearl or mother-of-pearl effects. It covers surface patterns where mineral glitter, iridescence or nacreous sheen is reproduced, such as on textiles, ceramics, wallpapers or other ornamental articles.
[B44F 9/10] of metallic or oxidised metallic surfaces -- B44F 9/10 covers decorative designs that imitate natural patterns-such as wood grain, stone, or other organic textures-applied to metallic or oxidised metallic surfaces. The classification pertains to special designs used in objects where the metal finish is patterned to resemble natural materials, often for aesthetic or branding purposes.
[B44F 9/12] of leather -- B44F 9/12 covers ornamental designs applied to leather that imitate natural patterns such as animal skin, wood grain, stone or other organic textures. It falls under the decorative arts hierarchy, specifically special designs or pictures that replicate natural motifs on leather surfaces. This classification is used to catalogue patents for patterned leather articles and related manufacturing processes.
[B44F 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- B44F 99/00 is a residual group within the Decorative Arts-Special Designs or Pictures subclass that captures any decorative or pictorial subject matter that does not fit into any of the defined groups of this subclass, serving as a catch-all for uncategorised designs.
